Notice: FR2 is not supported in CB012115.

## 5.4.3 Cell configuration

### 5.4.3.1 Overview

**5.4.3.1.0-1**  (ID: `11397310`)

Overview
The cell setup procedure is performed on per subcell basis after receiving the corresponding “UlCell\_SetupReq” message.

When the L1 SW receives the UlCell\_SetupReq message from the higher layer, it checks the incoming message about the processing subcell ID and creates the uplink subcell context. The L1 SW proceeds as follows:

1) Creation of an uplink subcell context.

2) Initialization of the L1-FPGA or L1 SoC.

3) Sending back the “UlCell\_SetupResp” message which contains the subcell ID on 8 bits.

Note 1: In this version of this requirement, it is assumed that this L1-FPGA or SoC initialization step is triggered by the reception of the UlCell\_SetupReq message at the L1-SW. Alternative solutions are acceptable, whereby this initialization step would be performed once for all (instead of once per subcell) e.g. as part of the board start-up procedure (out of the scope of this document). Whatever the solution, this initialization step must be completed before the L1-SW sends any UlCell\_SetupResp.

---

Loner and Loki
Sub-cell context creation by L1-SW

When the L1-SW receives the “UlCell\_SetupReq” message, a basic check (validity of the sub-cell ID) is done. If the check is successful, the L1-SW will create a sub-cell context as defined below. One sub-cell context can be done per “UlCell\_SetupReq” message. In 5G21B, “UlCell\_SetupReq” message carries one subcell.

In this sub-cell context, the following parameters are used for the PUSCH (the exact format of the parameters in the sub-cell context will be decided at L1-SW design, the notation [x.. y] is used below to indicate the allowed range of parameters, and the notation {a, b, c} is used to indicate a list of allowed values):

UlCell\_SetupReq message parameters:

 subcellId, [0.. ulSubcellIdMax].

The sub-cell ID “subcellId” will be configured in the L1-FPGA per subcell processing instance and used only for the response messages generation. Please refer to

5G\_UP\_1859 for the values supported for ulSubcellIdMax in each release.

 ulSubcellType {A2, A4, C2, C4,D2}.

This parameter defines the subcell type in UL. See ulSubcellType definition. In L1 release 5G21B, From 5G\_UP\_2142 it follows that the following subcell types are supported.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| TDD | FR1 | C2, C4 is supported. |
| FR2 | D2 is supported. |  |
| FDD |  | A4 and A2 are supported |

 physCellId, [0..1007].

This parameter is the physical-layer cell identity [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry close-up of text. The visible text appears to be "Ncell ID". The letters are in a sans-serif font and are colored with a gradient of blue, yellow, and orange. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_489.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0].

The Physical-layer Cell Identity is used to initialize pseudo-random sequence generators for PUSCH de-scrambling or for DMRS or PTRS generation (together with other parameters; see further details in requirement 3.4.4.3.4.1.3).

 ulBandwidth, see requirement 5G\_L1\_4221\_replaced([11397258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8xZRPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)): Table showing ulBandwidth supported in 5G.

This parameter defines the maximum number of uplink Physical Resource Block (PRB). See 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_920.

 cpriDialectIndication { CPRI-N, CPRI-A } Indicates CPRI type: CPRI-N or CPRI-A

See 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1558.

 scs, {15, 30, 120}.

The SCS parameter defines the sub carrier spacing. Table in the requirement 5G\_L1\_4221\_replaced([11397258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8xZRPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) provides the supported value for different cell bandwidths.

 rxScalingFactor, [-1300, -1000] (tenth of dBm).

RX Scaling factor for conversion of power values to dBm received on CPRI interface. The actual value to be applied for scaling is the rxScalingFactor / 10. (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_151) for CPRI.

Note: In case of eCPRI this value is not used, instead a hardcoded value of -1520 is used by the Radio module, please see requirement 5G\_L1\_3693\_replaced([11396980](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8vkGvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) section 9.1.1 for more details.

 phaseCompensationLutIndex,

OFDM symbol phase compensation look-up table index per OFDM symbol of a subframe for other than SS Block symbols (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_842). Note: phase compensation is done in FOC only for CPRI. In case of eCPRI it shall be performed at the Radio Unit.

 digitalOutputEnabled, bool {True, False}

Indicate whether the digital interface for Harq reporting is enabled for PUSCH conformance test.

 digitalOutputType, enum {serialKeysight, serialRs}

Defines the signal format to the Test Equipment for Harq reporting. Valid only when digitalOutEnabled is True. serialKeysight and serialRs (RS stands for Rohde & Schwarz) are supported in 5G21B.

 digitalOutputRate, enum {bitrate115dot2k, bitrate460dot8k, bitrate1dot92M}

Indicates the bit rate to TE. Valid only when digitalOutEnabled is True. If digitalOutType is serialRs, bitrate115dot2k and bitrate1dot92M are supported. Note: Please note that 115.2 kilobits is not enough for testing in FR2 (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1047).

 bbSelector, u8 [0..3]

Valid only when digitalOutEnabled is True.

 frequencyShift7pt5khz, bool {True, False}

If set to true, L1 needs to perform a frequency shift operation of -7.5 KHz before the FFT operation as it is specified in 38.104 §5.4.2.1.

For TDD, False value is supported.

For FDD, False value is supported for all ulBandwidth. True value is supported for ulBandwidth {52, 79, and 106} and False otherwise.

When frequencyShift7pt5khz = True, (value supported only for FDD 10, 15 and 20 MHz) the L1-FPGA (CPRI case) shall implement 7.5 kHz frequency shift on NR carriers. This shift shall be applied on I/Q data in time domaine before the FFT engine.

 ulsubcellPoolId u8 {0 .. ulSubcellPoolIdMax} Indicates the subcells that share eCPRI processing resources for virtual eAxCs. Used for FR1 TDD only in eCPRI mode. Max value defined in 5G\_UP\_1873.

 ulActDownSampling: bool {True, False}

Set to true to refer to the sampling rate reduction applied to the UL CPRI I/Q samples.

 ulIqCompression: bool {True, False}

Indicates whether µ-law compression (bit-width compression/de-compression) is active for IQ data on CPRI link (True - active, False - inactive).

Parameters from configuration files: (see requirement 5G\_L1\_3764\_replaced([11410621](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9z7FPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), section 3.4.4.12.3).

 pneRbThreshold: 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1419 provides all tables of combinations of rank and number of Rx. Each table is an array of 29 values. One integer value per MCS index (expressed in number of PRBs). Used when PTRS is enabled. See 5GMAX specification for details.

 pneAntThr: This value is used when comparing pne antenna-wise values and deciding if the stronger value is to be used instead of the antenna specific value.

[image-1][image\_desc]rdPuschThresholdsTableₓ[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_489.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] : The table number x with 43 values per table. 12 tables are provided in section “PUSCH DTX Detection” of the reference in requirement 5G\_L1\_3640\_replaced([11396785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV-_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the link to the Gerrit repository. Each table is associated to one configuration (see section “L1 PUSCH context creation in L1-SW”)

 riSelectAlphaThreshold. This parameter will be used by RI selection algorithm as in the reference in requirement 5G\_L1\_3640\_replaced([11396785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV-_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note that L1-SW shall provide the inverse of riSelectAlphaThreshold to L1-FPGA.

 rdFocEnabledFlag (rd\_foc\_enabled\_flag in "rd\_parameters.conf"): When this flag is TRUE FOC is performed and when FALSE FOC is not performed. This flag is for debug purpose only. Default value is TRUE.

Derived parameters:

 num\_of\_antennas (ant\_number from Programming model): Number of antennas streams per subcell. Derived from ulSubcellType {A2, A4, C2, C4, D2}. Set to 2 in case of A2, C2 and D2, set to 4 in case of A4 and C4.

 SYMBOL\_OFFSET : FFT offset[image-2][image\_desc](FFT offset)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_489.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]  representing the shift (in I/Q time domain samples) of FFT window towards CP.

[image-3][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the structure of an OFDM symbol with a cyclic prefix (CP). The OFDM symbol is divided into two parts: the CP and the main OFDM data. A curved arrow at the top indicates that the CP is a copy of the end portion of the OFDM symbol, which is prepended to the beginning of the symbol. Below the OFDM symbol, a dashed rectangle labeled "FFT window" shows the portion of the symbol that is processed by the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT). An arrow labeled "FFT time shift" indicates a delay or shift in the FFT window relative to the start of the CP. The CP is shown to extend beyond the start of the FFT window, and the FFT window is shown to extend beyond the end of the CP. The main OFDM data segment starts after the CP. The diagram visually represents how the cyclic prefix is used in OFDM systems to mitigate inter-symbol interference (ISI) caused by multipath propagation.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_489.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

Based on the reference in requirement 5G\_L1\_3640\_replaced([11396785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV-_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the FFT offset values are derived from FFT size ([image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N FFT" in a stylized font with a gradient of colors from blue to orange. The text is slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_489.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]) according to the following formula:

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$FFT\_{offset} = \frac{32 \* N\_{FFT}}{2048}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_489.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

 phase\_comp\_mem : L1-SW writes the complex coefficients to the L1-FPGA phase\_comp\_mem LUT, associated to the OFDM symbol phase compensation LUT indices in UlCell\_SetupReq message, corresponding to one subframe. These complex coefficients are noted by [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains mathematical notation. The text reads "K(Pμ(I))". The notation appears to be in a sans-serif font and is colored in shades of blue and white, with a slight yellow tint. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_489.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] where :

 Function [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry close-up of the letters "K(.)" in a blue and orange gradient. The background is black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_489.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]: Integer  complex float, is defined as

[image-8][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as: $K(n) = e^{j2\pi n/12288}$. The text is in a light blue color against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_489.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8], where [image-9][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, blurry shot of what appears to be a digital display or screen. The colors are muted and blend into each other, with hints of blue, purple, and orange. There are vertical lines that suggest a grid or pixelated structure. The overall impression is of abstract, colorful light. It is not possible to discern any specific objects or text due to the extreme blurriness.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_489.010.png[/image\_path][/image-9] is the index of the IFFT, [image-10][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "n ∈ {0, ..., 12287}". This indicates that 'n' is an element of the set of integers starting from 0 and going up to 12287, inclusive.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_489.011.png[/image\_path][/image-10] (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1325).

 Function [image-11][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical notation "Pμ(.)". The letter "P" is capitalized and followed by a subscript "μ". To the right of the subscript, there is an opening parenthesis, a dot inside the parenthesis, and a closing parenthesis, followed by a colon. The text is slightly blurry and has a reddish-blue hue.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_489.012.png[/image\_path][/image-11] Integer  Integer, is defined as

o  [image-12][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a light blue color against a white background. The expression reads "Pμ(I) =". The letter "P" is capitalized, followed by a subscript "μ" which is a Greek letter. Then, there is an opening parenthesis, followed by the letter "I", and then a closing parenthesis.  HARQ\_DDR\_INHIBIT: This flag, when set to true, allows to deactivate decoder HARQ DDR access to use DDR. This flag is used for debug purpose (HARQ combining not performed in this mode). This flag is used by all Rx decoder engines. Supported value of this flag are {True, False}.

 HARQ\_ALLOC\_MODE: A flag to determine if the HARQ address used by the decoder is computed by L1-SW or L1-FPGA. This flag is used by all Rx decoder engines. Supported value of this flage are {True, False}. When this flag equals to 1, L1-SW supersedes FPGA HARQ buffer allocation management in DDR (default mode, flag=0).

**Subcell context creation by L1-SW**

Upon reception of UlCell\_SetupReq message, 5G-L1-UL shall check the validity of subcell ID. If the check is successful, 5G-L1-UL shall create a subcell context as defined below. One subcell context shall be created for each UlCell\_SetupReq message. A valid UlCell\_SetupReq message carries at least one subcell.

In the subcell context, the following parameters are used for PUSCH. The exact format of the parameters in the subcell context will be decided at L1-SW design. The notation [x.. y] is used below to indicate the allowed range of parameters, and the notation {a, b, c} is used to indicate a list of allowed values.

UlCell\_SetupReq message parameters:

 subcellId, [0.. ulSubcellIdMax].

The subcell ID “subcellId” will be configured per subcell processing instance and used only for the response messages generation. Please refer to [11375909: ulSubcellIdMax maximum value](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueQLKvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw) in 5G UP System Level for the supported values of ulSubcellIdMax in each release.

 ulSubcellType {A2, A4, [CB013442-D] A8, [End CB013442-D] C2, C4, C8, D2}.

This parameter defines the subcell type in UL. See ulSubcellType definition. The following subcell types are supported.

TDD FR1: C2, C4, C8

TDD FR2: D2

FDD: A2, A4, [CB013442-D] A8 [End CB013442-D]

 physCellId, [0..1007].

This parameter is the physical-layer cell identity [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains blurry text. The visible text appears to be "Ncell" and "ID".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_490.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0].

The Physical-layer Cell Identity is used to initialize pseudo-random sequence generators for PUSCH de-scrambling (together with other parameters; see further details in requirement 3.4.4.3.4.1.3).

 ulBandwidth, see requirement [12556505: Supported Bandwidths](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_fpy4wCdcEfCNjfqfyUlvmw). This parameter defines the maximum number of uplink Physical Resource Block (PRB).

 cpriDialectIndication {CPRI-N, CPRI-A } Indicates CPRI type: CPRI-N or CPRI-A

See 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1558.

 scs, {15, 30, 120}.

The SCS parameter defines the sub carrier spacing. Table in the requirement 5G\_L1\_4221\_replaced([11397258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8xZRPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) provides the supported value for different cell bandwidths.

 rxScalingFactor, [-1300, -1000] (tenth of dBm).

RX Scaling factor for conversion of power values to dBm received on CPRI interface. The actual value to be applied for scaling is the rxScalingFactor / 10. (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_151) for CPRI.

Note: In case of eCPRI this value is not used, instead a hardcoded value of -1520 is used by the Radio module, please see requirement 5G\_L1\_3693\_replaced([11396980](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8vkGvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) section 9.1.1 for more details.

 phaseCompensationLutIndex,

OFDM symbol phase compensation look-up table index per OFDM symbol of a subframe for other than SS Block symbols (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_842). Note: phase compensation is done in FOC only for CPRI. In case of eCPRI it shall be performed at the Radio Unit.

 digitalOutputEnabled, bool {True, False}

Indicate whether the digital interface for Harq reporting is enabled for PUSCH conformance test.

 digitalOutputType, enum {serialKeysight, serialRs}

Defines the signal format to the Test Equipment for Harq reporting. Valid only when digitalOutEnabled is True. serialKeysight and serialRs (RS stands for Rohde & Schwarz) are supported in 5G21B.

 digitalOutputRate, enum {bitrate115dot2k, bitrate460dot8k, bitrate1dot92M}

Indicates the bit rate to TE. Valid only when digitalOutEnabled is True. If digitalOutType is serialRs, bitrate115dot2k and bitrate1dot92M are supported. Note: Please note that 115.2 kilobits is not enough for testing in FR2 (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1047).

 bbSelector, u8 [0..3]

Valid only when digitalOutEnabled is True.

 frequencyShift7pt5khz, bool {True, False}

If set to true, L1 needs to perform a frequency shift operation of -7.5 KHz before the FFT operation as it is specified in 38.104 §5.4.2.1.

For TDD, False value is supported.

For FDD, False value is supported for all ulBandwidth. True value is supported for ulBandwidth {52, 79, and 106} and False otherwise.

When frequencyShift7pt5khz = True, (value supported only for FDD 10, 15 and 20 MHz) the L1-FPGA (CPRI case) shall implement 7.5 kHz frequency shift on NR carriers. This shift shall be applied on I/Q data in time domaine before the FFT engine.

 ulsubcellPoolId u8 {0 .. ulSubcellPoolIdMax} Indicates the subcells that share eCPRI processing resources for virtual eAxCs. Used for FR1 TDD only in eCPRI mode. Max value defined in 5G\_UP\_1873.

Parameters from configuration files: (see requirement 5G\_L1\_3764\_replaced([11410621](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9z7FPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), section 3.4.4.12.3).

 pneRbThreshold: 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1419 provides all tables of combinations of rank and number of Rx. Each table is an array of 29 values. One integer value per MCS index (expressed in number of PRBs). Used when PTRS is enabled. See 5GMAX specification for details.

 pneAntThr: This value is used when comparing pne antenna-wise values and deciding if the stronger value is to be used instead of the antenna specific value.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "rdPuschThresholdsTableₓ". The text is in a light gray color with a slight blue tint and is slightly blurred. The letters are capitalized and lowercase. The "x" at the end of the text is a subscript.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_490.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] : The table number x with 43 values per table. 12 tables are provided in section “PUSCH DTX Detection” of the reference in requirement 5G\_L1\_3640\_replaced([11396785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV-_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the link to the Gerrit repository. Each table is associated to one configuration (see section “L1 PUSCH context creation in L1-SW”)

 riSelectAlphaThreshold. This parameter will be used by RI selection algorithm as in the reference in requirement 5G\_L1\_3640\_replaced([11396785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV-_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note that L1-SW shall provide the inverse of riSelectAlphaThreshold to L1-FPGA.

 rdFocEnabledFlag (rd\_foc\_enabled\_flag in "rd\_parameters.conf"): When this flag is TRUE FOC is performed and when FALSE FOC is not performed. This flag is for debug purpose only. Default value is TRUE.

Derived parameters:

 num\_of\_antennas (ant\_number from Programming model): Number of antennas streams per subcell. Derived from ulSubcellType {A2, A4, [CB013442-D] A8, [End CB013442-D] C2, C4, C8, D2}. It is set to 2 in case of A2, C2 and D2. It is set to 4 in case of A4 and C4. It is set to 8 in case of C8 [CB013442-D] and A8 **[End CB013442-D]**, [CB008465-AA] or in case of A4 with UL CoMP enabled (isNrUlCompEnabled = True in L1Config\_SwConfigurationReq message [11584802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BPk5LPwGEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with UEs configured for joint reception (mode = JointReception in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message [11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008465-AA]\*\*\*\*

 SYMBOL\_OFFSET : FFT offset[image-2][image\_desc](FFT offset)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_490.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]  representing the shift (in I/Q time domain samples) of FFT window towards CP.

[image-3][image\_desc]The image depicts a diagram illustrating the concept of an OFDM (Orthogonal Frequency-Division Multiplexing) symbol with a cyclic prefix (CP). The diagram shows a rectangular block representing the OFDM symbol, divided into two sections. The left section is labeled "CP" and represents the cyclic prefix. The right section is labeled "OFDM" and represents the main OFDM data.
A dashed rectangle outlines the "FFT window," which is positioned to cover the OFDM data portion and a portion of the cyclic prefix. An arrow curves from the end of the OFDM data section, over the CP section, and points to the beginning of the FFT window, indicating the cyclic nature of the CP.
Below the main block, a horizontal line with an arrow indicates "FFT time shift," showing the offset of the FFT window relative to the start of the OFDM symbol. The FFT window starts after the CP and extends into the OFDM data. The dashed lines indicate the boundaries of the FFT window.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_490.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

Based on the reference in requirement 5G\_L1\_3640\_replaced([11396785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV-_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the FFT offset values are derived from FFT size ([image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N FFT" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient effect, transitioning from blue to orange. The background is white, and the text is centered within the frame.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_490.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]) according to the following formula:

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$FFT\_{offset} = \frac{32 \* N\_{FFT}}{2048}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_490.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

The text is in a light blue color.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_490.021.png[/image\_path][/image-21]  value in 5G21B.

 HARQ\_DDR\_INHIBIT: This flag, when set to true, allows to deactivate decoder HARQ DDR access to use DDR. This flag is used for debug purpose (HARQ combining not performed in this mode). This flag is used by all Rx decoder engines. Supported value of this flag are {True, False}.

 HARQ\_ALLOC\_MODE: A flag to determine if the HARQ address used by the decoder is computed by L1-SW. This flag is used by all Rx decoder engines. Supported value of this flage are {True, False}. When this flag equals to 1, L1-SW supersedes FPGA HARQ buffer allocation management in DDR (default mode, flag=0).

---

**Subcell context creation by L1-SW**

Upon reception of UlCell\_SetupReq message, 5G-L1-UL shall check the validity of subcell ID. If the check is successful, 5G-L1-UL shall create a subcell context as defined below. One subcell context shall be created for each UlCell\_SetupReq message. A valid UlCell\_SetupReq message carries at least one subcell.

In the subcell context, the following parameters are used for PUSCH. The exact format of the parameters in the subcell context will be decided at L1-SW design. The notation [x.. y] is used below to indicate the allowed range of parameters, and the notation {a, b, c} is used to indicate a list of allowed values.

UlCell\_SetupReq message parameters:

 subcellId, [0.. ulSubcellIdMax].

The subcell ID “subcellId” will be configured per subcell processing instance and used only for the response messages generation. Please refer to [11375909: ulSubcellIdMax maximum value](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueQLKvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw) in 5G UP System Level for the supported values of ulSubcellIdMax in each release.

 ulSubcellType {C2, C4, C8}.

This parameter defines the subcell type in UL. See ulSubcellType definition. The following subcell types are supported.

TDD FR1: C2, C4, C8

 physCellId, [0..1007].

This parameter is the physical-layer cell identity [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains blurry text. The visible text appears to be "Ncell" and "ID".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_490.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0].

The Physical-layer Cell Identity is used to initialize pseudo-random sequence generators for PUSCH de-scrambling (together with other parameters; see further details in requirement 3.4.4.3.4.1.3).

 ulBandwidth, see requirement [12556505: Supported Bandwidths](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_fpy4wCdcEfCNjfqfyUlvmw). This parameter defines the maximum number of uplink Physical Resource Block (PRB).

 cpriDialectIndication {CPRI-N, CPRI-A } Indicates CPRI type: CPRI-N or CPRI-A

See 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1558.

 scs, {15, 30, 120}.

The SCS parameter defines the sub carrier spacing. Table in the requirement 5G\_L1\_4221\_replaced([11397258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8xZRPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) provides the supported value for different cell bandwidths.

 rxScalingFactor, [-1300, -1000] (tenth of dBm).

RX Scaling factor for conversion of power values to dBm received on CPRI interface. The actual value to be applied for scaling is the rxScalingFactor / 10. (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_151) for CPRI.

Note: In case of eCPRI this value is not used, instead a hardcoded value of -1520 is used by the Radio module, please see requirement 5G\_L1\_3693\_replaced([11396980](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8vkGvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) section 9.1.1 for more details.

 phaseCompensationLutIndex,

OFDM symbol phase compensation look-up table index per OFDM symbol of a subframe for other than SS Block symbols (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_842). Note: phase compensation is done in FOC only for CPRI. In case of eCPRI it shall be performed at the Radio Unit.

 digitalOutputEnabled, bool {True, False}

Indicate whether the digital interface for Harq reporting is enabled for PUSCH conformance test.

 digitalOutputType, enum {serialKeysight, serialRs}

Defines the signal format to the Test Equipment for Harq reporting. Valid only when digitalOutEnabled is True. serialKeysight and serialRs (RS stands for Rohde & Schwarz) are supported in 5G21B.

 digitalOutputRate, enum {bitrate115dot2k, bitrate460dot8k, bitrate1dot92M}

Indicates the bit rate to TE. Valid only when digitalOutEnabled is True. If digitalOutType is serialRs, bitrate115dot2k and bitrate1dot92M are supported. Note: Please note that 115.2 kilobits is not enough for testing in FR2 (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1047).

 bbSelector, u8 [0..3]

Valid only when digitalOutEnabled is True.

 frequencyShift7pt5khz, bool {True, False}

If set to true, L1 needs to perform a frequency shift operation of -7.5 KHz before the FFT operation as it is specified in 38.104 §5.4.2.1.

For TDD, False value is supported.

For FDD, False value is supported for all ulBandwidth. True value is supported for ulBandwidth {52, 79, and 106} and False otherwise.

When frequencyShift7pt5khz = True, (value supported only for FDD 10, 15 and 20 MHz) the L1-FPGA (CPRI case) shall implement 7.5 kHz frequency shift on NR carriers. This shift shall be applied on I/Q data in time domaine before the FFT engine.

 ulsubcellPoolId u8 {0 .. ulSubcellPoolIdMax} Indicates the subcells that share eCPRI processing resources for virtual eAxCs. Used for FR1 TDD only in eCPRI mode. Max value defined in 5G\_UP\_1873.

Parameters from configuration files: (see requirement 5G\_L1\_3764\_replaced([11410621](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9z7FPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), section 3.4.4.12.3).

 pneRbThreshold: 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1419 provides all tables of combinations of rank and number of Rx. Each table is an array of 29 values. One integer value per MCS index (expressed in number of PRBs). Used when PTRS is enabled. See 5GMAX specification for details.

  
pneAntThr: This value is used when comparing pne antenna-wise values and deciding if the stronger value is to be used instead of the antenna specific value.

  rdPuschThresholdsTable The table number x with 43 values per table. 12 tables are provided in section “PUSCH DTX Detection” of 5GMax Algorithm Specification for the link to the Gerrit repository. Each table is associated to one configuration (see section [sec-PuschDtxDet](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschDtxDet)).

 riSelectThrd. This parameter will be used by RI selection algorithm as in 5GMax Algorithm Specification (see section [sec-RiSelectRiSe](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-RiSelectRiSe)).

 rdFocEnabledFlag (rd\_foc\_enabled\_flag in "rd\_parameters.conf"): When this flag is TRUE FOC is performed and when FALSE FOC is not performed. This flag is for debug purpose only. Default value is TRUE.

Derived parameters:

 num\_of\_antennas (ant\_number from Programming model): Number of antennas streams per subcell. Derived from ulSubcellType {C2, C4, C8}. It is set to 2 in case of C2, set to 4 in case of C4, and set to 8 in case of C8.

 SYMBOL\_OFFSET : FFT offset[image-1][image\_desc](FFT offset)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_490.003.png[/image\_path][/image-1]  representing the shift (in I/Q time domain samples) of FFT window towards CP.

[image-2][image\_desc]The image depicts a diagram illustrating the concept of an OFDM (Orthogonal Frequency-Division Multiplexing) symbol with a cyclic prefix (CP). The diagram shows a rectangular block representing the OFDM symbol, divided into two sections. The left section is labeled "CP" and represents the cyclic prefix. The right section is labeled "OFDM" and represents the main OFDM data.
A dashed rectangle outlines the "FFT window," which is positioned to cover the OFDM data portion and a portion of the cyclic prefix. An arrow curves from the end of the OFDM data section, over the CP section, and points to the beginning of the FFT window, indicating the cyclic nature of the CP.
Below the main block, a horizontal line with an arrow indicates "FFT time shift," showing the offset of the FFT window relative to the start of the OFDM symbol. The FFT window starts after the CP and extends into the OFDM data. The dashed lines indicate the boundaries of the FFT window.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_490.004.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

Based on the reference in requirement 5G\_L1\_3640\_replaced([11396785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV-_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the FFT offset values are derived from FFT size ([image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N FFT" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient effect, transitioning from blue to orange. The background is white, and the text is centered within the frame.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_490.005.png[/image\_path][/image-3]) according to the following formula:

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$FFT\_{offset} = \frac{32 \* N\_{FFT}}{2048}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_490.006.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

 phase\_comp\_mem : L1-SW writes the complex coefficients to the L1-FPGA phase\_comp\_mem LUT, associated to the OFDM symbol phase compensation LUT indices in UlCell\_SetupReq message, corresponding to one subframe. Following "Pμ" is another opening parenthesis, then the letter "I", and finally two closing parentheses.  N\_BAND: set to [image-19][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Nband" with a slight blur and chromatic aberration, giving the letters a subtle rainbow effect. All subcells are expected to have the same [image-20][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Nband" with a slight blur and chromatic aberration, giving the letters a subtle rainbow effect. The text is in a light blue color.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_490.021.png[/image\_path][/image-20]  value in 5G21B.

 HARQ\_DDR\_INHIBIT: This flag, when set to true, allows to deactivate decoder HARQ DDR access to use DDR. This flag is used for debug purpose (HARQ combining not performed in this mode). This flag is used by all Rx decoder engines. Supported value of this flag are {True, False}.

 HARQ\_ALLOC\_MODE: A flag to determine if the HARQ address used by the decoder is computed by L1-SW. This flag is used by all Rx decoder engines. Supported value of this flage are {True, False}. When this flag equals to 1, L1-SW supersedes FPGA HARQ buffer allocation management in DDR (default mode, flag=0).

---

### 5.4.3.3 L1-FPGA subcell parameters initialization

**5.4.3.3.0-1**  (ID: `11397545`)

L1-FPGA subcell parameters initialization
When sub-cell context creation is completed, the L1-SW will initialize the L1-FPGA PUSCH releated registers and memory. Please for the complete list of registers of L1-FPGA, refere to the corresponding programming model as in the reference in requirement 5G\_L1\_3713\_replaced([11397003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8vkIfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In addition, L1-SW shall provide the initial configuration parameters of the UL output buffer used to host response messages for L2. These initial configuration parameters include start addresses and buffer length (see “Configuration interface” section in the reference in requirement 5G\_L1\_3705\_replaced([11396884](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8u9CvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

L1-SW shall write the DTX UCI table in the respective registers at decoder setup.

---

### 5.4.3.4 Configuration for error reporting

**5.4.3.4.0-1**  (ID: `11397558`)

Configuration for error reporting
After cell set-up, L1SW shall fill the timeout detection values of PUSCH block depending on the numerology as follows:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Register name | Field name | FR1-FDD | FR1-TDD | FR2-TDD |
| DEBUG\_PUSCH\_CFG0 | FIFO\_FULL\_TIMEOUT | 20000 | 10000 | 2500 |
| DEBUG\_PUSCH\_CFG1 | PUSCH\_DMRS\_TIMEOUT | 20000 | 10000 | 2500 |
| DEBUG\_PUSCH\_CFG1 | PUSCH\_DATA\_PTRS\_TIMEOUT | 20000 | 10000 | 2500 |
| DEBUG\_PUSCH\_CFG2 | PUSCH\_DFTSOFDM\_TP0\_TIMEOUT | 3000 | N/A | N/A |
| DEBUG\_PUSCH\_CFG2 | PUSCH\_DFTSOFDM\_TP1\_TIMEOUT | 7500 | N/A | N/A |

---

## 5.4.4 Dynamic Configuration

### 5.4.4.1 Supported PUSCH Configuration

#### 5.4.4.1.1 For 7-2a split

##### 5.4.4.1.1.1 FDD

##### 5.4.4.1.1.2 TDD

#### 5.4.4.1.2 For 7-2e split

##### 5.4.4.1.2.1 Thor

**5.4.4.1.2.1.0-5**  (ID: `11397640`)

Thor
For subcell type other than C8'8:

5G-L1-UL(BB) shall support PUSCH reception of **1 UE in FR1-TDD-eCPRI-7-2e in the following configuration:**

* subcell type **[Before CB008235-SR-C] C4'2**, [End CB008235-SR-C] C8'2, C8'4\*\*\*\*
* 1 or 2 layers per UE,
* CP-OFDM waveform,
* PTRS disabled,
* unrestricted MCS range**,**

Multiplexing with long PUCCH and PRACH is supported.

Frequency-domain multiplexing of multiple UEs is supported (see 5G\_L1\_15199\_replaced([11409412](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9t0dfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For subcell type C8'8:

5G-L1-UL(BB) shall support PUSCH reception of **[Before CB008235-SR-C] 9 [CB008235-SR-C] 16 [End CB008235-SR-C] UE in FR1-TDD-eCPRI-7-2e in the following cofiguration:**

* subcell type C8'8
* 1 or 2 layers per UE,
* CP-OFDM waveform,
* PTRS disabled,
* unrestricted MCS range**,**

Multiplexing with long PUCCH and PRACH is supported.

Frequency-domain multiplexing of multiple UEs is supported (see 5G\_L1\_15199\_replaced([11409412](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9t0dfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

##### 5.4.4.1.2.2 Odin

**5.4.4.1.2.2.0-1**  (ID: `12788733`)

Odin
5G-L1-UL(BB) shall support PUSCH reception in FR1-TDD-eCPRI-7-2e in the following configuration:

* subcell types C8'2 and C8'4,
* 1 or 2 layers per UE,
* CP-OFDM waveform,
* PTRS disabled,
* unrestricted MCS range.

Multiplexing with long PUCCH and PRACH is supported.

Frequency-domain multiplexing of multiple UEs is supported. (See [11409412](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9t0dfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for the supported number of scheduled UEs.)

---

#### 5.4.4.1.3 Multiplexing with other channels

##### 5.4.4.1.3.0-2 Multiplexing with other channels 5G-L1-UL shall support long PUCCH, PUSCH, and PRACH in the same slot as requested by scheduler. The requirement is common for all Technologies. (ID: 12709909)

### 5.4.4.2 PUSCH messages

#### 5.4.4.2.1 Legacy Requirements for Loner (legacy, status=Proposed, to be verified)

##### 5.4.4.2.1.1 Addressing procedure

**5.4.4.2.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `11397678`)

Addressing procedure
When the L1-SW receives the “UlData\_AddressReq” message, it checks that the cell setup procedure is completed, and sends the “UlData\_AddressResp” message to the 5G-L2-PS with the following information:

• The sub-cell identity.

• puschReceiveReqAddress, corresponding to the EQID for the “UlData\_PuschReceiveReq” message.

At this step, the L1-SW can manage the requests for a PUSCH reception procedure.

---

##### 5.4.4.2.1.2 Slot configuration procedure

###### 5.4.4.2.1.2.0-1 Slot configuration procedure To allow the PUSCH reception for a given NR-slot, the L1-SW must receive a slot configuration through “UlData_PuschReceiveReq” message for that NR-slot. (the order of reception does not matter). Note: Slot configuration is needed by 5G-L1-UL to know in advance about which type of channel is going to occur in each NR-slot. (ID: 11397692)

##### 5.4.4.2.1.3 Timing validation

**5.4.4.2.1.3.0-1**  (ID: `11397706`)

Timing validation
Upon reception of the UlData\_PuschReceiveReq, L1-SW checks if the message was received in the expected timing window (see timing requirements in the requirement 5G\_L1\_3751\_replaced([11493644](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fEi1M_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Detection and handling of late or missing UlData\_PuschReceiveReq messages are described in document [see requirement 5G\_L1\_3673\_replaced([11396896](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8u9DPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ]. Only basic principles are summarized below:

L1-SW detects abnormal situations occurring on the L2-L1 interface.

L1-FPGA may detect abnormal situations occurring on the interface between L1-SW and L1-FPGA.

L1-SW checks if UlData\_PuschReceiveReq arrives within the specified timing window (see requirement 5G\_L1\_5131\_replaced([11409625](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9vClPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for CPRI case and the requirement 5G\_L1\_3753\_replaced([11409692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9vCnfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for eCPRI case). If not:

• L1-SW increments a counter of messages which were not received within specified time window.

• As per DOORS requirement 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1457, L1-SW shall print a warning or an error into the log file, and no response message shall be sent to L2.

• PUSCH processing parameters are dropped (not written into the L1-FPGA).

L1-FPGA checks if PUSCH receiver parameters are available when it starts the processing of the UL data NR-slot. If not, PUSCH receiver does not do any processing.

---

##### 5.4.4.2.1.4 Parameter validation

**5.4.4.2.1.4.0-1**  (ID: `11397719`)

Parameter validation
In order to catch errors at possibly an early stage of the integration of L1 and L2, L1-SW shall check some parameters in the incoming UlData\_PuschReceiveReq messages.

L1-SW shall check if the value of each parameter is in the expected range of values for the considered L1 release.

The parameters to be checked are those whose value may change in subsequent L1 releases of 5G21B (sanity check). L1-SW shall check if a given parameter is in the supported range of the current L1 release version. If any of the parameter does not meet the expected range or values, such NR-slot shall not be processed by L1 and no message shall be sent to L2 for this NR-slot. Also, in such a case, the counter puschReceiveReqRejected shall be incremented and information about omitted NR-slot shall be stored in the log file.

---

##### 5.4.4.2.1.5 Pusch_SCH messages

###### 5.4.4.2.1.5.1 Pusch_SCH message content

**5.4.4.2.1.5.1.0-1**  (ID: `11397736`)

Pusch\_SCH message content
The Pusch\_SCH message is built and sent on a subcell basis after all UL-SCH have been processed for all the subcells, for more details on the read/write procedure refer to 5G\_L1\_3714\_replaced([11397011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8vkIvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).  This message contains a header with sfn, slot, subcellId, and some timestamp parameters to identify it, as below.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Msg field name** |  | **format** |  | **Range** |  | **Meaning** |  |  |
| sfn |  | u16 |  | 0..1023 |  | System Frame Number for processing |  |  |
| slot |  | u8 |  | 0..79 |  | NR-slot number for pocessing |  |  |
| subcellId |  | u8 |  | Full range |  | Subcell identifier |  |  |
|  | Timestamp\_sfn |  | u16 |  | 0..1023 |  | RX SFN at message build |  |
|  | Timestamp\_slot |  | u8 |  | 0 to 79 |  | RX Slot at message build |  |
|  | Timestamp\_symb |  | u8 |  | 0 to 13 |  | RX OFDM symbol at message build |  |
|  | Timestamp\_3\_84M |  | u16 |  | 0 to 277 |  | Number of 3.84 MHz cycle in RX OFDM symbol at message build. |  |
|  | numOfPuschSch |  | u8 |  | *1 to MaxPusUe* |  | Number of PUSCH decoded with UL-SCH in this slot for the subcell. |  |
|  | HeaderPadding |  | u8 |  | NA |  | Padding |  |
| Pusch\_sch\_info |  | Pusch\_sch\_info[numOfPuschSch] |  | full |  | Pusch SCH info structure array |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

---

###### 5.4.4.2.1.5.2 Pusch_sch_info IE content

**5.4.4.2.1.5.2.0-1**  (ID: `11397753`)

Pusch\_sch\_info IE content
Pusch\_SCH message contains a field called Pusch\_sch\_info[numOfPuschSch], which size depends on the number of PUSCH decoded with UL-SCH in this slot for the subcell (numOfPuschSch), and which is the field of interest for the reporting for debug purposes, and to enable UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message generation by L1SW.

The structure of Pusch\_sch\_info is as follows.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Msg field name** |  | **format** |  | **Range** |  | **Meaning** |  |  |
| PUSCH\_cfg\_e\_index |  | u8 |  | full |  | Extended PUSCH configuration index link to this decoding: MSB is driven by decoder engine instance (0 -> PUSB-DEC0, 1 -> PUSB-DEC1). The 7 LSB are the PUSCH configuration index in DYN\_PUS0\_CFG\_RAM or DYN\_PUS1\_CFG\_RAM |  |  |
|  | crc |  | u8 |  | {OK=0, NOK=1} |  | Crc computed on the receive transport block (combination from all CB CRC and TB CRC) |  |
|  | dtx |  | u8 |  | {DTX=1, nonDTX=0} |  | Indicates whether DTX or non DTX has been detected in the specific PUSCH resource. |  |
|  | initalTx |  | u8 |  | 0,1 |  | Indicate if First TX. |  |
|  | C |  | u8 |  | 1 to 152 |  | CB number in TB |  |
|  | LdpcMaxIter |  | u8 |  | 1 to 63 |  | MaxIter setting for LDPC decoding |  |
|  | FirstCbCrcFailed |  | u8 |  | 0 to 152 |  | First CB to get CB CRC fail. 1 means first, 2 is second, …If all CB CRC pass or if C=1, FirstCbCrcFailed=0 |  |
|  | FirstLdpcSyndFailed |  | u8 |  | 0 to 152 |  | First CB to get LDPC syndrom not verified. 1 means first, 2 is second, … If all CB get LDPC syndrom verified, FirstLdpcSyndFailed =0 |  |
|  | TbCrc |  | u8 |  | {OK=0, NOK=1} |  | Specific TB CRC status. |  |
|  | MaxLdpcEffort |  | u8 |  | 1 to 64 |  | Highest number of iterations done for all CB of the TB up to the first CB with LDPC syndrome not verified  (excluded). |  |
| RespLoOffset |  | u16 |  | 10 bits |  | PuschReceiveRespLo offset in UL\_OUT PuschReceiveRespLo buffer in 256B word. |  |  |
| HarqBuffStartAdd |  | u32 |  | 19 bits |  | 1st CB HARQ buffer start address in HARQ DDR in 4KB word. |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

---

###### 5.4.4.2.1.5.3 L1-FPGA reported parameters per PUSCH_cfg_e_index

**5.4.4.2.1.5.3.0-1**  (ID: `11397769`)

L1-FPGA reported parameters per PUSCH\_cfg\_e\_index
L1-FPGA reported parameters per PUSCH\_cfg\_e\_index are:

- PUSCH\_cfg\_e\_index

- crc: see crc, in section 5.2.4.4.11 WoP!

- dtx: see dtx, in section 5.2.4.4.11

- initalTx: see freshHarqTrans, in section 5.2.4.1.3.1

- C: see numOfCodeBlocks, in section 5.2.4.1.3.1

- LdpcMaxIter

- FirstCbCrcFailed

- FirstLdpcSyndFailed

- TbCrc

- MaxLdpcEffort

- RespLoOffset

- HarqBuffStartAdd

---

##### 5.4.4.2.1.6 Pusch_CSI messages to L1SW

###### 5.4.4.2.1.6.1 The Pusch_CSI message content

**5.4.4.2.1.6.1.0-1**  (ID: `11397783`)

The Pusch\_CSI message content
The Pusch\_CSI message is built and sent on a subcell basis after all PUSCH UCI (AN+CSI) have been processed for all the subcells, for more details on the read/write procedure refer to 5G\_L1\_3714\_replaced([11397011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8vkIvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). This message contains a header with sfn, slot, subcellId, and some timestamp parameters to identify it, as below.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Msg field name** |  | **format** |  | **Range** |  | **Meaning** |  |  |
| sfn |  | u16 |  | 0..1023 |  | System Frame Number for processing |  |  |
| slot |  | u8 |  | 0..79 |  | NR-slot number for pocessing |  |  |
| subcellId |  | u8 |  | Full range |  | Subcell identifier |  |  |
|  | Timestamp\_sfn |  | u16 |  | 0..1023 |  | RX SFN at message build |  |
|  | Timestamp\_slot |  | u8 |  | 0 to 79 |  | RX Slot at message build |  |
|  | Timestamp\_symb |  | u8 |  | 0 to 13 |  | RX OFDM symbol at message build |  |
|  | Timestamp\_3\_84M |  | u16 |  | 0 to 277 |  | Number of 3.84 MHz cycle in RX OFDM symbol at message build. |  |
|  | numOfPuschCsi |  | u8 |  | 1 to *MaxPusUe* |  | Number of PUSCH decoded including UCI of type CSI. |  |
|  | HeaderPadding |  | u8[3] |  | NA |  | Padding |  |
| Pusch\_csi\_info |  | Pusch\_csi\_info[numOfPuschCsi] |  |  |  | Pusch CSI info structure array |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

---

###### 5.4.4.2.1.6.2 Pusch_csi_info IE content

**5.4.4.2.1.6.2.0-1**  (ID: `11397798`)

Pusch\_csi\_info IE content
In particular, this message contains a field called Pusch\_csi\_info[numOfPuschCsi], which size depends on the number of PUSCH decoded including UCI of type CSI in this slot for the subcell (numOfPuschCsi), and which is the field of interest for the reporting for debug purposes. The structure of Pusch\_csi\_info is as follows.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Msg field name** |  | **format** |  | **Range** |  | **Meaning** |  |  |
| PUSCH\_cfg\_e\_index |  | u8 |  | full |  | Extended PUSCH configuration index link to this decoding: MSB is driven by decoder engine instance (0 -> PUSB-DEC0, 1 -> PUSB-DEC1). The 7 LSB are the PUSCH configuration index in DYN\_PUS0\_CFG\_RAM or DYN\_PUS1\_CFG\_RAM |  |  |
|  | PUSCH\_cfg\_e\_index\_padding |  | u8[3] |  | NA |  | Padding |  |
|  | CSI\_part1\_dtx |  | u8 |  | { nonDTX =0, DTX=*1}* |  | CSI\_part1 DTX result. |  |
|  | CSI\_part1\_crc |  | u8 |  | {CRC\_OK=*0, CRC\_NOK=* 1}\*\* |  | CSI\_part1 CRC result. CRC is valid in case O\_uci\_part1 is greater than 11 bits. Always 0 otherwise. |  |
|  | O\_uci\_part1 |  | u16 |  | 0 to 64 |  | CSI part 1 size (without CRC). |  |
| uciCsiPart1Bits |  | u8[8] |  |  |  | CSI part 1 bits of UCI report. Valid entries correspond to indexes from 0 to O\_uci\_part1 - 1. Serialization needs to be compliant. |  |  |
| CSI\_part1\_metric |  | u16 |  | Q0.?.? (O<=2) Q0.13.3 (2<O<12) Q0.?.? (O>11) |  | CSI part 1detection metric. |  |  |
|  | CSI\_part1\_metric\_padding |  | u8[2] |  | NA |  | Padding |  |
|  | CSI\_part2\_dtx |  | u8 |  | { nonDTX =0, DTX=*1}* |  | CSI part2 DTX result. |  |
|  | CSI\_part2\_crc |  | u8 |  | {CRC\_OK=*0, CRC\_NOK=* 1}\*\* |  | CSI part2 CRC result. CRC is valid in case O\_uci\_part2 is greater than 11 bits. Always 0 otherwise. |  |
|  | O\_uci\_part2 |  | u16 |  | 0 to 64 |  | CSI part 2 size (without CRC). |  |
| uciCsiPart2Bits |  | u8[8] |  |  |  | CSI part 2 bits of UCI report. Valid entries correspond to indexes from 0 to O\_uci\_part2 - 1. Serialization needs to be compliant. |  |  |
| CSI\_part2\_metric |  | u16 |  | Q0.?.? (O<=2) Q0.13.3 (2<O<12) Q0.?.? (O>11) |  | CSI part 2detection metric. |  |  |
| Subband\_set\_num |  | u8 |  | 0 to 16 |  | Number of even or odd subband set report. E;g. 3 means first report with subband CSI have been transmitted by Ue, and only the even subband report set has been transmitted for second CSI report including subband CSI. In case there is only wideband reports this field is set to 0. |  |  |
| CSI\_part2\_subband\_set\_num\_padding |  | u8 |  | NA |  | Padding |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

---

##### 5.4.4.2.1.7 UlData_PuschReceiveRespLo

###### 5.4.4.2.1.7.0-1 UlData_PuschReceiveRespLo (to be completed in the step 2 of refactoting CNI) (ID: 11397820)

#### 5.4.4.2.2 Message from L2-PS

##### 5.4.4.2.2.1 Trigger of PUSCH decoding procedure

**5.4.4.2.2.1.0-3**  (ID: `12706116`)

Trigger of PUSCH decoding procedure
PUSCH decoding procedure in 5G-L1-UL (BB) is triggered by the reception of message *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq ([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Upon the reception of this message, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall attempt decoding of the PUSCH signals in the specified SFN and slot.*

All necessary scheduling information are given in the message.

PUSCH decoding procedure is common to Technologies and is described in [11403150: PUSCH Decoder](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9MQDvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and [11405223: Uplink Control Information](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WoG_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

The PUSCH decoding algorithms are described in "5Gmax algorithm specification" in the following sections:

* [Soft Demodulation](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschSoftDemod),
* [LDPC decoder](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-CommonAlgosLdpcDe) for PUSCH data,
* [UCI Decoding and Detection](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschUciDecDet),
* [DTX detection](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschDtxDet).

[CB012115-IA] The requirement is common for all the Technologies in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

##### 5.4.4.2.2.2 Reception of message UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru

**5.4.4.2.2.2.0-2**  (ID: `12789097`)

Reception of message UlData\_PuschReceiveReqL1ru
5G-L1-UL(BB) in eCPRI 7.2e split, on Thor [CB012115-GA] and Odin [End CB012115-GA] shall support the reception of message *UlData\_PuschReceiveReqL1ru from L2-PS (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14960\_replaced([11513820](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy-AvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).*

On reception of this message*, L1-BB shall create message* UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveReq (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14982\_replaced([11517049](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSIVKPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and send it ro L1(Radio) in order to trigger PUSCH reception procedure. All parameters of message *UlData\_PuschReceiveReqL1ru are copied into message* UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveReq.\*\*\*\*

L1-BB shall accept and proceed with the message *UlData\_PuschReceiveReqL1ru only if it is configured in eCPRI 7.2e (*fronthaulMode=6 in message *L1Config\_SwConfigurationReq). Otherwise, the message shall be ignored.*\*\*

---

##### 5.4.4.2.2.3 Message validation, 7-2e split

**5.4.4.2.2.3.0-2**  (ID: `12789120`)

Message validation, 7-2e split
L1-BB shall check the consistency of both messages *UlData\_PuschReceiveReqL1ru and* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq. Invalid parameter combinations for these messages are described in 5G\_L1\_IF\_14983\_replaced([11517052](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSIVKfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) respectively.\*\*

As *UlData\_PuschReceiveReqL1ru contains a subset of parameters of* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq, it may happen that *UlData\_PuschReceiveReqL1ru is validated and processed by L1(Radio), whereas* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is not validated. In this case, L1-BB shall not perform PUSCH decoding for the current slot and all response messages from L1(Radio) shall be ignored.\*\*\*\*

The requirement is supported in Thor. [CB012115-GA] It shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-GA]

---

##### 5.4.4.2.2.4 Missed message UlData_PuschReceiveReq

###### 5.4.4.2.2.4.0-2 Missed message UlData_PuschReceiveReq When in eCPRI7-2e scenario the expected message UlData_PuschReceiveReq is not received after receiving the message UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru, L1 SW (BB) shall print the warning to the journal log. The requirement is supported in Thor. [CB012115-GA] It shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-GA] (ID: 12789103)

##### 5.4.4.2.2.5 Missed message UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru

###### 5.4.4.2.2.5.0-2 Missed message UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru When in eCPRI7-2e scenario the expected message UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru was not received, L1 SW (BB) shall print the error to the journal log. The requirement is supported in Thor. [CB012115-GA] It shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-GA] (ID: 12789112)

##### 5.4.4.2.2.6 PUSCH Message validation

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.1 patternID validation

**5.4.4.2.2.6.1.0-1**  (ID: `12742754`)

patternID validation
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that patternId is not greater than 0, if UlData\_PuschReceiveReq was received for subcell with ulEcpriFdBeamforming set to false.

In case patternId greater than 0 in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall log warning to journal log.

Note: From system perspective, patternId shall be not used without beamforming, but it not prevents PUSCH processing.

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11513756: UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message content:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_NV2wcPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.2 eCPRIFlag validation

**5.4.4.2.2.6.2.0-1**  (ID: `12742757`)

eCPRIFlag validation
~~5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that eCPRIFlag is not equal to 0 if ceAxCIndex is greater than  2.~~

~~In case eCPRIFlag is equal to 0 in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.~~

Parameter eCPRIFlag does not exit - this case shall be reworked.

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11513756: UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message content:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_NV2wcPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.3 mcsTable validation

**5.4.4.2.2.6.3.0-1**  (ID: `12742756`)

mcsTable validation
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that mcsTable is not equal to max256QAM if blerTarget is set to blerTarget\_0\_point\_01

In case mcsTable is equal to max256QAM in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall log unique warning.

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11513756: UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message content:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_NV2wcPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.4 puschTransformPrecoderFlag validation

**5.4.4.2.2.6.4.0-1**  (ID: `12742771`)

puschTransformPrecoderFlag validation
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that puschTransformPrecoderFlag is not equal to 1 if blerTarget is set to blerTarget\_0\_point\_01

In case puschTransformPrecoderFlag is equal to 1 in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall log unique warring .

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition [11513756: UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message content:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_NV2wcPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.5 ulDmrsAddPos validation

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.5.1 Checker 1

**5.4.4.2.2.6.5.1.0-1**  (ID: `12742774`)

Checker 1
~~5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that ulDmrsAddPos is not equal to any from the following set {1, 2, 3} if ulPtrsFlag is set to 1~~

~~In case ulDmrsAddPos is equal to 1 or 2 or 3 in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.~~

Note: Invalid case in interface is outdated and not compliant with requirements For example: [11401456](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9DtIPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

Note: this requirement was derived from interface definition 11513764: PuschDmrsConfig:

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.5.2 Checker 2

**5.4.4.2.2.6.5.2.0-1**  (ID: `12742777`)

Checker 2
~~5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that ulDmrsAddPos is not equal to 0 if pucchModeSelect is set to 1 and actUlDataDmrsFdm = true~~

~~In case ulDmrsAddPos is equal to 0 in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.~~

**This invalid case is outdated - There is not parameter pucchModeSelect  in interface**

Note: this requirement was derived from interface definition 11513764: PuschDmrsConfig:

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.5.3 Checker 3

**5.4.4.2.2.6.5.3.0-1**  (ID: `12742779`)

Checker 3
~~5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that ulDMRSAddPos is not smaller than 2 if actHighSpeedCell = true~~

~~In case ulDmrsAddPos is smaller than 2 in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.~~

Note: this requirement was derived from interface definition 11513764: PuschDmrsConfig:

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.6 numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData validation

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.6.1 Checker 1

**5.4.4.2.2.6.6.1.0-1**  (ID: `12742782`)

Checker 1
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData is not equal to 1 if “puschTransformPrecoderFlag=1

In case numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData is equal to 1 in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition 11513764: PuschDmrsConfig:

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.6.2 Checker 2

**5.4.4.2.2.6.6.2.0-1**  (ID: `12742781`)

Checker 2
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData is not equal to 1 if “ulDmrsLen” = 2

In case numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData  is equal to 1 in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition 11513764: PuschDmrsConfig:

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.7 antPort validation

**5.4.4.2.2.6.7.0-1**  (ID: `12742784`)

antPort validation
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that antPort is not equal to 0xA000 (0,2)  if “numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData” = 1

In case antPort  is equal to 0xA000 in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived from interface definition 11513764: PuschDmrsConfig: and 11513774: PuschSpatialConfig:

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.8 modulationOrder validation

**5.4.4.2.2.6.8.0-1**  (ID: `12742787`)

modulationOrder validation
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that *modulationOrder is not equal to 1  if:*

* *dmrsSequenceType =* Type2 and\*\*
* *puschTransformPrecodingFlag = 1*

In case *modulationOrder is equal to 1 in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.*

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition 11513764: PuschDmrsConfig:

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.9 startSymbol validation

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.9.1 Checker 1

**5.4.4.2.2.6.9.1.0-1**  (ID: `12742805`)

Checker 1
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that startSymbol is not greater than ulDmrsTypeAPos

In case startSymbol is greater than ulDmrsTypeAPos, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11513768: PuschAllocationConfig:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_Nft3EPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.9.2 Checker 2

**5.4.4.2.2.6.9.2.0-1**  (ID: `12742806`)

Checker 2
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that startSymbol is not greater than (14 - numOfPuschSymbols)

In other words startSymbol + numOfPuschSymbols have to be smaller than 14

In case startSymbol is greater than (14 - numOfPuschSymbols), 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11513768: PuschAllocationConfig:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_Nft3EPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.9.3 Checker 2

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.10 startPrb validation

**5.4.4.2.2.6.10.0-1**  (ID: `12742807`)

startPrb validation
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that sum of startPrb and numOfPrb is not greater than ulBandwidth.

In case sum of startPrb and numOfPrb is greater than ulBandwidth, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11513768: PuschAllocationConfig:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_Nft3EPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.11 numOfLayers validation

**5.4.4.2.2.6.11.0-1**  (ID: `12742810`)

numOfLayers validation
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that numOfLayers is not greater than 1, if antPort = 0

In case numOfLayers is greater than 1 in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition [11513774: PuschSpatialConfig:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_NjPsMPvwEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

Checker 1
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that codebookIndex is not greater than 2, if puschTransCoherence =  nonCoherent and

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.13 ulPtrsFlag validation

**5.4.4.2.2.6.13.0-1**  (ID: `12742820`)

ulPtrsFlag validation
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that ulPtrsFlag is not equal to 1, if

ulPtrsTimeDensity validation
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that ulPtrsTimeDensity is not equal to 0,

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.16 mcsTable validation

**5.4.4.2.2.6.16.0-1**  (ID: `12742823`)

mcsTable validation
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that mcsTable is not equal to qam64LowSE , if puschTransformPrecoderFlag= 1

In case mcsTable is equal to qam64LowSE in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

###### 5.4.4.2.2.6.17 suSplitpanelMode validation

**5.4.4.2.2.6.17.0-1**  (ID: `13018902`)

suSplitpanelMode validation
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall validate that suSplitpanelMode is in valid range {0, 9, 10, 11, 12}, if UlData\_PuschReceiveReq was received for subcell with ulSubcellType set to A8.

In case suSplitpanelMode is not valid in such configuration, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall drop the request and log unique error.

For other subcell types, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall ignore parameter suSplitpanelMode.

Note: this requirement is derived form interface definition 12884359: eCpriConfig

#### 5.4.4.2.3 Messages to L2-PS

##### 5.4.4.2.3.1 PUSCH reports from L1 to the L2 schedulers (UlData_PuschReceiveRespPs)

**5.4.4.2.3.1.0-1**  (ID: `11397894`)

PUSCH reports from L1 to the L2 schedulers (UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs)
Upon reception of the RX read trigger, the L1-SW reads the measurement results final DTX result from the FPGA, for the considered NR-slot.

The L1-SW then computes the output parameters as described in  requirement 5G\_L1\_4742\_replaced([11404902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WBDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Then, the L1-SW creates the UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs feedback message to L2 and sends it (them) to the destination address (addrPuschReceiveRespPs) stored in the PUSCH context (see section 5.2.4.1.3) previously created for that NR-slot and sub-cell:

This message can be sent once for multiple sub-cells, for a given SFN and NR-slot. Each message contains the responses for all UE scheduled in uplink for these sub-cells during that NR-slot (3 subcells for FDD and 1 subcell for TDD and one UE per NR-slot per sub-cell in L1 release 5G21B).

UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs includes the following parameters:

sfn

slot: NR-slot number

numSubcell (“length” field in the C-code var\_array\_t structure “subcells” generated by MuLTI i.e. “subcells.length”, also called “subcellsLength” in the .html output file from MuLTI): number of sub-cells included in the message.

L1 release 5G21B: this parameter is set to 1 for TDD and FDD.

Per sub-cell:

subcellId: see 5.2.1.1 for the values supported in 5G21B.

processInRealTime bool {True, False} Indicates whether the subcell was processed in real time.

sfnForProcessing u16[0..1023] SFN for the slot when the PUSCH has been processed by L1.

slotForProcessing u8[0..79] NR-slot index for the slot when the PUSCH has been processed by L1.

noisePower: as described in requirement  requirement 5G\_L1\_4742\_replaced([11404902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WBDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

numOfGrants (“length” field in the C-code var\_array\_t structure “grants” generated by MuLTI i.e. “grants.length”, also called “grantsLength” in the .html output file from MuLTI): number of grants included in the message (can take the values from [0..4] for TDD and [0..2] for FDD in L1 release 5G21B). 0 grants is also possible to enable RTWP measurements in case of no UL traffic. See 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1390.

rtwpOfAnt:[CB009363-D] array [4] dynamic float array based on subcell type, refer to 5G\_L1\_IF\_200\_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB009363-D], reported per antenna. Unit is in dBm with step of 0.1 dBm.

Per Grant (parameters are computed as described in requirement 5G\_L1\_4742\_replaced([11404902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WBDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) unless otherwise mentioned):

•   rnti: RNTI as previously stored at PUSCH context creation [CB008124-B] from field rnti of message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq [End CB008124-B].

• harqProcessIndex: as previously stored at PUSCH context creation.

• absoluteHarqProcessIndex: as previously stored at PUSCH context creation.

• dtx

• shortTermCfoMetric

•   shortTermTaMetric

• shortTermTaPeakAmp

• rxPower

• sinr

• rssi

• ulPmiRank1 (see requirement 5G\_L1\_4742\_replaced([11404902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WBDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

• ulPmiRank1Sinr (see  requirement 5G\_L1\_4742\_replaced([11404902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WBDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

• ulPmiRank2 (see requirement 5G\_L1\_4742\_replaced([11404902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WBDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

• ulPmiRank2Sinr (see requirement 5G\_L1\_4742\_replaced([11404902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WBDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

• channelCorrMetric (see requirement 5G\_L1\_4742\_replaced([11404902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WBDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

• ulRank (see 5.2.5.1.2)

• dtxCsiPart1 u8 {DTX, nonDTX} Indicate whether DTX or non DTX has been detected in the specific PUSCH resource. The nonDTX supports a range of values > 0.

• dtxMetricCsiPart1 u16 DTX metric value in fixed point representation.

• dtxThresholdCsiPart1 u16 DTX threshold value in fixed point representation.

• dtxCsiPart2 u8 {DTX, nonDTX} Indicate whether DTX or non DTX has been detected in the specific PUSCH resource.

• dtxMetricCsiPart2 u16 DTX metric value in fixed point representation.

• dtxThresholdCsiPart2 u16 DTX threshold value in fixed point representation.

• uciCsiPart1Bits  array [6]u64 CSI part 1 bits of UCI report. Valid entries correspond to indexes from 0 to numOfUciCsiPart1Bits - 1.Bits are filled from the MSB to LSB and padding bits are located on the least significant bits of the last octet. If number of UCI bits is lower than 8, padding bits are also set on least significant bits of first octet.

• csiPart1CrcCheck u8{0=Pass, 1=Fail} When the number of CSI Part 1 bits is higher than 11 bit. The check is performed. If there are less than or equal to 11 bits, the IE is ignored by L2-Ps

• numOfUciCsiPart2Bits  u8[0..312 ] Number of CSI bits (part 2) in UCI report.

• uciCsiPart2Bits array [5] of u64 CSI part 2 bits of UCI report. Valid entries correspond to indexes from 0 to numOfUciCsiPart2Bits - 1. Bits are filled from the MSB to LSB and padding bits are located on the least significant bits of the last octet. If number of UCI bits is lower than 8, padding bits are also set on least significant bits of first octet.

• csiPart2CrcCheck u8{0=Pass, 1=Fail} When the number of CSI Part 2 bits is higher than 11 bit, the check is performed. If there are less than or equal to 11 bits, the IE is ignored by L2-Ps.

• numOfSubbandSets  u8[0...2] number of subband sets after the CSI Part2 omission.

• ackNack1BitUci u8{1= Ack, 0= Nack} 1 bit to report Harq-ACK feedback when 1-bit UCI decoding is applied.

• dtxAck1Bit u8 {DTX, nonDTX} Indicate whether DTX or non DTX has been detected in the specific PUSCH resource. The DTX supports a range of values > 0.

• dtxMetricAck1Bit u16 DTX metric value in fixed point representation.

• dtxThresholdAck1Bit u16 DTX threshold value in fixed point representation

• ackNackUciMoreThan1Bit u16 Each bit is {1= Ack, 0= Nack} Indicate the Harq-ACK reporting on PUSCH for the data sent on PDSCH in DL NR-slot when more than 1-bit decoding.

• dtxAckMoreThan1Bit u8 {DTX, nonDTX} Indicate whether DTX or non DTX has been detected in the specific PUSCH resource

• dtxMetricAckMoreThan1Bit u16 DTX metric value in fixed point representation.

• dtxThresholdAckMoreThan1Bit u16 DTX threshold value in fixed point representation

• ackCrcCheck u8{0=Pass, 1=Fail} It is used when more than 11 Harq bits are used.

• rssiOfAnt: array [4] float, in struct PuschMeasurementsResp, reported per antenna and normalized to a single PRB. Unit is in dBm with step of 0.1 dBm.

---

###### 5.4.4.2.3.2.2 Loner and Loki

**5.4.4.2.3.2.2.0-2**  (ID: `11397930`)

Loner and Loki
5G-L1-UL shall report invalid values for following measurements of the UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_[200, 201, 203, 206]):

* rssi
* rxPower
* noisePower
* noisePerPrb (not supported on Loner, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8122\_replaced([11108347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kopdLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* ulRank
* ulPmiRank1
* ulPmiRank2
* ulPmiRank1Sinr
* ulPmiRank2Sinr
* sinr
* shortTermTaMetric
* shortTermTaPeakAmp
* shortTermTaMetricOfAnt
* shortTermTaPeakAmpOfAnt
* rxPowerOfAnt
* shortTermCfoMetric
* dtx
* dtxCsiPart1
* dtxCsiPart2

5G-L1-UL shall also report invalid values for the following antenna-wise measurements if all received samples from (e)CPRI interface are zero-valued:

* linRssiOfAnt
* linNoiseOfAnt

Invalid value for both these measurements is 10 attoWatt (10-17 Watt).
