##### 5.4.4.2.3.3 Forwarding L2_Ctxt_AN_Mgt to L2-PS

###### 5.4.4.2.3.3.1 Loner

**5.4.4.2.3.3.1.0-1**  (ID: `11400810`)

Loner
L1-L2 interface shall support a new IE in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message called **L2\_Ctxt\_AN\_Mgt (L2CtxtAnMgt).**

L1 shall support the reception of a new IE of array[8] of type U8.

This enabler content is private to 5G-L2-PS, and 5G-L1-UL shall only copy it from the request message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq to the decoder, and send it back in the response message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD to 5G-L2-PS, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_211\_replaced([11513924](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR0MEfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**[CB007814-E] This requirement also applies to ABIO FR1-FDD-eCPRI.** [End CB007814-E]\*\*\*\*

---

###### 5.4.4.2.3.3.2 Loki

**5.4.4.2.3.3.2.0-1**  (ID: `11400818`)

Loki
L1-L2 interface shall support a new IE in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message called **L2\_Ctxt\_AN\_Mgt (L2CtxtAnMgt).**

L1 shall support the reception of a new IE of array[8] of type U8.

This enabler content is private to 5G-L2-PS, and 5G-L1-UL shall only copy it from the request message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq to the decoder, and send it back in the response message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD to 5G-L2-PS, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_211\_replaced([11513924](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR0MEfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

This requirement also applies to ABIO FR1-FDD-eCPRI.

---

###### 5.4.4.2.3.3.3 Thor

**5.4.4.2.3.3.3.0-3**  (ID: `11400842`)

Thor
L1-L2 interface shall support a new IE in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message called **L2\_Ctxt\_AN\_Mgt (L2CtxtAnMgt).**

L1 shall support the reception of a new IE of array[8] of type U8.

This enabler content is private to 5G-L2-PS, and 5G-L1-UL shall only copy it from the request message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq to the decoder, and send it back in the response message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD to 5G-L2-PS, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_211\_replaced([11513924](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR0MEfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The requirement is also applicable for **L1 Thor for FR1-TDD-eCPRI.**

The requirement is also applicable for **L1 Thor for FR1-FDD-CPRI.**

The requirement is also applicable for **L1 Thor for FR1-TDD-CPRI.**

**[CB007595-B] This requirement is also applicable for** L1 Thor in FR1-TDD-eCPRI-7-2e. **[End CB007595-B]**\*\*\*\*

**[CB010576-D] This requirement is also applicable for** L1 Thor in FR2-TDD-eCPRI. **[End CB010576-D]**\*\*\*\*

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Technology / HW | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | **[CB010969-SR-CB] Supported** [End CB010969-SR-CB]\*\*\*\* |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI | Supported |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported |

---

###### 5.4.4.2.3.3.4 Odin

**5.4.4.2.3.3.4.0-1**  (ID: `12706107`)

Odin
L1/L2 interface shall support the reception of **L2CtxtAnMgt (L2\_Ctxt\_AN\_Mgt) IE ([11513804: PuschUciConfig](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9__vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message, as array[8] of type U8.**

This enabler content is private to 5G-L2-PS, and 5G-L1-UL shall only copy it from the request message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq to the decoder, and send it back in the response message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD to 5G-L2-PS, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_211\_replaced([11513924](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR0MEfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The requirement is common for all Technologies in **L1 Odin.**

---

##### 5.4.4.2.3.4 Grouping of reponses for latency optimization

**5.4.4.2.3.4.0-1**  (ID: `13378106`)

Grouping of reponses for latency optimization
When latency optimization is active ([13367749](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_j89WoPdbEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)):

1. Per reception of UlData\_PuschReceiveReqL1ru message, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall make a list of scheduled UEs satisfying the following conditions:
2. If paired with other UEs, the virtual UEs contains less than 5 scheduled layers for C8\_8 ulSubcellType, less than 3 scheduled layers for C8\_4 ulSubcellType.
3. Not paired UE.
4. For UEs in the list, group the UE-wise responses in a single UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. For UEs in the list with uplink Harq information, group the UE-wise responses in a single UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU message. For UEs in the list with downlink Harq information, group the UE-wise responses together in a single UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD message.
5. For UEs NOT in the list, response to L2-PS follow legacy method.

---

#### 5.4.4.2.4 Messages to L2-Lo

##### 5.4.4.2.4.1 Total Transport Block Size (Bytes)

###### 5.4.4.2.4.1.0-1 Total Transport Block Size (Bytes) L1L2 interface shall support a new IE in UlData_PuschReceiveRespLo message called totalTbSizeBytes. L1 shall fill this IE with the total size of the transport block, sum of the sizes of all fragments. (ID: 11400848)

##### 5.4.4.2.4.2 Forwarding information from L2-PS to L2-LO

**5.4.4.2.4.2.0-1**  (ID: `11397978`)

Forwarding information from L2-PS to L2-LO
5G-L1-UL shall read values of fields *isDcGbrCompliantSched and* numOfSlotsSinceUlGrantForInitRxPusch from *PuschHarqConfig structure (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_197\_replaced([11513788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_fvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message and forward it to L2-LO in *isDcGbrCompliantSched and* numOfSlotsSinceUlGrantForInitRxPusch fields of *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLoL1sw message (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_208\_replaced([11513875](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlCvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).***\***\*

HW technology map:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Loki | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI | N/A | Not supported |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Not supported | Not supported |

###### 5.4.4.2.4.2.1 Loki

###### 5.4.4.2.4.2.1.0-1 Loki 5G-L1-UL shall read values of fields isDcGbrCompliantSched and numOfSlotsSinceUlGrantForInitRxPusch from PuschHarqConfig structure (see 5G_L1_IF_197_replaced( 11513788 )) of UlData_PuschReceiveReq message and forward it to L2-LO in isDcGbrCompliantSched and numOfSlotsSinceUlGrantForInitRxPusch fields of UlData_PuschReceiveRespLoL1sw message (see 5G_L1_IF_208_replaced( 11513875 )). * * (ID: 12519632)

###### 5.4.4.2.4.2.2 Thor

###### 5.4.4.2.4.2.2.0-1 Thor 5G-L1-UL shall read values of fields isDcGbrCompliantSched and numOfSlotsSinceUlGrantForInitRxPusch from PuschHarqConfig structure (see 5G_L1_IF_197_replaced( 11513788 )) of UlData_PuschReceiveReq message and forward it to L2-LO in isDcGbrCompliantSched and numOfSlotsSinceUlGrantForInitRxPusch fields of UlData_PuschReceiveRespLoL1sw message (see 5G_L1_IF_208_replaced( 11513875 )). * * [CB014106-SR-A] The requirement shall be supported regardless of Duplex or Fronthaul mode. [End CB014106-SR-A] (ID: 12519634)

##### 5.4.4.2.4.3 Forwarding K2 value for average UL air-interface delay counter

###### 5.4.4.2.4.3.0-1 Forwarding K2 value for average UL air-interface delay counter For Loki and Thor (ID: 11397992)

**5.4.4.2.4.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11397998`)

Forwarding K2 value for average UL air-interface delay counter
Content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 25R2 release. For later releases please refer to 5G\_L1\_18579\_replaced([11398005](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e80cl_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L1\_18578\_replaced([11398018](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e80cmfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

5G-L1-UL shall get k2 value from L2-PS in message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_197\_replaced([11513788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_fvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and forward it to L2-LO in message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLo (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_208\_replaced([11513875](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlCvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the update of average UL air-interface delay counter.

Notes:

1. Configured grant is out of the scope of average UL air-interface delay counter. In that case, the k2 value received from L2-PS is expected to be invalid (0). L1 shall forward it as it is to L2-LO.

2. In case of slot aggregation, UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLo is sent on the last repetition (slotAggregationCountDown = 0 in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq). L1 shall then fill k2 with the value received for the last repetition.

HW technology map:

| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Loki | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI | N/A | Not supported |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported |

---

#### 5.4.4.2.5 Message to L1-Radio

##### 5.4.4.2.5.1 Transmission of multiple fragments of message UlDagaFH_PuschReceiveReq

###### 5.4.4.2.5.1.1 Thor

**5.4.4.2.5.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `11398043`)

Thor
If the number of UEs per slot grows in such a way that the size of a single message *UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveReq exceeds the maximum eCPRI packet size, L1-BB shall transmit this message on FH interface by splitting it into multiple fragments. Each fragment is an individual message of type* UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveReq with a reduced number of grants.\*\*

In each individual message:

* IE *numOfGrants (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14982\_replaced([11517049](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSIVKPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) indicates the number of grants included in the message*
* IE *totalNumOfGrants indicates the total number of UE grants to be processed in the slot.*

The maximum number of grants per fragment and the number of fragments per slot shall be set in such a way that each fragment fits in a eCPRI packet. This depends on the structure of the message *UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveReq. Figures are given in 5G\_L1\_IF\_15228\_replaced([11517074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSIVLfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

---

###### 5.4.4.2.5.1.2 Odin

**5.4.4.2.5.1.2.0-1**  (ID: `12791705`)

Odin
If the number of UEs per slot grows in such a way that the size of a single message *UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveReq exceeds the maximum eCPRI packet size, L1-BB shall transmit this message on FH interface by splitting it into multiple fragments. Each fragment is an individual message of type* UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveReq with a reduced number of grants.\*\*

In each individual message:

* IE *numOfGrants (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14982\_replaced([11517049](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSIVKPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) indicates the number of grants included in the message*
* IE *totalNumOfGrants indicates the total number of UE grants to be processed in the slot.*

The maximum number of grants per fragment and the number of fragments per slot shall be set in such a way that each fragment fits in a eCPRI packet. This depends on the structure of the message *UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveReq. (Figures are given in [11517074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSIVLfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and the consequent requirements.)*

Notice!

(Temporary Note) Due to the limited number of scheduled UEs to be handled in CB012115, this requirement may not be tested properly. Future parity features should revisit this.

---

#### 5.4.4.2.6 Messages from L1-Radio

##### 5.4.4.2.6.1 Reception of messages from L1 Radio

###### 5.4.4.2.6.1.1 Thor

###### 5.4.4.2.6.1.1.0-2 Thor 5G-L1_UL (BB) shall support the reception of messages UlDataFH_PuschReceiveRespCellPs (see 5G_L1_IF_14996_replaced( 11517087 )) and UlDataFH_PuschReceiveRespUePs (see 5G_L1_IF_14998_replaced( 11517101 )) from L1(Radio). On reception of both these messages, L1-BB shall build message UlData_PuschReceiveRespPs (see 5G_L1_IF_200_replaced( 11513831 )) with the measurements sent from L1(Radio) and additional measurements resulting from data decoding. ** [CB013204] If at least one UE in a slot has 1 or 2 Prb allocation and UCI in PUSCH, 5G-L1_UL (BB) shall wait U lDataFH_PuschReceiveRespUePs (see 5G_L1_IF_14998_replaced( 11517101 )) from L1(Radio) and use its dtx info to decide final UCI DTX which is sent in message PuschReceiveRespHarqD. **[End CB013204] ** (ID: 11398069)

###### 5.4.4.2.6.1.2 Odin

###### 5.4.4.2.6.1.2.0-1 Odin 5G-L1_UL (BB) shall support the reception of messages UlDataFH_PuschReceiveRespCellPs (see 5G_L1_IF_14996_replaced( 11517087 )) and UlDataFH_PuschReceiveRespUePs (see 5G_L1_IF_14998_replaced( 11517101 )) from L1(Radio). On reception of both these messages, L1-BB shall build message UlData_PuschReceiveRespPs (see 5G_L1_IF_200_replaced( 11513831 )) with the measurements sent from L1(Radio) and additional measurements resulting from data decoding. ** (ID: 12789600)

##### 5.4.4.2.6.2 Loss of message UlDataFH_PuschReceiveRespCellPs

**5.4.4.2.6.2.0-2**  (ID: `12791733`)

Loss of message UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespCellPs
If message *UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespCellPs is not received when the end of L1-BB FH reception window is reached, as defined in [11590319: Timing for receive response L2-L1 FH message transmission/reception for slot#i](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BR5NW_wGEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), the message shall be considered as lost.*

In this case, L1-BB shall populate cell-level measurements in message *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs as follows:*

* cell-level noise (IE *noisePower) shall be set to INVALID value.*
* size of *noisePerPrb shall be set to 0.*

This requirement applies in the same way if PUSCH reception did not happen in L1(Radio) due to the loss of the whole message *UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveReq.*

The requirement is supported in Thor. [CB012115-GA] It shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-GA]

---

Thor
L1-BB shall support the reception of fragmented response message UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespUePs. In this case, each fragment is an individual message of type UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespUePs with a reduced number of grants.

In each individual message:

* IE numOfGrants (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14998\_replaced([11517101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSI8MvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) indicates the number of grants included in the message
* IE totalNumOfGrants indicates the total number of UE grants to be processed in the slot.

The maximum number of grants per fragment and the number of fragments per slot depend on message structure and on the number of receiving spatial streams. Figures are given in 5G\_L1\_IF\_15229\_replaced([11517134](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSI8OfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

L1-BB shall consider that all fragments are received when:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in italics. The equation is:
$$ \sum\_{f} \text{numOfGrants}(f) = \text{totalNumOfGrants} $$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_572.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where f is the fragment index.

Message UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespCellPs and all fragments of UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespUePs shall be received within L1-BB FH reception window [Ta4\_min\_cp..Ta4\_max\_cp] (see BTSC\_L1\_Req\_10883\_replaced([11590319](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BR5NW_wGEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). L1-BB shall build the message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs based on the fragments received inside this window.

12792245:
Odin
L1-BB shall support the reception of fragmented response message *UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespUePs. In this case, each fragment is an individual message of type* UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespUePs with a reduced number of grants.\*\*

In each individual message:

* IE *numOfGrants (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14998\_replaced([11517101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSI8MvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) indicates the number of grants included in the message*
* IE *totalNumOfGrants indicates the total number of UE grants to be processed in the slot.*

The maximum number of grants per fragment and the number of fragments per slot depend on message structure and on the number of receiving spatial streams. Figures are given in 5G\_L1\_IF\_15229\_replaced([11517134](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSI8OfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

L1-BB shall consider that all fragments are received when:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in italics. The equation is:
$$ \sum\_{f} \text{numOfGrants}(f) = \text{totalNumOfGrants} $$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_572.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where *f is the fragment index.*

Message *UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespCellPs and all fragments of* UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespUePs shall be received within L1-BB FH reception window [Ta4\_min\_cp..Ta4\_max\_cp] (see BTSC\_L1\_Req\_10883\_replaced([11590319](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BR5NW_wGEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). L1-BB shall build the message *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs based on the fragments received inside this window.*\*\*

Notice!

(Temporary Note) Due to the limited number of scheduled UEs to be handled in CB012115, this requirement may not be tested properly. Future parity features should revisit this.

---

##### 5.4.4.2.6.4 Loss of fragments of message UlDataFH_PuschReceiveRespUePs

###### 5.4.4.2.6.4.1 Thor

**5.4.4.2.6.4.1.0-1**  (ID: `11398116`)

Thor
If some of the fragments of message *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespUePs are not received when the end of L1-BB FH reception window is reached, as defined in BTSC\_L1\_Req\_10883\_replaced([11590319](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BR5NW_wGEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), these fragments shall be considered as lost.*

In that case, L1-BB shall populate information elements related to missing grants in message *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs and* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU as follows:\*\*

* *dtx information shall be set to DTX,*
* *ulRank shall be set to dedicated value NOTRECEIVED (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_206\_replaced([11513848](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlA_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),*
* the size of antenna-wise measurements array shall be set to 0,
* all other measurements shall be set to their respective INVALID values (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_201\_replaced([11513837](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L1\_IF\_206\_replaced([11513848](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlA_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

This requirement applies in the same way if some grants could not be processed in L1(Radio) due to the loss of some fragments of *UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveReq or of the whole message.*

---

###### 5.4.4.2.6.4.2 Odin

**5.4.4.2.6.4.2.0-1**  (ID: `12792256`)

Odin
If some of the fragments of message *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespUePs are not received when the end of L1-BB FH reception window is reached, as defined in BTSC\_L1\_Req\_10883\_replaced([11590319](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BR5NW_wGEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), these fragments shall be considered as lost.*

In that case, L1-BB shall populate information elements related to missing grants in message *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs and* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU as follows:\*\*

* *dtx information shall be set to DTX,*
* *ulRank shall be set to dedicated value NOTRECEIVED (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_206\_replaced([11513848](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlA_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),*
* the size of antenna-wise measurements array shall be set to 0,
* all other measurements shall be set to their respective INVALID values (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_201\_replaced([11513837](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L1\_IF\_206\_replaced([11513848](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlA_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

This requirement applies in the same way if some grants could not be processed in L1(Radio) due to the loss of some fragments of *UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveReq or of the whole message.*

Notice!

(Temporary Note) Due to the limited number of scheduled UEs to be handled in CB012115, this requirement may not be tested properly. Future parity features should revisit this.

---

#### 5.4.4.2.7 eCPRI 7-2e UL FH interface resolution

##### 5.4.4.2.7.1 Configurable Mantissa Size for Normalization Factor

###### 5.4.4.2.7.1.0-2 Configurable Mantissa Size for Normalization Factor 5G-L1-UL shall support configurable mantissa size for PUSCH normalization factor (β in 11380423: Uplink fronthaul stream, data format and interface resolution ) in eCPRI 7-2e FH. Refer to 'mantissaSizePuschBeta' in 11513484: eCpriStaticConfig . The requirement is supported in Thor. [CB012115-GA] It shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-GA] (ID: 12632589)

##### 5.4.4.2.7.2 Configurable FH Interface Resolution

###### 5.4.4.2.7.2.0-2 Configurable FH Interface Resolution 5G-L1-UL shall support configurable FH interface resolution factor instead of scaling factor for both PUSCH IQ data and PUSCH normalized factor in eCPRI 7-2e FH. Refer to IEs 'xEc' and 'betaEc' in 11513484: eCpriStaticConfig , also in 11380423: Uplink fronthaul stream, data format and interface resolution . The requirement is supported in Thor. [CB012115-GA] It shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-GA] (ID: 12632591)

### 5.4.4.3 Resource allocation

#### 5.4.4.3.1 Time Domain Allocation

##### 5.4.4.3.1.1 Start Symbol and Number of Symbols

###### 5.4.4.3.1.1.1 Legacy

**5.4.4.3.1.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `11398142`)

Legacy
**Frozen Requirement; See below artifacts.**

**Start symbol and number of symbols for PUSCH**

Parameter *startSymbol in message* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq (*puschAllocationConfig structure, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_193\_replaced([11513768](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-fvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) indicates the index of the first OFDM symbol of PUSCH in a slot, regardless of the mapping type.*\*\*

Parameter *numOfPuschSymbols  of message* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq (*puschAllocationConfig structure, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_193\_replaced([11513768](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-fvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) indicate the total number of OFDM symbols used by a UE for PUSCH in a given slot, including DMRS.*\*\*

The table below gives the supported number of PUSCH symbols (see 5G\_L1\_14629\_replaced([11398150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e81DrfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for **Loner, 5G\_L1\_14630\_replaced([11398172](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e81qsvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for** Loki, 5G\_L1\_14631\_replaced([11398202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e81qtvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for **Thor, and [12710019: Odin](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_9pAygGX6EfCwWbD4x7Iu-g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))**\*\*\*\*

---

###### 5.4.4.3.1.1.2 Loner

**5.4.4.3.1.1.2.0-3**  (ID: `12746468`)

Loner
**Supported start symbol and number of symbols for PUSCH** for Loner\*\*\*\*

For Loner L1 HW, supported start symbols of PUSCH see below table:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency** **range** | **TDD PUSCH start symbol** | **FDD PUSCH start symbol** |
| FR1 | {0} | {0, 2 } |
| FR2 | {0} | N/A |

For Loner L1 HW the supported *numOfPuschSymbols  of PUSCH see below table:*

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency range** | **TDD PUSCH number of symbols** | **FDD PUSCH number of symbols** |
| FR1 | {9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14} | {10, 11, 12, 13, **14}** |
| FR2 | {10, 11, 12, 13, 14} **For conformance test: {14}** | **N/A** |

For FR1-FDD the 5G-L1-UL shall support mixed DMRS mapping type A and type B configuration, mixed DMRS position, different symbol number and different start symbol for different UEs in a cell within the same slot.

[Before PR846224] Note: For FR1- FDD, number of symbol L=10 is applicable only with combination of DMRS mapping type B. [End PR846224]

Note: According to 3GPP 38.214 chapter 6.1.2.1 for DMRS mapping type A for startSymbol parameter only allowed value  is {0}.

Rationale, PR846224:  
L2PS may schedule different combinations of start symbol and number of symbols according to a variety of considerations including, but not limited to, mixture of type A and type B, and retransmission request in Tx switch. L1 shall not validate those scheduler considerations; rather, L1 shall only validate the ranges and invalid combinations specified in [11513768: PuschAllocationConfig](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-fvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

---

###### 5.4.4.3.1.1.3 Loki

**5.4.4.3.1.1.3.0-5**  (ID: `13430430`)

Loki
**Supported start symbol and number of symbols for PUSCH** for Loki\*\*\*\*

For Loki L1 HW the supported *startSymbol  of PUSCH see below table:*

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency** **range** | **TDD PUSCH start symbol** | **FDD PUSCH start symbol** |
| FR1 | {0} | {0, 2, [CB015279-D] 3 [End CB015279-D]} |
| FR2 | {0} | N/A |

For Loki L1 HW the supported *numOfPuschSymbols  of PUSCH see below table:*

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency** **range** | **TDD PUSCH number of symbols** | **FDD PUSCH number of symbols** |
| FR1 | {10, 11, 12, 13, 14} | {[CB015279-D] 8, 9, [End CB015279-D]10, 11, 12, 13, 14} |
| FR2 | {10, 11, 12,13} {14} (Conformance test only) | N/A |

Note: According to 3GPP 38.214 chapter 6.1.2.1 for DMRS mapping type A for startSymbol parameter only allowed value  is {0}.

When multiple UEs are scheduled in the same subcell and slot by multiplexing in frequency domain or in spatial demain, the follwing limitations apply:

* the DMRS position shall be the same for all multiplexed UEs.
* the number of symbols may be different among multiplexed UEs, assuming that the condition above is met.

For FR1-FDD the 5G-L1-UL shall support mixed DMRS mapping type A and type B configuration, mixed DMRS position, different symbol number and different start symbol for different UEs in a cell within the same slot.

Rationale, PR846224:  
L2PS may schedule different combinations of start symbol and number of symbols according to a variety of considerations including, but not limited to, mixture of type A and type B, and retransmission request in Tx switch. L1 shall not validate those scheduler considerations; rather, L1 shall only validate the ranges and invalid combinations specified in [11513768: PuschAllocationConfig](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showArtifactPage&artifactURI=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fresources%2FMD_7RvDsPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ&oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9&artifactInModule=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fresources%2FBI_FRy9-fvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ&componentURI=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Frm-projects%2F_Cp08QO3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw%2Fcomponents%2F_CqjVAO3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw).

---

When multiple UEs are scheduled in the same subcell and slot by multiplexing in frequency domain or in spatial demain, the follwing limitations apply:

For FR1-FDD the 5G-L1-UL shall support mixed DMRS mapping type A and type B configuration, mixed DMRS position, different symbol number and different start symbol for different UEs in a cell within the same slot.

Note: According to 3GPP 38.214 chapter 6.1.2.1 for DMRS mapping type A for startSymbol parameter only allowed value  is {0}.

L1 shall not validate those scheduler considerations; rather, L1 shall only validate the ranges and invalid combinations specified in [11513768: PuschAllocationConfig](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showArtifactPage&artifactURI=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fresources%2FMD_7RvDsPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ&oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9&artifactInModule=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fresources%2FBI_FRy9-fvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ&componentURI=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Frm-projects%2F_Cp08QO3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw%2Fcomponents%2F_CqjVAO3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw).

###### 5.4.4.3.1.1.5 Odin

Odin

The supported *startSymbol  of PUSCH see below table:*

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency** **range** | **TDD PUSCH start symbol** | **FDD PUSCH start symbol** |

[CB012115-IB]

When multiple UEs are scheduled in the same subcell and slot by multiplexing in frequency domain or in spatial domain, the following limitations apply.

* The DMRS position shall be the same for all the multiplexed UEs,
* The number of symbols may be different among multiplexed UEs, assuming that the condition above is met.

[End CB012115-IB]

Note: According to 3GPP 38.214 chapter 6.1.2.1 for DMRS mapping type A for startSymbol parameter only allowed value is {0}.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported for TDD FR1 in Odin L1 Call. [End CB012115-IA]

[CB012115-JA][CB012115-LA] The requirement shall be supported for FDD FR1 in Odin L1 Call. [End CB012115-LA]whether CPRI or eCPRI. [End CB012115-JA]

##### 5.4.4.3.1.2 Number of Repetitions for SAg

#### 5.4.4.3.2 Frequency Domain Allocation

##### 5.4.4.3.2.1 Start PRB for PUSCH

###### 5.4.4.3.2.1.1 Common

**5.4.4.3.2.1.1.0-3**  (ID: `12710342`)

Common
**PUSCH Start PRB**

Parameter *startPrb in message* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq (*puschAllocationConfig structure, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_193\_replaced([11513768](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-fvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) gives the index of the first PRB allocated to the grant of the UE.*\*\*

Note: startPrb is provided by L2-PS with 1 PRB granularity ranging (in the most extreme case where there are the fewer PRBs to allocate) from [0..(ulBandwidth – Min(numOfPrb))] where numOfPrb is defined in the requirement 5G\_L1\_4484\_replaced([11398336](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e82RyPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

This table represents the supported PUSCH starting PRBs (see [11398269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e81qv_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for **Loner, [11398289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e82RwfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for** Loki, [11398302](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e82Rw_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for **Thor, and [12710069 for Odin)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rIWsAGYAEfCwWbD4x7Iu-g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).**\*\*\*\*

(Values are implicitly cumulative within L1 HWs.)

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency range** | **Cell bandwidth** | **Supported startPrb** |
| FDD (below 3GHz) | 5 MHz | [0..23] |
| 10 MHz | [0..50] |  |
| 15 MHz | [0..77] |  |
| 20 MHz | [0..104] |  |
| 25 MHz | [0..131] |  |
| 30 MHz (stop support on ABIL from 22R1 onward) | [0..158] |  |
| 35 MHz | [0..186] |  |
| 40 MHz (2x20  and 1x40 MHz) | [0..214] |  |
| 50 MHz (2*25 and 1*50) | [0..268] |  |
| TDD (FR1) | 10 MHz | [0..22] |
| 15 MHz | [0..36] |  |
| 20 MHz | [0..49] |  |
| 30 MHz | [0..76] |  |
| 40 MHz | [0..104] |  |
| 50 MHz | [0..131] |  |
| 60 MHz | [0..160] |  |
| 70 MHz | [0..187] |  |
| 80 MHz | [0..215] |  |
| 90 MHz | [0..243] |  |
| 100 MHz | [0..271] |  |
| TDD (FR2) | 100 MHz | [0..64] |

The requirement is fully supported in Loki and Thor.

[CB012115-IA] The parts of requirement that correspond to TDD FR1 shall be supported. [End CB012115-IA]

[CB012115-JA][CB012115-LA] The parts of requirement that correspond to FDD FR1 shall be supported. [End CB012115-LA] whether CPRI or eCPRI. [End CB012115-JA]

Notice: FR2 is not supported in CB012115.

PRB-min(number of PRB)  (number of PRB is defined in 5G\_RAN\_UP\_9357 for different bandwidth) is supported only from L1.   [CB005892-B, CB005892-BC] [0..UL\_bandwidth-min\_RBG\_size], where: - UL\_bandwidth is maximum number of PRBs in UL bandwidth, as specified in 5G\_RAN\_UP\_9357, - min\_RBG\_size is minimal number of PRBs in RBG, as specified in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_935. [End CB005892-B, CB005892-BC] |
| FR2 | [0..18] | [0..58] |  |  |

| Initial BWP | Active BWP | Initial BWP | Active BWP |  |
| FR1 | [0..271] (100 MHz) [0..215] (80 MHz) [0..160] (60 MHz) [0..104] (40 MHz) [0..49] (20 MHz) | Value 0.. UL\_bandwidth - min\_nb\_RB where: - UL\_bandwidth is maximum number of RBs in UL bandwidth, as specified in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_920 - min\_nb\_RB is minimal number of RBs, as specified in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_935. | [0..23] (5MHz) [0..50] (10MHz) [0..77] (15MHz) [0..104] (20MHz)   [CB006761-A] [0..158] (30MHz) [End CB006761-A] | Value 0.. UL\_bandwidth - min\_nb\_RB where: - UL\_bandwidth is maximum number of RBs in UL bandwidth, as specified in 5G\_L1\_4884\_replaced([11497223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fExeu_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) - min\_nb\_RB is minimal number of RBs, as specified in 5G\_L1\_2818\_replaced([11398355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e82RyvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).   [CB005892-B, CB005892-BC] [0..UL\_bandwidth-min\_RBG\_size], where: - UL\_bandwidth is maximum number of PRBs in UL bandwidth, as specified in 5G\_L1\_4884\_replaced([11497223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fExeu_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), - min\_RBG\_size is minimal number of PRBs in RBG, as specified in 5G\_L1\_2818\_replaced([11398355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e82RyvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB005892-B, CB005892-BC] |

| L1-SW uses the startPrb in the PUSCH context. Note that for DMRS pilot generation, the L1-FPGA considers the startPrb value for the mapping of DMRS symbols to subcarriers (See precoding and mapping to physical resources section 6.4.1.1.3 in [1]). Thus the 1st subcarrier to be used by DMRS pilot generation is equal to [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "startPrb × N\_sc^RB". The text is in a light gray color against a white background. The expression is written in a standard mathematical font. The term "startPrb" is written in italics, while "N\_sc^RB" is in a regular font with "RB" as a superscript.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_596.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]. |  |  |  |  |

###### 5.4.4.3.2.1.3 Loki

**5.4.4.3.2.1.3.0-1**  (ID: `11398295`)

Loki
The supported values of starting PRB is as follows:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency range** | **TDD PUSCH starting PRB** |  | **FDD/SUL PUSCH starting PRB** |  |
| **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** | **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** |  |
| FR1 | [0…271] | [0.. UL\_bandwidth - min\_RBG\_size] | [0…104] | [0.. UL\_bandwidth - min\_RBG\_size] |
| FR2 | [0…63] | **[5GC001402-I][CB007403-C] [0…64]** [End 5GC001402-I][End CB007403-C]***\** \*[5GC001402-N][CB007403-D] For conformance tests: 0** [End 5GC001402-N][End CB007403-D]\*\*\*\* | **NA** | **NA** |

Values in the table are implicitly cumulative within FR and BWP, the *UL\_bandwidth is specified in 5G\_L1\_4884\_replaced([11497223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fExeu_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and min\_RBG\_size is minimal number of PRBs in RBG, as specified in 5G\_L1\_2818\_replaced([11398355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e82RyvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));*

Note: 5G-L1-UL shall validate that startPrb + numOfPrb <= max(numOfPrb)

**[CB008205-B] 5G-L1-UL shall also support requirements above in ABIO for O-RAN compliant FR1 FDD eCPRI.**[End CB008205-B]\*\*\*\*

---

###### 5.4.4.3.2.1.4 Thor

**5.4.4.3.2.1.4.0-3**  (ID: `11398322`)

Thor
The supported values of starting PRB is as follows:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency range** | **TDD PUSCH starting PRB** |  | **FDD/SUL PUSCH starting PRB** |  |
| **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** | **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** |  |
| FR1 | **[RP003187-A] [0…271]** [End RP003187-A]\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-A] [0.. UL\_bandwidth - min\_RBG\_size]** [End RP003187-A]\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-H] [0… UL\_bandwidth - min\_RBG\_size]** [End RP003187-H]\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-H] [0.. UL\_bandwidth - min\_RBG\_size]** [End RP003187-H]\*\*\*\* |
| FR2 | N/A | N/A | N/A | N/A |

Values in the table are implicitly cumulative within FR and BWP, the *UL\_bandwidth is maximum number of PRBs in UL bandwidth, and min\_RBG\_size is minimal number of PRBs in RBG.*

Note: 5G-L1-UL shall ensure that startPrb + numOfPrb <= max(numOfPrb)

**[CB009576-AA, CB009576-AC]The requirement is also applicable for** ASOG&ASOH for FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI and FR1-FDD-CPRI . The *UL\_bandwidth is specified in 5G\_L1\_12609\_replaced([11395623](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8oPUfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).* *[End CB009576-AA, CB009576-AC]***\***\*

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| *Technology* | *Feature / support* |
| FR1 FDD CPRI | supported |
| FR1 FDD OBSAI | **[CB010969-SR-CB] supported** [End CB010969-SR-CB]\*\*\*\* |
| FR1 FDD 7-2a eCPRI | supported |
| FR1 TDD 7-2a eCPRI | supported |

---

###### 5.4.4.3.2.1.5 Odin

**5.4.4.3.2.1.5.0-1**  (ID: `12710072`)

Odin
5G-L1-UL shall support starting PRB values as follows.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency range** | **TDD PUSCH starting PRB** |  | **FDD/SUL PUSCH starting PRB** |  |
| **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** | **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** |  |
| FR1 | [0.. UL\_bandwidth - min\_RBG\_size] | [0.. UL\_bandwidth - min\_RBG\_size] | [0.. UL\_bandwidth - min\_RBG\_size] | [0.. UL\_bandwidth - min\_RBG\_size] |
| FR2 | N/A | N/A | N/A | N/A |

5G-L1-UL shall validate that startPrb + numOfPrb <= ulBandwidth, in accordance with the subcell bandwidth ([12556505](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fnfLoCdcEfCNjfqfyUlvmw)) and supported startPrb ([12710331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_c3rTUWYOEfCwWbD4x7Iu-g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

Note: In practice, the initial BWP and Active BWP are within [0.. UL\_bandwidth - min\_RBG\_size] range. ([11101535: Start PRB for PUSCH](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knY44fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) The *UL\_bandwidth is maximum number of PRBs in UL bandwidth, and min\_RBG\_size is minimal number of PRBs in RBG to support PRACH and PUCCH. These values are determined by CPRT ([11275482: Cell parameters for L2](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpGnSPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), and L2PS schedules accordingly. That process is transparent to L1, so L1 is required to support the fixed values of [12710331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_c3rTUWYOEfCwWbD4x7Iu-g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).*

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported by Odin for TDD FR1. [End CB012115-IA]

[CB012115-JA][CB012115-LA] The requirement shall be supported by Odin for FDD FR1. [End CB012115-LA]whether CPRI or eCPRI. [End CB012115-JA]

---

##### 5.4.4.3.2.2 Number of PRBs

###### 5.4.4.3.2.2.1 Common

**5.4.4.3.2.2.1.0-2**  (ID: `12710381`)

Common
**Number of PRBs for PUSCH**

Parameter *numOfPrb in message* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq gives the number of PRBs allocated to the UE.\*\*

The following table represents the supported numbers of PRBs. (See [11398347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e82RyfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for **Loner, [11398369](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e82RzPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for** Loki, [11398386](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8240PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for **Thor, and [12710390 for Odin](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_IUaRV2YTEfCwWbD4x7Iu-g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).)**\*\*\*\*

Values are implicitly cumulative within L1 HWs.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency range** | **Cell bandwidth** | **Supported numOfPrb** |
| FDD (below 3GHz) | 5 MHz | [2..25] |
| 10 MHz | [2..52] |  |
| 15 MHz | [2..79] |  |
| 20 MHz | [2..106] |  |
| 25 MHz | [2..133] |  |
| 30 MHz (stop support on ABIL from 22R1 onward) | [2..160] |  |
| 35 MHz | [0..188] |  |
| 40 MHz (2x20 and 1x40 MHz) | [2..216] |  |
| 50 MHz (2*25 and 1*50) | [0..270] |  |
| TDD (FR1) | 10 MHz | [2..24] |
| 15 MHz | [2..38] |  |
| 20 MHz | [2..51] |  |
| 30 MHz | [2..78] |  |
| 40 MHz | [2..106] |  |
| 50 MHz | [2..133] |  |
| 60 MHz | [2..162] |  |
| 70 MHz | [2..189] |  |
| 80 MHz | [2..217] |  |
| 90 MHz | [2..245] |  |
| 100 MHz | [2..273] |  |
| TDD (FR2) | 100 MHz | 2, 3, 4, 8, 16, 32, 66 |
| 100 MHz | 2, 3, 4, 8, 14, 16, 32, 48, 64, 66 (32, 64 for conformance test 5GC001402-N) (2, 3, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64 for transform precoding enabled) |  |

The requirement is fully supported in Loki and Thor.

[CB012115-IA] The parts of requirement that correspond to TDD FR1 shall be supported. [End CB012115-IA]

[CB012115-JA][CB012115-LA] The parts of requirement that correspond to FDD FR1 shall be supported. [End CB012115-LA] whether CPRI or eCPRI. [End CB012115-JA]

Notice: FR2 is not supported in CB012115.

---

###### 5.4.4.3.2.2.2 Loner

**5.4.4.3.2.2.2.0-3**  (ID: `11398361`)

Loner
Values from this table match the numOfPrbs from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message and indicate the total number of PRBs within BWP used by UE in given slot.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency range** | **TDD num of PUSCH PRBs** |  | **FDD/SUL num of PUSCH PRBs** |  |
| **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** | **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** |  |
| FR1 | {48,14} | [2..273] | 24 (5 MHz) 48 (10, 15, 20 MHz) 48 (30MHz) | [2..UL\_bandwidth], where UL\_bandwidth is maximum number of PRBs in UL bandwidth, as specified in 5G\_L1\_4884\_replaced([11497223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fExeu_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| FR2 | {48,14} | {2, 3, 4, 8, 14, 16, 32, 64, 66} | **-** | **-** |

**[Before CNI-103786] For the different PRB and MCS combinations, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5091\_replaced([11203399](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKgfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for detailed information.** [End CNI-103786]\*\*\*\*

The supported maximum number of PRBs (UL\_bandwidth) is equal to the ulBandwidth in UlCell\_SetupReq message, refer to [12556505: Supported Bandwidths](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fnfLoCdcEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

Values from this table match the numOfPrbs from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message and indicate the total number of PRBs within BWP used by UE in given slot.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency range** | **TDD num of PUSCH PRBs** |  | **FDD/SUL num of PUSCH PRBs** |  |
| **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** | **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** |  |
| FR1 | {3,48} | [2…273] | {3,24,48} | [2..UL\_bandwidth] |
| FR2 | {3,48} **[5GC001402-I] [CB007403-C]** {3, 14, 48} **[End 5GC001402-I] [End CB007403-C]** | 66   **[5GC001402-I][CB007403-C]** {2, 3, 4, 8, 14, 16, 32, 64, 66} **[End 5GC001402-I][End CB007403-C]** **[5GC001402-N][CB007403-D] For conformance tests: {32, 64}** **[End 5GC001402-N][End CB007403-N]**   **[Draft for CB007438]** When transform precoding is enabled: {2, 3, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64} **[End Draft for CB007438]** | **NA** | **NA** |

When transform precoding is enabled number of PUSCH PRBs is limited to selected value according 3GPP formula:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression: $2^{a\_2} \cdot 3^{a\_3} \cdot 5^{a\_5}$. The expression consists of three terms multiplied together. Each term is a number raised to the power of a variable. The numbers are 2, 3, and 5, and the corresponding variables are $a\_2$, $a\_3$, and $a\_5$. The multiplication is indicated by a dot between each term.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_609.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where: (α2, α3, α5 is a set of non-negative integer)

The supported maximum number of PRBs (UL\_bandwidth) is equal to the ulBandwidth in UlCell\_SetupReq message, refer to [12556505: Supported Bandwidths](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fnfLoCdcEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

**[CB008205-B] 5G-L1-UL shall also support requirements above in ABIO for O-RAN compliant FR1 FDD eCPRI.**[End CB008205-B]\*\*\*\*

###### 5.4.4.3.2.2.4 Thor

**5.4.4.3.2.2.4.0-3**  (ID: `11398421`)

Thor
11398421:

Values from this table match the numOfPrbs from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message and indicate the total number of PRBs within BWP used by UE in given slot.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency range** | **TDD num of PUSCH PRBs** |  | **FDD/SUL num of PUSCH PRBs** |  |
| **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** | **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** |  |
| FR1 | **[RP003187-A] {3,48}** [End RP003187-A]\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-A]**[2…UL\_bandwidth] **[End RP003187-A]**\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-H]{3,24,48}** [End RP003187-H]\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-H] [2..UL\_bandwidth]** [End RP003187-H]\*\*\*\* |
| FR2 | **[CB010576-A] {3,48}** [End CB010576-A]\*\*\*\* | **[CB010576-A] {0...66}** [End CB010576-A]\*\*\*\* | N/A | N/A |

For **L1 Thor when transform precoding is enabled number of PUSCH PRBs is limited to selected value according 3GPP formula (see 3GPP TS 38.211, ch.6.3.1.4 'Transform precoding'):**

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression: $2^{a\_2} \cdot 3^{a\_3} \cdot 5^{a\_5}$. The expression consists of three terms multiplied together. Each term is a number raised to the power of a variable. The numbers are 2, 3, and 5, and the corresponding variables are $a\_2$, $a\_3$, and $a\_5$. The multiplication is indicated by a dot between each term.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_613.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where: (α2, α3, α5 is a set of non-negative integer).

The supported maximum number of PRBs (UL\_bandwidth) is equal to the ulBandwidth in UlCell\_SetupReq message, refer to [12556505: Supported Bandwidths](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fnfLoCdcEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

**[CB009576-AA, CB009576-AC]The requirement is also applicable for** ASOG&ASOH for FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI and FR1-FDD-CPRI . The *UL\_bandwidth is specified in 5G\_L1\_12609\_replaced([11395623](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8oPUfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).* *[End CB009576-AA, CB009576-AC]***\***\*

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| *Technology* | *Feature / support* |
| **FR1 FDD CPRI** | Supported |
| **FR1 FDD OBSAI** | **[CB010969-SR-CB] Supported** [End CB010969-SR-CB]\*\*\*\* |
| **FR1 TDD 7-2a eCPRI** | Supported |
| **FR2 TDD 7-2a eCPRI** | Supported |

Values from this table match the numOfPrbs from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message and indicate the total number of PRBs within BWP used by UE in given slot.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Frequency range** | **TDD num of PUSCH PRBs** |  | **FDD/SUL num of PUSCH PRBs** |  |
| **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** | **Initial BWP** | **Active BWP** |  |
| FR1 | {3,48} | [2…UL\_bandwidth] | {3,24,48} | [2..UL\_bandwidth] |
| FR2 | N/A | N/A | N/A | N/A |

When transform precoding (DTF-s-OFDM) is enabled, the number of PUSCH PRBs is limited to selected value according 3GPP formula (see 3GPP TS 38.211, ch.6.3.1.4 'Transform precoding'):

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression: $2^{a\_2} \cdot 3^{a\_3} \cdot 5^{a\_5}$. The expression consists of three terms multiplied together. Each term is a number raised to the power of a variable. The numbers are 2, 3, and 5, and the corresponding variables are $a\_2$, $a\_3$, and $a\_5$. The multiplication is indicated by a dot between each term.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_613.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where α2, α3, α5 is a set of non-negative integer.

The supported maximum number of PRBs (UL\_bandwidth) is equal to the ulBandwidth in UlCell\_SetupReq message, refer to [12556505: Supported Bandwidths](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fnfLoCdcEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported by Odin for TDD FR1. [End CB012115-IA]

[CB012115-JA][CB012115-LA] The requirement shall be supported by Odin for FDD FR1. [End CB012115-LA]whether CPRI or eCPRI. [End CB012115-JA]

---

##### 5.4.4.3.2.3 Restrictions

**5.4.4.3.2.3.0-3**  (ID: `12710955`)

Restrictions
5G-L1-UL shall validate the incoming requests against the following rules which restrict the set of possible starting PRBs and Number of PRBs for PUSCH.

1. The PRBs that are allocated by PUCCH shall not be allocated for PUSCH.
2. In PRACH slot, the PRBs which are occupied by the preamble (msg1) shall not be allocated for PUSCH.

The requirement is common for all Technologies.

The requirement is supported in Loner, Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement is also supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.4.3.3 Spatial Stream Allocation

##### 5.4.4.3.3.1 FDD 8Rx reception with split panel mode

###### 5.4.4.3.3.1.0-1 FDD 8Rx reception with split panel mode 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support 8Rx SU MIMO IRC receiver by receiving 8 streams in 4 beams. Every 2 continuous streams corresponding to one beam. The split panel mode of each beam is indicated by the IE suSplitPanelMode in UlData_PuschReceiveReq ( 12884359 ).** (ID: 13134610)

#### 5.4.4.3.4 UE pairing

##### 5.4.4.3.4.1 Configuration

##### 5.4.4.3.4.2 CP-OFDM

###### 5.4.4.3.4.2.1 Loki, Thor

**5.4.4.3.4.2.1.0-2**  (ID: `13367521`)

Loki, Thor
[Before CB008148-SR-C]

The content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 26R2. Refer to the below requirements.

L1 shall support for PUSCH transmission only UEs with CP-OFDM waveform for feature CB008360, it means UEs configured with DFT-s-OFDM waveform for PUSCH are excluded from MU pairing (**5GC000545 => Disabled)**

[CB008148-SR-C]

5G-L1-UL shall support reception of PUSCH grants for MU-MIMO paired UEs with CP-OFDM waveform.

In Multi User MIMO configuration, the UEs with identical pairedUe value ([11513756: UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) share PRBs in the same slot. pariedUe value indicates the RNTI of the Root UE.

For the concept of Root UE and candidate groups, you may refer to [11355469: UL Multi-User MIMO](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucROKvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw)

[End CB008148-SR-C]

Parity from **CB008360: This requirement is also applicable for** Thor for FR1-TDD-eCPRI.\*\*\*\*

---

###### 5.4.4.3.4.2.2 Odin

**5.4.4.3.4.2.2.0-1**  (ID: `12710993`)

Odin
5G-UL-L1 shall support UE pairing for CP-OFDM. In Multi User MIMO configuration, the UEs with identical pairedUe value ([11513756: UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) share PRBs in the same slot. pariedUe value indicates the RNTI of the Root UE.

The UEs that are configured with DFT-s-OFDM are excluded from UE pairing.

For the concept of Root UE and candidate groups, you may refer to [11355469: UL Multi-User MIMO](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucROKvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw)

[CB012115-IB] The requirement for Odin is on par with the legacy requirement for Loki and Thor as per CB008360. [End CB012115-IB]

---

##### 5.4.4.3.4.3 DFT-s-OFDM

**5.4.4.3.4.3.0-1**  (ID: `13367557`)

DFT-s-OFDM
5G-L1-UL shall support MU-MIMO UE pairing for PUSCH grants with DFT-s-OFDM waveform.

In Multi User MIMO configuration, the UEs with identical pairedUe value ([11513756: UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) share PRBs in the same slot. pariedUe value indicates the RNTI of the Root UE.

For the concept of Root UE and candidate groups, you may refer to [11355469: UL Multi-User MIMO](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucROKvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw)

---

##### 5.4.4.3.4.4 UE pairing cases for C8 subcell MU receiver

###### 5.4.4.3.4.4.1 Logic

###### 5.4.4.3.4.4.1.1 Thor

**5.4.4.3.4.4.1.1.0-2**  (ID: `11402006`)

Thor
When C8 subcell is configured, L1 shall support UL MU-MIMO pairing where root UE is paired with UE(s) having the same startPrb. Up to four UEs having one layer can be paired. For the parameter startPrb in the structure **PuschAllocationConfig in** UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message, refer to 5G\_L1\_IF\_193\_replaced([11513768](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-fvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For the concepts of root UE and paired UEs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8267\_replaced([11145665](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksBhQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

Note: the supported number of beams and ceAxcIndex for each paired UE is given in the following table.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | **number of beams per UE** | **numCeAxCIndex per UE** |
| **two paired UEs** | {2,2}*,* *[CB009363-OO-UPD001] {1,3}*[End CB009363-OO-UPD001]\*\*\*\* | {4,4}**,** [CB009363-OO-UPD001] {2,6} **[End CB009363-OO-UPD001]**\*\* |
| **three paired UEs** | {1,1,2} | {2,2,4} |
| **four paired UEs** | {1,1,1,1} | {2,2,2,2} |
| **four paired UEs** | {1,1,1,1} | {2,2,2,2} |

The order of the supported beams/ceAxcIndex in the table can be swapped, i.e., each of the paired UE is allowed to be configured with more beams than others.

* {2,2} means the first paired UE has two beams and the second paired UE has another 2 beams.

\*\* {4,4} means the first paired UE has four eAxCs and the second paired UE has another 4 eAxCs.

**[CB009363-OO-UPD001] \*\*\* It is allowed to allocate 3 beams to one of the paired UE and 1 beam to another paired UE in case of two UEs are paired, which also means that a rank2 UE may have only one beam.** [End CB009363-OO-UPD001]\*\*\*\*

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20049\_replaced([11148996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksL5UvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details on how beams are allocated for each paired UE.

---

###### 5.4.4.3.4.4.1.2 Odin

**5.4.4.3.4.4.1.2.0-1**  (ID: `12711013`)

Odin
When C8 subcell is configured, 5G-L1-UL shall support MU-MIMO pairing where root UE is paired with UE(s) having the same startPrb. Up to four UEs having one layer can be paired. For the parameter startPrb in the structure **PuschAllocationConfig in** UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message, refer to [11513768](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-fvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). For the concepts of Virtual UE, Root UE, and Paired UEs, refer to [11145665: Concept of virtual UE](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksBhQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).\*\*\*\*

Note: the supported number of beams and ceAxcIndex for each paired UE is given in the following table.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | **number of beams per UE** | **numCeAxCIndex per UE** |
| **two paired UEs** | {2,2}*,  {1,3}*\*\* | {4,4}\*\*, {2,6} |
| **three paired UEs** | {1,1,2} | {2,2,4} |
| **four paired UEs** | {1,1,1,1} | {2,2,2,2} |
| **four paired UEs** | {1,1,1,1} | {2,2,2,2} |

The order of the supported beams/ceAxcIndex in the table can be swapped, i.e., each of the paired UEs is allowed to be configured with more beams than others.

\* {2,2} means the first paired UE has two beams and the second paired UE has another 2 beams.

\*\* {4,4} means the first paired UE has four eAxCs and the second paired UE has another 4 eAxCs.

\*\*\* It is allowed to allocate 3 beams to one of the paired UE and 1 beam to another paired UE in case of two UEs are paired, which also means that a rank2 UE may have only one beam.

Refer to [11148996: Beam selection for MU-MIMO UEs in 8RX](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksL5UvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for details on how beams are allocated for each paired UE by L2.

 [xlsx\_desc]--- 2Rank1 + FDM ---
NaN NaN Layer NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN Note: NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN 1. pairedUe always points to the root UE for all paired UEs in one virtual UE NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
PRB 0.0 UE0 UE1 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN 2. For 3Rank1 case, 2 beams are used in rootUE. It is L2 to decide which UE can have two beams. No extra IF impact NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN 1.0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN 3. fcpStartPrbc and fcpNumPrbc are used to extend PRB region if needed. See 2Rank1+FDM for example to explain the FCP msg for each eAxC stream NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN 2.0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN 4. fcpAddSectionEnable is only activated to if different beams are used. NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN 3.0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN 5. sectionId notation {X,Y} used in some examples exploits the interpretation of adapted for MADE ES1. According to the notation used in this chapter, X corresponds to the starting beamforming region identifier, whereas Y indicates the number of regions spanned by unchanging beamId or zero-beam.  [xlsx\_desc]--- 2Rank1 + FDM ---
NaN NaN Layer NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN Note: NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN 1. pairedUe always points to the root UE for all paired UEs in one virtual UE NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
PRB 0.0 UE0 UE1 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN 2. For 3Rank1 case, 2 beams are used in rootUE. It is L2 to decide which UE can have two beams. No extra IF impact NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN 1.0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN 3. fcpStartPrbc and fcpNumPrbc are used to extend PRB region if needed. See 2Rank1+FDM for example to explain the FCP msg for each eAxC stream NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN 2.0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN 4. fcpAddSectionEnable is only activated to if different beams are used. NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN 3.0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN 5. sectionId notation {X,Y} used in some examples exploits the interpretation of adapted for MADE ES1. According to the notation used in this chapter, X corresponds to the starting beamforming region identifier, whereas Y indicates the number of regions spanned by unchanging beamId or zero-beam. (Refer to BTSC\_L1\_Req\_7397) NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN

In addition,
- 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support MU pairing case where root UE is paired with another UE candidate group with non-contiguous PRB allocation. The PRB gap can be compensated by adjusting *fcpNumPrbc per UE grant. See example below and more details in 5G\_UP\_13459.*
- 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall not support MU pairing case where root UE is paired with another UE candidate group with non-contiguous PRB allocation on FH domain. See example below and more details in 5G\_UP\_13460.
- 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support MU pairing case where root UE is paired with another UE candidate group with at least one of the paired UEs don't share common PRB with root UE.
 
**Example of non-contiguous PRB allocation (supported):**
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a screenshot of a spreadsheet with a grid representing resource allocation for different User Equipments (UEs) across Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) and DMRS ports.
The spreadsheet has columns labeled with numbers 0, 1, 2, and 3, representing DMRS ports. The rows are labeled PRB 0 through PRB 10, representing Physical Resource Blocks.
The grid shows the following allocations:
\* **DMRS Port 0:** This port is allocated to UE0, which occupies PRBs 0 through 10. This allocation is represented by a large green rectangle.
\* **DMRS Port 1:** This port is not explicitly allocated to any UE in the visible portion of the grid.
\* **DMRS Port 2:** This port is allocated to three different UEs in a stacked manner:
\* UE1 occupies PRBs 0 through 2 (represented by a yellow rectangle).
\* UE2 occupies PRBs 3 through 6 (represented by a blue rectangle).
\* UE3 occupies PRBs 7 through 9 (represented by an orange rectangle).
The title of the spreadsheet is "DMRS port".
Here is the table representation:

| PRB 5 | UE0 |  | UE2 |  |
| PRB 6 | UE0 |  | UE2 |  |
| PRB 7 | UE0 |  | UE3 |  |

| PRB 9 | UE0 |  | UE3 |  |
| PRB 10 | UE0 |  |  |  |

|  |  |  |  |  |

#### 5.4.4.3.5 Data slot types for PUSCH

##### 5.4.4.3.5.1 Overview

###### 5.4.4.3.5.1.0-1 Overview Requirement 5G_L1_3743_replaced( 11398566 ) presents the slot types for PUSCH reception except Msg3 and requirement 5G_L1_3977_replaced( 11398584 ) presents the slot types for PUSCH reception for Msg3 , where Uc means PUCCH, Ud stands for PUSCH data and PTRS (no DMRS), Udm represents DMRS associated with PUSCH, GP means Guard Period and SRS stands for Sounding Reference Signal [refer to requirements 5G_L1_3685_replaced( 11396968 )]. Note that the “PUSCH duration in symbols” is refered in the L2/L1 interface as numOfPuschSymbols. (ID: 11398559)

##### 5.4.4.3.5.2 Loner, non-MSG3

**5.4.4.3.5.2.0-1**  (ID: `11398566`)

• Udm: DMRS associated with PUSCH.

• SRS: Sounding Reference Signal.

Note that the “PUSCH duration in symbols” is referred in the L2-L1 interface as numOfPuschSymbols.

### 5.4.4.5 Spatial Configuration

#### 5.4.4.5.0-1 Spatial Configuration The following requirements pertain to 11513774: PuschSpatialConfig of UlData_PuschReceiveReq message. (ID: 12712202)

#### 5.4.4.5.1 Spatial Mode Configuration

##### 5.4.4.5.1.1 Legacy

###### 5.4.4.5.1.1.1 Common

**5.4.4.5.1.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `11398686`)

Common
Content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 23R4 release, For later releases please refer to the objects below.

SpatialMode parameter is described in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq  message. The supported transmission schemes on PUSCH as below:

-  Single Tx transmission

-  Spatial multiplexing rank1

-  Spatial multiplexing rank2

-  "1: Tx div" is not used.

-  [5GC000836-B] Single antenna and spatial multiplexing is supported for FDD.

[5GC001868-A] 5G-L1-UL shall support Single Tx transmission in Loki L1 HW [End 5GC001868-A]

[5GC001868-C, 5GC001868-D] 5G-L1-UL shall support Spatial multiplexing rank1 and rank2 in Loki L1 HW [End 5GC001868-C, 5GC001868-D]

[RP003187-A, RP003187-H]The requirement is valid for L1 Thor [End RP003187-A, RP003187-H]

[CB009444-B] This requirement is applicable for RINLINE1 for FR1-FDD-eCPRI. [End CB009444-B]

---

###### 5.4.4.5.1.1.2 Single Tx with CLSM

###### 5.4.4.5.1.1.2.0-1 Single Tx with CLSM Content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 23R4 release, For later releases please refer to the objects below. 5G-L1-UL shall support single Tx transmission with Closed Loop Spatial Multiplexing rank 1 or rank 2 with 2 PUSCH antenna ports {1000,1001} in L1 Thor. (ID: 11401872)

##### 5.4.4.5.1.2 Loner

**5.4.4.5.1.2.0-1**  (ID: `11398693`)

Loner
*SpatialMode parameter is described in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq  message.\*\*

- SINGLE\_ANTENNA

- TX\_DIVERSITY

- CL\_SPATIAL\_MUX

- OL\_SPATIAL\_MUX

The supported transmission schemes on PUSCH as below:

***\*-*\*Single Tx transmission**\*\*\*\*

***\*-*\*Spatial multiplexing rank1**\*\*\*\*

***\*-  Spatial multiplexing rank2***\*

***\*-* \*"1: Tx div" is not used.**\*\*\*\*

***\*- Single antenna and spatial multiplexing is supported* \*for FDD.**\*\*\*\*

**Note:**

'Spatial multiplexing rank2': 2 layers **2Tx transmission and parameter SpatialMode value is *CL\_SPATIAL\_MUX or OL\_SPATIAL\_MUX)***

'Spatial multiplexing rank1': 1 layer 2Tx diversity transmission and parameter SpatialMode value is TX\_DIVERSITY)

'Single Tx transmission': 1 layer Single Tx transmission and parameter *SpatialMode value is* SINGLE\_ANTENNA)\*\*

---

##### 5.4.4.5.1.3 Loki

**5.4.4.5.1.3.0-1**  (ID: `11398700`)

Loki
*SpatialMode parameter is described in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq  message.\*\*

- SINGLE\_ANTENNA

- TX\_DIVERSITY

- CL\_SPATIAL\_MUX

- OL\_SPATIAL\_MUX

The supported transmission schemes on PUSCH as below:

***\*-*\*Single Tx transmission**\*\*\*\*

***\*-*\*Spatial multiplexing rank1**\*\*\*\*

***\*-  Spatial multiplexing rank2***\*

***\*-* \*"1: Tx div" is not used.**\*\*\*\*

***\*-* \*Single antenna and spatial multiplexing is supported** for FDD.***\****\*

5G-L1-UL shall support Single Tx transmission in Loki L1 HW

5G-L1-UL shall support Spatial multiplexing rank1 and rank2 in Loki L1 HW

**Note:**

'Spatial multiplexing rank2': 2 layers **2Tx transmission and parameter SpatialMode value is *CL\_SPATIAL\_MUX or OL\_SPATIAL\_MUX)***

'Spatial multiplexing rank1': 1 layer 2Tx diversity transmission and parameter SpatialMode value is TX\_DIVERSITY)

'Single Tx transmission': 1 layer Single Tx transmission and parameter *SpatialMode value is* SINGLE\_ANTENNA)\*\*

---

##### 5.4.4.5.1.4 Thor

**5.4.4.5.1.4.0-3**  (ID: `12680513`)

Thor
*SpatialMode parameter is described in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message.\*\*

- SINGLE\_ANTENNA

- TX\_DIVERSITY

- CL\_SPATIAL\_MUX

- OL\_SPATIAL\_MUX

5G-L1-UL shall support transmission schemes on PUSCH as below:

***\*-*\*Single Tx transmission**\*\*\*\*

***\*-*\*Spatial multiplexing rank1**\*\*\*\*

***\*-  Spatial multiplexing rank2***\*

***\*-* \*"1: Tx div" is not used.**\*\*\*\*

***\*-* \*Single antenna and spatial multiplexing are supported** for FDD.***\****\*

5G-L1-UL shall support 8Rx MMSE IRC SU receiver for PUSCH with up to 2 layers in Thor HW when C8 [CB013442-D] or A8 [End CB013442-D] subcell is configured, [CB008465-AA] or A4 subcell with UL CoMP enabled (isNrUlCompEnabled = True in L1Config\_SwConfigurationReq message [11584802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BPk5LPwGEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for UEs configured with joint reception (mode = JointReception in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message [11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008465-AA]

**Note:**

**2 layers:**

- 'Spatial multiplexing rank2': 2 layers **2Tx transmission and parameter SpatialMode value is *CL\_SPATIAL\_MUX or OL\_SPATIAL\_MUX)***

**1 layer:**

- 'Spatial multiplexing rank1': 1 layer 2Tx diversity transmission and parameter SpatialMode value is TX\_DIVERSITY)

- 'Single Tx transmission': 1 layer Single Tx transmission and parameter *SpatialMode value is* SINGLE\_ANTENNA)\*\*

History:

The requirement was originally introduced for 5GC000532. It was ported to Thor in RP003187 for FR1 TDD eCPRI and FR1 FDD CPRI. It was tested for FR1 FDD OBSAI, A2 and A4 subcells, in CB010970. It was also tested for FR1 FDD 7-2a eCPRI in CB011178, for up to 4 streams. CB013442 is lifting all the previous restrictions for FDD.

---

##### 5.4.4.5.1.5 Odin

**5.4.4.5.1.5.0-1**  (ID: `12711059`)

Odin
*SpatialMode parameter is described in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message.\*\*

- SINGLE\_ANTENNA

- TX\_DIVERSITY

- CL\_SPATIAL\_MUX

- OL\_SPATIAL\_MUX

5G-L1-UL shall support transmission schemes on PUSCH as below:

***\*-*\*Single Tx transmission**\*\*\*\*

***\*-*\*Spatial multiplexing rank1**\*\*\*\*

***\*-  Spatial multiplexing rank2***\*

***\*-* \*"1: Tx div" is not used.**\*\*\*\*

-  [CB012115-LA] Single antenna and spatial multiplexing are supported **for FDD. [End CB012115-LA]**

5G-L1-UL shall also support 8Rx MMSE IRC SU receiver for PUSCH with up to 2 layers in Odin HW when 8-stream subcell is configured.

**Note:**

**2 layers:**

- 'Spatial multiplexing rank2': 2 layers **2Tx transmission and parameter SpatialMode value is *CL\_SPATIAL\_MUX or OL\_SPATIAL\_MUX***

**1 layer:**

- 'Spatial multiplexing rank1': 1 layer 2Tx diversity transmission and parameter SpatialMode value is TX\_DIVERSITY - Not Used

- 'Single Tx transmission': 1 layer Single Tx transmission and parameter *SpatialMode value is* SINGLE\_ANTENNA\*\*

[CB012115-IA] This requirement shall be supported by Odin for FR1 TDD. [End CB012115-IA]

[CB012115-JA][CB012115-LA] This requirement shall be supported by Odin for FR1 FDD, [End CB012115-LA] whether CPRI or eCPRI. [End CB012115-JA]

---

#### 5.4.4.5.2 Spatial Multiplexing

**5.4.4.5.2.0-2**  (ID: `12711123`)

Spatial Multiplexing
The number of PUSCH antenna ports is not provided in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message. The antPort IE indicates the DMRS PUSCH ports; see [11398865: PUSCH DMRS Antenna Ports](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e84G_vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).*

The PUSCH DMRS logical antenna port(s) corresponds to the signals at the input of "Precoding" block. The number of PUSCH antenna ports (now after Precoding block) is equal to the numOfSRSPorts ([12536635: srsReceiveReqUEs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_z5VkYB9-EfCQJ5daVHVkgg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

Please refer to[11103228: Overview PUSCH,PTRS,SRS antenna ports](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kntB_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), for illustration of UL antenna ports after precoding.

If spatialMode IE is set to "single antenna", the number of PUSCH antenna ports is 1 and its index value is {1000}. Note that in the case of single Tx, there is no SRS transmitted for SU MIMO channel acquisition (srs resource with usage codebook).

Otherwise, the number of PUSCH antenna ports is 2 and their index values are {1000,1001}. Note that the number of SRS ports is 2 too and have the same index values, that is, the number of SRS/PUSCH ports are {1000,1001} when numOfSRSPorts = 2.

The requirement is common for all Technologies.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.4.5.3 Antenna Ports (PUSCH DMRS)

##### 5.4.4.5.3.1 Legacy

Content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 23R4 release, For later releases please refer to the objects below.

PUSCH DMRS antenna port(s)

Antenna port values are indicated by antPort parameter in UlDataUe\_PuschSendReq by setting the bit to '1'. Most significant bit (MSB) represents PUSCH DMRS antenna port number 0, MSB-1 represents PUSCH DMRS antenna port number 1 etc. Bits 4-7 are currently unused and are reserved for DMRS configuration 2, currently set to 0. Bits 0-3 are unused and always set to 0. The bitmap rule is shown below:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a row of 16 white rectangular boxes against a black background. Each box contains a single digit, representing a number. The numbers are arranged in descending order from left to right, starting with 15 and ending with 0.
The boxes and their contents are as follows:
Box 1: 15
Box 2: 14
Box 3: 13
Box 4: 12
Box 5: 11
Box 6: 10
Box 7: 9
Box 8: 8
Box 9: 7
Box 10: 6
Box 11: 5
Box 12: 4
Box 13: 3
Box 14: 2
Box 15: 1
Box 16: 0[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_655.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The bitmap is only applicable for PUSCH DMRS antenna ports, and no more for PUSCH logical antenna ports.

The parameters antPort and numOfLayers sent in UlDataUe\_PuschSendReq message. antPort in UL DCI will be converted to bitmap according to above rules:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Antenna port number(s) \* PUSCH DMRS: antenna port number | antPort value in IF |
| 0 | 0x8000 |
| 1 | 0x4000 |
| 2 | 0x2000 |
| 3 | 0x1000 |
| 0, 1 | 0xC000 |
| 2, 3 | 0x3000 |
| 0-2 | 0xE000 |
| 0-3 | 0xF000 |
| 0,2 | 0xA000 |
| 4 | 0x0800 |
| 5 | 0x0400 |
| 6 | 0x0200 |
| 7 | 0x0100 |
| 4, 5 | 0x0C00 |
| 6, 7 | 0x0300 |
| 0, 4 | 0x8800 |
| 2, 6 | 0x2200 |
| 0, 1, 4 | 0xC800 |
| 2, 3, 6 | 0x3200 |
| 0, 1, 4, 5 | 0xCC00 |
| 2, 3, 6, 7 | 0x3300 |
| 0, 2, 4, 6 | 0xAA00 |

The PUSCH DMRS logical antenna port(s), the antPort values in DCI, numOfLayers and the number of physical ports as well as the polarizations are given for each transmission scheme. Supported values for the PUSCH DMRS antenna ports, parameters antPort and numOfLayers are as described in below table.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Transmission scheme | numOf Layers | antPort in UL DCI (38.212 table 7.3.1.1.2-12~15) | PUSCH DMRS logical antenna port(s) CP-OFDM | PUSCH DMRS logical antenna port(s) DFT-s-OFDM | Number of physical ports & polarization(s) | L1 Loner HW | L1 Loki HW | L1 Thor HW |
| Single Tx Transmission | 1 | 2 | 0 | 2 | 1, polarizations +45° | supported | supported | RP003187-A, RP003187-H CB008325-5G-AA(DFT-s\_OFDM) CB008325-5G-BA(DFT-s\_OFDM) |
| 0 | 0 | 0 | 1, polarizations +45° | supported | supported | RP003187-E, RP003187-L CB008325-5G-AA(DFT-s\_OFDM) CB008325-5G-BA(DFT-s\_OFDM) |  |  |
| Spatial multiplexing rank 1 | 1 | 2 | 0 | 2 | 2, polarizations +/-45° | supported | supported | RP003187-A, RP003187-H CB008325-5G-AA(DFT-s\_OFDM) CB008325-5G-BA(DFT-s\_OFDM) |
| 0 | 0 | 0 | 2, polarizations +/-45° | supported | supported | RP003187-E, RP003187-L CB008325-5G-AA(DFT-s\_OFDM) CB008325-5G-BA(DFT-s\_OFDM) |  |  |
| 6 | 0 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | NA | supported | NA |  |  |
| 3 or 7 | 1 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | [CB008360-B] supported [End CB008360-B] | NA (F) |  |  |  |
| 4 or 8 | 2 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | [CB008360-B] supported [End CB008360-B] | NA (F) |  |  |  |
| 5 or 9 | 3 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | [CB008360-B] supported [End CB008360-B] | NA (F) |  |  |  |
| 10 (Invalid when PTRS is ON) | 4 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | NA | NA |  |  |  |
| 11 (Invalid when PTRS is ON) | 5 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | NA | NA |  |  |  |
| 12 (Invalid when PTRS is ON) | 6 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | NA | NA |  |  |  |
| 13 (Invalid when PTRS is ON) | 7 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | NA | NA |  |  |  |
| Spatial multiplexing rank 2 | 2 | 0 | 0 and 1 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | supported | supported | RP003187-E, RP003187-L |
| 1 | 0 and 1 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | supported | supported | RP003187-A, RP003187-H (F) |  |  |
| 3 | 0 and 2 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | supported | supported for FR1 (Feature?) | RP003187-D RP003187-K |  |  |
| 4 | 0 and 1 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | supported | NA | NA |  |  |
| 2 or 5 | 2 and 3 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | [CB008360-B] supported [End CB008360-B] | NA (F) |  |  |  |
| 6 (Invalid when PTRS is ON) | 4 and 5 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | NA | NA |  |  |  |
| 7 (Invalid when PTRS is ON) | 6 and 7 | N/A | 2, polarizations +/-45° | NA | NA |  |  |  |

When PTRS is activated, the ports 4, 5, 6 and 7 cannot be used as the first port of the UE.(cf. 38.214: 6.2.2 UE DM-RS transmission procedure).

Note:

(1) The column "PUSCH DMRS logical antenna port(s)" corresponds to the signals at the input of "Precoding" block.

(2) The number of PUSCH antenna ports (now after Precoding block) is equal to the numofSRSPorts. And the number of SRS/PUSCH ports are {1000,1001} when numofSRSPorts = 2.

[RP003187-A, RP003187-H]The requirement is valid for L1 Thor. [End RP003187-A, RP003187-H]

Notes for the UEs configured for Transform Precoding:

-     Transform Precoding only supports Rank 1 and dmrs-Type=1, maxLength=1;

- The column "PUSCH DMRS logical antenna port(s)" corresponds to the signals at the input of "Precoding" block;

-     See 38.212 Table 7.3.1.1.2-6: Antenna port(s), transform precoder is enabled, dmrs-Type=1, maxLength=1

[CB009028-5G-A] Parity from CB008360:

This requirement is also applicable for Thor for FR1-TDD-FDD. [End CB009028-5G-A]
