|  |  |  |  |  |
|  harqProcessIndex, [0..15]. |  |  |  |  |
| Indicates the harq process index of an Harq process. |  |  |  |  |
|  absoluteHarqProcessIndex, [0.. MAX\_Harq\_CTX-1]. |  |  |  |  |
| Indicates the absolute Harq process index of a Harq process. MAX\_Harq\_CTX equals to 1024. The current supported Harq processing index is [0…191]. MAX\_Harq\_CTX corresponds to the maximum number of pending Harq processes as per defined in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1045. See Harq details in section 5.2.4.4.13. |  |  |  |  |
|  freshHarqTrans, Boolean: {0(False), 1(True)}. |  |  |  |  |
| This parameter indicates if the UE transmission corresponds to a fresh one (True) or a retransmission (False) which requires to apply the Harq recombining process (see section 5.2.4.4.13 for more details). |  |  |  |  |
|  longTermCfoMetric, complex . |  |  |  |  |
| Long term CFO metric computed at 5G-L2-PS. (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3324\_replaced([11150688](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksVDQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3333\_replaced([11151157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3337\_replaced([11151189](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). |  |  |  |  |
| ~~If rdFocEnabledFlag == True~~ |  |  |  |  |
| ~~L1-SW shall use the IE longTermCfoMetric in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message and shall perform CFO compensation in time domain for both data and DMRS by calculating the longTermFreqOffsetEst parameter (see below) based on longTermCfoMetric.~~ |  |  |  |  |
| ~~Else~~ |  |  |  |  |
| ~~L1-SW ignores the IE longTermCfoMetric in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message and sets its value to 1. This effectively disables the CFO compensation.~~ |  |  |  |  |
| ~~End~~ |  |  |  |  |
| ~~(See 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_972,22024,16469 for details.)~~ |  |  |  |  |
|  longTermCfoMetricOfAnt, complex . longTermCfoMetricOfAnt array [4] complex Long term CFO metric computed at 5G-L2-PS for each Rx antenna stream (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3322\_replaced([11150515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksT1LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Refer to 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_982 for details L1 Loner functional requirements. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9896\_replaced([11102750](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knm7UvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for 0+0j value behavior. |  |  |  |  |
| Long term CFO metric computed at 5G-L2-PS. |  |  |  |  |
| If rdFocEnabledFlag == True |  |  |  |  |
| L1-SW shall use the IE longTermCfoMetricOfAnt in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message and shall perform CFO compensation in time domain for both data and DMRS by calculating the longTermFreqOffsetEst\_i parameter (see below) based on longTermCfoMetricOfAnt per antenna. |  |  |  |  |
| Else |  |  |  |  |
| L1-SW ignores the IE longTermCfoMetricOfAnt in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message and sets its value to 1 for all antennas. This effectively disables the CFO compensation. |  |  |  |  |
| End |  |  |  |  |
|  foeValid, Boolean: {0(False), 1(True)}. |  |  |  |  |
|  baseGraph {1, 2}. |  |  |  |  |
| LDPC base graph number. |  |  |  |  |
|  numOfCodeBlocks [1..152]. |  |  |  |  |
| Number of code blocks to be used. |  |  |  |  |
|  codeBlockSize [24..8448]. |  |  |  |  |
| Code block size. Parameter K as defined in 38.212 §5.2.2. |  |  |  |  |
|  numOfFillerBits [0..8447]. |  |  |  |  |
| Number of filler bits at the beginning of first code block. Parameter F as defined in 38.212 §5.2.2. |  |  |  |  |
|  liftSize [0..384]. |  |  |  |  |
| Lift size. Parameter Z as defined in 38.212 §5.2.2. |  |  |  |  |
|  liftSizeColumnIndex [0..7]. |  |  |  |  |
| Lift size column index. |  |  |  |  |
|  modulationOrder {1, 2, 4, 6, 8}. |  |  |  |  |
| Modulation order. Supported values are {2, 4, 6} when transform precoding is disabled and {1, 2, 4, 6} when transform precoding is enabled. |  |  |  |  |
|  rvIndex [0..3]. |  |  |  |  |
| Redundancy version index. |  |  |  |  |
|  ncb [0..25344]. |  |  |  |  |
| The parameter ncb is used for rate matching (see 38.212 §5.4.2.1). |  |  |  |  |
|  k0divZ [0..56]. |  |  |  |  |
| K0 / Zc for rvIndex = 1, 2 or 3 (table 5.4.2.1-2 of 38.212 §5.4.2.1). For rvIndex = 0, k0\_divZ = 0. This parameter is used for the rate matching. |  |  |  |  |
|  numOfLayers [1..4]. |  |  |  |  |
| Number of layers. Supported values are {1, 2}. |  |  |  |  |
|  puschTransCoherence enum {nonCoherent, partialNonCoherent, fullCoherent} |  |  |  |  |
| Supported values are {nonCoherent, fullCoherent}. When this parameter is equal to fullCoherent: full set of UL codebook shall be be considered in PMI selection component (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1006 for the full set of UL codebook). When this parameter is equal to nonCoherent: only a subset of UL codebook shall be considered in PMI selection component (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1007 for the subset of UL codebook). |  |  |  |  |
|  harqAddress. |  |  |  |  |
| In L1 release 5G21B harqAddress in L1-SW is set to zero because harqAddress is internally computed in the L1-FPGA. Note: For future implementations harq address computation maybe implemented in L1-SW as a work around to the L1-FPGA computation. |  |  |  |  |
|  numCeAxcIndex u8 {2 or 4} Number of ceAxcIndex that are valid for this grant’s PUSCH request. |  |  |  |  |
|  ceAxcIndex  array[4] of u8 Set of eAxC indices to be used for the PUSCH reception for this grant on eCPRI Fronthaul. Valid configurations for the above parameters |  |  |  |  |

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CPRI/eCPRI | numCeAxcIndex | ceAxcIndex |
| eCPRI | 2 | [0,1] or [2,3] |
| eCPRI | 4 | [0,1,2,3] |
| CPRI | 2 | [0,0] |
|  patternId array[2] of u16 beam patternIds to be used for PUSCH reception for this grant, and the eCPRI control message generation. Each patternId is used with two consecutive ceAxcIndex. Note: For eCPRI, beam patternId 0x0000 is only used for non-beamforming RUs. In case of CPRI (eCpriFlag = FALSE), patternIds is set to 0. |  |  |
|  L2CtxtAnMgt: This parameter (L2CtxtAnMgt array[8]) is forwarded by L1-SW to decoder. The decoder will not handle this parameter as its content is private to L2, it will just use it (add it) when building the UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_210) message. |  |  |
|  numOfUciCsiPart1Bits u8[0..50] Number of CSI bits (part 1) in UCI report.  For supported values see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1031. Supported value for FDD is from [0..11] and TDD from [0..50]. |  |  |
|  numOfUciCsiPart1Symbols u16[0.. 45864] Number of CSI modulation symbols used for CSI bits (part 1) in UCI report. Maximum value is specified as 14 symbols \* 273 PRBs \* 12 subcarriers. ~~The maximum supported value in 21B is set to 4096~~ **[PR712608] For the specific value** see at 5G\_L1\_IF\_199\_replaced([11513804](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9__vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))***\*[End PR712608].*****\*** |  |  |
| ~~~~~~maxNumOfUciCsiPart2Symbols~~ ~~u16[0..46200]~~ ~~as defined in 38.212~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~maxNumOfUciCsiPart2BitsPlusCrc~~~~u16[0..22]~~~~Maximum integer value for (OCSI-2+L CSI-2 =8x2+6) that matches the inequation as defined in section 5.2.3 of 38.214.~~ |  |  |
|  numOfUciAckBits u8[0..11] Number of ACK bits to decode CRC excluded (i.e. OACK in 38.212). For supported values see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1038. |  |  |
|  numOfUciAckSymbols u16[0.. 45864] Number of ACK modulation symbols used for ACK bits in UCI report per layer (i.e. Q’ACK as defined in 38.212 6.3.2.4). numOfUciAckSymbols is needed and used by L1 if either numOfUciAckBits > 0 or if numOfUciCsiPart1Bits > 0, otherwise L1 ignores it. In L1, ~~this range is supported: u16[0...278] for numOfUciAckBits <=2 and u16[1...2048] for numOfUciAckBits >2~~ **[PR712608] for the specific value** see at 5G\_L1\_IF\_199\_replaced([11513804](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9__vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))***\*[End PR712608].*****\*** |  |  |
|  numOfUciAckSymbols1bit u16[0..2151] number of modulation symbols when numOfUciAckBits <=2. numOfUciAckSymbols1bit Maps to QP\_AN1              "In case O\_AN<=2, QP\_AN1 is expected to be the number of RE used for Ack Nack report in PUSCH, as specified in 38.212 section 6.3.2.4.1.1 for the specific value ~~O\_ACK = 1. Valid range is 1 to 139~~." **[PR712608] see at 5G\_L1\_IF\_199\_replaced([11513804](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9__vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))****[End PR712608].**\*\*\*\* |  |  |
|  uciOnly bool {True, False} Set to True when PUSCH does not include any UL-SCH data. The supported value is false. |  |  |
| ~~~~~~csiPart2Coderate= float(BetaOffsetCsi2\*numResForUciOnPusch)/numCodedBits~~ |  |  |
|  numOfCsiReports u8 [0..1] Number of report multiplexed in CSI report. |  |  |
| Per CSI report |  |  |
| ~~~~~~riStartPos~~ ~~u8~~~~Position of the 1st bit of the RI field in CSI part 1~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~riUsageMap~~~~u8~~~~Describes the rank the Ue is allowed to report and then the RI encoding regarding the reported rank. Bit i of riUsageMap maps the rank i+1.Bit i set to 1 means rank i+1 can be reported by Ue. Bit i set to 0 means rank i+1 cannot be reported by Ue.~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~Rank size is~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~RI=0 reports the lowest rank that can be reported.~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~RI=1 reports second rank that can be reported, starting evaluation in increasing order of rank.~~ |  |  |
|  csiPart1Only bool {True, False} Set to True if CSI report doesn’t include CSI part 2. deduced decoder parameter CSI1\_ONLY\_MAP shall be set always to 0xFF as 1817 is descoped in 20B |  |  |
| ~~~~~~numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRi1~~~~u8[0..5]~~ ~~Wideband CSI part 2 size for report for RI = 1~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRi2~~~~u8[0..5]~~ ~~Wideband CSI part 2 size for report for RI = 2~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRi3~~~~u8[0..4]~~ ~~Wideband CSI part 2 size for report for RI = 3~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRi4~~~~u8[0..4]~~ ~~Wideband CSI part 2 size for report for RI = 4~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~numOfCsiPart2Subbands~~~~u8[0..18]~~~~Number of subbands in the CSI Part2~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~numberofBitsPerSubbandRi1~~~~u8[0…2]~~~~Bit size of each subband RI = 1~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~numberofBitsPerSubbandRi2~~~~u8[0,1]~~ ~~Bit size of each subband RI = 2~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~numberofBitsPerSubbandRi3~~~~u8[0,1]~~ ~~Bit size of each subband RI = 3~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~numberofBitsPerSubbandRi4~~~~u8[0,1]~~ ~~Bit size of each subband RI = 4~~ |  |  |
|  CSI1\_ONLY\_MAP "Indicates for each report if CSI are encoded in a single block (no CSI part 2). CSI2 dropping mechanism is not (cannot be) taken into account here, such a bit is set in CSI1\_only\_map if the corresponding CSI report is such that there is actually no CSI part2 content. Bit i maps the report index i. Bit with index lager than the number of CSI\_REPORT\_NUM-1 shall be set to 1. (extracted from csi1Part1Only)." |  |  |
| ~~~~~~QP\_CSI2\_MAX~~~~"This is: min{8192,~~ ~~⌈~~~~α~~~~∙∑~~~~\_l(M\_sc^UCI (l))~~~~⌉~~~~-Q'\_ACK-Q'\_CSI1} as defined in 38.212 or (min(8192, maxNumOfUciCsiPart2Symbols)) with maxNumOfUciCsiPart2Symbols cominng from receive request. Value is saturated to 8192 as Polar decoding cannot support rate matching of more than 2x8192 bits. Valid range is 0 to 8192."~~ |  |  |
| ~~CSI2\_CR\_EXACT~~~~"This is a flag that indicates when set to 1 that CSI-2CR is an exact value, i.e. the below equation equality is verified. When reset to 0 means it means~~ ~~~~~~CSI2\_CR\*2^9 has been actually floored . β\_offset~~~~∙∑~~~~\_l(M\_sc^UCI (l)/(~~~~∑~~~~\_r (K\_r )~~~~∙~~~~2^9=~~~~⌊~~~~(β\_offset~~~~∙∑~~~~\_l(M\_sc^UCI (l)/(~~~~∑~~~~\_r(K\_r )~~~~∙~~~~2^9~~ ~~⌋~~ ~~To be computed from csiPart2Coderate from L2 receive request."~~ |  |  |
| ~~~~~~CSI2\_CR~~~~"This CSI part 2 coding rate defined as:~~ ~~⌊~~~~β\_offset~~~~∙∑~~~~\_l(M\_sc^UCI (l)/(~~~~∑~~~~\_r(K\_r )~~~~∙~~~~2^9~~ ~~⌋~~~~∙~~~~2^(-9). To be computed from csiPart2Coderate from L2 receive request. Quantization is Q0.1.9."~~ |  |  |
| These parameters control buffered UL (5G\_UP\_1731). 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_264 |  |  |
|  processInRealTime, bool {True, False}.  True: means the streams of the subcell are processed in real time. False means that the streams of the subcell will be buffered to be processed in the DL slot defined by (sfnForProcessing, slotForProcessing). |  |  |
|  sfnForProcessing u16[0..1023] SFN for the slot when the PUSCH/PUCCH/PRACH/SRS needs to be inserted from IQ capture for processing. sfnForProcessing can be at most 1 unit larger (modulo 1024) than the air interface timing indicated by sfn IE in the parent message. |  |  |
| slotForProcessing u8[0..79] NR-slot index for the slot when the PUSCH/PUCCH/PRACH/SRS needs to be inserted from IQ capture for processing. slotForProcessing can be at most 3 units larger (modulo 80 for SCS=120, modulo 20 for SCS=30, and modulo 10 for SCS=15KHz ) than the air interface timing indicated by slot IE in the parent message.              If processInRealTime is set to True, then slotForProcessing and sfnForProcessing are set to the same values as slot and sfn from given message. If processInRealTime is set to False, then slotForProcessing and sfnForProcessing are set to the slot and SFN selected for delayed (non-real time) processing. |  |  |

---

##### 5.4.4.10.1.2 Loki

**5.4.4.10.1.2.0-1**  (ID: `11400251`)

Loki
For Loki, 5G-L1-UL shall determine PTRS configuration according to the IEs from *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq (*PuschPtrConfig structure, see 5G\_RAN\_IF\_196) the supported value as below,\*\*

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | PTRS enable Flag | LPT-RS | KPT-RS | number of antenna ports for PTRS | *UL-PTRS-RE-offset* |
|  | *ulPtrsFlag {0,1}* | *ulPtrsTimeDensity* | *ulPtrsFrequencyDensity* | *ulPtrsNumOfPorts* | *ulPtrsResElemOffset* |
| **TDD FR1** | {0} **[5GC002056-AA]** {0, 1} | **[5GC002056-AA]** {1} | **[5GC002056-AA]** {2,4} | **[5GC002056-AA]** {1} | **[5GC002056-AA]** {0} |
| **TDD FR2** | **[CB007403-C]** {0, 1} | **[CB007403-C]** {1} | **[CB007403-C]** {2,4} | **[CB007403-C]** {1} | **[CB007403-C]** {0} |
| **FDD FR1** | **[End 5GC001868-D]{0}** **[5GC002481-AA] {0,1}** | **[5GC002481-AA] {1}** | **[5GC002481-AA] {2,4}** | **[5GC002481-AA] {1}** | **[5GC002481-AA] {0}** |

When UL PTRS is not configured (i.e. *ulPtrsFlag set to 0), the parameters ulPtrsTimeDensity, ulPtrsFrequencyDensity, ulPtrsNumOfPorts and ulPtrsResElemOffset are meaningless.*

---

##### 5.4.4.10.1.3 Thor

**5.4.4.10.1.3.0-1**  (ID: `11400258`)

Thor
For Thor, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall determine PTRS configuration according to the IEs from *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq (*ulPtrConfig structure, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_196\_replaced([11513783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) the supported value as below,\*\*

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Technology | Waveform | PTRS enable Flag | LPT-RS | KPT-RS | number of antenna ports for PTRS | *UL-PTRS-RE-offset* |
|  |  | *ulPtrsFlag {0,1}* | *ulPtrsTimeDensity* | *ulPtrsFrequencyDensity* | *ulPtrsNumOfPorts* | *ulPtrsResElemOffset* |
| **TDD FR1** | CP-OFDM | **[RP003187-A] {0, 1}** [End RP003187-A]\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-A] {1}** [End RP003187-A]\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-A] {2,4}** [End RP003187-A]\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-A] {1}** [End RP003187-A]\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-A] {0}** [End RP003187-A]\*\*\*\* |
| DFT-s-OFDM | **[CB008325-5G-AA] N/A** [End CB008325-5G-AA]\*\*\*\* |  |  |  |  |  |
| **FDD FR1** | CP-OFDM | **[RP003187-A] {0, 1}** [End RP003187-A]\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-A] {1}** [End RP003187-A]\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-H] {2,4}** [End RP003187-H]\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-A] {1}** [End RP003187-A]\*\*\*\* | **[RP003187-A] {0}** [End RP003187-A]\*\*\*\* |
| DFT-s-OFDM | **[CB008325-5G-BA] N/A** [End CB008325-5G-BA]\*\*\*\* |  |  |  |  |  |
| **TDD FR2** | CP-OFDM | **[CB010576-A] {0, 1}** [End CB010576-A]\*\*\*\* | **[CB010576-A] {1}** [End CB010576-A]\*\*\*\* | **[CB010576-A] {2, 4}** [End CB010576-A]\*\*\*\* | **[CB010576-A] {1}** [End CB010576-A]\*\*\*\* | **[CB010576-A] {0}** [End CB010576-A]\*\*\*\* |

When UL PTRS is not configured (i.e. *ulPtrsFlag set to 0), the parameters ulPtrsTimeDensity, ulPtrsFrequencyDensity, ulPtrsNumOfPorts and ulPtrsResElemOffset are meaningless.*

**[RP003187-A, RP003187-H]The requirement is valid for** L1 Thor environment. **[End RP003187-A, RP003187-H]**\*\*\*\*

**[CB008325-5G-AA, CB008325-5G-BA] N/A in the table means that the field shall be ignored by L1.** [End CB008325-5G-AA, CB008325-5G-BA]\*\*\*\*

---

##### 5.4.4.10.1.4 Odin

**5.4.4.10.1.4.0-1**  (ID: `12567021`)

Odin
For Odin, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall determine PTRS configuration according to the IEs from *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq (*ulPtrConfig structure, see [11513783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The supported value are as follows.\*\*

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Technology | Waveform | PTRS Enable Flag | LPT-RS | KPT-RS | number of antenna ports for PTRS | *UL-PTRS-RE-offset* | First Feature |
|  |  | *ulPtrsFlag {0,1}* | *ulPtrsTimeDensity* | *ulPtrsFrequencyDensity* | *ulPtrsNumOfPorts* | *ulPtrsResElemOffset* |  |
| **TDD FR1** | CP-OFDM | {0, 1} | {1} | {2,4} | {1} | {0} | 5GC000580 |
| DFT-s-OFDM | N/A | 5GC000545 |  |  |  |  |  |
| **FDD FR1** | CP-OFDM | {0, 1} | {1} | {2,4} | {1} | {0} | 5GC000580 |
| DFT-s-OFDM | N/A | 5GC000545 |  |  |  |  |  |
| **TDD FR2** | N/A | N/A | N/A | N/A | N/A | N/A | CB007596 |

* PTRS is not supported in 7-2e split.
* When UL PTRS is not configured (i.e. *ulPtrsFlag set to 0), the parameters ulPtrsTimeDensity, ulPtrsFrequencyDensity, ulPtrsNumOfPorts and ulPtrsResElemOffset shall be ignored.*
* "N/A" in the table means that the field shall be ignored by L1.
* LPT-RS and KPT-RS are in accordance with 3GPP TS 38.211 §6.4.1.2.2, Uplink>Physical signals>Phase-tracking reference signals>Mapping to physical resources.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported for CP-OFDM and TDD FR1 in Odin L1 Call. [End CB012115-IA]

[CB012115-JA][CB012115-LA] The requirement shall be supported for CP-OFDM and FDD FR1 in Odin L1 Call. [End CB012115-LA] whether CPRI or eCPRI. [End CB012115-JA]

[CB012115-MA] The requirement shall be supported for DFT-s-OFDM and FR1 (TDD and FDD) in Odin. [End CB012115-MA]

---

#### 5.4.4.10.2 PTRS Parameters

**5.4.4.10.2.0-2**  (ID: `12714529`)

PTRS Parameters
5G-L1-UL shall determine PTRS configuration according to the IEs from *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq (*PuschPtrConfig structure, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_196\_replaced([11513783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):\*\*

* \*ulPtrsFlag determines whether PTRS transmission is enabled or not.
* TDD: Both values {0, 1} are supported.
* FDD: Both values {0, 1} are supported.\*

When UL PTRS is disabled (i.e. *ulPtrsFlag = 0), the below IEs shall be ignored and considered by 5G-L1-UL as default value 0.*

* \*ulPtrsTimeDensity is equivalent to the parameter L PT-RS from 38.211 §6.4.1.2.2.1
* When PTRS is present (i.e. IE *ulPtrsFlag = 1), value 1 is supported.*\*
* \*ulPtrsFrequencyDensity is equivalent to the parameter K PT-RS from 38.211 §6.4.1.2.2.1
* For CP-OFDM, when PTRS is present (i.e. IE ulPtrsFlag = 1) values  {2, 4} are supported.
* For DFT-s-OFDM, when PTRS is present (i.e. IE ulPtrsFlag = 1) refer to 38.211 Table 6.4.1.2.2.2-1.\*
* \*ulPtrsNumOfPorts indicates the number of antenna ports for PTRS transmission. DMRS antenna port values for PTRS are specified in 38.214 §6.2.3.
* When PTRS is present (i.e. IE ulPtrsFlag = 1), value 1 is supported.\*
* *ulPtrsResElemOffset is equivalent to* UL-PTRS-RE-offset from 38.211 §6.4.1.2.2.1
* When PTRS is present (i.e. IE ulPtrsFlag = 1), value 0 is supported.\*\*

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported in Odin. PTRS is not supported in 7-2e split. The requirement is common for all other Technologies. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.4.10.3 PTRS Sequence Parameters

**5.4.4.10.3.0-2**  (ID: `12714403`)

PTRS Sequence Parameters
5G-L1-UL shall generate PTRS sequences according to the following rules:

If Transform Precoding (DFT-s-OFDM) is disabled (i.e. IE puschTransformPrecoderFlag is set to disabled, see [11513778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

* ulPtrsFrequencyDensity should be used by L1 (see 5G\_L1\_2851).

If Transform Precoding (DFT-s-OFDM) is enabled (i.e. IE puschTransformPrecoderFlag is set to enabled, see [11513778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

if the IE ulPtrsFlag is set to True, then

* ulPtrsTimeDensityTransformPrecoding is equivalent to the parameter L PT-RS from 38.211 §6.4.1.2.2.2 (see 5G\_UP\_IF\_473) and is limited to 1.
* ulPtrsNumOfGroups and ulPtrsNumOfSamplesPerGroup are equivalent to NgroupPT-RS and NsampPUSCHof 38.211 section 6.4.1.2.1 respectively. They are selected according to 38.214 Table 6.2.3.2-1.

else if no PTRS is present, then

* L1 should ignore the parameters ulPtrsTimeDensityTransformPrecoding, ulPtrsNumOfGroups, and ulPtrsNumOfSamplesPerGroup.

Note: If the transform precoder is enabled the IE ulPtrsFlag is set to false when

1) the number of scheduled RBs is less than NRB0 if NRB0 > 1 or

2) if the RNTI equals TC-RNTI.

For supported configurations see [12714394: PTRS Configurations (Supported Combinations)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_KdhqMGiVEfCk8v_SG2h2xg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

History:

Loner, Loki: CB007814, 5GC000545

Thor: RP003187

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported for CP-OFDM (when puschTransformPrecoderFlag = 0) and FR1 TDD 7-2a in Odin L1 Call. [End CB012115-IA]

[CB012115-JA][CB012115-LA] The requirement shall be supported for CP-OFDM (when puschTransformPrecoderFlag = 0) and FDD FR1 in Odin L1 Call. [End CB012115-LA] whether CPRI or eCPRI [End CB012115-JA]

[CB012115-MA] The requirement shall be supported for DFT-s-OFDM (when puschTransformPrecoderFlag = 1) and FR1 (TDD and FDD) in Odin. [End CB012115-MA]

---

3GPP Tables
max64QAM (Transform Precoding)

3GPP TS 38.214 v16, Table 6.1.4.1-1, MCS Index Table (Transform Precoding and Max 64QAM)

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a table with four columns: "MCS Index", "Modulation Order", "Target code Rate R x 1024", and "Spectral efficiency". The table lists different Modulation and Coding Scheme (MCS) indices, their corresponding modulation order, target code rate, and spectral efficiency. The MCS indices range from 0 to 31. Some entries in the "Target code Rate R x 1024" and "Spectral efficiency" columns are marked as "reserved".

| MCS Index $I\_{MCS}$ | Modulation Order $Q\_m$ | Target code Rate R x 1024 | Spectral efficiency |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | q | 240/q | 0.2344 |
| 1 | q | 314/q | 0.3066 |
| 2 | 2 | 193 | 0.3770 |
| 3 | 2 | 251 | 0.4902 |
| 4 | 2 | 308 | 0.6016 |
| 5 | 2 | 379 | 0.7402 |
| 6 | 2 | 449 | 0.8770 |
| 7 | 2 | 526 | 1.0273 |
| 8 | 2 | 602 | 1.1758 |
| 9 | 2 | 679 | 1.3262 |
| 10 | 4 | 340 | 1.3281 |
| 11 | 4 | 378 | 1.4766 |
| 12 | 4 | 434 | 1.6953 |
| 13 | 4 | 490 | 1.9141 |
| 14 | 4 | 553 | 2.1602 |
| 15 | 4 | 616 | 2.4063 |
| 16 | 4 | 658 | 2.5703 |
| 17 | 6 | 466 | 2.7305 |
| 18 | 6 | 517 | 3.0293 |
| 19 | 6 | 567 | 3.3223 |
| 20 | 6 | 616 | 3.6094 |
| 21 | 6 | 666 | 3.9023 |
| 22 | 6 | 719 | 4.2129 |
| 23 | 6 | 772 | 4.5234 |
| 24 | 6 | 822 | 4.8164 |
| 25 | 6 | 873 | 5.1152 |
| 26 | 6 | 910 | 5.3320 |
| 27 | 6 | 948 | 5.5547 |
| 28 | q | reserved |  |
| 29 | 2 | reserved |  |
| 30 | 4 | reserved |  |
| 31 | 6 | reserved |  |

---

#### 5.4.4.11.2 Supported MCS

##### 5.4.4.11.2.1 Legacy

**5.4.4.11.2.1.0-3**  (ID: `11400594`)

Legacy
Content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 23R4 release. For later releases please refer to the objects below.

5G-L1-UL shall support PUSCH reception of UE configured with Low SE MCS Table (defined in 3GPP 38.214, Table 5.1.3.1-3) and disabled Transform Precoding. The field *mcsTable in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message is set to *qam64LowSE in that case.*\*\*

**[CB012255-A] This requiremnt is also applicable for Loki FR1-FDD-eCPRI 7.2a** [End CB012255-A]\*\*\*\*

**[CB009936-F] This requirement is also applicable to FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI on Thor-BB.** [End CB009936-F]\*\*\*\*

---

##### 5.4.4.11.2.2 Loner

###### 5.4.4.11.2.2.1 Supported MCS

**5.4.4.11.2.2.1.0-1**  (ID: `11400367`)

Supported MCS
Parameters mcs  and mcsTable of message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq are not used for coding purpose but only for choosing the correct PNE threshold table pneRbThreshold (see 5G\_L1\_13435\_replaced([11407840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9kqkPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The selection of the MCS table depends on MCS-Table-PUSCH at RRC level which is set depending on UE capabilities and 256-QAM support at gNB side.

If CP-OFDM wareform is in use, the following tables are supported.

64-QAM (CP-OFDM) table:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 0 -> 9 | QPSK |
| 10 -> 16 | 16QAM |
| 17 -> 28 | 64QAM |

256-QAM (CP-OFDM) table:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 0 -> 4 | QPSK |
| 5 -> 10 | 16QAM |
| 11 -> 19 | 64QAM |
| 20 -> 27 | 256QAM |

In practice, from the selected MCS value, 5G-L2-PS computes all code block segmentation parameters (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6874\_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and send them to 5G-L1-UL in the UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message, together with the modulationOrder, the number of layers numOfLayer and the redundancy version index rvIndex.

When transform precoding is enabled 5G-L1-UL shall support MCS 0-27 according to the following tables: (See 3GPP TS 38.214, table 6.1.4.1-1).

64-QAM (DFT-s-OFDM) table:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 0 -> 1 | Pi/2-BPSK or QPSK |
| 2 -> 9 | QPSK |
| 10 -> 16 | 16QAM |
| 17 -> 27 | 64QAM |

HW-Technology Map:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | 64QAM table | 256QAM table | 64QAM DFT-s-OFDM table |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Not supported | Not supported | Not supported |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Not supported | Not supported |

---

###### 5.4.4.11.2.2.2 Supported Low Spectral Efficiency MCS (lowSE)

###### 5.4.4.11.2.2.2.0-1 Supported Low Spectral Efficiency MCS (lowSE) 5G-L1-UL shall support PUSCH reception for SU receiver of UE configured with Low SE MCS Table (defined in 3GPP 38.214, Table 5.1.3.1-3) and disabled Transform Precoding. The field mcsTable in UlData_PuschReceiveReq message is set to qam64LowSE in that case. ** The requirement is common for all Technologies. (ID: 12717823)

##### 5.4.4.11.2.3 Loki

###### 5.4.4.11.2.3.1 Supported MCS

Supported MCS
Parameters mcs  and mcsTable of message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq are not used for coding purpose but only for choosing the correct PNE threshold table pneRbThreshold (see 5G\_L1\_13435\_replaced([11407840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9kqkPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The selection of the MCS table depends on MCS-Table-PUSCH at RRC level which is set depending on UE capabilities and 256-QAM support at gNB side.

| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 0 -> 9 | QPSK |
| 10 -> 16 | 16QAM |

| --- | --- |
| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 0 -> 4 | QPSK |
| 5 -> 10 | 16QAM |
| 11 -> 19 | 64QAM |
| 20 -> 27 | 256QAM |

In practice, from the selected MCS value, 5G-L2-PS computes all code block segmentation parameters (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6874\_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and send them to 5G-L1-UL in the UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message, together with the modulationOrder, the number of layers numOfLayer and the redundancy version index rvIndex.

When transform precoding is enabled 5G-L1-UL shall support MCS 0-27 according to the following tables: (See 3GPP TS 38.214, table 6.1.4.1-1).

64-QAM (DFT-s-OFDM) table:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 0 -> 1 | Pi/2-BPSK or QPSK |
| 2 -> 9 | QPSK |
| 10 -> 16 | 16QAM |
| 17 -> 27 | 64QAM |

###### 5.4.4.11.2.3.2 Supported Low Spectral Efficiency MCS (lowSE)

###### 5.4.4.11.2.3.2.0-1 Supported Low Spectral Efficiency MCS (lowSE) 5G-L1-UL shall support PUSCH reception for SU receiver of UE configured with Low SE MCS Table (defined in 3GPP 38.214, Table 5.1.3.1-3) and disabled Transform Precoding. The field mcsTable in UlData_PuschReceiveReq message is set to qam64LowSE in that case. ** The requirement is common for all Technologies. (ID: 12717826)

##### 5.4.4.11.2.4 Thor

###### 5.4.4.11.2.4.1 Supported MCS

**5.4.4.11.2.4.1.0-1**  (ID: `11400396`)

Supported MCS
Parameters mcs  and mcsTable of message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq are not used for coding purpose but only for choosing the correct PNE threshold table pneRbThreshold (see 5G\_L1\_13435\_replaced([11407840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9kqkPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The selection of the MCS table depends on MCS-Table-PUSCH at RRC level which is set depending on UE capabilities and 256-QAM support at gNB side.

If CP-OFDM wareform is in use, the following tables are supported.

64-QAM (CP-OFDM) table:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 0 -> 9 | QPSK |
| 10 -> 16 | 16QAM |
| 17 -> 28 | 64QAM |

256-QAM (CP-OFDM) table:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 0 -> 4 | QPSK |
| 5 -> 10 | 16QAM |
| 11 -> 19 | 64QAM |
| 20 -> 27 | 256QAM |

In practice, from the selected MCS value, 5G-L2-PS computes all code block segmentation parameters (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6874\_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and send them to 5G-L1-UL in the UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message, together with the modulationOrder, the number of layers numOfLayer and the redundancy version index rvIndex.

When transform precoding is enabled 5G-L1-UL shall support MCS 0-27 according to the following tables: (See 3GPP TS 38.214, table 6.1.4.1-1).

| MCS Index | Modulation |

| 2 -> 9 | QPSK |
| 10 -> 16 | 16QAM |
| 17 -> 27 | 64QAM |

###### 5.4.4.11.2.4.2 Supported Low Spectral Efficiency MCS (lowSE)

**5.4.4.11.2.4.2.0-1**  (ID: `11401887`)

Supported Low Spectral Efficiency MCS (lowSE)
5G-L1-UL shall support PUSCH reception for SU receiver of UE configured with Low SE MCS Table (defined in 3GPP 38.214, Table 5.1.3.1-3) and disabled Transform Precoding. The field *mcsTable in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message is set to *qam64LowSE in that case.*\*\*

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Thor BB** |  |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | **[CB010970-B] Supported** [End CB010970-B]\*\*\*\* |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | **[CB008246-N] Supported** [End CB008246-N]\*\*\*\* |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | **[CB010414-AB] Supported** [End CB010414-AB]\*\*\*\* |

##### 5.4.4.11.2.5 Odin

###### 5.4.4.11.2.5.1 Supported MCS

**5.4.4.11.2.5.1.0-1**  (ID: `12717323`)

Supported MCS
Parameters *mcs  and* mcsTable of message *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq are not used for coding purpose but only for choosing the correct PNE threshold table* pneRbThreshold (see [11407840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9kqkPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (The parameter modulationOrder in [11513795: PuschDecoderConfig](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_vvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) is used for decoding.)\*\*\*\*

The selection of the MCS table depends on *MCS-Table-PUSCH at RRC level which is set depending on UE capabilities and 256-QAM support at gNB side.*

If CP-OFDM wareform is in use, the following tables are supported.

64-QAM (CP-OFDM) Table ([12720493: max64QAM](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_E3MmoWyBEfCRGai1q1VTeQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **MCS Index** | **Modulation** |
| 0 -> 9 | QPSK |
| 10 -> 16 | 16QAM |
| 17 -> 28 | 64QAM |

256-QAM (CP-OFDM) Table ([12720503: max256QAM](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tzu1kGyBEfCRGai1q1VTeQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **MCS Index** | **Modulation** |
| 0 -> 4 | QPSK |
| 5 -> 10 | 16QAM |
| 11 -> 19 | 64QAM |
| 20 -> 27 | 256QAM |

In practice, from the selected MCS value, 5G-L2-PS computes all code block segmentation parameters (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6874\_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and send them to 5G-L1-UL in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message, together with the* modulationOrder, the number of layers *numOfLayer and the redundancy version index* rvIndex.\*\*\*\*

When **transform precoding is enabled 5G-L1-UL shall support MCS 0-27 according to the following tables: (See 3GPP TS 38.214, table 6.1.4.1-1).**

64-QAM (DFT-s-OFDM) Table ([12720527: max64QAM (Transform Precoding)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ciXmAGyCEfCRGai1q1VTeQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **MCS Index** | **Modulation** |
| 0 -> 1 | Pi/2-BPSK or QPSK |
| 2 -> 9 | QPSK |
| 10 -> 16 | 16QAM |
| 17 -> 27 | 64QAM |

[CB012115-IA] For Odin, "64-QAM (CP-OFDM) Table" shall be supported in L1 Call. [End CB012115-IA]

[CB012115-MA] The requirement shall be fully supported in Odin. [End CB012115-MA]

---

###### 5.4.4.11.2.5.2 Supported Low Spectral Efficiency MCS (lowSE)

###### 5.4.4.11.2.5.2.0-1 Supported Low Spectral Efficiency MCS (lowSE) 5G-L1-UL shall support PUSCH reception for SU receiver of UE and CP-OFDM (disabled Transform Precoding) configured with Low SE MCS Table (defined in 3GPP 38.214, Table 5.1.3.1-3, 12720540: qam64LowSE ) for MCS indices 0 to 31. The field mcsTable in UlData_PuschReceiveReq message is set to qam64LowSE in that case. ** For Odin, the requirement is common for all Technologies. (ID: 12717318)

#### 5.4.4.11.3 Reserved MCS (Retransmission)

##### 5.4.4.11.3.1 Re-transmission for 64-QAM, CP-OFDM

###### 5.4.4.11.3.1.1 Legacy

**5.4.4.11.3.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `11400418`)

Legacy
Content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 23R3 release. For later releases please refer to the objects below.

L1 shall support reserved MCS indexes 29 to 31 (re-transmission) for 64-QAM as below :

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| MCS Index | Modulation |
| 29 | QPSK |
| 30 | 16QAM |
| 31 | 64QAM |

64-QAM table (CP-OFDM)

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | TDD FR1 | FDD FR1 | TDD FR2 | First Feature |
| Loner L1 HW | [CB010659-A] supported [End CB010659-A] | [CB010659-A] supported [End CB010659-A] | - | CB010659-A |
| Loki L1 HW | supported | supported | - | CB008662-A |
| VDU L1 HW | - | - | - |  |
| Thor L1 HW | Supported | Supported | - | CB009028-5G-A |

[CB009028-5G-A] Parity from CB008662:

This requirement also applies to Thor for FR1-TDD-FDD.

[End CB009028-5G-A]

###### 5.4.4.11.3.1.4 Thor

**5.4.4.11.3.1.4.1.0-1**  (ID: `11400447`)

### 5.4.4.12 Modulation Scheme

#### 5.4.4.12.1 Modulation Schemes for CP-OFDM

##### 5.4.4.12.1.1 Legacy

###### 5.4.4.12.1.1.0-5 Legacy The content of this requirement is frozen. Please see the below requirements. 5G-L1-UL shall support 8Rx IRC receiver for PUSCH with up to 256QAM modulation when C8 [CB013442-D] or A8 [End CB013442-D] subcell is configured. (ID: 12687085)

##### 5.4.4.12.1.2 Loner

**5.4.4.12.1.2.0-1**  (ID: `12723182`)

Loner
5G-L1-UL shall support modulation schemes 4-QAM, 16-QAM, 64-QAM and 256-QAM for PUSCH in CP-OFDM mode for all supported subcell types.

5G-L1-UL shall report the following information elements back to L2Lo through the **UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLoLoner ([11513883](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlDPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) message:**

* fragmentIndex,
* totalFragmentNum,
* tbStatus.

Those new IEs are used by both L1 and L2-LO to be able to fragment the transport block on L1 side, and defragment it on L2-LO side. The maximum transport block size defined in may exceed the maximum payload size in L1-L2 interface due to the increased throughput brought by 256-QAM in uplink. See [11513745](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

---

##### 5.4.4.12.1.3 SoC (Loki, Thor, Odin)

**5.4.4.12.1.3.0-1**  (ID: `12723143`)

SoC (Loki, Thor, Odin)
5G-L1-UL shall support modulation schemes 4-QAM, 16-QAM, 64-QAM and 256-QAM for PUSCH in CP-OFDM mode for all supported subcell types.

5G-L1-UL shall report the following information elements back to L2Lo through the **UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLo ([11513875](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlCvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) message:**

* fragmentIndex,
* totalFragmentNum,
* tbStatus.

Those new IEs are used by both L1 and L2-LO to be able to fragment the transport block on L1 side, and defragment it on L2-LO side. The maximum transport block size defined in may exceed the maximum payload size in L1-L2 interface due to the increased throughput brought by 256-QAM in uplink. See [11513745](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported for up to 64-QAM in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.4.12.2 Modulation Schemes for DFT-s-OFDM

**5.4.4.12.2.0-2**  (ID: `12723294`)

Modulation Schemes for DFT-s-OFDM
5G-L1-UL shall support max 64QAM with Transform Precoding (DFT-s-OFDM) waveform.

Note: Scheduler switches the modulation scheme from 256QAM to max 64QAM when the waveform is switched to DFT-s-OFDM, unless certain conditions hold (2 additional DMRS in High Speed mode; see [12723270: Modulation Scheme for DFT-s-OFDM in High-speed Cell](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_62m04W4UEfCmnYHxzZN44w?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). L1 shall not put any further restrictions.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement is common for all Technologies. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.4.12.3 Modulation Schemes for DFT-s-OFDM in High-speed Cell

##### 5.4.4.12.3.0-2 Modulation Schemes for DFT-s-OFDM in High-speed Cell 5G-L1-UL shall support 256-QAM modulation scheme with DFT-s-OFDM waveform when two additional DMRS symbols are configured. The case pertains to High Speed User feature CB008792. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported for FR1 TDD in Odin. [End CB012115-IA] (ID: 12723275)

#### 5.4.4.12.4 Modulation Schemes for DFT-s-OFDM in 7-2e split

##### 5.4.4.12.4.0-1 Modulation Schemes for DFT-s-OFDM in 7-2e split L1 shall support interworking between 256-QAM and DFT-s-OFDM mode for PUSCH. Note that it consists in either passing to 64-QAM or CP-OFDM, based on L2-PS selection. (ID: 11400229)

#### 5.4.4.12.5 Modulation schemes for DFT-s-OFDM in MU receiver

##### 5.4.4.12.5.0-1 Modulation schemes for DFT-s-OFDM in MU receiver 5G-L1-UL(BB) shall support PUSCH reception for MU receiver for MU-MIMO paired UEs with enabled Transform Precoding with MCS 0-27 (table 64-QAM (DFT-s-OFDM) in 11400334 , table 6.1.4.1-1 in 3GPP TS 38.214). (ID: 13383252)

### 5.4.4.13 Decoder

#### 5.4.4.13.1 LDPC Decoder configuration

**5.4.4.13.1.0-2**  (ID: `12721565`)

LDPC Decoder configuration
5G-L1-UL shall use the following parameters from messate *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq for LDPC decoder configuratiion (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_198\_replaced([11513795](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_vvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) -* PuschDecoderConfig structure).\*\*

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Parameter** | **Values** | **Description** |
| baseGraph | {1, 2} | Base Graph number |
| numOfCodeBlocks | [1..152] | Number of code blocks to be used. |
| codeBlockSize | [24..8448] | Code block size. Parameter *K as defined in 3GPP TS 38.212 §5.2.2* |
| numOfFillerBits | [0..8447] | Number of filler bits in each code block. Parameter *F as defined in 3GPP TS 38.212 §5.2.2* |
| liftSize | [0..384] | Lift size. Parameter *Z as defined in 3GPP TS 38.212 §5.2.2* |
| liftSizeSetIndex | [0..7] | Lift size set index. |
| liftSizeColumnIndex | [0..7] | Lift size column index. |
| modulationOrder | {1, 2, 4, 6, 8} | Modulation order. |
| rvIndex | [0..3] | Redundancy version index. 3GPP TS 38.212 §5.4.2.1 |
| ncb | [0..25344] | Ncb parameter in 3GPP TS 38.212 §5.4.2.1 |
| k0divZ | [0..56] | *K0/*Zc for rvIndex = 1, 2 or 3 (table 5.4.2.1-2 in 3GPP TS 38.212 §5.4.2.1). For rvIndex = 0, k0\_divZ = 0.\*\* |
| numOfLayers | {1,2} | Number of layers, selected following [11139906](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krqU8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). |

In practice, 5G-L2-PS computes all code block segmentation parameters and modulation order (see [11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) from the selected MCS value. These are sent to 5G-L1-UL in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message, along with the number of layers* numOfLayer and the redundancy version index *rvIndex.*\*\*

[CB012115-IA] The requirement is common for all Technologies in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.4.13.2 Transport Block Size

**5.4.4.13.2.0-3**  (ID: `12723094`)

Transport Block Size
The number of layers (for each grant) is derived from the *numOfLayers parameter in [11513795: PuschDecoderConfig](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_vvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of the message* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq,\*\*

The number of layers is needed to get the transport block size for the demodulation and decoding on PUSCH, in addition to modulation order and code block segmentation parameters.

L1 supports 1 and 2 layers.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement is common for all Technologies in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

### 5.4.4.14 Fake UE

#### 5.4.4.14.1 Loner and Loki

**5.4.4.14.1.0-2**  (ID: `11400721`)

Loner and Loki
5G-L1-UL shall support Fake UE configuration for computing RSSI and noise measurements on non-scheduled PUSCH PRBs.

A fake UE is a dummy grant that shall be used for measuring RSSI and noise (thermal noise and interference) over non-scheduled PRBs.

It is configured as a regular grant in message *ULData\_PuschReceiveReq with an explicit flag* fakeUE (see [11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) set to True.\*\*

A Fake UE is expected only if no UE is scheduled on PUSCH in the slot.

The configuration of a fake UE shall be valid as specified in [11215726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxU4C_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). No UCI reporting shall be configured.

5G-L1-UL shall perform parameter checking and PUSCH reception process similar to a regular UE. All response messages shall be sent to L2-PS, as described in [11513726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy98PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

The *fakeUe indication shall be reported in message* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs ([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*

Only RSSI and noise power measurement shall be considered as valid in this case.

On Loki, noise per PRB is also reported for PRBs allocated to a fake UE. Noise per PRB is derived from noise estimator, as for regular UEs.

---

#### 5.4.4.14.2 Thor

**5.4.4.14.2.0-5**  (ID: `11400757`)

Thor
A fake UE is a dummy grant that shall be used for measuring RSSI and noise (thermal noise and interference) over non-scheduled PRBs.

It is configured as a regular grant in message ULData\_PuschReceiveReq with an explicit flag fakeUE (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

A fake UE is expected only if no UE is scheduled on PUSCH in the slot.

The configuration of a fake UE shall be valid, as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17491\_replaced([11215726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxU4C_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). No UCI reporting shall be configured.

5G-L1-UL shall perform parameter checking and PUSCH reception process as for a regular UE. All response messages shall be sent to L2-PS, as described in 5G\_L1\_IF\_185\_replaced([11513726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy98PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The fakeUe indication shall be reported in message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_200\_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Only RSSI and noise power measurement shall be considered as valid in this case.

Noise per PRB is also reported for PRBs allocated to a fake UE. Noise per PRB is derived from noise estimator, as for regular UEs.

The requirement is also applicable for FR2-TDD-eCPRI.

This requirement also applies for FR1-TDD-CPRI.

5G-L1-UL shall also support requirements above for FR1 FDD 7-2a eCPRI for Thor.

[CB009936-F] 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall also support this requirement in FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI. [End CB009936-F]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI | [CB009936-F] Supported [End CB009936-F] |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported |

---

#### 5.4.4.14.3 Odin

**5.4.4.14.3.0-1**  (ID: `12723234`)

Odin
A fake UE is a dummy grant that shall be used for measuring RSSI and noise (thermal noise and interference) over non-scheduled PRBs.

It is configured as a regular grant in message *ULData\_PuschReceiveReq with an explicit flag* fakeUE ([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) set to True.\*\*

A fake UE is expected only if no UE is scheduled on PUSCH in the slot.

The configuration of a fake UE shall be valid, as specified in [11215726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxU4C_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). No UCI reporting shall be configured.

5G-L1-UL shall perform parameter checking and PUSCH reception process similar to a regular UE. All the response messages shall be sent to L2-PS, as described in [11513726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy98PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

The *fakeUe indication shall be reported in message* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs ([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Only RSSI and noise power measurement shall be considered as valid in this case.\*\*

Noise per PRB is also reported for PRBs allocated to a fake UE. Noise per PRB is derived from noise estimator, as for regular UEs.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement is common for all Technologies in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

### 5.4.4.15 Configured Grant (CG)

#### 5.4.4.15.1 Loner

**5.4.4.15.1.0-1**  (ID: `12723243`)

Loner
5G-L1-UL shall support PUSCH reception of UE scheduled with configured grant (CG) type 2.

When a UE is scheduled with CG type 2, field *rnti of message* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq contains the CS-CRNTI, while field *cRnti refers to the C-RNTI.*\*\*

* Value of *rnti shall be used for descrambling and PTRS demapping. It shall be reported in field* rnti of messages *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs,* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqULoner and *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqDLoner.*\*\*\*\*
* Value of *cRnti shall be reported in field* rnti of message *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLoLoner.*\*\*

For a non CG-scheduled UE, fields *rnti and* cRnti of message *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq are equal, both set to C-RNTI.*\*\*

DFT-s-OFDM is not supported for UEs with configured grant.

---

#### 5.4.4.15.2 Loki

**5.4.4.15.2.0-1**  (ID: `11400858`)

Loki
5G-L1-UL shall support PUSCH reception of UE scheduled with configured grant (CG) type 2.

When a UE is scheduled with CG type 2, field rnti of message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq contains the CS-CRNTI, while field cRnti refers to the C-RNTI.

* Value of rnti shall be used for descrambling and PTRS demapping. It shall be reported in field rnti of messages UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs, UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU and UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD.
* Value of cRnti shall be reported in field rnti of message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLo.

For a non CG-scheduled UE, fields rnti and cRnti of message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq are equal, both set to C-RNTI.

DFT-s-OFDM is not supported for UEs with configured grant.

---

#### 5.4.4.15.3 Thor

**5.4.4.15.3.0-2**  (ID: `11400873`)

Thor
5G-L1-UL shall support PUSCH reception of UE scheduled with configured grant (CG) type 2.

When a UE is scheduled with CG type 2, field *rnti of message* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq contains the CS-CRNTI, while field *cRnti refers to the C-RNTI.*\*\*

* Value of *rnti shall be used for descrambling and PTRS demapping. It shall be reported in field* rnti of messages *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs,* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU and *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD.*\*\*\*\*
* Value of *cRnti shall be reported in field* rnti of message *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLo.*\*\*

For a non CG-scheduled UE, fields *rnti and* cRnti of message *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq are equal, both set to C-RNTI.*\*\*

DFT-s-OFDM is not supported for UEs with configured grant.

---

#### 5.4.4.15.4 Odin

**5.4.4.15.4.0-1**  (ID: `12723241`)

Odin
5G-L1-UL shall support PUSCH reception of UE scheduled with configured grant (CG) type 2.

When a UE is scheduled with CG type 2, field *rnti of message* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq contains the CS-CRNTI, while field *cRnti refers to the C-RNTI.*\*\*

* Value of *rnti shall be used for descrambling and PTRS demapping. It shall be reported in field* rnti of messages *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs,* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU and *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD.*\*\*\*\*
* Value of *cRnti shall be reported in field* rnti of message *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLo.*\*\*

For a non CG-scheduled UE, fields *rnti and* cRnti of message *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq are equal, both set to C-RNTI.*\*\*

DFT-s-OFDM is not supported for UEs with configured grant.

---

Thor Classic: PUSCH Detection
[[Latest Version](https://eseemds78.emea.nsn-net.net/collaborator/document/73f24bd3-ef09-49b7-9997-2552ab5c4217/diagram/5ed01d4c-1885-499d-9a13-5694a6beb39b)]
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a process flow for "Detection [PUSCH]". The diagram uses activity and action notation to represent different stages and functions within the process.
**Key elements of the diagram:**
\* **Activity:** The main activity is labeled "Detection [PUSCH]".
\* **Inputs (in):**
\* `HrsDb`
\* `LtFo`
\* `StFo`
\* `PNE`
\* `Yds`
\* `Rdd`
\* `Npwr`
\* **Outputs (out):**
\* `LLR`
\* `pSINR`
\* `YptrsTd`
\* `InvRdd`
\* **Actions/Functions:** These are represented by rounded rectangles and are labeled with `<>` followed by a description.
\* `CE TD Interpolation`
\* `Inter-symbol FOC`
\* `PNC`
\* `Scrambling Sequence Generation`
\* `Linear Detection`
\* `Descrambling`
\* `Rdd Processing [PUSCH]`
\* **Control Flow:** Arrows indicate the flow of data and control between actions.
\* **Decision Points:** Diamond shapes represent decision points, such as the one labeled "[If DFT-S-OFDM and PNE based PNC]".
\* **Synchronization Bars:** Vertical bars indicate parallel processing or synchronization points.
\* **Metadata:** The top right corner shows "Last modified by taskinen" and "Modification date 1/31/25, 2:10 PM".
**Process Flow Description:**
1. The process starts with input `HrsDb` feeding into `CE TD Interpolation`.
2. `LtFo` is also an input, and it connects to `Inter-symbol FOC`.
3. `CE TD Interpolation` outputs `HinterpTd` and `Hin` which feed into `Inter-symbol FOC`.
4. `Inter-symbol FOC` outputs `Hout`. A decision point based on "DFT-S-OFDM and PNE based PNC" determines the next step.
5. If the condition is met, `Hout` leads to `Yin`.
6. `StFo` and `PNE` are inputs that feed into `PNC`. `Yin` also feeds into `PNC`.
7. `PNC` outputs `Yout`.
8. `Yout` leads to a decision point that outputs `Hds`.
9. `LtFo` and `PNE` are inputs to `Linear Detection`. `Yds` is also an input to `Linear Detection`.
10. `Linear Detection` outputs `LLR`, `pSINR`, and `YptrsTd`.
11. `Scrambling Sequence Generation` takes `ScrSeq` as input and outputs `ScrSeq`.
12. `Descrambling` takes `LLR` and `ScrSeq` as inputs and outputs `LlrDescr`.
13. `LlrDescr` is an output that goes to `out LLR`.
14. `pSINR` is an output that goes to `out pSINR`.
15. `YptrsTd` is an output that goes to `out YptrsTd`.
16. `InvRdd` is an input that feeds into `Rdd Processing [PUSCH]`.
17. `Rdd` and `Npwr` are inputs to `Rdd Processing [PUSCH]`.
18. `Rdd Processing [PUSCH]` outputs `InvRdd`, which goes to `out InvRdd`.

Informative Text; Original Requirement: 5G\_L1\_8278\_replaced([11414368](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e-Fn7PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), Release: 19A

The Timing Advance (TA) Command indicates the number of TA units that 5GUE shall advance or delay. One (1) TA unit corresponds to [image-0][image\_desc]16Ts/2μ[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1042.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0], where [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Ts" in a stylized font. The letters are rendered with a gradient effect, transitioning through various colors like orange, yellow, blue, and purple. The background is white, and the text is enclosed within a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1042.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] and µ has been defined in 4.1-4.2 3GPP 38.211 v.16.6.0, 5G\_UP\_IF\_15 and in 5G\_UP\_IF\_21

1TA command is also computed as above in Loki L1 HW for μ = 1.

1TA command is also computed as above in Loki L1 HW for μ = 0.

---

#### 5.4.5.2.2 Timing Advance overview

**5.4.5.2.2.0-1**  (ID: `11414349`)

Timing Advance overview
5GNB estimates the initial Timing Advance (TA) for a given 5GUE based on the detected RACH preamble. Then, it sends TA command in random access response (RAR). Once 5GUE is in connected mode, 5GNB tracks continuously the TA of UL signals and sends TA command through MAC Control Element if correction is required.

Receive Rx timing

The 5GNB should control the UE timing in such a manner that the UE is slightly late relative to the 5GNB Rx window where the signal is processed (i.e. where the FFT is performed). This is to insure a margin is kept in the case where the UE is slightly in advance versus the expected timing (in the case where the UE is slightly late compared to the expected timing, the Cyclic Prefix also serves as a margin). The interest of the margin is to make sure that the full UE signal is received such that there is no distortion introduced by the conversion to frequency domain.

Only relative delay shall be considered, i.e. the FFT window shift (illustrated in chapter 2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications, see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall not be considered in TA command and the target delay shall be 0Ts.

[5GC001868-A] Time alignment procedure shall be also supported in L1 Loki HW as explained above. [End 5GC001868-A]

[RP003187-F] The requirement is also applicable for L1 Thor for FR1-TDD-eCPRI. [End RP003187-F]

[RP003187-M] The requirement is also applicable for L1 Thor for FR1-FDD-CPRI. [End RP003187-M]

### 5.4.5.3 Front-end processing

#### 5.4.5.3.1 Timing offset compensation

##### 5.4.5.3.1.1 Loner, Loki, Thor

**5.4.5.3.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `11402139`)

Loner, Loki, Thor
Timing offset compensation

Timing offset introduces a rotation of the channel in the frequency domain. In between TA corrections triggered by MAC CE command, the current estimated timing offset must be compensated at the PUSCH receiver.

[Before 5GC002006-A]

The time offset compensation (TOC) is performed after raw channel estimation for PUSCH DMRS symbols.

The time offset compensation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section 2.3.3 of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_L1\_2794).

[PR477735] For FDD, TOC shall be disabled in 5G19A and beyond release.[End PR477735]

[End 5GC002006-A]

[5GC002006-A]

The time offset compensation (TOC) is moved to front end, it is performed before frequency offset compensation for PUSCH DMRS and data symbols.

The time offset compensation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section "2.3.2 Time Offset Compensation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_L1\_2794).

[End 5GC002006-A]

[5GC001868-A] 5G-L1-UL shall perform TOC after raw channel estimation as described above(same as 5GC002006-A) in Loki L1 HW [End 5GC001868-A]

[CB007814-G] This requirement also applies to ABIO FR1-FDD-eCPRI. [End CB007814-G]

[RP003187-D] L1 Thor shall perform TOC as described in section "2.3.2 Time Offset Compensation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification for FR1-TDD-eCPRI [End RP003187-D]

[RP003187-K] L1 Thor shall perform TOC as described in section "2.3.2 Time Offset Compensation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification for FR1-FDD-CPRI [End RP003187-K]

---

##### 5.4.5.3.1.2 Odin

###### 5.4.5.3.1.2.0-1 Odin 5G-L1-UL shall compensate the time offset of received symbols. The timing offset is a result of phase rotation in frequency domain due to FFT time shift. TOC algorithm is described in 5GMax Algorithm Specification section #sec-PuschTimeOffCom . The algorithm shall be implemented at the frontend, before frequency offset compensation for PUSCH DMRS and data symbols. (ID: 12726578)

#### 5.4.5.3.2 Intra-symbol FOC on DMRS symbols (Frontend)

**5.4.5.3.2.0-2**  (ID: `12727429`)

Intra-symbol FOC on DMRS symbols (Frontend)
Intra-symbol FOC on DMRS symbols (Frontend)

5G-L1-UL (BB), Loki, Thor, and [CB012115-IA] Odin [End CB012115-IA], shall perform Intra-symbol Frequency Offset Compensation on DMRS symbols in the frontend (SxC domain) in single user receiver (including when in MU-MIMO, the UE is not paired), also when the long term frequency offset (longTermCFOmetric) received from scheduler ([11513756: UlData\_PuschReceiveReq](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is valid (foeValid = True). Intra-symbol FOC makes corrections per symbol. At least one available longTermCFOmetricOfBeam is enough to perform intra-symbol FOC.

HST (High Speed) vs. Non-HST:

DMAP supports 11-tap Intra-symbol FOC filters. It shall be configured so that either 5 or 11 tap filters are effectively used, as specified in section [#sec-PuschFreqOffComUpdVer](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschFreqOffComUpdVer) of 5GMax Algorithm Specification:

* Loki, Thor and [CB012115-IA] Odin [End CB012115-IA] in non-HST shall use 5-tap filters. (in FR1 and FR2 if supported)
* Loki, Thor and [CB012115-IA] Odin [End CB012115-IA] in HST scenario shall use 11-tap filters. (in FR1)

CP-OFDM and DTF-s-OFDM:

The algorithm is identical for CP-OFDM and DFT-s-OFDM (Transform Precoding disabled or enabled).

MU-MIMO Receiver:

The intra-symbol FOC on DMRS symbols is not required.

FOC Optimization:

Refer to [11401568: FOC Optimization](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9EUNfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported in L1 call for CP-OFDM, SU Receiver, without FOC Optimization, for non-HST cell. [End CB012115-IA]

---

### 5.4.5.4 DMRS sequence generation

- if transform precoding is enabled,[image-22][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$\tilde{a}*{k,l}^{(\tilde{p}*{j},\mu)} = w\_f(k')w\_t(l')r(2n+k')$
The equation involves several variables and functions, including:
- $\tilde{a}*{k,l}^{(\tilde{p}*{j},\mu)}$: This appears to be a tensor or a coefficient indexed by $k$, $l$, $\tilde{p}*{j}$, and $\mu$.
- $w\_f(k')$: A function of $k'$, likely representing a weight or a filter.
- $w\_t(l')$: A function of $l'$, also likely representing a weight or a filter.
- $r(2n+k')$: A function of $2n+k'$, where $n$ and $k'$ are likely indices or parameters.
The equation suggests a relationship where the term $\tilde{a}*{k,l}^{(\tilde{p}\_{j},\mu)}$ is calculated as the product of three functions, each depending on different variables. The primes on $k'$ and $l'$ might indicate that these are related to the original variables $k$ and $l$ in some way, perhaps through a transformation or a different indexing scheme. The term $2n+k'$ suggests a linear combination of $n$ and $k'$.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_706.019.png[/image\_path][/image-22]

The intermediate quantity [image-29][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written as:
$\tilde{a}*{k,l}^{(\vec{p}*{j},\mu)}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_706.002.png[/image\_path][/image-29] shall be precoded, multiplied with the amplitude scaling factor [image-30][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_706.024.png[/image\_path][/image-30] in order to conform to the transmit power specified in section 6 of the requirement 5G\_L1\_3720\_replaced([11396761](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV9_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and mapped to physical resources according to

[image-31][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_706.025.png[/image\_path][/image-31]

where

- the precoding matrix [image-32][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, stylized letter "W" with a gradient of blue and orange colors. The background is white, and the letter is outlined with a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_706.026.png[/image\_path][/image-32] is given by clause 6.3.1.5 [see the requirement 5G\_L1\_3635\_replaced([11396741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV9PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))]. numOfLayers, spatialMode, codebookIndex, and puschTransCoherence are used to choose the correct matrix [image-33][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, stylized letter "W" with a gradient of blue and orange colors. The background is white, and the letter is outlined with a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_706.026.png[/image\_path][/image-33].

* the set of antenna ports [image-34][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in curly braces. The expression is "{p₀....pρ−1}". The curly braces indicate a set. The elements of the set are denoted by "p" with subscripts. The subscripts start from 0 and go up to "ρ-1", with "...." indicating a sequence of elements in between. The "ρ" symbol is a Greek letter, commonly used in mathematics.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_706.027.png[/image\_path][/image-34] is given by clause 6.3.1.5,
* the set of antenna ports [image-35][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible objects, text, or features.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_706.028.png[/image\_path][/image-35] is given by section 6 of the requirement 5G\_L1\_3720\_replaced([11396761](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV9_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), derived from antPort.

[image-36][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$\beta\_{PUSCH}^{DMRS} = 10^{-\frac{\beta\_{DMRS}}{20}}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_706.029.png[/image\_path][/image-36] where [image-37][image\_desc]The image contains the text "βDMRS" in a stylized font. The "β" is a Greek letter beta, and "DMRS" appears to be an acronym or abbreviation. The text is rendered in a gradient of blue and orange, with a slight blur effect.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_706.030.png[/image\_path][/image-37] is given by  the requirement 5G\_L1\_4970\_replaced([11399920](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e86jOfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

5G-L1-UL shall generate the DMRS sequence in case of CP-OFDM waveform (*puschTransformPrecoderFlag = False) for each DMRS symbol of each slot according to [11513764: PuschDmrsConfig](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) request per UE. The generated DMRS sequence shall be delivered to Raw Channel Estimation job. See [11398859: DMRS Allocation and Configuration](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e84G_fvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for supported configurations.*

If transform precoding for PUSCH is not enabled, the sequence [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry close-up of the text "r₁(n)". The text is white with a blue and orange outline. The background is black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1049.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] shall be generated according to

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text on a blurred background. The equation is:
$r\_1(n) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(1 - 2c\_1(2n)) + j\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(1 - 2c\_1(2n + 1))$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1049.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1].

where the pseudo-random sequence [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "c₁(n)". The text is blurry and appears to be in a light blue color against a dark background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1049.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] is defined in clause 5.2.1 in the requirement 5G\_L1\_3635\_replaced([11396741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV9PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (3GPP 38.211 v.15.8.0). The pseudo-random sequence generator shall be initialized with

[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$C\_{init} = (2^{17} \cdot (14 \cdot n\_s + 1 + 1) \times (2 \cdot N\_{ID}^{nscid} + 1) + 2 \cdot N\_{ID}^{nscid} + n\_{scid}) \pmod{2^{31}}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1049.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

Where:

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of text. The text appears to be "N" followed by "ID" in a smaller font below and to the right of the "N". Above and to the right of the "ID" is "nscld" in a smaller font. The colors are muted, with shades of blue and purple.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1049.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] = *dmrsScramblingSequenceInt parameter in the receive request per grant.*

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains blurry text that appears to be "nscid". The text is light blue and orange.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1049.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5] = *nscid parameter in the receive request per grant.*

[image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of the letters "m" and "g" with a subscript "s". The letters are colored in shades of blue, orange, and white, with a gradient effect. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1049.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] = *slot parameter in the receive request.*

[image-7][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract composition of light and color. It appears to be a digital rendering or a photograph with heavy post-processing. The dominant colors are soft whites, pale oranges, and cool blues, blended together in a gradient effect. There are vertical and horizontal lines of light that create a sense of depth and structure, almost like a distorted grid or a window. The right side of the image transitions into a dark, blurred area, creating a strong contrast. The overall impression is one of ethereal light and subtle color shifts, with no discernible objects or text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1049.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]= the OFDM symbol number carrying the DMRS within the slot. The supported values of [image-8][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract composition of light and color. It appears to be a digital rendering or a photograph with heavy post-processing. The dominant colors are soft whites, pale oranges, and cool blues, blended together in a gradient effect. There are vertical and horizontal lines of light that create a sense of depth and structure, almost like a distorted grid or a window. The right side of the image transitions into a dark, blurred area, creating a strong contrast. The overall impression is one of ethereal light and subtle color shifts, with no discernible objects or text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1049.008.png[/image\_path][/image-8] can be derived from the tables provided in the requirement 5G\_L1\_3635\_replaced([11396741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV9PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In 5G21B, [image-9][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract composition of light and color. It appears to be a digital rendering or a photograph with heavy post-processing. The dominant colors are soft whites, pale oranges, and cool blues, blended together in a gradient effect. There are vertical and horizontal lines of light that create a sense of depth and structure, almost like a distorted grid or a window. The right side of the image transitions into a dark, blurred area, creating a strong contrast. The overall impression is one of ethereal light and subtle color shifts, with no discernible objects or text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1049.008.png[/image\_path][/image-9] is given by Table 6.4.1.1.3-3 of the requirement 5G\_L1\_3635\_replaced([11396741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV9PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) here below in the requirement  5G\_L1\_4964, where **ulDmrsLen = 1 and frequency hopping disabled.**

Note: In general, the total number of DMRS pseudo-random sequences [image-10][image\_desc]The image contains the text "c₁(n)". The text is blurry and appears to be in a light blue color against a dark background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1049.003.png[/image\_path][/image-10] in slot per subcell is given by (**ulDmrsLen+ *ulDmrsAddPos) x *numOfGrants. This number can be further optimized (reduced) if many grants has the same* dmrsScramblingSequenceInt and \*nscid values.***\*\*\*

[CB012115-IA] The requirement is supported in Odin. It is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side. [End CB012115-IA]

The content of this requirement is frozen and valid until release 24R2. For later releases, please refer to the objects below.

For 5G21B, DFT-s-OFDM is supported with neither PUSCH group, nor sequence hopping are enabled. The DMRS sequence is generated as follows,

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as: $r(n) = r\_{u,0}^{0.1}(n)$. The text is in a light blue color against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1052.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "u = dmr sScramblingSequenceInt mod 30". The text is in a light blue color and appears to be part of a mathematical equation or code snippet.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1052.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image\_desc]n = 0 ... $N\_{sc}^{RB}$ \* $\frac{num0fPrb}{2}$ - 1.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1052.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

Where [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a blurry, light blue font against a dark background. The expression appears to be:
$r\_{u,v}^{\alpha, \delta}(n)$
Here's a breakdown of the components:
\* **r**: This is the main letter, likely representing a variable or function.
\* **u,v**: These are subscripts to the letter 'r'.
\* **α, δ**: These are superscripts to the letter 'r', positioned to the upper left.
\* **(n)**: This is in parentheses and appears to be an argument to the function or variable represented by the rest of the expression.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1052.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] is described in section 5.2.2 of the requirement 5G\_L1\_3635\_replaced([11396741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV9PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

\* **(n)**: This is in parentheses and appears to be an argument to the function or variable represented by the rest of the expression.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1054.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] is described in section 5.2.2 of 3GPP TS 38.211.

DFT-s-OFDM (Transform Precoding)
DFT-s-OFDM is supported with neither PUSCH group nor sequence hopping are enabled. The DMRS sequence is generated as follows,

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as: $r(n) = r\_{u,0}^{0.1}(n)$. The text is in a light blue color against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1056.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the following text: u = dmr sScramblingSequenceInt mod 30[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1056.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image\_desc]n = 0 ... $N\_{sc}^{RB}$ \* $\frac{num0fPrb}{2}$ - 1.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1056.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[CB007103-A]

If parameter dmrsSequenceType of message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is set to type1, release-15 DMRS sequence type is selected. In this case, [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a subscript and superscript notation. The base of the expression is "r". The subscripts are "u,v". The superscripts are "α,δ". Following the superscripts, there is a parenthesis containing the letter "n". The expression is blurry and difficult to read clearly.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1056.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] is generated as described in section 5.2.2 of 3GPP TS 38.211. PUSCH data may be using any modulation type in this case.

If BPSK modulation is used and parameter dmrsSequenceType of message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is set to type2, release-16 low-PAPR DMRS sequence type is used. In this case, [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a blurry, light blue font against a dark background. The expression is:
$r\_{u,v}^{\alpha, \delta}(n)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1056.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] is generated as described in section 5.2.3 of 3GPP TS 38.211.

[End CB007103-A]

Technology map:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Technology | Rel-15 DMRS sequence | Rel-16 Low-PAPR DMRS sequence |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | Not supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Not supported |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported | [CB007103-A] Supported [End CB007103-A] |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | [CB007103-A] Supported [End CB007103-A] |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Not supported | Not supported |

The text is in a light blue color against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1058.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[CB007103-A]

If parameter dmrsSequenceType of message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is set to type1, release-15 DMRS sequence type is selected. If BPSK modulation is used and parameter dmrsSequenceType of message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is set to type2, release-16 low-PAPR DMRS sequence type is used.
