11401369:
DMRS based FOE
In DMRS based FOE, rotation between DMRS symbols is detected

In CP-OFDM and DFT-S non-HST case this is measured by using 2 first DMRS. If there are 3 DMRS symbols, still only 2 first ones are used.

In HST case with 3 DMRS, FOE is measured by using 2 last DMRS, because distance between 2 last ones are in most of the cases shorter than between 2 first ones.

Calculation is defined in chapter 2.3.3.2 for CP-OFDM and 2.3.3.8.2 for DFT-S-OFDM in [5gmax algo spec], and calculation is same.

Calculation is multiple steps and below is part of step 3, which shows that difference between two channel estimates which are calculated from DMRS (2) and DMRS (1):

[image-0][image\_desc]No description could be generated for this image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_915.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note that noise is taken also into account in above formula.

Antenna specific calculation is done when N\_RX=4, and in that case both antenna specific values C\_ST\_raw\_ant (not shown in formula), and summed value C\_ST\_raw are calculated.

C\_ST\_raw is raw FOE. Final C\_ST value, which is sent to L2 in shortTermCfo in Ul\_Phydata\_PuschReceiveRespPs message, shall contain C\_ST\_raw + C\_LT (if that was received in PuschReceiveReq message).

Note that last step in chapter 2.3.3.1 in [5gmax algo spec] contains also formula how C\_ST is calculated back to frequency offset which is used in DMRS based PNC. Same formula is also used to calculate longTermCfo back to FO for FOC purpose.

---

###### 5.4.5.13.1.2.2 Loki

**5.4.5.13.1.2.2.0-1**  (ID: `11401374`)

Loki
In DMRS based FOE, rotation between DMRS symbols is detected

In CP-OFDM and DFT-S non-HST case this is measured by using 2 first DMRS. If there are 3 DMRS symbols, still only 2 first ones are used.

In HST case with 3 DMRS, FOE is measured by using 2 last DMRS, because distance between 2 last ones are in most of the cases shorter than between 2 first ones.

Calculation is defined in chapter 2.3.3.2 for CP-OFDM and 2.3.3.8.2 for DFT-S-OFDM in [5gmax algo spec], and calculation is same.

Calculation is multiple steps and below is part of step 3, which shows that difference between two DMRS symbols is detected:

Note that noise is taken also into account in above formula.

Antenna specific calculation is done when NRX=4. In that case, antenna specific values   and summed value   are calculated:

is raw FOE. Final FOE value  shall contain   and   (if foeValid is set to 'true' in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message), as indicated in "5GMax algorithm specification" § 2.3.3.2XXX:

[03221058] Range validation is done on short-term FOE angle, as follows:

Maximum phase is defined as:

If PhaseAdj > PhaseMax

PhaseAdj = PhaseMax

If PhaseAdj < - PhaseMax

PhaseAdj = - PhaseMax

Otherwise

PhaseAdj is unchanged

[End 03221058]

means long term CFO (longTermCfoMetric)

the symbols interval when compute

[03221058]

foeGap is the symbols interval when computes short term correlation

foeGapLargest refers to IE foeGapLargest in message UlData\_PuschReceiveRequest (5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

For more details about rdUlStFoeScaler please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24702\_replaced([11110529](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpFiB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End 03221058]

Final FOE value is sent to L2 in shortTermCfoMetric and shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt in message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs.

Note that last step in chapter 2.3.3.1 in "5GMax algorithm specification" contains also formula calculate CST back to frequency offset which is used in DMRS based PNC. Same formula is also used to calculate longTermCfo back to FO for FOC purpose.

###### 5.4.5.13.1.2.3 Thor

###### 5.4.5.13.1.2.3.0-1 Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: Thor 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE 11401430: DMRS based FOE In DMRS based FOE, rotation between DMRS symbols is detected In CP-OFDM and DFT-S non-HST case this is measured by using 2 first DMRS. If there are 3 DMRS symbols, still only 2 first ones are used. In HST case with 3 DMRS, FOE is measured by using 2 last DMRS, because distance between 2 last ones are in most of the cases shorter than between 2 first ones. Calculation is defined in chapter 2.3.3.2 for CP-OFDM and 2.3.3.8.2 for DFT-S-OFDM in [5gmax algo spec], and calculation is same. Calculation is multiple steps and below is part of step 3, which shows that difference between two DMRS symbols is detected: [image-0][image_desc]No description could be generated for this image.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] Note that noise is taken also into account in above formula. Antenna specific calculation is done when NRX=4. In that case, antenna specific values [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written as "C" with a superscript "cant" and a subscript "ST".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] and summed value [image-2][image_desc]The image contains a blurry, stylized representation of text. The text appears to be "C'aw" above "ST". The letters are in a dark, possibly black, color against a lighter background. The overall image quality is low, making precise interpretation difficult.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] are calculated: [image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation starts with an approximation symbol (≈) followed by three summation symbols. The first summation is over 'ant' from 0 to $N_{RX}-1$. The second summation is over 'l' from 0 to $N_{layer}^{DMRS}-1$. The third summation is over 'r' in $ScSet_l^{DMRS}$. Following the summations, there is an exponential term: $e^{j2\pi(N_{PPT}+N_{CP})\cdot(S_l^{DMRS}(z)-S_l^{DMRS}(1))\cdot T_s \cdot \delta f}$. The equation then continues with a fraction. The numerator of the fraction is the product of three terms, each enclosed in absolute value bars: 1. $|H_{S_l^{DMRS}(2),l,r,ant}^{smoothed}|$ 2. $|H_{S_l^{DMRS}(1),l,r,ant}^{smoothed}|$ 3. $S_{ant}^{com}$ The denominator of the fraction is $\hat{\sigma} {noise,ant}^2$. The overall equation appears to be related to signal processing or communications, possibly involving channel estimation or interference cancellation.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.004.png[/image_path][/image-3] [image-4][image_desc]The image contains a blurry, stylized representation of text. The text appears to be "C'aw" above "ST". The letters are in a dark, possibly black, color against a lighter background. The overall image quality is low, making precise interpretation difficult.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.003.png[/image_path][/image-4]is raw FOE. Final FOE value [image-5][image_desc]The image contains the text "CST" in a stylized font. The letters are dark gray and appear to be slightly blurred, giving them a soft, almost glowing effect. The "C" is larger than the "S" and "T", and the "S" and "T" are connected. The text is positioned in the center of the image.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.005.png[/image_path][/image-5] shall contain [image-6][image_desc]The image contains a blurry, stylized representation of text. The text appears to be "C'aw" above "ST". The letters are in a dark, possibly black, color against a lighter background. The overall image quality is low, making precise interpretation difficult.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.003.png[/image_path][/image-6] and [image-7][image_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "C {lr}".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.006.png[/image_path][/image-7] (if foeValid is set to 'true' in UlData_PuschReceiveReq message), as indicated in "5GMax algorithm specification" § 2.3.3.2XXX: [image-8][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is: angle(C_ST) = angle(C_ST^raw) * (ltv / foeGap) + angle(C_LT)[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.007.png[/image_path][/image-8] Range validation is done on short-term FOE angle, as follows: [image-9][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $C_{ST} = [C_{ST}^{raw}] \cdot \exp \left( j \cdot \left( \text{angle}(C_{ST}^{raw}) \cdot \frac{ltv}{foeGap} + \text{angle}(C_{LT}) \right) \right)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.008.png[/image_path][/image-9] [image-10][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in an italicized font. The equation is: PhaseAdj = angle(CSTraw). $\frac{I tv}{foeGap}$ + angle(CLT)[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.009.png[/image_path][/image-10] Maximum phase is defined as: [image-11][image_desc]PhaseMax = $\frac{\pi \cdot ltv}{foeGapLargest}$ * rdULStFoeScaler[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.010.png[/image_path][/image-11] If PhaseAdj &gt; PhaseMax PhaseAdj = PhaseMax If PhaseAdj &lt; - PhaseMax PhaseAdj = - PhaseMax Otherwise PhaseAdj is unchanged [image-12][image_desc]The image contains the text "CLT" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient effect, transitioning from blue to orange and yellow. The "C" is larger than the "L" and "T", which are of similar size. The "L" and "T" are positioned slightly below and to the right of the "C".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.011.png[/image_path][/image-12]means long term CFO (longTermCfoMetric) [image-13][image_desc]The image contains the blurry text "ltv".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.012.png[/image_path][/image-13]the symbols interval when compute [image-14][image_desc]The image contains the text "C.ST" in a stylized font. The letters are a light blue color with a gradient effect, transitioning to orange and brown at the edges. The "C" is larger and bolder than the "ST", which is smaller and appears to be a subscript. The text is slightly blurry, giving it a soft, almost glowing appearance.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_917.013.png[/image_path][/image-14] foeGap is the symbols interval when computes short term correlation foeGapLargest refers to IE foeGapLargest in message UlData_PuschReceiveRequest (5G_L1_IF_191_replaced( 11513756 )) For more details about rdUlStFoeScaler please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_24702_replaced( 11110529 ) Final FOE value is sent to L2 in shortTermCfoMetric and shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt in message UlData_PuschReceiveRespPs. Note that last step in chapter 2.3.3.1 in "5GMax algorithm specification" contains also formula calculate CST back to frequency offset which is used in DMRS based PNC. Same formula is also used to calculate longTermCfo back to FO for FOC purpose. (ID: 11401430)

5G-L1-UL(BB) shall calculate the rotation between DMRS symbols for DMRS based FOE.

In CP-OFDM and DFT-s-OFDM for non-HST case, this is measured by using 2 first DMRS. If there are 3 DMRS symbols, still only 2 first ones are used.

In HST case with 3 DMRS, FOE is measured by using 2 last DMRS, because distance between 2 last ones are in most of the cases shorter than between 2 first ones.

Calculation is defined in chapter 2.3.3.2 for CP-OFDM and 2.3.3.8.2 for DFT-S-OFDM in [5gmax algo spec], and calculation is same.

Calculation is multiple steps and below is part of step 3, which shows that difference between two channel estimates which are calculated from DMRS (2) and DMRS (1):

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$C\_{SST}^{DMRS} = \sum\_{n=0}^{N\_{DMRS}-1} \sum\_{l=0}^{N\_{layer}-1} \sum\_{r \in S\_{Seq}^{DMRS}} \frac{H\_{spmrs}^{smoothed}(r,l) \cdot S\_{com}^{ant}}{ \sigma\_{noise,ant}^2 } \cdot S\_{com}^{ant}$
The formula involves summations over indices $n$, $l$, and $r$. It also includes terms like $N\_{DMRS}$, $N\_{layer}$, $H\_{spmrs}^{smoothed}(r,l)$, $S\_{com}^{ant}$, and $\sigma\_{noise,ant}^2$. The formula appears to be related to signal processing or communications, possibly in the context of a DMRS (Demodulation Reference Signal) or similar concept. The red circles highlight parts of the formula, specifically the terms $(r,l)$ and $(r,l)$ within the $H\_{spmrs}^{smoothed}$ function.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_915.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note that noise is taken also into account in above formula.

Antenna specific calculation is done when N\_RX=4, and in that case both antenna specific values C\_ST\_raw\_ant (not shown in formula), and summed value C\_ST\_raw are calculated.

C\_ST\_raw is raw FOE. Final C\_ST value, which is sent to L2 in shortTermCfo in Ul\_Phydata\_PuschReceiveRespPs message, shall contain C\_ST\_raw + C\_LT (if that was received in PuschReceiveReq message).

Note that last step in chapter 2.3.3.1 in [5gmax algo spec] contains also formula how C\_ST is calculated back to frequency offset which is used in DMRS based PNC. Same formula is also used to calculate longTermCfo back to FO for FOC purpose.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

---

12733661:
PTRS based FOE
5G-L1-UL(BB) shall calculate the rotation between PTRS symbols for DMRS based FOE. The distance between selected PTRS symbols which shall be detected, is defined by foeGap parameter which could be eg 4.

Calculation is defined in chapter 2.3.3.3 for CP-OFDM and 2.3.3.8.1 for DFT-S-OFDM  in [5gmax algo spec].

Algorithms are slightly different as in DFT-S-OFDM case processing is done after IDFT which is not done in CP-OFDM case.

Input for the PTRS based FOE is knowledge from PTRS sequence (based on 3GPP spec) and received PTRS signal.

Calculation is multiple steps, and below is part of step 3 from CP-OFDM, which compares different symbols which have FoeGap difference:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is for $C\_{ST}^{ant}$ and is given by:
$C\_{ST}^{ant} = \sum\_{l=0}^{N\_{layer}^{PTRS}-1} \sum\_{r \in S c S e t\_{l}^{P T R S}} \sum\_{i \in S\_{v a l i d(d, \lambda, r)}} \frac{H\_{i+F o e G a p, l, r, a n t}^{u, P T R S, r o w}\left(H\_{i, l, r, a n t}^{u, P T R S, r o w}\right)^{\*}}{\hat{\sigma}\_{n o i s e, a n t}^{2}}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_919.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note that noise is taken also into account in above formula. Noise is also taken into account in DFT-S-OFDM case.

In CP-OFDM, antenna specific values are calculated in N\_RX=4 case. In DFT-S-ODFM case it is not possible because FOE calculation is done after equalization when antenna specific data is not existing anymore. In that case same value is copied for every antenna. Reporting to L2 in both cases is done in same manner as in DMRS based FOE.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

---

##### 5.4.5.13.1.4 PTRS + DMRS based FOE

**5.4.5.13.1.4.0-2**  (ID: `11401456`)

PTRS + DMRS based FOE
This estimation method is selected in HST cell when UE has:

-2 DMRS symbols, and

-PTRS, and

-rdUlCfoEstimationHstMethod is set to 0. ([11110283: rdUlCfoEstimationHstMethod](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpCesfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

-CP-OFDM transmission

Algorithm is described in chapter 2.3.3.3.3 in [5gmax algo spec].

Idea is that DMRS FOE is used but as that does not support full FO range with 2 DMRS symbols, PTRS is used to correct its phase ambiquity.

The resulting C\_ST is converted to frequency offset, and that is used in PNC.

Note that PNE is not calculated from PTRS.

---

#### 5.4.5.13.2 Phase Noise Estimation (PNE)

##### 5.4.5.13.2.1 PNE Selection

###### 5.4.5.13.2.1.1 Legacy

**5.4.5.13.2.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `11401203`)

Legacy
Content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 23R4 release. For later releases please refer to the objects below.

According to the number of allocated RBGs, the MCS index and the validity of CFO estimate, either FOE (long term FOE from 5G-L2-PS) or PN estimate shall be selected to perform PNC (Phase Noise Compensation) on PUSCH at 5G-L1-UL. The information needed for the selection (i.e. MCS index, # of allocated PRBs and CFO validity) comes within the UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message sent by 5G-L2-PS.

For TDD, for PUSCH scheduled by RAR UL grant, no PTRS is available (ulPtrsFlag ), but multiple DM-RS are sent (ulDmrsAddPos ). In this case, the PNC shall be processed with Short Term FOE (ST FOE). The needed parameters are provided by the UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message sent from 5G-L2-PS.

For FDD, PTRS is deactivated for PUSCH scheduled by RAR UL grant. Same processing as for TDD should be applied.

5G-L1-UL shall choose the appropriate estimate according to the following algorithm:

If foeValid == TRUE

Then

if (numOfPrb > pneRbThreshold [mcs]) && (ulPtrsFlag == 1)

Then

process PNE

process PNC with PNE

Else

skip PNE and PNC

Endif

Else

if ulPtrsFlag == 0

Then

For Loner L1 HW If  (numOfDmrsSymbols == 3)

[5GC002056-AA, 5GC002481-AA] For Loki L1 HW If  (numOfDmrsSymbols ≥ 2) [End 5GC002056-AA, 5GC002481-AA]

Then

[Before 5GC002244-D] For Loner L1 HW [Before 5GC001294-B] and Loki L1 HW  [End 5GC001294-B] Process PNC with ST FOE based on multiple DMRS

[5GC002244-D]

For Loner L1 HW [5GC001294-B] and Loki L1 HW [End 5GC001294-B], Process PNC with antenna-wise ST FOE (shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt) based on multiple DMRS

[End 5GC002244-D]

Else

skip PNE and PNC

Endif

Else

process PNE

process PNC with PNE

Endif

Endif

With

 foeValid, numOfPrb, mcs, ulPtrsFlag and ulDmrsAddPos are got from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message;

    numOfDmrsSymbols stands for the number of DMRS symbols per slot that might be used by CFO estimation (see 5G\_L1\_7339\_replaced([11401166](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9B38vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

 process PNE means that PN estimation shall be performed at 5G-L1-UL as described in section "2.3.9.1 Phase Noise Estimation" of the 5GMax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

[5GC000545-A; 5GC000545-B; 5GC000545-C, RP003187-E, RP003187-L, CB007814-G]

When transform precoding is enabled (i.e. puschTransformPrecoderFlag = 1, see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_194 5G\_L1\_IF\_195\_replaced([11513778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UL PTRS is activated (i.e. ulPtrsFlag = 1, see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_195 5G\_L1\_IF\_196\_replaced([11513783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

5G-L1-UL shall compute PNE after IDFT according to the algorithm described in section 2.3.9.5.2.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

5G-L1-UL shall perform PNC after IDFT as given in section 2.3.9.5.2.3 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

[End 5GC000545-A; 5GC000545-B; 5GC000545-C, RP003187-E, RP003187-L, CB007814-G]

 [RP003187-E] process PNC with ST FOE means that PN compensation shall be performed at 5G-L1-UL as described in section "2.3.3.3 Frequency Offset Estimation" the 5GMax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The input of this block would be ST FOE; [End RP003187-E]

[5GC000545-A; 5GC000545-B; 5GC000545-C, RP003187-E, RP003187-L, CB007814-G]

When transform precoding is enabled (i.e. puschTransformPrecoderFlag = 1, see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_194 5G\_L1\_IF\_195\_replaced([11513778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UL PTRS is disabled (i.e. ulPtrsFlag = 0, see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_195 5G\_L1\_IF\_196\_replaced([11513783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), ST FOE is used for PNC in the similar way as when transform precoding is disabled as described in section 2.3.9.5.1 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

[End 5GC000545-A; 5GC000545-B; 5GC000545-C, RP003187-E, RP003187-L, CB007814-G]

 skip PNE and PNC means that 5G-L1-UL shall not perform PNE and PNC;

 pneRbThreshold is an array of 29 elements whose values depend on the ulSubcellType in UlCell\_SetupReq message (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_152\_replaced([11513449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRwhwfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the numOfLayers parameter from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as below:

[Before 5GC000545-A; 5GC000545-B; 5GC000545-C, RP003187-E, RP003187-L]

| C4, A4 | 1 | rdPneRbThresholdLB\_64QAMLowSE\_4RxSingleLayer in 5G\_L1\_10242\_replaced([11407443](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9i1VfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | N/A | CB008246-C |
| 2 | rdPneRbThresholdLB\_64QAMLowSE\_4RxTwoLayer in 5G\_L1\_10243\_replaced([11407452](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9i1VvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | N/A |  |  |

[End CB008246-C]

Note: the ST CFO estimate (or ST FOE) is computed at 5G-L1-UL by using multiple DMRS in PUSCH scheduled by RAR UL grant, as stated in 5G\_L1\_7337\_replaced([11401132](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9BQ7PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: the derivation of foeValid value at 5G-L2-PS is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3333\_replaced([11151157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

More details about the PNC handling at L1 are given in "2.3.9.4 Implementation steps for PNE and PNC" of the 5GMax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[5GC001868-A] For FR1, 5G-L1-UL shall support PNE and PNC with ulPtrsFlag=0, numOfDmrsSymbols ={1, 3}  in Loki L1 HW [Before 5GC002056-AA, 5GC002481-AA] with pneRbThreshold values according to Table1 in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_986 [End 5GC002056-AA, 5GC002481-AA]

[End 5GC001868-A]

[5GC001868-F] In addition for FR1 TDD, 5G-L1-UL shall support PNE and PNC with ulPtrsFlag=0, numOfDmrsSymbols = 2 in Loki L1 HW [Before 5GC002056-AA, 5GC002481-AA] with pneRbThreshold values according to Table1 in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_986 [End 5GC002056-AA, 5GC002481-AA]

[End5GC001868-F]

[5GC002056-AA, 5GC002481-AA] In addition for FR1, 5G-L1-UL shall support PNE and PNC with ulPtrsFlag=1, numOfDmrsSymbols = 1 in Loki L1 HW [End 5GC002056-AA, 5GC002481-AA]

[Before PR578583]

[5GC002056-AA, 5GC002481-AA] In addition for FR1, 5G-L1-UL shall support PNE and PNC with ulPtrsFlag=1, numOfDmrsSymbols = {2, 3} in Loki L1 HW [End 5GC002056-AA, 5GC002481-AA]

[End PR578583]

[PR578583]

For Loki L1 HW only, FR1 and when ulPtrsFlag=1 and numOfDmrsSymbols={2, 3}, 5G-L1-UL does not perform PNE and PNC as described in section 2.3.9 of the 5GMax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[End PR578583]

[RP003187-D, RP003187-K] L1 Thor shall support PNE and PNC as described in "2.3.9 Phase Noise Estimation and Compensation" of the 5GMax algorithm specifications. Applicable for C2/C4 or A2/A4 subcells. [End RP003187-D, RP003187-K]

[RP003187-E, RP003187-L] L1 Thor shall support PNE and PNC as described in "2.3.9 Phase Noise Estimation and Compensation" of the 5GMax algorithm specifications when transform precoding is enabled. Applicable for C2/C4 or A2/A4 subcells. [End RP003187-E, RP003187-L]

[Before CB008325-5G-AA]

[RP003187-D, RP003187-K]

L1 Thor for FR1 shall also skip PNE and PNC when (foeValid=True and ulPtrsFlag=1 and numOfDmrsSymbols={2, 3}) as described with PR578583 above.

If (foeValid=False and numOfDmrsSymbols={2, 3}) then L1 Thor shall process PNC with antenna-wise ST FOE (shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt) based on multiple DMRS (this case does not depend on ulPtrsFlag value).

[End CB008325-5G-AA, RP003187-D, RP003187-K]

[CB008325-5G-AA, CB008325-5G-BA]  L1 Thor shall use the algorithm  as described in "2.3.9 Phase Noise Estimation and Compensation" of the  5GMax algorithm specifications v.11.004 [End CB008325-5G-AA, CB008325-5G-BA]

[CB008205-B, CB008205-C] 5G-L1-UL shall also support requirements above in ABIO for O-RAN compliant FR1 FDD eCPRI. [End CB008205-B, CB008205-C]

[CB009443-C] This requirement is also appliable for RINLINE 1 HW for FR1-TDD-eCPRI [end CB009443-C]

---

###### 5.4.5.13.2.1.2 Loner

**5.4.5.13.2.1.2.0-1**  (ID: `11401217`)

Loner
For Loner

According to the number of allocated RBGs, the MCS index and the validity of CFO estimate, either FOE (long term FOE from 5G-L2-PS) or PN estimate shall be selected to perform PNC (Phase Noise Compensation) on PUSCH at 5G-L1-UL. The information needed for the selection (i.e. MCS index, # of allocated PRBs and CFO validity) comes within the UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message sent by 5G-L2-PS.

For PUSCH scheduled by RAR UL grant, no PTRS is available (ulPtrsFlag ), but multiple DM-RS are sent (ulDmrsAddPos ). In this case, the PNC shall be processed with Short Term FOE (ST FOE). The needed parameters are provided by the UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message sent from 5G-L2-PS.

5G-L1-UL shall choose the appropriate estimate according to the following algorithm:

If foeValid == TRUE

Then

if (numOfPrb > pneRbThreshold [mcs]) && (ulPtrsFlag == 1)

Then

process PNE

process PNC with PNE

Else

skip PNE and PNC

Endif

Else

if ulPtrsFlag == 0

Then

If  (numOfDmrsSymbols == 3)

Then

process PNC with antenna-wise ST FOE (shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt) based on multiple DMRS

Else

skip PNE and PNC

Endif

Else

process PNE

process PNC with PNE

Endif

Endif

With

 foeValid, numOfPrb, mcs, ulPtrsFlag and ulDmrsAddPos are got from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message;

    numOfDmrsSymbols stands for the number of DMRS symbols per slot that might be used by CFO estimation (see 5G\_L1\_7339\_replaced([11401166](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9B38vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

 process PNE means that PN estimation shall be performed at 5G-L1-UL as described in section "2.3.9.1 Phase Noise Estimation" of the 5GMax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

 When transform precoding is enabled (i.e. puschTransformPrecoderFlag = 1, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_195\_replaced([11513778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UL PTRS is activated (i.e. ulPtrsFlag = 1, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_196\_replaced([11513783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- 5G-L1-UL shall compute PNE after IDFT according to the algorithm described in section 2.3.9.5.2.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

- 5G-L1-UL shall perform PNC after IDFT as given in section 2.3.9.5.2.3 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

 When transform precoding is enabled (i.e. puschTransformPrecoderFlag = 1, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_195\_replaced([11513778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UL PTRS is disabled (i.e. ulPtrsFlag = 0, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_196\_replaced([11513783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), ST FOE is used for PNC in the similar way as when transform precoding is disabled as described in section 2.3.9.5.1 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

 process PNC with ST FOE means that PN compensation shall be performed at 5G-L1-UL as described in section "2.3.3.3 Frequency Offset Estimation" the 5GMax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The input of this block would be ST FOE;

 skip PNE and PNC means that 5G-L1-UL shall not perform PNE and PNC;

 pneRbThreshold is an array of 32 elements whose values depend on the ulSubcellType in UlCell\_SetupReq message (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_152\_replaced([11513449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRwhwfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the numOfLayers parameter from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as below:

- for 256QAM table: 5G\_L1\_12692\_replaced([11401230](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9B3_fvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- for 64QAM table: [CB008246-I] 5G\_L1\_11972\_replaced([11401235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9CfAPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB008246-I]

- for 64QAMLowSE table: [CB008246-I] 5G\_L1\_11828\_replaced([11401241](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9CfAfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB008246-I]

Note: the ST CFO estimate (or ST FOE) is computed at 5G-L1-UL by using multiple DMRS in PUSCH scheduled by RAR UL grant, as stated in 5G\_L1\_7337\_replaced([11401132](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9BQ7PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: the derivation of foeValid value at 5G-L2-PS is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3333\_replaced([11151157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

More details about the PNC handling at L1 are given in "2.3.9.4 Implementation steps for PNE and PNC" of the 5GMax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

###### 5.4.5.13.2.1.3 Loki

**5.4.5.13.2.1.3.0-1**  (ID: `11401224`)

Loki
For Loki

According to the number of allocated RBGs, the MCS index and the validity of CFO estimate, either FOE (long term FOE from 5G-L2-PS) or PN estimate shall be selected to perform PNC (Phase Noise Compensation) on PUSCH at 5G-L1-UL. The information needed for the selection (i.e. MCS index, # of allocated PRBs and CFO validity) comes within the UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message sent by 5G-L2-PS.

For PUSCH scheduled by RAR UL grant, no PTRS is available (ulPtrsFlag ), but multiple DM-RS are sent (ulDmrsAddPos ). In this case, the PNC shall be processed with Short Term FOE (ST FOE). The needed parameters are provided by the UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message sent from 5G-L2-PS.

5G-L1-UL shall choose the appropriate estimate according to the following algorithm:

If foeValid == TRUE

Then

if (numOfPrb > pneRbThreshold [mcs]) && (ulPtrsFlag == 1)

Then

process PNE

process PNC with PNE

Else

skip PNE and PNC

Endif

Else

if ulPtrsFlag == 0

Then

If  (numOfDmrsSymbols ≥ 2)

Then

Process PNC with antenna-wise ST FOE (shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt) based on multiple DMRS

Else

skip PNE and PNC

Endif

Else

process PNE

process PNC with PNE

Endif

Endif

With

 foeValid, numOfPrb, mcs, ulPtrsFlag and ulDmrsAddPos are got from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message;

    numOfDmrsSymbols stands for the number of DMRS symbols per slot that might be used by CFO estimation (see 5G\_L1\_7339\_replaced([11401166](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9B38vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

 process PNE means that PN estimation shall be performed at 5G-L1-UL as described in section "2.3.9.1 Phase Noise Estimation" of the 5GMax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

 When transform precoding is enabled (i.e. puschTransformPrecoderFlag = 1, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_195\_replaced([11513778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UL PTRS is activated (i.e. ulPtrsFlag = 1, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_196\_replaced([11513783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- 5G-L1-UL shall compute PNE after IDFT according to the algorithm described in section 2.3.9.5.2.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

- 5G-L1-UL shall perform PNC after IDFT as given in section 2.3.9.5.2.3 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

 When transform precoding is enabled (i.e. puschTransformPrecoderFlag = 1, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_195\_replaced([11513778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UL PTRS is disabled (i.e. ulPtrsFlag = 0, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_196\_replaced([11513783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), ST FOE is used for PNC in the similar way as when transform precoding is disabled as described in section 2.3.9.5.1 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

 process PNC with ST FOE means that PN compensation shall be performed at 5G-L1-UL as described in section "2.3.3.3 Frequency Offset Estimation" the 5GMax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The input of this block would be ST FOE;

 skip PNE and PNC means that 5G-L1-UL shall not perform PNE and PNC;

 pneRbThreshold is an array of 32 elements whose values depend on the ulSubcellType in UlCell\_SetupReq message (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_152\_replaced([11513449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRwhwfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the numOfLayers parameter from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as below:

- for 256QAM table: 5G\_L1\_12692\_replaced([11401230](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9B3_fvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- for 64QAM table: [CB008246-I] 5G\_L1\_11972\_replaced([11401235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9CfAPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB008246-I]

- for 64QAMLowSE table: [CB008246-I] 5G\_L1\_11828\_replaced([11401241](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9CfAfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB008246-I]

Note: the ST CFO estimate (or ST FOE) is computed at 5G-L1-UL by using multiple DMRS in PUSCH [Before CB008792-A]scheduled by RAR UL grant, as stated in 5G\_L1\_7337\_replaced([11401132](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9BQ7PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB008792-A].

Note: the derivation of foeValid value at 5G-L2-PS is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3333\_replaced([11151157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

More details about the PNC handling at L1 are given in "2.3.9.4 Implementation steps for PNE and PNC" of the 5GMax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For FR1, 5G-L1-UL shall support PNE and PNC with ulPtrsFlag=0, numOfDmrsSymbols ={1, 3}.

In addition for FR1 TDD, 5G-L1-UL shall support PNE and PNC with ulPtrsFlag=0, numOfDmrsSymbols = 2.

In addition for FR1, 5G-L1-UL shall support PNE and PNC with ulPtrsFlag=1, numOfDmrsSymbols = 1 in Loki L1 HW.

For FR1 and when ulPtrsFlag=1 and numOfDmrsSymbols={2, 3}, 5G-L1-UL does not perform PNE and PNC as described in section 2.3.9 of the 5GMax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

###### 5.4.5.13.2.1.4 Thor

**5.4.5.13.2.1.4.0-1**  (ID: `11401250`)

Thor
**For Thor**

According to the number of allocated PRBs, the MCS index and the validity of CFO estimate, either FOE (long term FOE from 5G-L2-PS), ST FO estimate or PN estimate shall be selected to perform PNC (Phase Noise Compensation) on PUSCH at 5G-L1-UL. The information needed for the selection (i.e. MCS index, # of allocated PRBs and CFO validity) comes within the *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message sent by 5G-L2-PS.*

For PUSCH scheduled by RAR UL grant, no PTRS is available (*ulPtrsFlag ), but multiple DM-RS are sent (*ulDmrsAddPos ). In this case, the PNC shall be processed with Short Term FOE (ST FOE). The needed parameters are provided by the *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message sent from 5G-L2-PS.*\*\*

5G-L1-UL shall choose the appropriate estimate according the algorithm described in "2.3.9 Phase Noise Estimation and Compensation" of the  5GMax algorithm specifications [**Before CB008246-N] v.11.004** [CB008246-N] v.11.155 **[End CB008246-N].**\*\*\*\*

**L1 Thor shall support PNE and PNC as described in "2.3.9 Phase Noise Estimation and Compensation" of the 5GMax algorithm specifications when transform precoding is enabled**. Applicable for C2/C4 or A2/A4 subcells.\*\*\*\*

When transform precoding is enabled (i.e. *puschTransformPrecoderFlag = 1, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_195\_replaced([11513778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UL PTRS is activated (i.e.* ulPtrsFlag = 1, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_196\_replaced([11513783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))\*\*

5G-L1-UL shall compute PNE after IDFT according to the algorithm described in section 2.3.9.5.2.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

5G-L1-UL shall perform PNC after IDFT as given in section 2.3.9.5.2.3 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

When transform precoding is enabled (i.e. *puschTransformPrecoderFlag = 1, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_195\_replaced([11513778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UL PTRS is disabled (i.e.* ulPtrsFlag = 0, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_196\_replaced([11513783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), ST FOE is used for PNC in the similar way as when transform precoding is disabled as described in section 2.3.9.5.1 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));\*\*

Parameter *pneRbThreshold used in the algorithm is an array of 32 elements whose values depend on the* ulSubcellType in *UlCell\_SetupReq message (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_152\_replaced([11513449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRwhwfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the* numOfLayers parameter from *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_198\_replaced([11513795](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_vvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as below:*\*\*\*\*

- for 256QAM table: 5G\_L1\_13330\_replaced([11401267](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9CfBvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- for 64QAM table: **[CB008246-N] 5G\_L1\_13320\_replaced([11401255](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9CfBPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** [End CB008246-N]\*\*\*\*

***\*[CB008246-N]-* \*for 64QAMLowSE table:** 5G\_L1\_13321\_replaced([11401261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9CfBfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) **[End CB008246-N]*****\****\*

Note: the ST CFO estimate (or ST FOE) is computed at 5G-L1-UL by using multiple DMRS in PUSCH **[Before CB008792-A]scheduled by RAR UL grant, as stated in 5G\_L1\_7337\_replaced([11401132](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9BQ7PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**[End CB008792-A].\*\*\*\*

Note: the derivation of *foeValid value at 5G-L2-PS is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3333\_replaced([11151157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

**[CB010575-D] This requirement also applies to Thor for FR1-TDD-CPRI.** [End CB010575-D]\*\*\*\*

**[CB011178-A] 5G-L1-UL shall also support requirements above for FR1 FDD 7-2a eCPRI for Thor.** [End CB011178-A]\*\*\*\*

###### 5.4.5.13.2.1.5 Odin

###### 5.4.5.13.2.1.5-1 Odin

**5.4.5.13.2.1.5.0-1**  (ID: `12720609`)

Odin
For Odin:

5G-L1-UL shall select Phase Noise Compensation (PNC) algorithm according to the number of allocated PRBs, the MCS index, and the validity of CFO estimate.

Either FO (long term frequency offset) estimate (received from 5G-L2-PS), ST-FO (short-term frequency offset) estimate, or PN (phase noise) estimate shall be selected to perform PNC on PUSCH.

The information needed for the selection (i.e. MCS index, the number of allocated PRBs and CFO validity) comes within the *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message sent by 5G-L2-PS.*

For PUSCH scheduled by RAR UL grant, no PTRS is available (*ulPtrsFlag ), but multiple DM-RS are sent (*ulDmrsAddPos ). In this case, PNC shall be processed with ST-FO. The needed parameters are provided by the *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message sent from 5G-L2-PS.*\*\*

5G-L1-UL shall choose the appropriate estimate according the algorithm described in "2.3.9 Phase Noise Estimation and Compensation" of the  5GMax algorithm specifications.

5G-L1-UL shall support PNE and PNC as described in "2.3.9 Phase Noise Estimation and Compensation" of the 5GMax algorithm specifications when transform precoding is enabled**.**

* When transform precoding is enabled (i.e. *puschTransformPrecoderFlag = 1, see [11513778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))and UL PTRS is activated (i.e.* ulPtrsFlag = 1, see [11513783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))\*\*
* 5G-L1-UL shall compute PNE after IDFT according to the algorithm described in section 2.3.9.5.2.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications;
* 5G-L1-UL shall perform PNC after IDFT as given in section 2.3.9.5.2.3 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications ([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));
* When transform precoding is enabled (i.e. *puschTransformPrecoderFlag = 1, see [11513778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and UL PTRS is disabled (i.e.* ulPtrsFlag = 0, see [11513783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), ST FOE is used for PNC in the similar way as when transform precoding is disabled as described in section 2.3.9.5.1 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications.\*\*

Parameter *pneRbThreshold used in the algorithm is an array of 32 elements whose values depend on the* ulSubcellType in *UlCell\_SetupReq message (see [11513449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRwhwfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the* numOfLayers parameter from *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message (see [11513795](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_vvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) as below:*\*\*\*\*

* for 256QAM table: [12733672: PNE Thresholds for FR1, 256QAM](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_CElLUHYUEfCdZt6bzaG3fw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)
* for 64QAM table:[12733671: PNE Thresholds for FR1, 64QAM](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_54pEoHYTEfCdZt6bzaG3fw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)
* for 64QAMLowSE table: [12733673: PNE Thresholds for FR1, 64QAMLowSE](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_EZLrMHYUEfCdZt6bzaG3fw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

Logic:

If *foeValid == TRUE*

if (*numOfPrb > pneRbThreshold [*mcs]) && (*ulPtrsFlag == 1)*\*\*

process PNE

process PNC with PNE

Else

skip PNE and PNC

Endif

Else

if *ulPtrsFlag == 0*

if (numOfDmrsSymbols >= 2)

Process PNC with antenna-wise ST FOE (shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt) based on multiple DMRS

Else

skip PNE and PNC

Endif

Else

process PNE

process PNC with PNE

Endif

Endif

Note: the ST CFO estimate (or ST FOE) is computed at 5G-L1-UL by using multiple DMRS in PUSCH.

Note: the derivation of *foeValid value at 5G-L2-PS is described in 5G UP ALG [11151157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).*

Selection Logic for FR1
In FR1, PNE is selected with following logic:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process with decision points and actions. The flowchart begins with a start node and a decision point labeled "fee Valid false and 2DMRS". From this point, there are two branches: "true" and "false".
The "false" branch leads to a decision point labeled "foeValid". If "foeValid" is "true", the process proceeds to a decision point labeled "paired UE". If "foeValid" is "false", the process branches to a decision point labeled "#OfDMRS".
Following the "true" branch from "foeValid" leads to the "paired UE" decision point. If "paired UE" is "true", the process continues to a decision point labeled "#OfDMRS". If "paired UE" is "false", the process also leads to the "#OfDMRS" decision point.
The "#OfDMRS" decision point has two conditions: "x1" and "1".
If "#OfDMRS" is "x1", the process leads to a decision point labeled "PTRS and pneThreshold exceeded".
If "#OfDMRS" is "1", the process leads to a decision point labeled "PTRS".
The "PTRS" decision point has two branches: "true" and "false".
If "PTRS" is "true", the process leads to an action labeled "PTRS based PNE".
If "PTRS" is "false", the process leads to an action labeled "No PNE".
The "PTRS and pneThreshold exceeded" decision point also has two branches: "true" and "false".
If "PTRS and pneThreshold exceeded" is "true", the process leads to an action labeled "PTRS based PNE".
If "PTRS and pneThreshold exceeded" is "false", the process leads to an action labeled "No PNE".
There is also a path from the "foeValid" decision point, when it is "true", that leads to a decision point labeled "#OfDMRS". This "#OfDMRS" decision point has conditions "x1" and "1".
If "#OfDMRS" is "x1", it leads to a decision point labeled "PTRS and pneThreshold exceeded".
If "#OfDMRS" is "1", it leads to a decision point labeled "PTRS".
The "No PNE" action from the "PTRS" decision point and the "No PNE" action from the "PTRS and pneThreshold exceeded" decision point merge into a join node.
The "PTRS based PNE" action from the "PTRS" decision point and the "PTRS based PNE" action from the "PTRS and pneThreshold exceeded" decision point also merge into a join node.
There is another path from the initial "fee Valid false and 2DMRS" decision point, when it is "true", that leads to a decision point labeled "foeValid". If "foeValid" is "true", it leads to "paired UE". If "foeValid" is "false", it leads to "#OfDMRS".
The flowchart also shows a path from the "paired UE" decision point, when it is "true", leading to "#OfDMRS".
The flowchart includes several final action nodes: "DMRS based PNE", "PTRS based PNE", "No PNE", "LT FO based PNE", and "ST FO based PNE".
There is a note attached to the "ST FO based PNE" action, stating "calculated from DMRS or DMRS&PTRS".
The flowchart concludes with a final end node.
The text extracted from the image is:
fee Valid false
and 2DMRS
foeValid
true
false
paired UE
true
false

# OfDMRS

x1
1
PTRS
true
false
PTRS based PNE
No PNE

PNE

No PNE

PNE

DMRS or
DMRS&PTRS[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_927.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

11401505: Selection Logic for FR2 11401505: Selection Logic for FR2 In FR2, PNE is selected with following logic: [image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a decision-making process. The flowchart begins with a starting point, represented by a filled circle. From the start, there is a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "foe Valid". This node has two outgoing paths: "False" and "Time". The "False" path from "foe Valid" leads to another diamond-shaped decision node labeled "PTRS". This node also has two outgoing paths: "False" and "Time". The "False" path from "PTRS" leads to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "#OfDMR". This node has two outgoing paths, labeled "1" and "x1". The path labeled "1" leads to a rounded rectangle activity labeled "No PNE". The path labeled "x1" leads to a rounded rectangle activity labeled "DMRS based PNE". The "Time" path from "PTRS" leads to a rounded rectangle activity labeled "PTRS based PNE". The "Time" path from "foe Valid" leads to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "PTRS and pne Threshold exceeded". This node has two outgoing paths: "False" and "Time". The "False" path from "PTRS and pne Threshold exceeded" leads to a rounded rectangle activity labeled "No PNE". The "Time" path from "PTRS and pne Threshold exceeded" leads to a rounded rectangle activity labeled "PTRS based PNE". All the paths eventually converge to a final merging point, which then leads to an ending point, represented by a filled circle within a larger circle. The flowchart uses standard flowchart symbols: circles for start/end points, diamonds for decisions, and rounded rectangles for activities. The arrows indicate the flow of control.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_929.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] (ID: 11401505)

##### 5.4.5.13.2.2 PNE Thresholds

###### 5.4.5.13.2.2.0-1 PNE Thresholds 5G-L1-UL shall support dedicated PNE RB threshold tables for PUSCH transmissions configured with Low SE MCS Table and disabled Transform Precoding, as defined in 5GMax 5G New Radio Algorithm Specification v.11.057, chapter 2.3.9. The threshold tables are implemented as R&amp;D parameters defined in chapter 5G_L1_6345_replaced( 11407203 ). [CB012255-A] This requiremnt is also applicable for Loki FR1-FDD-eCPRI 7.2a [End CB012255-A] (ID: 11401802)

###### 5.4.5.13.2.2.1 PNE Thresholds (Loner)

###### 5.4.5.13.2.2.1.1 Overview

**5.4.5.13.2.2.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `11401230`)

Overview
pneRbThresholds values are defined by following R&D parameters:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ulSubcellType | numOfLayers | pneRbThreshold |  | Feature |
| Transform Precoding disabled | Transform Precoding enabled |  |  |  |
| D2 | 1 | rdPneRbThresholdHB\_2RxSingleLayer in 5G\_L1\_3381\_replaced([11407209](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9hnNPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | rdPneRbThresholdHB\_TpEnabled\_2RxSingleLayer in 5G\_RAN\_UP\_28917 |  |
| 2 | rdPneRbThresholdHB\_2RxTwoLayer in 5G\_L1\_3382\_replaced([11407218](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9hnNfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |  |  |  |
| C2, A2 | 1 | rdPneRbThresholdLB\_2RxSingleLayer in 5G\_L1\_3383\_replaced([11407226](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9hnNvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | rdPneRbThresholdLB\_TpEnabled\_2RxSingleLayer in 5G\_RAN\_UP\_28919 |  |
| 2 | rdPneRbThresholdLB\_2RxTwoLayer in 5G\_L1\_3384\_replaced([11407241](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9hnOPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |  |  |  |
| C4, A4 | 1 | rdPneRbThresholdLB\_4RxSingleLayer in 5G\_L1\_3385\_replaced([11407256](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9hnOvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | rdPneRbThresholdLB\_TpEnabled\_4RxSingleLayer in 5G\_RAN\_UP\_28921 |  |
| 2 | rdPneRbThresholdLB\_4RxTwoLayer in 5G\_L1\_3386\_replaced([11407272](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9hnPPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |  |  |  |

###### 5.4.5.13.2.2.1.2 PNE Thresholds for FR1, 64QAM

**5.4.5.13.2.2.1.2.0-1**  (ID: `11401235`)

PNE Thresholds for FR1, 64QAM
For Loner and Loki, FR1 TDD and FDD, when PUSCH transmission is configured with 64QAM table (mcsTable = max64QAM in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq), pneRbThresholds values are defined by following R&D parameters:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ulSubcellType | numOfLayers | pneRbThreshold |  |
| Transform Precoding disabled | Transform Precoding enabled |  |  |
| C2, A2 | 1 | [CB008246-K] rdPneRbThresholdLB\_2RxSingleLayer\_bler0point1 in 5G\_L1\_11776\_replaced([11407288](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9hnPvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))   rdPneRbThresholdLB\_2RxSingleLayer\_bler0point01 in 5G\_L1\_11785\_replaced([11407314](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9iOQvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB008246-K] | rdPneRbThresholdLB\_TpEnabled\_2RxSingleLayer in 5G\_L1\_3388\_replaced([11407341](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9iOR_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| 2 | [CB008246-K] rdPneRbThresholdLB\_2RxTwoLayer\_bler0point1 in 5G\_L1\_11777\_replaced([11407295](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9hnP_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))   rdPneRbThresholdLB\_2RxTwoLayer\_bler0point01 in 5G\_L1\_11786\_replaced([11407321](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9iOQ_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB008246-K] | N/A |  |
| C4, A4 | 1 | [CB008246-I] rdPneRbThresholdLB\_4RxSingleLayer\_bler0point1 in 5G\_L1\_11778\_replaced([11407302](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9iOQPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))   rdPneRbThresholdLB\_4RxSingleLayer\_bler0point01 in 5G\_L1\_11787\_replaced([11407328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9iORPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB008246-I] | rdPneRbThresholdLB\_TpEnabled\_4RxSingleLayer in 5G\_L1\_3389\_replaced([11407355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9iOSvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| 2 | [CB008246-I] rdPneRbThresholdLB\_4RxTwoLayer\_bler0point1 in 5G\_L1\_11779\_replaced([11407307](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9iOQfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))   rdPneRbThresholdLB\_4RxTwoLayer\_bler0point01 in 5G\_L1\_11788\_replaced([11407334](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9iORfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB008246-I] | N/A |  |

Note:

* for UlData\_PuschReceiveReq->blerTarget = blerTarget\_0\_point\_01 (1%) use R&D params ending with \*\_bler0point01 from the table above.
* for UlData\_PuschReceiveReq->blerTarget = blerTarget\_0\_point\_0 (10%) use R&D params ending with \*\_bler0point1 from the table above.

[CB012255-A] This requirement is also applicable for Loki FR1-FDD-eCPRI 7.2a [End CB012255-A]

---

###### 5.4.5.13.2.2.2 PNE Threshold (Thor, 8Rx IRC)

**5.4.5.13.2.2.2.0-2**  (ID: `12687124`)

PNE Threshold (Thor, 8Rx IRC)
5G-L1-UL shall use the following pneRbThreshold tables of 5GMax Algorithm Specification to determine the PNE threshold for an RB:

* the columns "256QAM", "64QAM", and "64QAM LowSE", from table [#tab-PneRBThresEightRxRankOne](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#tab-PneRBThresEightRxRankOne), "pneRbThreshold of 8RX, Rank 1"**,**

**- the columns "256QAM", "64QAM", and "64QAM LowSE", from table [#tab-PneRBThresEightRxRankTwo](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#tab-PneRBThresEightRxRankTwo), "pneRbThreshold of 8RX, Rank 2".**

[CB013442-D] The thresholds shall be used when 8Rx IRC receiver is implemented, for all supported cases of "Technology". [End CB013442-D]

See 5G Max algorithm document chapter [#sec-PuschImpSteForPnePnc](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschImpSteForPnePnc) for the details.

---

###### 5.4.5.13.2.2.3 PNE Thresholds (Odin)

**5.4.5.13.2.2.3.0-1**  (ID: `12733670`)

PNE Thresholds (Odin)
The parameter to control Resource Block Threshold for Phase Noise Estimation

References:

* 5GMax Specification, tables in section [#sec-PuschImpSteForPnePnc](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschImpSteForPnePnc),
* 5GMax Code, [/5gmax/source/ParameterSettings/NR5G/matlab/NrParamDeriveUlRxPne\_f.m](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/5gmax/source/ParameterSettings/NR5G/matlab/NrParamDeriveUlRxPne_f.m?r=e20a7d62)
* L1 SW Code, [/l1sw/app/common/nr/ul/PneRbThresholdConst.hpp](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/l1sw/app/common/nr/ul/PneRbThresholdConst.hpp?r=13a20ffd)

The choice of threshold depends on:

* Frequency Range: FR1 or FR2 (possibly FR3 in the future),
* waveform: CP-OFDM, DFT-s-OFDM (Transform Precoding),
* mcsTable (max 64-QAM, max 256-QAM, and Low Spectral Efficiency 64-QAM),
* number of streams (2, 4, or 8; determined according to subcell type),
* maximum rank, (1 or 2; determined according to antPort),
* blerTarget (0.1 or 0.01),
* and mcs (0..31, depending on the supported range)

The requirements should specify the R&D parameters for 5G-L1-UL according to the following table.

L1 Spec should keep the table up-to-date, according to 5GMax ongoing investigations.

---

12733662:

12733662:
DMRS based PNE
5G-L1-UL(BB) shall perform DMRS based PNE as described in chapter 2.3.9.1 for CP-OFDM and 2.3.9.5.1 in [5gmax algo spec]. Algorithm is the same in both case.

Input for DMRS based PNE is DMRS FOE (C\_ST) which is calculated earlier. It is first changed to frequency offset (with formula 2-73), which is then used to calculate PN for every symbol and antenna (if antenna based FOEs are present) as presented in the below formula.

Red rounded parameter below is frequency offset and symbol number is i.

Based on the formula, PN increases for every symbol.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$PN\_{i,1,ant} = 2\pi (N\_{0,i}^{gap} - N\_{0,5}^{gap}*{DMRS}) \cdot T\_s \cdot \delta\hat{f} = 2\pi \cdot N*{S}^{gap}\_{DMRS\_i} \cdot T\_s \cdot \delta\hat{f}$
The equation is numbered (2-219) on the right side. The term $\delta\hat{f}$ is circled in red.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_931.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

The requirement is supported in Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

##### 5.4.5.13.2.4 PTRS based PNE

**5.4.5.13.2.4.0-2**  (ID: `12733663`)

PTRS based PNE
5G-L1-UL(BB) shall perform the 5 steps of PTRS based PNE as defined in chapter 2.3.9.1 for CP-OFDM and 2.3.9.5.2.1 for DFS-s-OFDM in [5gmax algo spec].

Algorithms are slightly different as in DFT-s-OFDM case processing is done after IDFT which is not done in CP-OFDM case.

Input for the PTRS based PNE is PTRS sequence what UE has transmitted (known by 3GPP standard), received PTRS signal, and in CP-OFDM case also PTRS channel estimates which are calculated during channel estimation.

Result is phase noise for every symbol i, layer l and antenna ant.

Phase noise is also calculated for DMRS symbol(s).

Phase noise is also calculated for symbols which does not have PTRS, this is done by interpolation.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

The requirement is supported in Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

##### 5.4.5.13.2.5 ST FOE based PNE

###### 5.4.5.13.2.5.0-1 ST FOE based PNE This is same as DMRS based PNE, see chapter 2.3.9.5.1.1 in [5gmax algo spec]. (see 5G_L1_10583_replaced( 11401484 ) when this is used) (ID: 11401540)

##### 5.4.5.13.2.6 LT FOE based PNE

###### 5.4.5.13.2.6.0-1 LT FOE based PNE This is same as DMRS based PNE, see chapter 2.3.9.5.1.1 in [5gmax algo spec]. (see 5G_L1_10583_replaced( 11401484 ) when this is used) (ID: 11401549)

11401312:
Frequency Offset and Phase Noise estimation (FOE/PNE) overview
As an example, below picture presents one PRB, which has 12 subcarriers, 14 symbols and DMRS is in symbols 2 and 11. Data is not multiplexed into DMRS symbols.

Picture shows what resource elements are used to calculate FOE and PNE.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid representing a communication signal structure, likely within a wireless communication system like 5G NR. The grid has rows labeled from 0 to 10 and columns labeled from 0 to 13.
The grid is divided into several sections, indicated by different colors and labels:
\* **Green Sections:** These represent data symbols.
\* **Yellow Rectangles:** Labeled "DMRS", these indicate Demodulation Reference Signals. They are strategically placed within the grid.
\* **Blue Rectangles:** Labeled "PTRS", these indicate Phase Tracking Reference Signals. They appear in the bottom row of the grid.
Several annotations and arrows explain the relationships and calculations within this structure:
\* **"DMRS FOE, rotation between DMRS symbols"**: A long red arrow spans horizontally across the grid, connecting two sets of DMRS symbols located at column 1 and column 10. This suggests a calculation or measurement of frequency offset (FOE) based on the phase difference between these DMRS symbols.
\* **"PTRS FOE, rotation between symbols at foeGap distance"**: A series of red curved arrows are shown in the lower part of the grid, originating from PTRS symbols in columns 0, 1, and 2, and extending towards other PTRS symbols. This indicates a calculation of frequency offset (FOE) for PTRS symbols, possibly considering a specific "foeGap distance".
\* **"PTRS PNE, calculated for each symbol"**: A yellow text box with red arrows pointing upwards from the PTRS symbols in the bottom row. This signifies that PTRS Phase Noise Estimation (PNE) is calculated for each individual PTRS symbol.
\* **"DMRS PNE, calculated from DMRS FOE, and interpolated to every data symbols"**: Another yellow text box with a large red arrow pointing upwards from the DMRS symbol at column 11. This explains that DMRS PNE is derived from the DMRS FOE (calculated earlier) and then interpolated to estimate the phase noise for all the data symbols (green sections).
The columns are numbered from 0 to 13, and the rows are numbered from 0 to 10. The PTRS symbols are located in the bottom row (row 0), and DMRS symbols are placed in specific columns and rows within the grid. The overall diagram illustrates a method for estimating and compensating for phase noise in a communication signal using reference signals.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_906.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The difference between frequency offset (FO) and phase noise (PN) in general is that FO is one value and it causes fixed phase difference between symbols, and phase noise is symbol based value and phases are not fixed between symbols.

Parameters
UlCell\_SetupReq and PuschReceiveReq message contains these parameters which are related to FOE/PNE/FOC/PNC selection:

ULCell\_SetupReq:

-ulSubcellType, whether cell is FR1 or FR2

-actHighSpeedCell

-actUlMuMimo, defines if UEs can be paired into same Prbs

In UlData interface messages are following:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a UML sequence diagram illustrating a communication between two entities labeled "L1" and "L2".
The diagram shows two vertical lifelines, one for L1 on the left and one for L2 on the right.
There are two messages exchanged between L1 and L2:
1. **Message 1:** A solid arrow originates from L1 and points to L2. The message is labeled "1: HwdiReceiveReq (DT: CH0)". This indicates a request message being sent from L1 to L2.
2. **Message 2:** A dashed arrow originates from L2 and points back to L1. The message is labeled "2: HwdiReceiveAccept (DT: CH0)". This indicates an acceptance message being sent from L2 back to L1, likely in response to the first message.
The diagram depicts a synchronous communication pattern where L1 sends a request and waits for an acceptance from L2.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_908.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

PuschReceiveReq:

-foeValid, defines whether longTermCfoMetric and longTermCfoMetricOfAnt contain valid value

-ulDmrsAddPos, defines if UE sends additional DMRS, number of DMRS symbols is 1+ulDmrsAddPos

-ulPtrsFlag, defines whether UE has PTRS

-puschTransformPrecoderFlag, defines if UE has DFT-S-OFDM

-pairedUe, defined is UE is paired with another UE in same PRBs

-longTermCfoMetric

-longTermCfoMetricOfAnt

PuschReceiveRespPs:

-shortTermCfoMetric

-shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt

rdParameters:

rdFocEnabledFlag

[CB008792, 22R3]: rdUlCfoEstimationHstMethod, defines if PTRS+DMRS or PTRS based FOE is selected.

Hardcoded parameters:

pneRbThreshold, this parameters defines how many PRBs UE must transmit that PNE can be calculated in some cases from PTRS. There are multiple pneThresholds; depending on mcs, modulation, etc criteria. These are defined in chapter 2.3.9 in [5gmax algo doc] and 5G\_L1\_6345\_replaced([11407203](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9hnM_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

FocMethod, FdFocOpt, these relates how FD-FOC coefficients are pre-calculated for intra-symbol FOC, see 2.3.3.6 in [5gmax algo doc].

---

Note that it does not contain UL MU-MIMO cases, because that would double the amount of rows needed and cases are simple:

-Paired UEs have CP-OFDM

-Paired UEs will have LT CFO in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message and foeValid==True

-Paired UEs have rather small frequency offset, because that is one criteria to select UEs for pairing

-FOC is not done, instead,

-PNC is done in FDEQ/ARXE based on LT CFO. [5gmax algo doc] uses term Inter-Symbol FOC for that case, see chapter 2.3.3.5.

-Paired UEs does not have PTRS (addition 17.11.2022)

-in Thor (CB009028-5G-A), Intra-FOC is done in DMAP based on LT CFO (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14838\_replaced([11102627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knlGIfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) (addition 15.3.2023)

Following activity diagram shows the logic to do FOC/PNC in FOC MDAB, FDEQ or DMAP:

Note that short "PTRS" decision means that "PTRS can be used" and pneRbThreashold is taken into account

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart depicting a process with decision points and actions. The flowchart starts with a circular node at the top, indicating the start of the process.
From the start node, the flow goes to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "pairedUE ?".
- If the answer is "true", the flow proceeds to another diamond-shaped decision node labeled "foeValid ?".
- If the answer is "false", the flow proceeds to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "foeValid ?".
From the "foeValid ?" node (reached from "pairedUE ?"),
- If the answer is "true", the flow proceeds to a rounded rectangle action node labeled "process FOC in FOC MDAB".
- If the answer is "false", the flow proceeds to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "PTRS or >1DMRS ?".
From the "process FOC in FOC MDAB" node, the flow goes to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "PNC needed?".
- If the answer is "true", the flow proceeds to a rounded rectangle action node labeled "process PNC in FDEQ".
- If the answer is "false", the flow proceeds to a diamond-shaped merge node.
From the "PTRS or >1DMRS ?" node (reached from the second "foeValid ?"),
- If the answer is "true", the flow proceeds to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "DFT-s".
- If the answer is "false", the flow proceeds to a diamond-shaped merge node.
From the "DFT-s" node,
- If the answer is "true", the flow proceeds to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "1DMRS and PTRS".
- If the answer is "false", the flow proceeds to a diamond-shaped merge node.
From the "1DMRS and PTRS" node,
- If the answer is "true", the flow proceeds to a rounded rectangle action node labeled "process PNC in DMAP".
- If the answer is "false", the flow proceeds to a diamond-shaped merge node.
The "process PNC in FDEQ" node leads to a diamond-shaped merge node.
The "process PNC in DMAP" node leads to a diamond-shaped merge node.
There are three diamond-shaped merge nodes in the flowchart. Each of these merge nodes leads to the final circular node at the bottom, indicating the end of the process.
The text extracted from the image is:
foeValid?
pairedUE
?
foeValid
?
PTRS or

> 1DMRS?
> process FOC in
> FOC MDAB
> DFT-s
> PNC
> needed?
> 1DMRS and
> PTRS
> process PNC in
> FDEQ
> process PNC in
> DMAP[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_949.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[11402834: Intra-symbol FOC on DMRS symbols (Frontend)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9Ka1_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

[11401687: Intra-symbol FOC on data symbols (DMAP)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9E7S_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

[11402959: Inter-symbol FOC](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9Lo8_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

Following activity diagram shows the logic to do FOC/PNC in ARXE or DMAP:

Note that short "PTRS" decision means that "PTRS can be used" and pneRbThreashold is taken into account.

"PNC needed?=True" in DFT-S=False branch is the case when UE has 1 DMRS and PTRS. In that case LT FO is not used in ARXE, instead PNE (ST FO) which is calculated from PTRS.

"PNC needed?=True" in DFT-S=True branch is the case when UE has 1 DMRS and PTRS. In that case LT FO is used in ARXE and DMAP, and PTRS based PNE in DMAP.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart depicting a process. The flowchart starts with a circular node labeled "DFT-s". From this node, there are two outgoing paths, labeled "True" and "False".
The "True" path leads to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "foeValid?". This node also has two outgoing paths, "True" and "False".
The "False" path from "DFT-s" leads to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "RNC needed?". This node also has two outgoing paths, "True" and "False".
Let's trace the paths:
**Path 1 (from "DFT-s" -> "True" -> "foeValid?" -> "True"):**
This path leads to a process node labeled "process InterFOC in ARXE". From here, it goes to another process node labeled "process IntraFOC in DMAP". This node then leads to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "RNC needed?".
\* If "RNC needed?" is "True", it goes to a process node labeled "process PNC in DMAP".
\* If "RNC needed?" is "False", it goes to a merge node.
**Path 2 (from "DFT-s" -> "True" -> "foeValid?" -> "False"):**
This path leads to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled ">1DMRS or PTRS". This node has two outgoing paths, "True" and "False".
\* If ">1DMRS or PTRS" is "True", it leads to a process node labeled "process PNC in ARXE". This node then goes to a merge node.
\* If ">1DMRS or PTRS" is "False", it leads to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "PTRS". This node has two outgoing paths, "True" and "False".
\* If "PTRS" is "True", it leads to a process node labeled "process PNC in DMAP". This node then goes to a merge node.
\* If "PTRS" is "False", it leads to a merge node.
**Path 3 (from "DFT-s" -> "False" -> "RNC needed?" -> "True"):**
This path leads to a process node labeled "process interFOC in ARXE". From here, it goes to a process node labeled "process PNC in ARXE". This node then goes to a merge node.
**Path 4 (from "DFT-s" -> "False" -> "RNC needed?" -> "False"):**
This path leads to a process node labeled "process PNC in ARXE". This node then goes to a merge node.
There are several merge nodes in the flowchart, indicated by diamond shapes with multiple incoming arrows converging into one outgoing arrow. These merge nodes eventually lead to a final merge node, which then connects to a circular end node.
The text extracted from the image is:
"DFT-s"
"foeValid?"
"True"
"False"
"RNC needed?"
"process InterFOC in ARXE"
"process IntraFOC in DMAP"
"process PNC in DMAP"
">1DMRS or PTRS"
"process PNC in ARXE"
"PTRS"
"process PNC in DMAP"
"process interFOC in ARXE"
"process PNC in ARXE"[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_957.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
