##### 5.4.5.14.1.3 Frozen Requirements

**5.4.5.14.1.3.0-4**  (ID: `12523650`)

Frozen Requirements
The content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 27R1. For the latest requirements refer to the following:

[11402834: Intra-symbol FOC on DMRS symbols (Frontend)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9Ka1_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

[11401687: Intra-symbol FOC on data symbols (DMAP)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9E7S_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

[11402959: Inter-symbol FOC](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9Lo8_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

FOC (Frequency Offset Correction) is done by using longTermCfo (LT CFO) which is received in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message when foeValid==True.

If LT CFO is present, there two different kind of FOCs;  intra-symbol FOC and inter-symbol FOC.

FOC algorithm is defined in chapter 2.3.3.6.  [5gmax algo spec]. Algoritm is same for  for CP-OFDM and DFT-S-OFDM.

Intra-Symbol FOC makes correction per symbol, and each subcarrier has different correction. This is realised by using 5 or 11 tap filter. Loki non-HST and Thor non-HST cell uses 5 taps in FR1, and Loki HST and Thor HST cell **uses 11 taps in FR1. Loki non-HST cell and Thor non-HST cell uses 5 taps in FR2.**

Inter-Symbol FOC is adjustment per symbol, and each subcarrier has same correction, but symbols have different value.

Corrections are per antenna when LT CFO contains value for each antenna.

In Loki, when UE is paired (CP-OFDM UE), intra-Symbol FOC is skipped, only inter-Symbol FOC is done, and that is done in different phase as normally, see the processing logic diagram in 5G\_L1\_10414\_replaced([11401606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9EUPPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In Thor, with when UE is paired (CB009028), Thor will follow Loki approach.

[CB014106-SR-A] In 7-2e split, when the cell is HST and SU receiver is used, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall perform intra-symbol FOC for data symbols using 11-tap filter, as specified in section [#sec-PuschFreqOffComUpdVer](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschFreqOffComUpdVer) of 5GMax Algorithm Specification. [End CB014106-SR-A]

Note: If MU receiver is used,

- Neither inter- nor intra-symbol FOC on DMRS symbols are required.

- The inter-symbol FOC on data symbols is done inside FDEQ in Loki, and ARXE in Thor.

- In 7-2e, the inter-symbol FOC on data symbols is performed by 5G-L1-UL (Radio).

- The intra-symbol FOC on post-equalized data symbols is done by DMAP in Thor. It is not done in Loki due to DSP capacity shortage.

---

#### 5.4.5.14.2 Inter-symbol FOC

**5.4.5.14.2.0-2**  (ID: `12727430`)

Inter-symbol FOC
Inter-symbol FOC

5G-L1-UL (BB), Loki, Thor, and [CB012115-IA] Odin [End CB012115-IA], shall perform Inter-symbol Frequency Offset Compensation, after time domain interpolation (and before equalization/PNC) in single user receiver (including when in MU-MIMO, the UE is not paired), also when the long term frequency offset (longTermCFOmetric) received from scheduler ([11513756: UlData\_PuschReceiveReq](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is valid (foeValid = True).

The inter-symbol FOC on data symbols is done by FDEQ in Loki, and ARXE in Thor and [CB012115-IA] Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

FO is configured with Phase as the input for equalizer.

HST (High Speed) vs. Non-HST:

5G-UL-L1 shall support 5-tap and 11-tap filters, as specified in section [#sec-PuschFreqOffComUpdVer](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschFreqOffComUpdVer) of 5GMax Algorithm Specification;

* 5G-L1-UL (BB) in non-HST (not High Speed Train/User) scenario shall use 5-tap filters. (in FR1 and FR2 if supported)
* 5G-L1-UL (BB) in HST scenario shall use 11-tap filters. (in FR1)

CP-OFDM and DFT-s-OFDM:

The algorithm is identical for CP-OFDM and DFT-s-OFDM (Transform Precoding disabled or enabled).

DMRS symbols:

The inter-symbol FOC on DMRS symbols is not required.

The requirement is supported in Loki and Thor.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported with CP-OFDM and non-HST in Odin L1 Call. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.5.14.3 Intra-symbol FOC on data symbols (DMAP)

**5.4.5.14.3.0-2**  (ID: `12727431`)

Intra-symbol FOC on data symbols (DMAP)
Intra-symbol FOC on data symbols (Post-equalization)

5G-L1-UL (BB), Loki, Thor, and [CB012115-IA] Odin [End CB012115-IA] shall perform Intra-symbol Frequency Offset Compensation on the post-equalization data symbols in the in single user receiver (including when in MU-MIMO, the UE is not paired), also when the long term frequency offset (longTermCFOmetric) received from scheduler ([11513756: UlData\_PuschReceiveReq](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is valid (foeValid = True). Intra-symbol FOC makes corrections per symbol. At least one available longTermCFOmetricOfBeam is enough to perform intra-symbol FOC.

The intra-symbol FOC on post-equalized data symbols is done by DMAP in Thor and [CB012115-IA] Odin [End CB012115-IA]. It is not done in Loki due to DSP capacity shortage.

HST (High Speed) vs. Non-HST:

DMAP supports 11-tap Intra-symbol FOC filters. It shall be configured so that either 5 or 11 tap filters are effectively used, as specified in section [#sec-PuschFreqOffComUpdVer](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschFreqOffComUpdVer) of 5GMax Algorithm Specification:

* 5G-L1-UL (BB) in non-HST (not High Speed Train/User) scenario shall use 5-tap filters. (in FR1 and FR2 if supported)
* 5G-L1-UL (BB) in HST scenario shall use 11-tap filters. (in FR1)

CP-OFDM and DTF-s-OFDM:

The algorithm is identical for CP-OFDM and DFT-s-OFDM (Transform Precoding disabled or enabled).

MU-MIMO Receiver:

The intra-symbol FOC on post-equalized data symbols is done by DMAP in Thor. It is not done in Loki due to DSP capacity shortage.

7-2e split:

5G-L1-UL shall meet the requirement also in 7-2e split. (CB014106)

FOC Optimization:

Refer to [11401568: FOC Optimization](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9EUNfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

The requirement is supported in Loki and Thor.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported with CP-OFDM and non-HST, without FOC Optimization, in Odin L1 Call. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.5.14.4 FOC Optimization

**5.4.5.14.4.0-2**  (ID: `12523670`)

FOC Optimization
Intra-symbol FOC optimization method shall be determined based on L1 Variant and actHighSpeedCell in the UlCell\_SetupReq message (refer to 5G\_L1\_IF\_152\_replaced([11513449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRwhwfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) as follows.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | actHighSpeedCell |  |
| False | True |  |  |
| Loki |  | 'Off' | 'On' |
| Thor | DMRS Symbol | 'Real' | 'On' |
| Data Symbol | 'On' | 'On' |  |

The details of the optimization methods, {'Off', 'Real', 'On'}, are described in [#sec-PuschFreqOffComUpdVer](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschFreqOffComUpdVer) chapter of "5GMax – 5G New Radio Algorithm Specification".

[CB014106-SR-A] In 7-2e split, when HST is enabled, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support this requirement for data symbols in DMAP. [End CB014106-SR-A]

Note: In 7-2e split, FOC is done by 5G-L1-UL (Radio) for DMRS symbols.

---

#### 5.4.5.14.5 Phase Noise Correction (PNC)

**5.4.5.14.5.0-1**  (ID: `11401582`)

Phase Noise Correction (PNC)
PNC is done based on the estimated PNE.

PNC algorithm is defined in chapter 2.3.9.2. for CP-OFDM and is chapter 2.3.9.5.1.2 and 2.3.9.5.2.2 for DFT-S-OFDM in  [5gmax algo spec].

Algoritm is same for CP-OFDM and DFT-S-OFDM when PNE is calculated from DMRS, but is different when calculated from PTRS, because PNE is available only after IDFT.

---

FDEQ UE Phase configuration
UE phases are configured by setting FDEQ\_RDCFG\_UE\_PHASE8R\_S register because it gives enough bits to configure 8Rx antennas.

Configuration can be different for each symbol and antenna, and this is so called inter-symbol FOC or PNC, depending how UE phases are calculated.

If phases are calculated from PTRS, phases can be different in each symbol with independent phase, and if phases are calculated from frequency offset, then difference between symbols is fixed.

Below is picture from Hardware Reference Manual:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a hierarchical diagram illustrating the "FDEQ\_RDCFG\_UE\_PHASE\_NRXR\_S Parameters". The main component on the left is a large blue rectangle labeled "UE0 phase". This component is connected by a dashed line to a series of pink rectangles on the right, representing different symbol phases.
The pink rectangles are arranged vertically and are labeled as follows:
- "Symbol 0 phase"
- "Symbol 1 phase"
- A vertical ellipsis (:) indicating a continuation of the pattern.
- "Symbol 13 phase"
To the right of the "Symbol 0 phase" and "Symbol 1 phase" rectangles, there are two parameter names:
- "FDEQ\_RDCFG\_UE\_PHASE\_8R\_S"
- "or FDEQ\_RDCFG\_UE\_PHASE\_16R\_S"
These parameter names appear to be associated with the symbol phases, suggesting that each symbol phase can be configured with either the 8R\_S or 16R\_S parameter. The diagram visually represents a breakdown of the "UE0 phase" into individual symbol phases, each with specific configuration parameters.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_952.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

DMAP Phase configuration
In case of DFT-S-OFDM and PTRS based PNE, phase correction (PNC) is done in DMAP.

Correction is done symbol basis, but correction cannot be done antenna bases as antennas are not visible any more after FDEQ. Correction is also done for one layer as DFT-S-OFDM has only one layer.

Below is picture from Hardware Reference Manual:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a section of a document titled "23.1.7.1 Phase Compensation". The text explains that prior to LLR calculation, DMAP multiplies I/Q samples by a complex number to compensate for residual phase. This complex number is represented as `[DMP_COMP_COEFFI] + (i * [DMP_COMP_COEFFQ])`. The text further states that these complex coefficient values are stored in the register `DMAP(0..1)_JD(0..2)_CFG3`. There is also a watermark overlaying parts of the text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_954.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Configuration to ARXE is done by configuring the Phase parameters.

Below is picture from thor HRM (Hardware Reference Manual)

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the inputs and outputs of "Mode 11". The diagram shows a central rectangular box labeled "Mode 11". To the left of this box are several inputs, indicated by arrows pointing towards the box. These inputs are:
\* $\hat{R}*d^{-1}$ (blue arrow)
\* H (blue arrow)
\* $y*{ds}$ (green arrow)
\* Phase (green arrow, circled in red)
Below the inputs, there is a legend indicating that blue arrows represent "PMEM".
To the right of the "Mode 11" box are several outputs, indicated by arrows pointing away from the box. These outputs are:
\* $X\_{IRC}$ (green arrow)
\* $\beta\_{IRC}$ (green arrow)
Below the outputs, there is a legend indicating that green arrows represent "SMEM". The text "Figure 34-2 Mode-11 Inputs and Outputs" is displayed at the top of the image. There is also some faint text in the upper right corner that appears to be "Netw".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_960.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

FOC) for DMAP 11401709: PNC (/Inter-Symbol FOC) for DMAP 11401709: PNC (/Inter-Symbol FOC) for DMAP 11401709: PNC (/Inter-Symbol FOC) for DMAP 11401709: PNC (/Inter-Symbol FOC) for DMAP 11401709: PNC (/Inter-Symbol FOC) for DMAP 11401709: PNC (/Inter-Symbol FOC) for DMAP 11401709: PNC (/Inter-Symbol FOC) for DMAP Below is picture from thor HRM (Hardware Reference Manual) [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a section of a document titled "24.2.9.1 Phase Compensation". The text explains how DMAP multiplies I/Q samples by complex numbers to compensate for residual phase. It lists three specific cases with corresponding conditions and register references. The first case involves [PHASE_REAL_0] + (i * [PHASE_IMAG_0]) when DMAP(0..1)_AB(0..1)_JD(0.3)_CFGO[PROCESSING_TYPE] is 3 and 6. The second case involves [PHASE_REAL_n] + (i * [PHASE_IMAG_n]) for n from 0 to num_layers-1, if DMAP(0..1)_AB(0..1)_JD(0.3)_CFGO[PROCESSING_TYPE] is 6. The third case also involves [PHASE_REAL_n] + (i * [PHASE_IMAG_n]) but for n from 0 to num_sym-1, if DMAP(0..1)_AB(0..1)_JD(0.3)_CFGO[PROCESSING_TYPE] is 3. Finally, it states that these complex coefficient values are contained in the register DMAP(0..1)_AB(0..1)_JD(0.3)_CFG2 to DMAP(0..1)_AB(0..1)_JD(0..3)_CFG6. There are also some faint watermarks on the right side of the image.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_964.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] (ID: 11401709)

##### 5.4.5.14.5.3 Odin

###### 5.4.5.14.5.3.0-1 Odin 5G-L1-UL(BB) shall perform Phase Noise Compensation when the result of PNE is available, by setting the Phase input of ARXE in case of CP-OFDM, or configuring DMAP in case of DFT-s-OFDM. The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side. (ID: 12733665)

### 5.4.5.15 Equalization (Detection)

#### 5.4.5.15.1 Maximum Ratio Combining (MRC) Receiver

##### 5.4.5.15.1.1 2Rx and 4Rx MRC Receiver

###### 5.4.5.15.1.1.0-1 2Rx and 4Rx MRC Receiver MRC can be achieved by proper configuration of the IRC Receiver. Loner does not support IRC, so the support for MRC is kept. Loki still supports 2Rx MRC, but for 4Rx, only IRC is used. Starting Thor, MRC is not supported as a functionality. Please refer to UP Algorithm : PUSCH receiver algorithm supported for SU receiver . (ID: 12576456)

##### 5.4.5.15.1.2 Legacy

###### 5.4.5.15.1.2.0-1 Legacy The content of this requirement is frozen and valid for the mentioned system release. Please refer to the requirements below. In ABIL, L1 shall support 4Rx MRC receiver for PUSCH in FDD FR1 CPRI, and L1 in ABIO before 5GC001294 (4RX IRC). (ID: 11401738)

##### 5.4.5.15.1.3 Loner

###### 5.4.5.15.1.3.1 4Rx MRC Receiver, FDD FR1 CPRI

###### 5.4.5.15.1.3.1.0-1 4Rx MRC Receiver, FDD FR1 CPRI 5G-L1-UL shall support 4Rx MRC receiver in Loner for PUSCH in FDD FR1 CPRI. (ID: 12576408)

###### 5.4.5.15.1.3.2 4Rx MRC Receiver, TDD FR1 CPRI, non-BF

###### 5.4.5.15.1.3.2.0-1 4Rx MRC Receiver, TDD FR1 CPRI, non-BF L1 shall support 4Rx MRC receiver for PUSCH in TDD FR1 CPRI without beamforming. All supported TDD FR1 bandwidths are considered. PUSCH SCS equals 30 kHz. (ID: 11401754)

###### 5.4.5.15.1.3.3 4Rx MRC Receiver, TDD FR1 eCPRI, BF, FOC per antenna

**5.4.5.15.1.3.3.0-1**  (ID: `11401760`)

4Rx MRC Receiver, TDD FR1 eCPRI, BF, FOC per antenna
L1 shall support single user 4Rx MRC receiver for PUSCH in TDD FR1 eCPRI with beamforming cells (beamformed SRS), for both Msg3 and Data slots. FOC shall be done per antenna.

UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message shall contain new measurements parameters,

* Power measurements shall be provided per antenna.
* SINR shall be provided per antenna.
* TA shall be provided per antenna.

Note that UE in RRC\_CONNECTED state will select two best beams to formulate 4Rx for PUSCH (with beamformed SRS). The 2 best beams are dynamically selected by 5G-L2-PS.

---

###### 5.4.5.15.1.3.4 4Rx MRC Receiver, TDD FR1 CPRI, BF, FOC per beam

**5.4.5.15.1.3.4.0-1**  (ID: `11401765`)

4Rx MRC Receiver, TDD FR1 CPRI, BF, FOC per beam
L1 shall support single user 4Rx MRC receiver for PUSCH in TDD FR1 eCPRI with beamforming cells (beamformed SRS), and with beam specific FO handling.

UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message shall contain new measurements parameters, in particular,

* LT FOE shall be provided per antenna.

UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message shall contain new measurements parameters, in particular,

* ST FOE shall be provided per antenna.

---

##### 5.4.5.15.1.4 Loki

###### 5.4.5.15.1.4.0-1 Loki 5G-L1-UL supports 2Rx MRC receiver for PUSCH in FR1 TDD and FR1 FDD. The support for 4Rx MRC is descoped starting 5GC001294, upon the introduction of 4Rx IRC receiver. (ID: 12576389)

#### 5.4.5.15.2 Interference Rejection Combining (IRC) Receiver

##### 5.4.5.15.2.1 IRC Receiver

**5.4.5.15.2.1.0-2**  (ID: `12731005`)

IRC Receiver
5G-L1-UL shall support the equalization types on PUSCH as below:

* 2 layers MIMO equalization for 2 layers spatial multiplexing,
* SIMO equalization for 1 layer spatial multiplexing
* SIMO equalization for singleTx.

The normalization factor in equalization is averaged per symbol.

The equalization operation is described in section “2.3.6 SIMO MIMO Equalizer” of 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_L1\_2794).

The information needed to choose the equalization type are the *numOfLayers and the* spatialMode parameters that come within the *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message described in 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*\*\*

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

##### 5.4.5.15.2.2 SU-MIMO and MU-MIMO IRC Receiver

###### 5.4.5.15.2.2.1 Loki - Equalization

###### 5.4.5.15.2.2.1.1 IRC Rx, non-BF

**5.4.5.15.2.2.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `11401770`)

IRC Rx, non-BF
L1 shall support 4Rx IRC for non-beamformed cells for both Msg3 and Data PUSCH for all supported BW. Applicable to TDD FR1 eCPRI/CPRI, and FDD FR1 CPRI.

Note:

* no L2/L1 interface impact.
* to improve the estimation of R\_dd, a procedure called ‘shrinkage’ is used.  See 5GMax document.

[Before-CNI-122313-A]

[CB009443-C] This requirement also applies to RINLINE 1 for FR1-TDD-eCPRI [end CB009443-C]

[CNI-122313-A]

RINLINE1 HW is supported until 24R1 inclusive. RINLINE1 HW is not supported since 24R2.

[End CNI-122313-A]

---

###### 5.4.5.15.2.2.1.2 IRC Rx, BF

**5.4.5.15.2.2.1.2.0-1**  (ID: `11401777`)

IRC Rx, BF
L1 shall support 4Rx IRC for beamformed cells for both Msg3 and Data PUSCH for all supported BW. Applicable to TDD FR1 eCPRI/CPRI.

Note:

* no L2/L1 interface impact.
* to improve the estimation of R\_dd, a procedure called ‘shrinkage’ is used.  See 5GMax document.

[Before-CNI-122313-A]

[CB009443-C] This requirement also applies to RINLINE 1 for FR1-TDD-eCPRI [end CB009443-C]

[CNI-122313-A]

RINLINE1 HW is supported until 24R1 inclusive. RINLINE1 HW is not supported since 24R2.

[End CNI-122313-A]

---

###### 5.4.5.15.2.2.1.3 IRC Rx, BF, FOC per antenna

**5.4.5.15.2.2.1.3.0-1**  (ID: `11401781`)

IRC Rx, BF, FOC per antenna
L1 shall support 4Rx IRC receiver for PUSCH in TDD FR1 eCPRI/CPRI, and FDD FR1 CPRI, for both Msg3 and Data slots. FOC shall be done per antenna.

[CB007326-A] ASOE shall support 4Rx IRC receiver for PUSCH in TDD FR1 eCPRI, for both Msg3 and Data slots. FOC shall be done per antenna. [End CB007326-A]

[CB007271-C] ABIN shall support 4Rx IRC receiver for PUSCH in FDD FR1 CPRI, for both Msg3 and Data slots. FOC shall be done per antenna. [End CB007271-C]

UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message shall contain new measurements parameters,

* Power measurements shall be provided per antenna.
* SINR shall be provided per antenna.
* TA shall be provided per antenna.

[Before-CNI-122313-A]

[CB009443-C] This requirement also applies to RINLINE 1 for FR1-TDD-eCPRI [end CB009443-C]

[CNI-122313-A]

RINLINE1 HW is supported until 24R1 inclusive. RINLINE1 HW is not supported since 24R2.

[End CNI-122313-A]

---

###### 5.4.5.15.2.2.1.4 IRC Rx, BF, FOC per beam

**5.4.5.15.2.2.1.4.0-1**  (ID: `11401786`)

IRC Rx, BF, FOC per beam
L1 shall support 4Rx IRC receiver for PUSCH in TDD FR1 eCPRI/CPRI, and FDD FR1 CPRI, and with beam specific FO handling.

[CB007326-A] ASOE shall support 4Rx IRC receiver for PUSCH in TDD FR1 eCPRI, and with beam specific FO handling. [End CB007326-A]

UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message shall contain new measurements parameters, in particular,

* LT FOE shall be provided per antenna.

[Before-CNI-122313-A]

[CB009443-C] This requirement also applies to RINLINE 1 for FR1-TDD-eCPRI [end CB009443-C]

[CNI-122313-A]

RINLINE1 HW is supported until 24R1 inclusive. RINLINE1 HW is not supported since 24R2.

[End CNI-122313-A]

---

###### 5.4.5.15.2.2.2 Thor - Equalization

###### 5.4.5.15.2.2.2.1 IRC Rx

###### 5.4.5.15.2.2.2.1.0-1 IRC Rx L1 shall support 2Rx/[RP003187-B, RP003187-I] 4Rx [End RP003187-B, RP003187-I] IRC receiver algorithm for L1 Thor. Applicable to TDD FR1 eCPRI and FDD FR1 CPRI. (ID: 11401827)

###### 5.4.5.15.2.2.2.2 IRC Rx, non-BF

**5.4.5.15.2.2.2.2.0-1**  (ID: `11401851`)

IRC Rx, non-BF
L1 shall support 4Rx IRC for non-beamformed cells for both Msg3 and Data PUSCH for all supported BW. Applicable to TDD FR1 eCPRI/CPRI, and FDD FR1 CPRI.

Note:

* no L2/L1 interface impact.

[RP003187-D]  L1 Thor shall support 4Rx IRC for non-beamformed cells for both Msg3 and Data PUSCH for all supported BW in TDD FR1 eCPRI. Note: Thor does not support 'shrinkage', there is matrix legalization in ARXS instead [End RP003187-D]

[RP003187-K] L1 Thor shall support 4Rx IRC for non-beamformed cells for both Msg3 and Data PUSCH for all supported BW in FDD FR1 CPRI. Note: Thor does not support 'shrinkage', there is matrix legalization in ARXS instead[End RP003187-K]

[CB011178-A] 5G-L1-UL shall also support requirements above for FR1 FDD 7-2a eCPRI for Thor. [End CB011178-A]

[CB010575]

Refer to 5G\_L1\_12469\_replaced([11403011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9Lo-fvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB010575]

12687098:
IRC SU Receiver
**Thor PUSCH IRC SU receiver algorithm for FR1**

For Thor L1 HW, MMSE-IRC receiver is used when following subcell types are configured during L1 cell setup:

-A2 (FDD 2RX IRC)

-A4 (FDD 4RX IRC)

* [CB013442-D] A8 (FDD 8Rx IRC) [End CB013442-D]

-C2 (TDD 2RX IRC)

-C4 (TDD 4RX IRC)

-C8 (TDD 8RX IRC)

MMSE-IRC shrinkage receiver is used with 8Rx, [CB008465-AA] or 4RX with UL CoMP enabled and UEs configured with joint reception (check Note 2). [End CB008465-AA]

Depending on the R&D parameter *rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag (5G\_UP\_ALG\_22026\_replaced([11110446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), a hybrid MMSE-IRC method (legalization + shrinkage) can be used with 8Rx, [CB008465-AA] or 4RX with UL CoMP enabled and UEs configured with joint reception (check Note 2) [End CB008465-AA]:*

* When R&D parameter *rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag = 1, the hybrid MMSE-IRC method is applied. Noise floor estimation is based on 'Shrinkage-based' method. The shrinkage part shall be skipped if the computed shrinkage coefficient [image-0][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, blurred shot of a person's eye. The iris is a mix of blue and brown, with a bright white reflection in the center. The pupil is a dark, circular shape. The surrounding skin is a light tan color, with some subtle shading. The image is cropped tightly on the eye, with the right side of the image fading to black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1133.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] is less than* **rdHybridIrcThrShrinkage (5G\_UP\_ALG\_24766\_replaced([11110465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE68_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Otherwise, shrinkage part is also activated. For legalization part, a diagonal loading component is added to the estimated covariance matrix (Rdd), whose amplitude is determined by the product of *rdHybridIrcBetaLegalization (5G\_UP\_ALG\_24768\_replaced([11110481](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE6-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and estimated noise floor level.***\*\*
* When R&D parameter *rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag = 0, legacy IRC method with shrinkage only is used for 8Rx, [CB008465-AA] or 4RX with UL CoMP enabled and UEs configured with joint reception (check Note 2). [End CB008465-AA]*

Note1: legalization method is conducted in Thor HW ARXL.

[CB008465-AA] Note 2: The 8RX MMSE-IRC shrinkage or hybrid receiver algorithms with both *rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag options (0, 1) should be used if 4RX is configured (A4 subcell, ulSubcellType = 5 in UlCell\_SetupReq message [11513449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRwhwfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with UL CoMP enabled (isNrUlCompEnabled = True in L1Config\_SwConfigurationReq message [11584802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BPk5LPwGEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with UEs configured for joint reception (mode = JointReception in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message [11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008465-AA]*

When there are multiple DMRS symbols, all DMRS symbols are used and averaged for Rdd calculation

Refer to “5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification” section#2.3.6.2 MMSE-IRC algorithm specification.

---

IRC MU Receiver
Thor PUSCH IRC MU receiver algorithm for FR1 TDD

For Thor L1 HW, MMSE-IRC MU receiver is used when following subcell types are configured during L1 cell setup:

-  C4 (TDD 4Rx MMSE IRC)

-  C8 (TDD 8Rx MMSE IRC)

The MU receiver supported in FR1 TDD as follows:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | 4Rx MMSE IRC | First Feature | 8Rx MMSE IRC | First Feature |
| legalization | - |  | - |  |
| shrinkage | supported | CB009029-5G-P | supported | CB009363-A |
| hybrid | - |  | [CB009363-OO] supported [End CB009363-OO] | CB009363-OO |

[CB009363-OO] Depending on the R&D parameter rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag (5G\_UP\_ALG\_22026\_replaced([11110446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), a hybrid MMSE-IRC method (legalization + shrinkage) can be used with 8Rx.

* When R&D parameter rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag = 1, the hybrid MMSE-IRC method is applied. Noise floor estimation is based on 'Shrinkage-based' method. The shrinkage part shall be skipped if the computed shrinkage coefficient [image-0][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, blurry, abstract depiction of a human eye. The iris is a swirl of blues, browns, and whites, with a bright highlight in the center. The pupil is a dark, indistinct shape. The surrounding skin tones are a blend of warm browns and soft whites, with a subtle gradient that suggests light and shadow. The overall impression is one of depth and subtle color variation, creating a somewhat ethereal and artistic representation of an eye.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1135.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] is less than[Before CB009363-OO-UPD002] 0.5 [CB009363-OO-UPD002] rdHybridIrcThrShrinkage (5G\_UP\_ALG\_24766\_replaced([11110465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE68_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB009363-OO-UPD002]. Otherwise, shrinkage part is also activated. [CB009363-OO-UPD002] For legalization part, a diagonal loading component is added to the estimated covariance matrix (Rdd), whose amplitude is determined by the product of rdHybridIrcBetaLegalization (5G\_UP\_ALG\_24768\_replaced([11110481](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE6-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and estimated noise floor level. [End CB009363-OO-UPD002]
* When R&D parameter rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag = 0, legacy IRC method with shrinkage only is used for 8Rx.

Note: legalization method is conducted inThor HW ARXL. [End CB009363-OO]

When there are multiple DMRS symbols, all DMRS symbols are used and averaged for Rdd calculation

Refer to “5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification” section#2.3.6.2 MMSE-IRC algorithm specification.

---

###### 5.4.5.15.2.2.2.8 7-2e split - Post Equalization SINR Compensation

###### 5.4.5.15.2.2.2.8.0-1 7-2e split - Post Equalization SINR Compensation 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall implement Post Equalization SINR Compensation in DMAP for 16Rx IRC receiver in eCPRI 7-2e split, when Hybrid IRC (Shrinkage+Legalization) is performed, that is, rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag16Rx ( 11110455 ) is set to 1, and R&amp;D parameter rdNrUlCompensationPostSinr16Rx ( 12689981 ) is set to 1. In this case, pSINR correction for PRB 1 to 5 is applied. (ID: 12694159)

###### 5.4.5.15.2.2.3 Odin - Equalization

IRC SU Receiver
**PUSCH IRC SU receiver algorithm**

5G-L1-UL (BB) shall implement MMSE-IRC Single User MIMO receiver, configurable with the number of input beams/streams (2, 4, 8), for all supported bandwidths.

In case of 2 and 4 beams/streams, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall use the Shrinkage method to estimate disturbance covariance matrix Rdd.

In case of 8 beams/streams, depending on the R&D parameter *rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag ([11110446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the shrinkage method or the hybrid (legalization + shrinkage) method shall be used by 5G-L1-UL (BB) to estimate the covariance matrix of disturbance (Rdd):*

* If *rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag = 1, the hybrid MMSE-IRC method is applied. Noise floor estimation is based on 'Shrinkage-based' method. The shrinkage part shall be skipped if the computed shrinkage coefficient [image-0][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, blurred shot of a person's eye. The iris is a mix of blue and brown, with a bright white reflection in the center. The pupil is a dark, circular shape. The surrounding skin is a light tan color, with some subtle shading. The image is cropped tightly on the eye, with the right side of the image fading to black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1133.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] is less than* **rdHybridIrcThrShrinkage ([11110465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE68_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Otherwise, shrinkage part is also activated. For legalization part, a diagonal loading component is added to the estimated covariance matrix (Rdd), whose amplitude is determined by the product of *rdHybridIrcBetaLegalization ([11110481](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE6-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and estimated noise floor level.***\*\*
* If *rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag = 0, IRC method with shrinkage shall be used for 8Rx.*

Note 1: Both legalization and shrinkage methods are configurable in ARXL of Odin. Refer to [11402980: Rdd Matrix Estimation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9Lo9_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

Note 2: When there are multiple DMRS symbols, all DMRS symbols are used and averaged for Rdd calculation.

Refer to section [#sec-PuschMmseIrc](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschMmseIrc) of the 5GMax Algorithm Specification.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported for up to 4 input streams, in Odin L1 Call. [End CB012115-IA]

---

12733621:
IRC MU Receiver
**PUSCH IRC MU receiver algorithm for FR1 TDD**

5G-L1-UL (BB) shall implement MMSE-IRC Multi User MIMO receiver, configurable with the number of input beams (4, 8), for all supported bandwidths.

The MU receiver supported in FR1 TDD as follows:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | 4Rx MMSE IRC | First Feature | 8Rx MMSE IRC | First Feature |
| legalization | - |  | - |  |
| shrinkage | supported | CB012115-IB | supported | CB012115-IB |
| hybrid | - |  | supported | CB012115-IB |

In case of 2 and 4 beams/streams, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall use the Shrinkage method to estimate disturbance covariance matrix Rdd.

In case of 8 beams/streams, depending on the R&D parameter *rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag ([11110446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpET8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the shrinkage method or the hybrid (legalization + shrinkage) method shall be used by 5G-L1-UL (BB) to estimate the covariance matrix of disturbance (Rdd):*

* If *rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag = 1, the hybrid MMSE-IRC method is applied. Noise floor estimation is based on 'Shrinkage-based' method. The shrinkage part shall be skipped if the computed shrinkage coefficient [image-0][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, blurred shot of a person's eye. The iris is a mix of blue and brown, with a bright white reflection in the center. The pupil is a dark, circular shape. The surrounding skin is a light tan color, with some subtle shading. The image is cropped tightly on the eye, with the right side of the image fading to black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1133.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] is less than* **rdHybridIrcThrShrinkage ([11110465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE68_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Otherwise, shrinkage part is also activated. For legalization part, a diagonal loading component is added to the estimated covariance matrix (Rdd), whose amplitude is determined by the product of *rdHybridIrcBetaLegalization ([11110481](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE6-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and estimated noise floor level.***\*\*
* If *rdHybridIrcEnabledFlag = 0, IRC method with shrinkage shall be used for 8Rx.*

Note 1: Both legalization and shrinkage methods are configurable in ARXL of Odin. Refer to [11402980: Rdd Matrix Estimation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9Lo9_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

Note 2: When there are multiple DMRS symbols, all DMRS symbols are used and averaged for Rdd calculation.

Refer to section [#sec-PuschMmseIrc](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschMmseIrc) of the 5GMax Algorithm Specification.

The requirement applies to FR1-TDD Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

---

###### 5.4.5.15.2.2.4 Specific case: Random Access Response (RAR) UL grant

**5.4.5.15.2.2.4.0-2**  (ID: `12731016`)

Specific case: Random Access Response (RAR) UL grant
The single antenna transmission in UL shall be also supported to decode PUSCH scheduled by RAR UL grant during the random access procedure. For this scheme, SIMO equalization (described in [#sec-PuschSimoMimoEqu](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschSimoMimoEqu) of the 5Gmax algorithm specification) shall be applied.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

##### 5.4.5.15.2.3 8Rx IRC Equalizer

###### 5.4.5.15.2.3.1 SU-MIMO

**5.4.5.15.2.3.1.0-5**  (ID: `12731174`)

SU-MIMO
5G-L1-UL shall support 8Rx MMSE IRC Single User (SU) receiver for PUSCH when the subcell is configured with 8 streams, with up to 2 layers (numOfLayers = 2).

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

###### 5.4.5.15.2.3.2 MU-MIMO

**5.4.5.15.2.3.2.0-5**  (ID: `12731178`)

MU-MIMO
When subcell is configure with 8 streams, L1 shall support UL MU-MIMO UE pairing where root UE is paired with multiple UEs. For the concepts of root UE, and paired UEs, refer to 5G UP ALG [11355469: UL Multi-User MIMO](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucROKvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw).

For the supported configurations, refer to [11398477: UE pairing](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8243_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

###### 5.4.5.15.2.3.3 High Speed Cell

###### 5.4.5.15.2.3.3.1 C8 subcell

###### 5.4.5.15.2.3.3.1.0-2 C8 subcell When C8 subcell is configured and actHighSpeedCell=True in UlCell_SetupReq, L1 shall support actMuMimo=False and therefore PUSCH 8Rx SU-MIMO receiver. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported in Odin for FR1 TDD 7-2a. [End CB012115-IA] (ID: 12731181)

###### 5.4.5.15.2.3.3.2 A8 subcell

###### 5.4.5.15.2.3.3.2.0-1 A8 subcell When ulSubcellType = "A8" and actHighSpeedCell="True" in UlCell_SetupReq ( 11513449 ), L1 shall support actMuMimo=False and therefore PUSCH 8Rx SU-MIMO receiver. (ID: 13353831)

##### 5.4.5.15.2.4 Rdd Matrix Estimation

###### 5.4.5.15.2.4.1 Legacy

###### 5.4.5.15.2.4.1.0-1 Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy 11402994: Legacy PUSCH IRC receiver algorithm for FR1 MMSE-IRC shrinkage receiver with 4 Rx spatial streams is supported for FR1 NR cells. Once ulSubcellType C4 or A4 is signaled to L1 during cell setup phase, 4Rx IRC is taken into use by Loki L1. Below computations in IRC receiver algorithm are performed per PRB per slot:  the covariance matrix of “disturbance” [image-0][image_desc]The image contains the text "Rdd" in a blurry, stylized font. The letters are in a dark color with a gradient of orange, yellow, and purple hues. The background is white.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_1117.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] (matrix size is 4x4 for 4Rx IRC [CB008325-5G-AA] and 2x2 for 2RxIRC [End CB008325-5G-AA]), *  shrinkage operation (including shrinkage coefficient calculation and scaling of  [image-1][image_desc]The image contains the text "Rdd" in a blurry, stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient of brown, orange, and purple. The background is white.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_1117.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] entries),  matrix inversion in IRC equalizer. When there are multiple DMRS symbols, only the first DMRS symbol is used to calculate shrinkage coefficient. Reason behind:  Simulation results show that performance of using the first DMRS symbol to calculate shrinkage coefficient is quite close to that of using multiple DMRS symbols (  Use the first DMRS symbol only can save computation efforts Refer to “5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification” section#2.3.6.2 - #2.3.6.4 for MMSE-IRC shrinkage algorithm specification. Refer to 5G_L1_IF_152_replaced( 11513449 ) for configured ulSubcellType. Refer to 5G_L1_2797_replaced( 11481465 ) for mapping of ulSubcellType to number of Rx antennas. *[Before CB008325-5G-AA] [RP003187-D, RP003187-K] For L1 Thor HWs, MMSE-IRC legalization receiver with 4 Rx spatial streams is supported for FR1 NR cells. Note: L1 Thor does not support 'shrinkage', there is matrix legalization in ARXS instead [End RP003187-D, RP003187-K] * *[CB008325-5G-AA] For L1 Thor HWs, MMSE-IRC legalization receiver with 4Rx and 2Rx spatial streams is supported for FR1 NR cells. Note: L1 Thor does not support 'shrinkage', there is matrix legalization in ARXS instead. [End CB008325-5G-AA] * *[Before-CNI-122313-A] [CB009443-C] This requirement is also appliable for RINLINE 1 HW for FR1-TDD-eCPRI. [end CB009443-C] * *[CNI-122313-A] RINLINE1 HW is supported until 24R1 inclusive. RINLINE1 HW is not supported since 24R2. [End CNI-122313-A] [CB009363-A, CB009363-C] For ABIP HW, MMSE-IRC shrinkage receiver with 8Rx spatial streams is supported for FR1 TDD NR cells when C8 subcell is configured. [End CB009363-A, CB009363-C]**** (ID: 11402994)
