#### 5.4.7.2.5 Invalid CFO

**5.4.7.2.5.0-4**  (ID: `12732282`)

Invalid CFO
For Odin:

For each scheduled UE on PUSCH, per antenna (or per beam) ST CFO metric is computed and reported to 5G-L2-PS. 5G-L1-UL shall report invalid measurement for antenna-wise FOE(shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt) with message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs **when the Rx is failed (typically the case of RU lost or fiber broken or Rx failure).**

Message detail is defined in [11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11513842](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Invalid value of shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt shall be set to 0+0j (both real and imaginary parts set to zero).

---

### 5.4.7.3 Received Signal Power

#### 5.4.7.3.1 Received Signal Power per Antenna

##### 5.4.7.3.1.1 Legacy

**5.4.7.3.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `11404038`)

Legacy
Frozen Requirement; Please refer to the below chapters.

Loki-L1-~~Only~~ **[RP003187-F, RP003187-M] and Thor** [End RP003187-F, RP003187-M].\*\*\*\*

5GNB 5G-L1-UL shall estimate per rx antenna UL received power on DMRS associated to PUSCH and report it to 5G-L2-PS within the parameter *rxPowerOfAnt sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. The *rxPowerOfAnt parameter is defined in dBm unit as illustrated in ~~5G\_L1\_5G21A\_199~~ 5G\_L1\_IF\_200\_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*\*\*

When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module or eCPRI interface for each the receiving antenna stream, 5G-L1-UL shall set the *rxPowerOfAnt parameter sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message to -140dBm.\*\*

PUSCH rx antenna power for each UL received antenna stream is estimated as described in section 2.3.5.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications **(see ~~5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916~~ 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

Note: although antenna-wise value is introduced for FR1 TDD Rx beamforming cells with eCPRI RU, L1's behavior is the same among:

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX beamforming

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 TDD 4Rx non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming

- **[CB008205-B] For Loki L1 HW, eCPRI** FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming **[End CB008205-B]**\*\*\*\*

- **[CB008205-C] For Loki L1 HW, eCPRI** FR1 FDD 2Rx non beamforming **[End CB008205-C]**\*\*\*\*

- FR2 TDD 2Rx

**[RP003187-F] The requirement is also applicable for** L1 Thor for FR1-TDD-eCPRI. **[End RP003187-F]**\*\*\*\*

**[RP003187-M] The requirement is also applicable for** L1 Thor for FR1-FDD-CPRI. **[End RP003187-M]**\*\*\*\*

**[Before-CNI-122313-A]**

**[CB009443-C] This requirement is also appliable for RINLINE 1 HW for FR1-TDD-eCPRI.** [end CB009443-C]\*\*\*\*

**[CNI-122313-A]**

RINLINE1 HW is supported until 24R1 inclusive. RINLINE1 HW is not supported since 24R2.

**[End CNI-122313-A]**

---

##### 5.4.7.3.1.2 Loner

**5.4.7.3.1.2.0-2**  (ID: `11404032`)

Loner
[CNI-59405]: CP2 part moved

Loner-L1-Only. FR1 TDD eCPRI beamforming only.

5GNB 5G-L1-UL shall estimate per rx antenna UL received power on DMRS associated to PUSCH and report it to 5G-L2-PS within the parameter rxPowerOfAnt sent in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. The rxPowerOfAnt parameter is defined in dBm unit as illustrated in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_199.

When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module or eCPRI interface for each the receiving antenna stream, 5G-L1-UL shall set the rxPowerOfAnt parameter sent in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message to -140dBm.

PUSCH rx antenna power for each UL received antenna stream is estimated as described in section 2.3.5.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916).

Note: although antenna-wise value is introduced for FR1 TDD Rx beamforming cells with eCPRI RU, L1's behavior is the same among:

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX beamforming

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 TDD 4Rx non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming

- FR2 TDD 2Rx

[End CNI-59405]

---

##### 5.4.7.3.1.3 Loki

**5.4.7.3.1.3.0-1**  (ID: `12885065`)

Loki
5G-L1-UL shall estimate per rx antenna UL received power on DMRS associated to PUSCH and report it to 5G-L2-PS within the parameter *rxPowerOfAnt sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. The *rxPowerOfAnt parameter is defined in dBm unit as illustrated in [11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).*\*\*

When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module or eCPRI interface for each the receiving antenna stream, 5G-L1-UL shall set the *rxPowerOfAnt parameter sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message to -140dBm.\*\*

PUSCH rx antenna power for each UL received antenna stream is estimated as described in section 2.3.5.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications **(see [11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

Note: although antenna-wise value is introduced for FR1 TDD Rx beamforming cells with eCPRI RU, L1's behavior is the same among:

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX beamforming

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 TDD 4Rx non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming

- HW, eCPRI **FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming**

- eCPRI **FR1 FDD 2Rx non beamforming**

- FR2 TDD 2Rx

Notice: the requirement has a history starting release 21A. For the history please refer to [11404038](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9Rvm_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

---

##### 5.4.7.3.1.4 Thor

**5.4.7.3.1.4.0-2**  (ID: `12687120`)

Thor
5GNB 5G-L1-UL shall estimate per rx antenna UL received power on DMRS associated to PUSCH and report it to 5G-L2-PS within the parameter *rxPowerOfAnt sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. The *rxPowerOfAnt parameter is defined in dBm unit as illustrated in 5G\_L1\_IF\_200\_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*\*\*

When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module or eCPRI interface for each the receiving antenna stream, 5G-L1-UL shall set the *rxPowerOfAnt parameter sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message to -140dBm.\*\*

[CB008465-AA] For NR FDD non-beamforming deployments, when UL CoMP is enabled, for each UE scheduled on PUSCH and configured as joint reception, 5G-L1-UL shall estimate received power per rx antenna stream for the Helper subcell in addition to the Serving subcell. For UL CoMP, the number of measurements would be twice the number of antennas for a single cell (8 in case of A4 subcell) and then shall be reported to 5G-L2-PS using UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs.PuschAntMeas.*rxPowerOfAnt with Serving subcell measurements always placed first and that Helper subcell measurements follow contiguously.*

The mapping between received power and CPRI AxC spatial streams of Serving and Helper subcells is as follows

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type | Subcell | SINR for antenna i | CPRI spatial stream | **First feature** |
| A4 | Serving | **puschAntMeas[0].*rxPowerOfAnt*** | AxC0 | CB008465-AA |
| **puschAntMeas[1].*rxPowerOfAnt*** | AxC1 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[2].*rxPowerOfAnt*** | AxC2 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[3].*rxPowerOfAnt*** | AxC3 |  |  |  |
| Helper | **puschAntMeas[4].*rxPowerOfAnt*** | AxC0 |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[5].*rxPowerOfAnt*** | AxC1 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[6].*rxPowerOfAnt*** | AxC2 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[7].*rxPowerOfAnt*** | AxC3 |  |  |  |

[End CB008465-AA]

PUSCH rx antenna power for each UL received antenna stream is estimated as described in section 2.3.5.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications **(see ~~5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916~~ 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

Note: although antenna-wise value is introduced for FR1 TDD Rx beamforming cells with eCPRI RU, L1's behavior is the same among:

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX beamforming

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 TDD 4Rx non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming

-    For Thor L1 HW, eCPRI **FR1 TDD 8Rx beamforming**

-    [CB013442-D] For Thor L1 HW, eCPRI  FR1 FDD 8Rx beamforming/non-beamforming [End CB013442-D]

[CB008577-C] Note: For NR FDD beamforming deployments, as signal power is computed from receiver interference and thermal noise power and PUSCH RSSI, the compensation in case of split-panel beamforming is conducted automatically. No extra operation is requested. [End CB008577-C]

History:

As of CB010575-D, this requirement applies to FR1-TDD-CPRI. Parity from 5GC001294 requires supporting all 2Rx and 4Rx cases listed above.

As of CB011178-A, this requirement applied to FR1 FDD 7-2a eCPRI, for both 2Rx and 4Rx.

As of CB010969-CB, OBSAI has also been supported.

As of CB0009363, C8 in eCPRI 7-2a is supported. CB013442 adds the support for A8.

---

##### 5.4.7.3.1.5 Odin

**5.4.7.3.1.5.0-1**  (ID: `12732287`)

Odin
5GNB 5G-L1-UL shall estimate per rx antenna UL received power on DMRS associated to PUSCH and report it to 5G-L2-PS within the parameter *rxPowerOfAnt sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. The *rxPowerOfAnt parameter is defined in dBm unit as illustrated in [11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).*\*\*

When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module or eCPRI interface for each the receiving antenna stream, 5G-L1-UL shall set the *rxPowerOfAnt parameter sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message to -140dBm.\*\*

PUSCH rx antenna power for each UL received antenna stream is estimated as described in section 2.3.5.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications.

Note: although antenna-wise value is introduced for FR1 TDD Rx beamforming cells with eCPRI RU, L1's behavior is the same among:

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX beamforming

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 TDD 4Rx non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming

-    For Thor L1 HW, eCPRI **FR1 TDD 8Rx beamforming**

---

#### 5.4.7.3.2 Received Signal Power per UE

##### 5.4.7.3.2.1 Legacy

**5.4.7.3.2.1.0-2**  (ID: `11404026`)

Legacy
Frozen Requirement; Please refer to the below chapters.

5GNB 5G-L1-UL shall estimate an average UL received power on DMRS associated to PUSCH and report it to 5G-L2-PS within the parameter *rxPower sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message for long term (LT) averaging. The *rxPower parameter is defined in dBm unit as illustrated in ~~5G\_L1\_5G21A\_199~~ 5G\_L1\_IF\_200\_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*\*\*

When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module or CPRI interface for all the receiving antennas (eg. broken antenna scenario), 5G-L1-UL shall set the *rxPower parameter sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message to -140dBm.\*\*

The UL received power estimation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section ~~2.5.3.1~~ "2.7.4.1 PUSCH" of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see ~~5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916~~ 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

**5G-L1-UL shall also estimate the RX power on PUSCH and report this estimate to 5G-L2-PS as above in Loki L1 HW. It shall take the above assumption when only zero-valued samples are received.**

**[RP003187-F] The requirement is also applicable for** L1 Thor for FR1-TDD-eCPRI. **[End RP003187-F]**\*\*\*\*

**[RP003187-M] The requirement is also applicable for** L1 Thor for FR1-FDD-CPRI. **[End RP003187-M]**\*\*\*\*

---

[image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $rxPower_{L1-SW} = \frac{rssi_{L1-SW} - noisePower_{L1-SW}(n)}{(DMRS_{BoostPUSCH})^2} [mW]$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_1258.002.png[/image_path][/image-1], where [image-2][image_desc]The image is a close-up, blurry, and abstract representation of what appears to be a logo or graphic design. It features a series of vertical bars with varying colors and gradients, suggesting a sense of depth and light. ##### 5.4.7.3.2.3 Loki

**5.4.7.3.2.3.0-1**  (ID: `12899220`)

Loki
For each scheduled UE, 5G-L1-UL(BB) shall estimate an UL received power, averaged over all the allocated REs for DMRS associated to PUSCH and over all the layers aggregated over all the receiving antennas. The result shall be normalized to one PRB and reported to 5G-L2-PS through the parameter *rxPower sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message for long term (LT) averaging. *rxPower is defined in dBm unit as described in [11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).*\*\*

When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module or CPRI interface for all the receiving antennas (eg. broken antenna scenario), 5G-L1-UL shall set the *rxPower parameter sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message to -140dBm.\*\*

The UL received power estimation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section [#sec-MeasUlRecSigPowPusch](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-MeasUlRecSigPowPusch) of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The scaling factor applied at the radio module to reach a target noise bitwidth shall be compensated at 5G-L1-UL for all the UL power measurements (in [11404026](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9RvmfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) & [11458509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fAv6W_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) using the IE *rxScalingFactor got from* UlCell\_SetupReq message (see  [11513449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRwhwfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*

Note: rxScalingFactor value is set as described in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2450.

---

##### 5.4.7.3.2.4 Thor

**5.4.7.3.2.4.0-2**  (ID: `12874885`)

Thor
For each scheduled UE, 5G-L1-UL(BB) shall estimate an UL received power, averaged over all the allocated REs for DMRS associated to PUSCH and over all the layers aggregated over all the receiving antennas. The result shall be normalized to one PRB and reported to 5G-L2-PS through the parameter *rxPower sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message for long term (LT) averaging. *rxPower is defined in dBm unit as described in [11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).*\*\*

[CB008465-AA] For NR FDD non-beamforming deployments, when UL CoMP is enabled, for each UE scheduled on PUSCH and configured as joint reception, 5G-L1-UL shall estimate the average UL received power over all antennas of the Serving and Helper subcells and report it to 5G-L2-PS within the parameter *rxPower sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message ([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for long term (LT) averaging.  [End CB008465-AA]\*\*

When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module or CPRI interface for all the receiving antennas (eg. broken antenna scenario), 5G-L1-UL shall set the *rxPower parameter sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message to -140dBm.\*\*

The UL received power estimation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section [#sec-MeasUlRecSigPowPusch](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-MeasUlRecSigPowPusch) of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The scaling factor applied at the radio module to reach a target noise bitwidth shall be compensated at 5G-L1-UL for all the UL power measurements (in [11404026](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9RvmfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) & [11458509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fAv6W_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) using the IE *rxScalingFactor got from* UlCell\_SetupReq message (see  [11513449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRwhwfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*

Note: rxScalingFactor value is set as described in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2450.

Note: For NR FDD beamforming deployments, as signal power is computed from receiver interference and thermal noise power and PUSCH RSSI, the compensation in case of split-panel beamforming is conducted automatically. No extra operation is requested.

---

##### 5.4.7.3.2.5 Odin

**5.4.7.3.2.5.0-1**  (ID: `12885089`)

Odin
For each scheduled UE, 5G-L1-UL(BB) shall estimate an UL received power, averaged over all the allocated REs for DMRS associated to PUSCH and over all the layers aggregated over all the receiving antennas. The result shall be normalized to one PRB and reported to 5G-L2-PS through the parameter *rxPower sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message for long term (LT) averaging. *rxPower is defined in dBm unit as described in [11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).*\*\*

When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module or CPRI interface for all the receiving antennas (eg. broken antenna scenario), 5G-L1-UL shall set the *rxPower parameter sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message to -140dBm.\*\*

The UL received power estimation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section [#sec-MeasUlRecSigPowPusch](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-MeasUlRecSigPowPusch) of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see [11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The scaling factor applied at the radio module to reach a target noise bitwidth shall be compensated at 5G-L1-UL for all the UL power measurements (in [11404026](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9RvmfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) & [11458509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fAv6W_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) using the IE *rxScalingFactor got from* UlCell\_SetupReq message (see  [11513449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRwhwfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*

Note: rxScalingFactor value is set as described in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2450.

#### 5.4.7.3.3 Invalid Value for the Received Power

**5.4.7.3.3.0-1**  (ID: `11404019`)

Invalid Value for the Received Power
[Before PR577374] Invalid value of rxPower and rxPowerOfAnt shall be set to -255dBm.[End PR577374]

[PR577374] In the case of invalid measurements for UL Received Power [PR703743] and antenna-wise UL received power, [End PR703743] 5G-L1-UL shall report to 5G-L2-PS the value: -140 dBm. See 5G\_L1\_8324\_replaced([11414448](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e-GO__vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End PR577374]

[RP003187-F] The requirement is also applicable for L1 Thor for FR1-TDD-eCPRI. [End RP003187-F]

[RP003187-M] The requirement is also applicable for L1 Thor for FR1-FDD-CPRI [End RP003187-M]

| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | NA | Supported | Supported |

| FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI | NA | NA | [CB009514-F] Supported for PUCCH [End CB009514-F] |

| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported | Supported |

### 5.4.7.4 Received Interference and Noise Power

#### 5.4.7.4.1 Legacy

**5.4.7.4.1.0-1**  (ID: `11404081`)

Legacy
The content of this requirement is frozen and valid until the mentioned “Last System Release” attribute. For the most recent requirement, please refer to the artifacts below.

**[Before 5GC002006-A]**

The noise power used in frequency offset estimation (FOE) is averaged noise including interference, as described in section “2.3.3.1 Frequency Offset Estimation based on PTRS” and “2.3.3.2 Frequency Offset Estimation based on DMRS” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The average noise estimation is described in section “2.3.4.1 Interference estimation” of 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_L1\_2794).

**[End 5GC002006-A]**

**[5GC002006-A]**

The noise power used in frequency offset estimation (FOE) based on DMRS is DMRS based noise, as described in section “2.3.3.2 Frequency Offset Estimation based on DMRS” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The DMRS based noise estimation is described in section “2.3.1.4 Noise Power Estimation” of 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_L1\_2794).

The noise power used in frequency offset estimation (FOE) based on PTRS is compensated DMRS based noise, as described in section “2.3.3.1 Frequency Offset Estimation based on PTRS” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The compensated DMRS based noise estimation is described in section “2.3.1.5 Noise Power Estimation Compensation” of 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_L1\_2794).

**[End 5GC002006-A]**

**[5GC001868-A] 5G-L1-UL shall support DMRS based noise estimation and compensasion as described above(same as 5GC002006-A) in Loki L1 HW** [End 5GC001868-A]\*\*\*\*

**[CB007814-G] This requirement also applies to ABIO FR1-FDD-eCPRI.** [End CB007814-G]\*\*\*\*

**[RP003187-D] L1 Thor shall support noise estimation and compensasion as described above** for FR1-TDD-eCPRI **[End RP003187-D]**\*\*\*\*

**[RP003187-K] L1 Thor shall support noise estimation and compensasion as described above** for FR1-FDD-CPRI **[End RP003187-K]**\*\*\*\*

**[Before-CNI-122313-A]**

**[CB009444-B] This requirement is applicable for RINLINE1 for FR1-FDD-eCPRI.** [End CB009444-B]\*\*\*\*

**[CNI-122313-A]**

RINLINE1 HW is supported until 24R1 inclusive. RINLINE1 HW is not supported since 24R2.

**[End CNI-122313-A]**

**[CB010575-D] This requirement also applies to Thor for FR1-TDD-CPRI.** [End CB010575-D]\*\*\*\*

**[CB011178-A] 5G-L1-UL shall also support requirements above for FR1 FDD 7-2a eCPRI for Thor.** [End CB011178-A]\*\*\*\*

**Notice: This requirement is not HW, FH Mode, nor Duplex Mode dependent. L1SW is advised to implement it in a higher-level class, so that the HW instances could inherit the functionality. The following table does not impose any technical restriction, nor it provides any information in that regard. It only contains the history of implementation. (table to be completed during the Spec refactoring in compliance with Release Based Versioning)**

HW-Technology Map:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Loner | Loki | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI |  |  | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI |  |  | **[CB011178-A]Supported**[End CB011178-A]\*\*\*\* |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI |  |  | **[CB010969-SR-CB] Supported** [End CB010969-SR-CB]\*\*\*\* |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI |  |  | **[CB010575-D] Supported** [End CB010575-D]\*\*\*\* |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI |  |  |  |
| FR2-TDD-CPRI |  |  |  |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI |  |  |  |

#### 5.4.7.4.2 Noise Power per Antenna

##### 5.4.7.4.2.1 Loner

**5.4.7.4.2.1.0-1**  (ID: `12822600`)

Loner
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall perform antenna-wise DMRS based Noise Power Estimation and report them in linear scale to L2PS through linNoiseOfAnt in [11513842: PuschAntMeas](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs.The unit is attoWatt (10-18), with the invalid value of 10 (attoWatt).

The noise power used in frequency offset estimation (FOE) based on DMRS is DMRS based noise, as described in section “2.3.3.2 Frequency Offset Estimation based on DMRS” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The DMRS based noise estimation is described in section “2.3.1.4 Noise Power Estimation” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The noise power used in frequency offset estimation (FOE) based on PTRS is compensated DMRS based noise, as described in section “2.3.3.1 Frequency Offset Estimation based on PTRS” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The compensated DMRS based noise estimation is described in section “2.3.1.5 Noise Power Estimation Compensation” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

---

##### 5.4.7.4.2.2 Loki

**5.4.7.4.2.2.0-1**  (ID: `12822597`)

Loki
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall perform antenna-wise DMRS based Noise Power Estimation and report them in linear scale to L2PS through linNoiseOfAnt in [11513842: PuschAntMeas](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs.The unit is attoWatt (10-18), with the invalid value of 10 (attoWatt).

The noise power used in frequency offset estimation (FOE) based on DMRS is DMRS based noise, as described in section “2.3.3.2 Frequency Offset Estimation based on DMRS” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The DMRS based noise estimation is described in section “2.3.1.4 Noise Power Estimation” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The noise power used in frequency offset estimation (FOE) based on PTRS is compensated DMRS based noise, as described in section “2.3.3.1 Frequency Offset Estimation based on PTRS” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The compensated DMRS based noise estimation is described in section “2.3.1.5 Noise Power Estimation Compensation” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

---

##### 5.4.7.4.2.3 Thor

**5.4.7.4.2.3.0-2**  (ID: `12822625`)

Thor
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall perform antenna-wise DMRS based Noise Power Estimation and report them in linear scale to L2PS through linNoiseOfAnt in [11513842: PuschAntMeas](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs.The unit is attoWatt (10-18), with the invalid value of 10 (attoWatt).

The noise power used in frequency offset estimation (FOE) based on DMRS is DMRS based noise, as described in section “2.3.3.2 Frequency Offset Estimation based on DMRS” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The DMRS based noise estimation is described in section “2.3.1.4 Noise Power Estimation” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The noise power used in frequency offset estimation (FOE) based on PTRS is compensated DMRS based noise, as described in section “2.3.3.1 Frequency Offset Estimation based on PTRS” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The compensated DMRS based noise estimation is described in section “2.3.1.5 Noise Power Estimation Compensation” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

For NR FDD beamforming deployments, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall apply scaling factor scomp according to suSplitpanelMode in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq ([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)) to compensate the measurement for each spatial stream in case of split-panel beamforming. scomp is 1 for full panel beamforming case and therefore it can be ignored. Refer to section §5.3.1.1 of [12708616: Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_aUzKUWMCEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for the definition of scaling factor scomp.

[CB008465-AA]

For NR FDD non-beamforming deployments, when UL CoMP is enabled, for each UE scheduled on PUSCH and configured as joint reception, 5G-L1-UL shall estimate noise power per antenna (spatial stream) for the Helper subcell in addition to the Serving subcell. For UL CoMP, the number of measurements would be twice the number of antennas for a single cell (8 in case of A4 subcell) and then shall be reported to 5G-L2-PS using UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs.PuschAntMeas.linNoiseOfAnt with Serving subcell measurements always placed first and that Helper subcell measurements follow contiguously.

When UL CoMP is enabled, for each UE scheduled on PUSCH and configured as joint reception, the mapping between noise power and CPRI AxC spatial streams of Serving and Helper subcells is as follows

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type | Subcell | Noise power for antenna i | CPRI spatial stream | **First feature** |
| A4 | Serving | **puschAntMeas[0].linNoiseOfAn** | AxC0 | CB008465-AA |
| **puschAntMeas[1].linNoiseOfAn** | AxC1 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[2].linNoiseOfAn** | AxC2 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[3].linNoiseOfAn** | AxC3 |  |  |  |
| Helper | **puschAntMeas[4].linNoiseOfAn** | AxC0 |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[5].linNoiseOfAn** | AxC1 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[6].linNoiseOfAn** | AxC2 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[7].linNoiseOfAn** | AxC3 |  |  |  |

[End CB008465-AA]

---

##### 5.4.7.4.2.4 Odin

**5.4.7.4.2.4.0-1**  (ID: `12732297`)

Odin
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall perform antenna-wise DMRS based Noise Power Estimation and report them in linear scale to L2PS through linNoiseOfAnt in [11513842: PuschAntMeas](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs.The unit is attoWatt (10-18), with the invalid value of 10 (attoWatt).

The noise power used in frequency offset estimation (FOE) based on DMRS is DMRS based noise, as described in section “2.3.3.2 Frequency Offset Estimation based on DMRS” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The DMRS based noise estimation is described in section “2.3.1.4 Noise Power Estimation” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The noise power used in frequency offset estimation (FOE) based on PTRS is compensated DMRS based noise, as described in section “2.3.3.1 Frequency Offset Estimation based on PTRS” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The compensated DMRS based noise estimation is described in section “2.3.1.5 Noise Power Estimation Compensation” of 5GMax algorithm specification.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

12732290:
Noise Power per PRB
**noisePerPrb**(See [11513831: UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

5G-L1-UL shall compute and report noise power estimation per PRB on all scheduled PRBs.

PRB-wise noise power is averaged over all received antennas (spatial streams) and over layers as follows:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
noisePower(prb) = $\frac{N\_{SC}^{RB}}{N\_{RX}N\_{Layer}} \sum\_{ant=1}^{N\_{Rx}} \sum\_{p=1}^{N\_{Layer}} \hat{\sigma}\_{noise,prb,p,ant}^2$ / $2^{tAgc}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1288.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where tAgc is the post-FFT scaling factor.

If MU-MIMO is enabled and multiple UEs are sharing the same PRB, PRB-wise noise is averaged over UEs:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
noisePower(prb) = $\frac{\frac{N\_{SC}^{RB}}{N\_{RX}N\_{u}N\_{Layer}} \sum\_{ant=1}^{N\_{RX}} \sum\_{u=1}^{N\_{u}} \sum\_{p=1}^{N\_{Layer}} \hat{\sigma}\_{noise,prb,u,p,ant}^2}{2^{tAgc}}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1288.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

L1 shall report PRB-wise noise power to L2-PS in logarithmic scale in dBm unit, using array *noisePerPrb in message* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs (see [11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Power scaling and conversion to dBm unit shall be done as specified in [11420640](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e-geqfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for Loki and [11421828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e-hFvfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for Thor-BB.\*\*

If measurement is invalid (all received I/Q data are filled with zeros), PRB-wise noise power shall be set to -140 dBm.

For NR FDD beamforming deployments in Thor, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall apply scaling factor scomp according to suSplitpanelMode in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq ([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)) to compensate the measurement for each spatial stream in case of split-panel beamforming. scomp is 1 for full panel beamforming case and therefore it can be ignored. Refer to section §5.3.1.1 of [12708616: Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_aUzKUWMCEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for the definition of scaling factor scomp.

The requirement is supported in Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]\*\*\*\*

---

#### 5.4.7.4.4 Noise Power per Subcell

##### 5.4.7.4.4.1 Legacy

**5.4.7.4.4.1.0-1**  (ID: `11404069`)

Legacy
The content of this requirement is frozen and valid until the mentioned “Last System Release” attribute. For the most recent requirement, please refer to the artifacts below.

For each configured subcell, 5GNB 5G-L1-UL shall estimate one measurement for the noise (interference + thermal noise power) received on UL channels (PUSCH and PUCCH). Each measurement shall be averaged over all the PRBs, averaged over all the layers and aggregated over all the receiving antennas. Then, one measurement normalized to one PRB shall be reported to L2. The noise measurements are subcell related and shall be separately performed for PUSCH and PUCCH.

NOTE: the thermal noise and the interference will be measured together, i.e., they won't be resolved independently.

**[5GC001868-A] 5G-L1-UL shall also perform noise power estimation as explained above in** Loki L1 HW. **[End 5GC001868-A]**\*\*\*\*

**[RP003187-F] The requirement is also applicable for** L1 Thor for FR1-TDD-eCPRI. UL receive noise power measurement is described in ch."2.7.3 UL Receive Noise Power" of 5GMax Algorithm Specification. See also 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1649\_replaced([11108232](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kooPE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

**[End RP003187-F]**

**[RP003187-M] The requirement is also applicable for** L1 Thor for FR1-FDD-CPRI. **UL receive noise power measurement is described in ch."2.7.3 UL Receive Noise Power" of 5GMax Algorithm Specification. See also 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1649\_replaced([11108232](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kooPE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**\*\*\*\*

**[End RP003187-M]**

**[Before-CNI-122313-A]**

**[CB009443-C] This requirement shall be supported in RINLINE1 HW for FR1-TDD-eCPRI.** [End CB009443-C]\*\*\*\*

**[CNI-122313-A]**

RINLINE1 HW is supported until 24R1 inclusive. RINLINE1 HW is not supported since 24R2.

**[End CNI-122313-A]**

**[CB010575-D] The requirement is also applicable for L1** Thor for FR1-TDD-CPRI **[End CB010575-D]**\*\*\*\*

HW-Technology Map:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Technology / HW | Loki | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-OBSAI | Supported | **[CB010969-SR-CB] Supported** [End CB010969-SR-CB]\*\*\*\* |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI | NA | **[CB009514-F] Supported for PUCCH** [End CB009514-F]\*\*\*\* |

##### 5.4.7.4.4.2 Loner, Loki

###### 5.4.7.4.4.2.0-1 Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Loner, Loki 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell 11404108: Noise Power per Subcell Noise power (measurement K1) Reported noise power per sub-cell (on allocated PUSCH resources) is the noise power on two or four antennas normalized to 1 PRB. Granularity of the reported value to L2-PS by 5G-L1-UL is 0.1 dBm. Its computation is described in section 2.7.3.1 of "5GMax algorithm specification". L1-FPGA (on Loner) or L1-SW (on Loki) computes noise power based on 1st DMRS symbol, it provides averaged noise power per antenna (spatial stream) and normalized to one RE (see 5G_L1_9064_replaced( 11401067 )). L1-SW shall normalize this noise power to one PRB: [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $K 1_{norm,a}(u) = N_{RB}^{SC} \times \hat{\sigma} a^2(u)$. The equation consists of the following elements: - A constant $K$. - A function $1 {norm,a}(u)$, which appears to be an indicator function or a normalization factor. - An equality sign $=$. - A term $N_{RB}^{SC}$, which likely represents a quantity related to subcarriers or resource blocks. - A multiplication sign $\times$. - A term $\hat{\sigma} a^2(u)$, which seems to be an estimated variance or power.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_1273.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] where u represents the grant index, from 1 to Ngrants, Then, noise power shall be aggregated over receiving antennas (spatial streams): [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $K1 {norm}(u) = \sum_{a=1}^{nRx} K1_{norm,a}(u)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_1273.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] where nRx represents the number of receiving antennas. The noise power per sub-cell is then averaged over grants: [image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $K1_{cell} = \frac{\sum_{u=1}^{N_{grants}} K1_{norm}(u)}{N_{grants}}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_1273.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] Noise power is converted to dBm, with application of the scaling factor. Formulas are given in: - 5G_L1_4198_replaced( 11420323 ) for Loner L1 HW, - 5G_L1_4202_replaced( 11420640 ) for Loki L1 HW. The minimum value of noise power is -140 dBm: - if K1&lt;-140 dBm then K1=-140 dBm In case all I/Q data are set to 0, noise power shall be also set to -140 dBm. Noise power per subcell is reported in field noisePower of message UlData_PuschReceiveRespPs (see 5G_L1_IF_200_replaced( 11513831 )). [CB009794-F] Noise power per antenna (spatial stream) and per grant K1norm,a(u) in linear scale shall also be reported to L2-PS in field linNoiseOfAnt of message UlData_PuschReceiveRespPs (see 5G_L1_IF_202_replaced( 11513842 )). Unit is attoWatt (1 aW = 10-18 W) with 1 aW precision. Scaling factor shall be applied, as specified in: - 5G_L1_11093_replaced( 11420409 ) for Loner, - 5G_L1_11094_replaced( 11420853 ) for Loki. When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module on (e)CPRI interface for all antennas (spatial streams), the reported value shall be 10 aW. In case of a fake UE (see 5G_L1_11189_replaced( 11400702 )), the reported noise value for each antenna shall be set equal to the RSSI value (see 5G_L1_8319_replaced( 11404480 )), with 32-bit saturation. [End CB009794-F] (ID: 11404108)

##### 5.4.7.4.4.3 Thor

###### 5.4.7.4.4.3.0-3 Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor 13241737: Thor Noise power (measurement K1) noisePower (See 11513831: UlData_PuschReceiveRespPs .) Reported noise power per sub-cell (on allocated PUSCH resources) is the noise power on two or four antennas normalized to 1 PRB. Granularity of the reported value to L2-PS by 5G-L1-UL is 0.1 dBm. Its computation is described in section 2.7.3.1 of "5GMax algorithm specification". L1-SW (on Thor) computes noise power based on 1st DMRS symbol, it provides averaged noise power per antenna (spatial stream) and normalized to one RE (see 5G_L1_9064_replaced( 11401067 )). L1-SW shall normalize this noise power to one PRB: [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $K 1_{norm,a}(u) = N_{RB}^{SC} \times \hat{\sigma} a^2(u)$. The equation involves variables K, u, and a, as well as mathematical operators like multiplication and subscripts. The term $N {RB}^{SC}$ appears to be a variable with superscripts and subscripts, and $\hat{\sigma}_a^2(u)$ is a variable with a hat, a subscript, and a superscript.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_1276.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] where u represents the grant index, from 1 to N grants, [CB008577-C] For NR FDD beamforming deployments in Thor, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall apply scaling factor scomp according to suSplitpanelMode in UlData_PuschReceiveReq ( 11513756 ) to compensate the measurement for each spatial stream in case of split-panel beamforming. scomp is 1 for full panel beamforming case and therefore it can be ignored. Refer to section §5.3.1.1 of 12708616: Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification for the definition of scaling factor scomp [End CB008577-C] Then, noise power shall be aggregated over receiving antennas (spatial streams): [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $K1_{norm}(u) = \sum_{a=1}^{nRx} K1_{norm,a}(u)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_1276.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] where nRx represents the number of receiving antennas. The noise power per sub-cell is then averaged over grants: [image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $K1_{cell} = \frac{\sum_{u=1}^{N_{grants}} K1_{norm}(u)}{N_{grants}}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_1276.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] Noise power is converted to dBm, with application of the scaling factor. Formulas are given in: - 5G_L1_8903_replaced( 11421828 ) for Thor L1 HW, The minimum value of noise power is -140 dBm: - if K1&lt;-140 dBm then K1=-140 dBm In case all I/Q data are set to 0, noise power shall be also set to -140 dBm. Noise power per subcell is reported in field noisePower of message UlData_PuschReceiveRespPs (see 5G_L1_IF_200_replaced( 11513831 )). Noise power per antenna (spatial stream) and per grant K1 norm,a (u) in linear scale shall also be reported to L2-PS in field linNoiseOfAnt of message UlData_PuschReceiveRespPs (see 5G_L1_IF_202_replaced( 11513842 )). Unit is attoWatt (1 aW = 10-18 W) with 1 aW precision. ** Scaling factor shall be applied, as specified in: - 5G_L1_11561_replaced( 11422089 ) for Thor. When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module on (e)CPRI interface for all antennas (spatial streams), the reported value shall be 10 aW. In case of a fake UE (see 5G_L1_11189_replaced( 11400702 )), the reported noise value for each antenna shall be set equal to the RSSI value (see 5G_L1_8319_replaced( 11404480 )), with 32-bit saturation. (ID: 13241737)

Unit is attoWatt (1 aW = 10-18 W) with 1 aW precision. ** Scaling factor shall be applied, as specified in: - 5G_L1_11561_replaced( 11422089 ) for Thor. ### 5.4.7.5 Signal to Noise Ratio (SNR)

#### 5.4.7.5.1 Post equalized SNR compensation for Loki

**5.4.7.5.1.0-1**  (ID: `12590319`)

Post equalized SNR compensation for Loki
12590319:
Post equalized SNR compensation for Loki
12590319:
Post equalized SNR compensation for Loki
12590319:

12590319:
Post equalized SNR compensation for Loki
**[Before CB010981-F]**

5G-L1-UL(BB)(Loki) shall apply post-equalized SNR compensation to measured PUSCH SNR to achieve more accurate SNR estimation, when postEqSnrComp of [UlData\_PuschReceiveReq(11513756)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) is set to 1 for the subcell. Given measured signal noise ratio SNR', compensated signal noise ratio SNR shall be calculated as:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is:
$SNR = \rho \times (SNR' - \gamma) \times (1 + \theta \times SNR' + (\theta \times SNR')^2)$
The equation defines SNR in terms of other variables: $\rho$, $SNR'$, $\gamma$, and $\theta$. The multiplication is indicated by the '×' symbol. The term $SNR'$ is squared in the last part of the equation.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07101414.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains three Greek letters: rho (ρ), gamma (γ), and theta (θ), separated by commas. The letters are written in a cursive or handwritten style.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07101408.png[/image\_path][/image-1]are parameters determined by simulations using static AWGN channel model.

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression $(\rho, \gamma, \theta)$. The expression is enclosed in parentheses and consists of three Greek letters separated by commas. The Greek letters are rho ($\rho$), gamma ($\gamma$), and theta ($\theta$).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07101417.png[/image\_path][/image-2]triplets are defined in table below depending on the following criteria:

* DMRS/data FDM or non-FDM
* According to numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData ([11399474: FDM of DMRS and UL-SCH data](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e858LvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))
* PRB allocation: 1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 or more than 8 PRB.
* According to numOfPrb ([11398240: Frequency Domain Allocation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e81qu_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))
* UE rank/layers: 1 or 2
* According to antPort ([11398679: Spatial Configuration](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e84G8PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))
* Receiver type: 2RX or 4RX
* According to ulSubcellType ([11397316: Subcell Context Creation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8xZS_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ID | Receiver | # PRB | DMRS/data FDM | # layers | [image-3][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, grayscale depiction of a single character. The character appears to be a lowercase Greek letter gamma (γ). The top portion of the character is curved, resembling an arc, and the bottom portion is a vertical stroke that curves slightly to the right at its base. The overall image quality is low, with significant blurring and pixelation, making precise details difficult to discern.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07101417.png[/image\_path][/image-3] | [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, black and white rendering of the Greek letter theta (θ).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07101418.png[/image\_path][/image-4] | [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, lowercase Greek letter "rho" (ρ).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-07101417.png[/image\_path][/image-5] |

Notice!

The postEqSnrComp IE is added to tag L1-L2 IF tag 700 (26R1), however, the feature is postponed to 26R2.

In 26R1, 5G-L1-UL shall ignore postEqSnrComp IE and hardcode it to 0 (deactivated).

**[CB010981-F]**

This function is descoped from original feature CB010981.

**[End CB010981-F]**

#### 5.4.7.5.2 Post Equalized SU SINR in MU-MIMO Receiver

##### 5.4.7.5.2.0-2 Post Equalized SU SINR in MU-MIMO Receiver When C8 subcell is configured, L1 shall support post equalized SU SINR measurement in MU-MIMO receiver. Refer to 11108937 and 11404372: Aditional SINR Measurement for Virtual UE . [CB012115-IB] The requirement shall be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IB] (ID: 12730499)

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "sinrOfAntResp" in a dark, blurry font against a white background. The text is slightly slanted to the right.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1291.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]  array [4] float SINR (in dB) of the measured rx antenna stream

Calculated as specified in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1407

In case of 2Rx, the last two elements of this array shall be filled with invalid values (i.e. set to -255dB.). Refer to 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1412 for invalid value definition.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in white text against a black background. The formula is:
$sinrOfAnt\_{Resp} = 10 \log\_{10} \left( \frac{rxPower^{i}*{L1-SW}}{noisePower^{i}*{L1-SW}(n)} \right) dB$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1291.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

Where i ∈ {0,..,numRx-1} represents the Rx antenna index.

[PR577374] In the case of invalid measurements for SINR 5G-L1-UL shall report to 5G-L2-PS the value: -255 dB. See 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1447 [end PR577374]

---

#### 5.4.7.6.3 SINR Measurement per Stream

##### 5.4.7.6.3.1 Loner

**5.4.7.6.3.1.0-1**  (ID: `11404353`)

Loner
[CNI-59405]: CP2 part moved

Loner-L1-Only. FR1 TDD eCPRI beamforming only. For each scheduled UE on PUSCH, 5G-L1-UL shall compute one SINR measurement per rx antenna stream and NR-slot for beam management usage. Depending on the number of antenna streams, 2 or 4 estimates shall be then provided to 5G-L2-PS in the UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message.

The SINR estimation per rx antenna stream on PUSCH/SRS is described in 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916).

Note: although antenna-wise value is introduced for FR1 TDD Rx beamforming cells with eCPRI RU, L1's behavior is the same among:

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX beamforming

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 TDD 4Rx non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming

- FR2 TDD 2Rx

[End CNI-59405]

---

##### 5.4.7.6.3.2 Legacy Loki, Thor

**5.4.7.6.3.2.0-1**  (ID: `11404358`)

Legacy Loki, Thor
The content of this requirement is frozen and valid until the mentioned “Last System Release” attribute. For the most recent requirement, please refer to the artifacts below.

Loki-L1-~~Onl~~y **[RP003187-F, RP003187-M] and Thor** [End RP003187-F, RP003187-M]. For each scheduled UE on PUSCH, 5G-L1-UL shall compute one SINR measurement per rx antenna stream and NR-slot for beam management usage. Depending on the number of antenna streams, 2 or 4 estimates shall be then provided to 5G-L2-PS in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message.*\*\*\*\*

The SINR estimation per rx antenna stream on PUSCH/SRS is described in section 2.7.5.1 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see ~~5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916~~ 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: although antenna-wise value is introduced for FR1 TDD Rx beamforming cells with eCPRI RU, L1's behavior is the same among:

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX beamforming

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 TDD 4Rx non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming

**-****[CB008205-B] For Loki L1 HW, eCPRI** FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming **[End CB008205-B]*****\****\*

-    **[CB008205-C] For Loki L1 HW, eCPRI** FR1 FDD 2Rx non beamforming **[End CB008205-C]**\*\*\*\*

- FR2 TDD 2Rx

**[RP003187-F] The requirement is also applicable for** L1 Thor for FR1-TDD-eCPRI. **[End RP003187-F]**\*\*\*\*

**[RP003187-M] The requirement is also applicable for** L1 Thor for FR1-FDD-CPRI. **[End RP003187-M]**\*\*\*\*

**[Before-CNI-122313-A]**

**[CB009443-C] This requirement is also appliable for RINLINE 1 HW for FR1-TDD-eCPRI.** [end CB009443-C]\*\*\*\*

**[CNI-122313-A]**

RINLINE1 HW is supported until 24R1 inclusive. RINLINE1 HW is not supported since 24R2.

**[End CNI-122313-A]**

---

##### 5.4.7.6.3.3 Loki

**5.4.7.6.3.3.0-1**  (ID: `12732438`)

Loki
For each scheduled UE on PUSCH, 5G-L1-UL shall compute one SINR measurement per rx antenna stream and NR-slot for beam management usage. Depending on the number of antenna streams, 2 or 4 estimates shall be then provided to 5G-L2-PS in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message.*

The SINR estimation per rx antenna stream on PUSCH/SRS is described in section 2.7.5.1 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see ~~5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916~~ 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: although antenna-wise value is introduced for FR1 TDD Rx beamforming cells with eCPRI RU, L1's behavior is the same among:

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX beamforming

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 TDD 4Rx non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming

**-**eCPRI **FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming**\*\*\*\*

-    eCPRI **FR1 FDD 2Rx non beamforming**

- FR2 TDD 2Rx

---

##### 5.4.7.6.3.4 Thor

**5.4.7.6.3.4.0-2**  (ID: `12687122`)

Thor
For Thor and SU MIMO Receiver:

For each scheduled UE on PUSCH, 5G-L1-UL shall compute one SINR measurement per rx antenna stream and NR-slot for beam management usage. Depending on the number of antenna streams, 2 or 4 or 8 estimates shall be then provided to 5G-L2-PS in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message.*

[CB008465-AA] For NR FDD non-beamforming deployments, when UL CoMP is enabled, for each UE scheduled on PUSCH and configured as joint reception, 5G-L1-UL shall compute one SINR measurement per rx antenna stream for the Helper subcell in addition to the Serving subcell. For UL CoMP, the number of measurements would be twice the number of antennas for a single cell (8 in case of A4 subcell) and then shall be reported to 5G-L2-PS using UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs.PuschAntMeas.sinrOfAnt with Serving subcell measurements always placed first and that Helper subcell measurements follow contiguously.

The mapping between SINR and CPRI AxC spatial streams of Serving and Helper subcells is as follows

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type | Subcell | SINR for antenna i | CPRI spatial stream | **First feature** |
| A4 | Serving | **puschAntMeas[0].sinrOfAnt** | AxC0 | CB008465-AA |
| **puschAntMeas[1].sinrOfAnt** | AxC1 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[2].sinrOfAnt** | AxC2 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[3].sinrOfAnt** | AxC3 |  |  |  |
| Helper | **puschAntMeas[4].sinrOfAnt** | AxC0 |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[5].sinrOfAnt** | AxC1 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[6].sinrOfAnt** | AxC2 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[7].sinrOfAnt** | AxC3 |  |  |  |

[End CB008465-AA]

The SINR estimation per rx antenna stream on PUSCH/SRS is described in section 2.7.5.1 of 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see ~~5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916~~ 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: although antenna-wise value is introduced for FR1 TDD Rx beamforming cells with eCPRI RU, L1's behavior is the same among:

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX beamforming

- CPRI or eCPRI FR1 TDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 TDD 4Rx non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 2RX non beamforming

- CPRI FR1 FDD 4Rx non beamforming

-    For Thor L1 HW, eCPRI **FR1 TDD 8Rx beamforming**

-    [CB013442-D] For Thor L1 HW, eCPRI FR1 FDD 8Rx beamforming/non-beamforming. [End CB013442-D]

History:

As of CB010575-D, this requirement applies to FR1-TDD-CPRI. Parity from 5GC001294 requires supporting all 2Rx and 4Rx cases listed above.

As of CB011178-A, this requirement applied to FR1 FDD 7-2a eCPRI, for both 2Rx and 4Rx.

As of CB010969-CB, OBSAI has also been supported.

As of CB0009363, C8 in eCPRI 7-2a is supported. CB013442 adds the support for A8.

---

##### 5.4.7.6.3.5 Odin

**5.4.7.6.3.5.0-1**  (ID: `12732440`)

Odin
For Thor and SU MIMO Receiver:

For each scheduled UE on PUSCH, 5G-L1-UL shall compute one SINR measurement per rx antenna stream and NR-slot for beam management usage. Depending on the number of antenna streams, 2, 4, or 8 estimates shall be provided to 5G-L2-PS in sinrOfAnt in [11513842: PuschAntMeas](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of the *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message.*

The SINR estimation per rx antenna stream on PUSCH is described in section [#sec-MeasSinrPusch](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-MeasSinrPusch) of 5Gmax algorithm specifications.

The requirement, and its implementation, is common for all the Technologies.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.7.6.4 sinr: Post-equalized SINR per layer

**5.4.7.6.4.0-5**  (ID: `12732426`)

sinr: Post-equalized SINR per layer
For each scheduled UE on either PUSCH, 5GNB 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall estimate one measurement for the SINR which can be used as an indicator for the UL link adaptation. Each SINR measurement is computed at the equalizer output, per layer and averaged over all the allocated REs for one NR-slot. The signals received on all the active antennas are taken into account to derive this measurement which shall be performed for UL data received on PUSCH.

The post-equalization SINR shall be reported to L2PS in suPostCombSinr IE of [11513831: UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). In the case of invalid measurements for SINR 5G-L1-UL shall report to 5G-L2-PS the value: -255 dB.

Note: The value of post-equalizer SINR is depends on the equalizer type (e.g., MRC, IRC, etc.).

The requirement is common for all the Technologies, as the post-equalization SINR shall be calculated in the DMAP.

The requirement is supported in Loner, Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

12732431:
suPostCombSinr: Aditional SINR Measurement for Virtual UE
12732431:
suPostCombSinr: Aditional SINR Measurement for Virtual UE
For MU MIMO Receiver:

When C8 is configured, 5G-L1-UL shall compute one additional SINR measurement for each scheduled UE in a virtual UE if number of paired UEs is larger than two and postSuSinr from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq ([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) equals True, and report it to L2 per layer for LA purpose. Refer to suPostCombSinr in [11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

As compared to normal SINR measurement in MU MIMO receiver, same covariance matrix is used but only channel estimate of the selected paired UE is taken into account (results of other layers are zeroed out). The algorithm is described in section 2.7.5.1 of 5gmax algorithm specifications.

Note:

1. Only the pairing of root UE and normal UE supports post-equalized SU SINR estimation. The pairing of root UE and UE candidate group is out of the scope.
2. The IE 'suPostCombSinr' should be set as valid value only when 'postSuSinr' = True in C8 subcell and number of paired UEs is larger than 2. For all other cases, both elements of  'suPostCombSinr' should be set as invalid value (-255 dB).
3. The number of PRBs used for post-equalized SU SINR estimation is determined by rdparameter *rdNbPrbSuSinr ([11110497](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpE7A_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and they are selected uniformly from the paired UE within a virtual UE that has minimal allocated PRBs. In below example where 4 Rank1 UEs are paired and UE2 has the minimal PRB allocation (PRB0 to PRB4), assume* rdNbPrbSuSinr = 3, the selected PRBs for post-equalized SU SINR estimation are PRB0, PRB2, and PRB4.\*\*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid-like structure, resembling a spreadsheet, with colored cells and text labels. The title "DMRS port" is centered at the top. The grid has row numbers on the left, from 0 to 17.
The colored cells represent different User Equipments (UEs):
- **UE0** is a large yellow rectangle spanning rows 0 to 9 in the first column.
- **UE1** is an orange rectangle spanning rows 0 to 4 in the second column.
- **UE2** is a green rectangle spanning rows 0 to 4 in the third column.
- **UE3** is a purple rectangle spanning rows 0 to 4 in the fourth column.
- **UE4** is a large blue rectangle spanning rows 10 to 17 in the first column.
The remaining cells in the grid are white and appear to be empty or reserved for future use. The grid lines are clearly defined, creating distinct cells.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1302.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[PR852695]

1. PRB which contain DC, is not used for the suPostCombSinr measurement. If UE has only 1 PRB allocation and it contains DC, suPostCombSinr can not be measured and invalid is reported to L2. See section 2.3.6 in 5gmax algorithm specification for more info.

[end PR852695]

The requirement is supported in Thor and  [CB012115-IB] Odin [End CB012115-IB] for 7.2a split.

---

#### 5.4.7.6.6 Short-term pre-equalized SINR for UL CoMP

##### 5.4.7.6.6.0-2 Short-term pre-equalized SINR for UL CoMP In the case of invalid measurements for short-term pre-equalized SINR ( 12804072 ) shortTermSinr in 12772527: ulCompInfo , 5G-L1-UL shall report to 5G-L2-PS the value: -255 dB. The requirement is supported for Thor. (ID: 12822829)

#### 5.4.7.6.7 Invalid SINR Measurement

**5.4.7.6.7.0-4**  (ID: `12732432`)

Invalid SINR Measurement
For PUSCH, 5G-L1-UL shall report invalid measurement for SINR (antenna-wise sinrOfAnt IE in [11513842: PuschAntMeas](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and layer-wise sinr IE in [11513837: PuschMeasurementsResp](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) in message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs **when the Rx is failed (typically the case of RU lost or fiber broken or Rx failure).**

Invalid value of sinrOfAnt shall be set to -255(dB).

The requirement is supported in Loner, Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---
