11404474:
Legacy
RSSI computation (measurement M1)

L1-FPGA (on Loner) or L1-SW (on Loki) computes RSSI per antenna based on 1st DMRS pilot for each grant u. RSSI is averaged over layers and normalized to one PRB.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$M1\_{norm,a}(u) = \frac{N\_{RB}^{SC}}{N\_{layer}} \sum\_{l=0}^{N\_{layer}-1} \left( \frac{1}{N\_{pilot}^{PUSCH}} \sum\_{k=0}^{N\_{pilot}^{PUSCH}-1} (\hat{H}*{l,k,sep}^{pilot,a})^\* \cdot \hat{H}*{l,k,sep}^{pilot,a} \right)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1313.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[before PR587516]

RSSI per user is aggregated over antennas as follows:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$M 1\_{norm}(u) = \sum\_{a=0}^{N\_{RX}-1} M 1\_{norm,a}(u)$
This equation represents the sum of $M 1\_{norm,a}(u)$ for $a$ from 0 to $N\_{RX}-1$.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1313.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[PR587516]

De-boosting is applied to RSSI in order to compensate frequency domain multiplexing of DMRS:

[Before PR689325]

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$M1\_{deboost, a}(u) = \frac{M1\_{norm, a}(u)}{S^2\_{DMRSBoost, PUSCH}}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1313.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[PR689325]

[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$M1\_{deboost,a}(u) = \frac{(M1\_{norm,a}(u) - K1\_{norm,a}(u))}{S\_{DMRSBoost,PUSCH}^2} + K1\_{norm,a}(u)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1313.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

where:

* K1norm,a(u) is the noise power estimator for antenna a and user u as described in 5G\_L1\_4743\_replaced([11404108](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9SWovvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* SDMRSBoost,PUSCH is the power boost factor for PUSCH DMRS (see 5GMax Algoithm Specification § 2.7.2.1).

[End PR689325]

RSSI per user is aggregated over antennas as follows:

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$M1\_{deboost}(u) = \sum\_{a=0}^{N\_{RX}-1} M1\_{deboost,a}(u)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1313.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

[end PR587516]

Aggregated RSSI and antenna-wise RSSI are reported to L2-PS as described in 5G\_L1\_8319\_replaced([11404480](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9UL0PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 5.4.7.7.2 linRssiOfAnt: Antenna-wise RSSI

##### 5.4.7.7.2.1 Loner

**5.4.7.7.2.1.0-1**  (ID: `12823697`)

Loner
5G-L1-UL shall estimate RSSI on PUSCH per slot, per antenna (spatial stream) and normalized to 1 PRB. 5G-L1-UL shall report it though *rssiOfAnt in [11513842: PuschAntMeas](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. The RSSI estimation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section [#sec-MeasRssiPusch](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-MeasRssiPusch) of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications. Measurements based deboosted channel estimate shall be reported.\*\*

The lin*RssiOfAnt parameter shall be reported in linear scale. Unit is attoWatt (1 aW = 10-18 W) with precision of 1 aW. When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module, the reported value shall be 10 aW.*

In case of a fake UE, RSSI measurement shall be reported similarly. As no real signal is supposed to be received, RSSI measurement are considered as noise and interference measurement in L2.

---

##### 5.4.7.7.2.2 Loki

**5.4.7.7.2.2.0-1**  (ID: `12823700`)

Loki
5G-L1-UL shall estimate RSSI on PUSCH per slot, per antenna (spatial stream) and normalized to 1 PRB. 5G-L1-UL shall report it though *rssiOfAnt in [11513842: PuschAntMeas](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. The RSSI estimation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section [#sec-MeasRssiPusch](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-MeasRssiPusch) of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications. Measurements based deboosted channel estimate shall be reported.\*\*

The lin*RssiOfAnt parameter shall be reported in linear scale. Unit is attoWatt (1 aW = 10-18 W) with precision of 1 aW. When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module, the reported value shall be 10 aW.*

In case of a fake UE, RSSI measurement shall be reported similarly. As no real signal is supposed to be received, RSSI measurement are considered as noise and interference measurement in L2.

---

##### 5.4.7.7.2.3 Thor

**5.4.7.7.2.3.0-3**  (ID: `12823707`)

Thor
5G-L1-UL shall estimate RSSI on PUSCH per slot, per antenna (spatial stream) and normalized to 1 PRB. 5G-L1-UL shall report it though *rssiOfAnt in [11513842: PuschAntMeas](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. The RSSI estimation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section [#sec-MeasRssiPusch](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-MeasRssiPusch) of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications. Measurements based deboosted channel estimate shall be reported.\*\*

The lin*RssiOfAnt parameter shall be reported in linear scale. Unit is attoWatt (1 aW = 10-18 W) with precision of 1 aW. When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module, the reported value shall be 10 aW.*

For NR FDD beamforming deployments, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall apply scaling factor scomp according to suSplitpanelMode in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq ([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)) to compensate the measurement for each spatial stream in case of split-panel beamforming. scomp is 1 for full panel beamforming case and therefore it can be ignored. Refer to section §5.3.1.1 of [12708616: Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_aUzKUWMCEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for the definition of scaling factor scomp.

In case of a fake UE, RSSI measurement shall be reported similarly. As no real signal is supposed to be received, RSSI measurement are considered as noise and interference measurement in L2.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Subcell Type | Subcell | RSSI for antenna i | CPRI spatial stream | **First feature** |
| A4 | Serving | **puschAntMeas[0].rssiOfAnt** | AxC0 | CB008465-AA |
| **puschAntMeas[1].rssiOfAnt** | AxC1 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[2].rssiOfAnt** | AxC2 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[3].rssiOfAnt** | AxC3 |  |  |  |
| Helper | **puschAntMeas[4].rssiOfAnt** | AxC0 |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[5].rssiOfAnt** | AxC1 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[6].rssiOfAnt** | AxC2 |  |  |  |
| **puschAntMeas[7].rssiOfAnt** | AxC3 |  |  |  |

---

##### 5.4.7.7.2.4 Odin

**5.4.7.7.2.4.0-1**  (ID: `12732846`)

Odin
5G-L1-UL shall estimate RSSI on PUSCH per slot, per antenna (spatial stream) and normalized to 1 PRB. 5G-L1-UL shall report it though *rssiOfAnt in [11513842: PuschAntMeas](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. The RSSI estimation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section [#sec-MeasRssiPusch](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-MeasRssiPusch) of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications. Measurements based deboosted channel estimate shall be reported.\*\*

The lin*RssiOfAnt parameter shall be reported in linear scale. Unit is attoWatt (1 aW = 10-18 W) with precision of 1 aW. When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module, the reported value shall be 10 aW.*

In case of a fake UE, RSSI measurement shall be reported similarly. As no real signal is supposed to be received, RSSI measurement are considered as noise and interference measurement in L2.

---

#### 5.4.7.7.3 rssi

##### 5.4.7.7.3.1 Loner

**5.4.7.7.3.1.0-1**  (ID: `12823689`)

Loner
5G-L1-UL shall estimate RSSI of a UE on PUSCH per slot, averaged over all antennas and normalized to 1 PRB. 5G-L1-UL shall report it through *rssi in [11513837: PuschMeasurementsResp](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. The RSSI estimation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section [#sec-MeasRssiPusch](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-MeasRssiPusch) of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications. Measurements based on deboosted channel estimate shall be reported.\*\*

The *rssi  parameter shall be reported in dBm unit, with precision of 0.1dBm. When only zero-valued samples are got from the radio module for all the receiving antennas (e.g. broken antenna scenario), 5G-L1-UL shall set the* rssi message to -140dBm.\*\*

In case of a fake UE, RSSI measurement shall be reported similarly. As no real signal is supposed to be received, RSSI measurements are be considered as noise and interference measurement in L2.

---

##### 5.4.7.7.3.2 Loki

**5.4.7.7.3.2.0-1**  (ID: `12823690`)

Loki
5G-L1-UL shall estimate RSSI of a UE on PUSCH per slot, averaged over all antennas and normalized to 1 PRB. 5G-L1-UL shall report it through *rssi in [11513837: PuschMeasurementsResp](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. The RSSI estimation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section [#sec-MeasRssiPusch](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-MeasRssiPusch) of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications. Measurements based on deboosted channel estimate shall be reported.\*\*

The *rssi  parameter shall be reported in dBm unit, with precision of 0.1dBm. When only zero-valued samples are got from the radio module for all the receiving antennas (e.g. broken antenna scenario), 5G-L1-UL shall set the* rssi message to -140dBm.\*\*

In case of a fake UE, RSSI measurement shall be reported similarly. As no real signal is supposed to be received, RSSI measurements are be considered as noise and interference measurement in L2.

---

##### 5.4.7.7.3.3 Thor

**5.4.7.7.3.3.0-3**  (ID: `12823691`)

Thor
5G-L1-UL shall estimate RSSI of a UE on PUSCH per slot, averaged over all antennas and normalized to 1 PRB. 5G-L1-UL shall report it through *rssi in [11513837: PuschMeasurementsResp](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. The RSSI estimation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section [#sec-MeasRssiPusch](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-MeasRssiPusch) of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications. Measurements based on deboosted channel estimate shall be reported.\*\*

For NR FDD beamforming deployments, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall apply scaling factor scomp according to suSplitpanelMode in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq ([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)) to compensate the measurement for each spatial stream in case of split-panel beamforming. scomp is 1 for full panel beamforming case and therefore it can be ignored. Refer to section §5.3.1.1 of [12708616: Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_aUzKUWMCEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for the definition of scaling factor scomp.

The *rssi  parameter shall be reported in dBm unit, with precision of 0.1dBm. When only zero-valued samples are got from the radio module for all the receiving antennas (e.g. broken antenna scenario), 5G-L1-UL shall set the* rssi message to -140dBm.\*\*

In case of a fake UE, RSSI measurement shall be reported similarly. As no real signal is supposed to be received, RSSI measurements are be considered as noise and interference measurement in L2.

---

##### 5.4.7.7.3.4 Odin

**5.4.7.7.3.4.0-1**  (ID: `12732858`)

Odin
5G-L1-UL shall estimate RSSI of a UE on PUSCH per slot, averaged over all antennas and normalized to 1 PRB. 5G-L1-UL shall report it through *rssi in [11513837: PuschMeasurementsResp](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) of the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. The RSSI estimation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section [#sec-MeasRssiPusch](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-MeasRssiPusch) of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications. Measurements based on deboosted channel estimate shall be reported.\*\*

The *rssi  parameter shall be reported in dBm unit, with precision of 0.1dBm. When only zero-valued samples are got from the radio module for all the receiving antennas (e.g. broken antenna scenario), 5G-L1-UL shall set the* rssi message to -140dBm.\*\*

In case of a fake UE, RSSI measurement shall be reported similarly. As no real signal is supposed to be received, RSSI measurements are be considered as noise and interference measurement in L2.

12732874:
Received Total Wideband Power (RTWP)
12732874:
Received Total Wideband Power (RTWP)
12732874:
Received Total Wideband Power (RTWP)
5G-L1-UL shall calculate the Received Total Wideband Power (RTWP) which is the power received in the whole configured UL bandwidth including all configured UL channels/signal as well as unallocated PRBs.

It shall be calculated as the sum of power of all subcarriers *Y (after FFT) in one OFDM symbol* l within *ulBandwidth defined in PRBs in* UlCell\_SetupReq calculated per antenna stream (AxC):\*\*\*\*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$RTWP\_{ant,l} = \sum\_{k=0}^{N\_{SC}^{RB} \* ulBandwidth - 1} |Y\_{k}^{ant,l}|^2 \left( \frac{rxScalingFactor}{[s\_{pre}(ant)]^2 N\_{FFT}} \right)$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1321.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N^RB\_sc". The text is in a stylized font with a gradient effect, appearing to be in shades of blue, orange, and yellow. The "N" is large and in the foreground, with "RB" superscripted above and to the right of it. The "sc" is subscripted below and to the right of the "N". The text is set against a white background with a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1321.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]*,* rxScalingFactor, spre, NFFT are defined in 5GMAX algorithm specification (sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.3).\*\*

The OFDM symbol *for RTWP measurement is chosen in a following way:*

If *numOfGrants in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq = 0, then\*\*

* The symbol for RTWP measurements is the first available UL symbol in a slot.

If *numOfGrants in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is > 0, then\*\*

* 5G-L1-UL calculates the symbol positions of the front-loaded DM-RS for each grant as specified in ~~5G\_L1\_5G21A\_947~~ 5G\_L1\_5906\_replaced([11493500](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fEhnHvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case of two symbol front-loaded DM-RS, these symbols are treated as separate ones.
* For each symbol which contains DM-RS, 5G-L1-UL calculates the total bandwidth occupancy in PRBs.
* The symbol for RTWP measurement is the one which has the highest bandwidth occupancy in PRBs.
* If two symbols have the same bandwidth occupancy in PRBs, the first (with the lower index *l) symbol is taken.*

RTWP is reported in dBm units with 0.1 dBm step for each antenna (AxC) in *rtwpOfAnt in [11513831: UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)* message.\*\*

For NR FDD beamforming deployments in Thor, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall apply scaling factor scomp according to suSplitpanelMode in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq ([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)) to compensate the measurement for each spatial stream in case of split-panel beamforming. scomp is 1 for full panel beamforming case and therefore it can be ignored. Refer to section §5.3.1.1 of [12708616: Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_aUzKUWMCEfC3KtQ77je1xA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for the definition of scaling factor scomp.

When only zero-valued samples are received from the radio module interface for the receiving antenna stream, 5G-L1-UL shall set the *rtwpOfAnt parameter to -140 dBm for given antenna sent in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message.\*\*

The requirement is supported in Loner, Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

### 5.4.7.9 Discontinued Transmission (DTX) - Partial Detection

#### 5.4.7.9.1 Algorithm

**5.4.7.9.1.0-3**  (ID: `12733005`)

Algorithm
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall implement DTX detection based on 5GMax Algorithm Specification. Please refer to chapter [#sec-PuschDtxDet](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschDtxDet), for [IFFT-based](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschIfftDtxDet) and [SNR-based](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PuschSnrDtxDet) DTX detection.

The number of RX antennas is one parameter for DTX threshold and there are specific tables for 8RX case. Please refer to the latest version of [NrDtxDetSnrBasedThrTablesPUSCH f.m](https://gerrit.ext.net.nokia.com/gerrit/gitweb?p=MN/5GMAX/5gmax.git;a=blob;f=source/SymbolProcessing/NR5G/ParamEst/matlab/NrDtxDetSnrBasedThrTablesPUSCH_f.m;hb=HEAD) of the 5GMax simulator for the up-to-date thresholds.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

The requirement is supported in Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.7.9.2 DTX Metric

**5.4.7.9.2.0-2**  (ID: `12733011`)

DTX Metric
5G-L1-UL shall compute the PUSCH DTX metric according to the algorithm described in section 2.3.8 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications. The IFFT based method shall be used to derive the DTX metric.

Then, 5G-L1-UL shall report its value to 5G-L2-PS in the two messages *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs (see [11513837: PuschMeasurementsResp](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU (see [11513897](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlD_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).\*\*

Note: The false alarm target for this metric is set to 0.5% with false alarm is defined as the probability to detect data whereas nothing was sent on PUSCH.

PUSCH DTX implementation is based on "antenna-wise" approach described in section ~~2.3.7~~ "2.3.8 PUSCH DTX Detection" of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications, with DTX thresholds are those associated to "Shadow spec version 15.2".

5G-L1-UL shall also compute PUSCH DTX metric and report it to 5G-L2-PS as above in Loki L1 HW.

The requirement is supported in Loner, Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.7.9.3 DTX Threshold Selection

##### 5.4.7.9.3.1 Loner

**5.4.7.9.3.1.0-1**  (ID: `11404604`)

Loner
For Loner. Depending on the number of layers (either one layer for single antenna transmission and spatial multiplexing rank 1, or 2 to 4 layers for spatial multiplexing rank 2 to 4) and the number of allocated PRBs, 5G-L1-UL shall select the appropriate threshold for DTX decision as below. This selection shall be performed for each UE scheduled on PUSCH.

1.  From UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message, 5G-L1-UL gets the two parameters numOfLayers and numOfPrb. Supported values for numOfPrb are given in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_935 5G\_L1\_2818\_replaced([11398355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e82RyvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Obtain the numOfEffectivePrb as below:

If numOfPrb >= 43

numOfEffectivePrb = 43

Else

numOfEffectivePrb = numOfPrb

EndIf

2.  According to the numOfAntennas, numOfLayers, numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData and Occ status, 5G-L1-UL shall consider the right R&D parameter in below table. Let's denote PuschThresholds the array (size of 43) associated to the selected R&D parameter.

• The numOfAntennas is derived according to 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_919 5G\_L1\_2797\_replaced([11481465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fDeeOPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

• The numOfLayers shall be derived directly from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_198\_replaced([11513795](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_vvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

• The Occ status is derived according to numOfLayers and antPort parameter in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_194\_replaced([11513774](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-vvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

o If numOfLayers equals to 1, then the Occ status is always No.

o If numOfLayers equals to 2, then if antPort equals to 0xA000, then the Occ status is No, else the Occ status is Yes.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| numOfAntennas | numOfLayers | Occ | R&D parameter |
| 2 | 1 | No | rdPuschThresholds2RxSingleLayer (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1488 5G\_L1\_3376) |
| 2 | No | rdPuschThresholds2RxTwoLayerWithoutOcc (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1489 5G\_L1\_3377) |  |
| 2 | Yes | rdPuschThresholds2RxTwoLayerWithOcc (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1490 5G\_L1\_3378) |  |

Then, 5G-L1-UL shall use dtxThreshold  = PuschThresholds [numOfEffectivePrb - 1] to compute the DTX value (see  5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1414 5G\_L1\_8345\_replaced([11404583](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9UL3fvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The DTX indication shall be provided to 5G-L2-PS as described in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1414 5G\_L1\_8345\_replaced([11404583](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9UL3fvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[5GC000836-P] [5GC001848-A] For 4Rx configuration, the applied dtxThreshold values are the thresholds of 2 antennas multiplied by the factor in 5GMAX algorithm spec chapter 2.3.7. 2.3.8 [End 5GC000836-P] [End 5GC001848-A]

[5GC001868-A] 5G-L1-UL shall support the algorithm descripted above with numOfLayers=1 and numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData=2, in Loki L1 HW.[End 5GC001868-A]

[5GC001868-C, 5GC001868-D, 5GC001868-E] In addition, 5G-L1-UL shall support the algorithm descripted above with numOfLayers=2 and numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData=2 in Loki L1 HW.[End 5GC001868-C, 5GC001868-D, 5GC001868-E]

[5GC002056-AA, 5GC002481-AA] 5G-L1-UL shall support the algorithm descripted above for all the configured values in L1 Loki HW [End 5GC002056-AA, 5GC002481-AA]

---

##### 5.4.7.9.3.2 SoC (Loki, Thor, Odin)

**5.4.7.9.3.2.0-2**  (ID: `12733024`)

SoC (Loki, Thor, Odin)
Depending on number of antennas, number of layers, number of DMRS CDM group without data, orthogonal code coverage status and the number of allocated PRBs, 5G-L1-UL shall select the appropriate threshold for DTX decision as below. This selection shall be performed for each UE scheduled on PUSCH.

1.  From *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message, 5G-L1-UL gets the two parameters* numOfLayers and *numOfPrb. Obtain the* numOfEffectivePrb as below:\*\*\*\*

If *numOfPrb >= 43*

*numOfEffectivePrb = 43*

Else

*numOfEffectivePrb =* numOfPrb\*\*

EndIf

2.  According to the *numOfAntennas, numOfLayers, numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData and* Occ status, 5G-L1-UL shall select proper set of PUSCH DTX thresholds (refer to the latest version of  [NrDtxDetSnrBasedThrTablesPUSCH\_f.m](https://gerrit.ext.net.nokia.com/gerrit/gitweb?p=MN/5GMAX/5gmax.git;a=blob_plain;f=source/SymbolProcessing/NR5G/ParamEst/matlab/NrDtxDetSnrBasedThrTablesPUSCH_f.m;hb=HEAD), and [11408082: DTX detection threshold](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9mfsfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)):\*\*

• The *numOfAntennas is derived according to ([11481465](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fDeeOPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))*

• The *numOfLayers shall be derived directly from* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message (see [11513795](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9_vvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for *PuschDecoderConfig)*\*\*

 The *numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData is derived directly from* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message (see [11513764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for *PuschDmrsConfig)*\*\*

• The *Occ status is derived according to* numOfLayers and *antPort parameter in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message (see [11513774](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9-vvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for *PuschSpatialConfig).*\*\*\*\*

o If *numOfLayers equals to 1, then the* Occ status is always No.\*\*

o If *numOfLayers equals to 2, then if* antPort equals to 0xA000, then the *Occ status is No, else the* Occ status is Yes.\*\*\*\*

The DTX indication shall be provided to 5G-L2-PS as described in [11404583](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9UL3fvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

For 4Rx configuration, the applied dtxThreshold values are the thresholds of 2 antennas multiplied by the factor in 5GMAX algorithm spec chapter 2.3.8.

5G-L1-UL shall support the algorithm descripted above with numOfLayers up to 2, and numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData=2*.*

The requirement is common for all the Technologies for which the equalizer resides on DU side.

The requirement is supported in Loki and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

### 5.4.7.10 Rank Indicator (RI)

#### 5.4.7.10.1 Rank computation

**5.4.7.10.1.0-6**  (ID: `12687130`)

Rank computation
5G-L1-UL shall compute the UL rank. The rank metric computation is described in section[#sec-RiSelectRiSel](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-RiSelectRiSel) of the 5GMax Algorithm Specification. The link level simulations [are available here](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?RootFolder=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FCFAMs%2F5GC0200%2D5GC0399%2F5GC000331%2D438%2DmMIMO%20Digital%20Beamforming%20%2D%2016%20stream%2Ftechnical%20material&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376). (“[RI selection optimization.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/CFAMs/5GC1800-5GC1999/5GC001981%20-%20L1%20algorithm%20changes%20-%205G19B/Simulation%20Reports/RI%20selection%20optimization.pptx)”)

The Number of subcarriers to be used to calculate UL rank is 6 per allocated subcarriers. The subcarriers used to calculate UL rank are distributed in equal distance. More details are described in section "RI Selection" in 5Gmax algorithm specification.

Per RX antenna SNR is used in RI selection.

The computation of the UL rank metric depends on parameter value of *spatialMode,* numOfLayerAndReMapping, *and in case of more than or equal to 4Rx receiver is used,* pBoost, as described in the algorithm document. These parameters are received in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message.*\*\*\*\*

For MU MIMO UEs following rules shall be applied:

1. For cell type C8 with **disabled enhancement L1 noise computation algorithm (actPmiSinrUlMuMimo==false in messages UlCell\_SetupReq or UlCell\_ParameterReconfigurationReq) 5G-L1-UL shall compute rank:**

- using noise from all DMRS REs,

- using own UE antennas or UE beams.

1. For cell type C8 with **enabled enhancement L1 noise computation algorithm (actPmiSinrUlMuMimo==true in messages UlCell\_SetupReq or UlCell\_ParameterReconfigurationReq) 5G-L1-UL shall compute rank:**

- using noise from own DMRS REs only,

* using all antennas.
* When subcell types not equal to C8, 5G-L1-UL shall ignore parameter actPmiSinrUlMuMimo and shall compute rank:

- using noise from all DMRS REs,

- using own UE antennas or UE beams.

The computation of the UL rank metric is done when PUSCH is received with spatial multiplexing rank 2 (numOfLayers==2). The computed UL rank shall be sent as *ulRank to 5G-L2-PS in the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs(U) message.\*\*

For spatial multiplexing rank 1 or single antenna Tx/singleTx, the UL rank metric is not computed and the parameter *ulRank is forced to INVALID value and sent to 5G-L2-PS in the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs(U) message.\*\*

*ulRank parameter is described in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs (response after PUSCH reception) in 5G\_L1\_IF\_200\_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*

The parameter *ulRiSelectionThreshold in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq shall be used for rank metric computation for 2 layer PUSCH reception. L1 shall use this parameter instead of R&D parameter *rdUlRiSelectionThresh under the doncitions described in 5G\_L1\_15264\_replaced([11404689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9VZ8vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*\*\*

Note: where *ulRiSelectionThreshold in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is *an UE specific parameter 'RI selection threshold', and is "alpha" in 5GMax Algorithm Specification. See 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*\*\*

This requirement also applies to Thor for FR2-TDD-eCPRI.

The requirement is also applicable for L1 **Thor (BB) for FR1-FDD-OBSAI.**

[CB013442-D] 5G-L1-UL shall perform Rank Indicator selection for SU receiver according to this requirement when A8 subcells in configured. [End CB013442-D]

---

##### 5.4.7.10.1.1 Odin

**5.4.7.10.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `12733131`)

Odin
5G-L1-UL shall compute the UL rank. The rank metric computation is described in section[#sec-RiSelectRiSel](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-RiSelectRiSel) of the 5GMax Algorithm Specification. The link level simulations [are available here](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?RootFolder=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FCFAMs%2F5GC0200%2D5GC0399%2F5GC000331%2D438%2DmMIMO%20Digital%20Beamforming%20%2D%2016%20stream%2Ftechnical%20material&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376). (“[RI selection optimization.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/CFAMs/5GC1800-5GC1999/5GC001981%20-%20L1%20algorithm%20changes%20-%205G19B/Simulation%20Reports/RI%20selection%20optimization.pptx)”)

The Number of subcarriers to be used to calculate UL rank is 6 per allocated subcarriers. The subcarriers used to calculate UL rank are distributed in equal distance. More details are described in section "RI Selection" in 5Gmax algorithm specification.

Per RX antenna SNR is used in RI selection.

The computation of the UL rank metric depends on parameter value of *spatialMode,* numOfLayerAndReMapping, *and in case of more than or equal to 4Rx receiver is used,* pBoost, as described in the algorithm document. These parameters are received in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message.*\*\*\*\*

[CB012115-IB]

For MU MIMO UEs following rules shall be applied:

1. For cell type C8 with **disabled enhancement L1 noise computation algorithm (actPmiSinrUlMuMimo==false in messages UlCell\_SetupReq or UlCell\_ParameterReconfigurationReq) 5G-L1-UL shall compute rank:**

- using noise from all DMRS REs,

- using own UE antennas or UE beams.

1. For cell type C8 with **enabled enhancement L1 noise computation algorithm (actPmiSinrUlMuMimo==true in messages UlCell\_SetupReq or UlCell\_ParameterReconfigurationReq) 5G-L1-UL shall compute rank:**

- using noise from own DMRS REs only,

* using all antennas.
* When subcell types not equal to C8, 5G-L1-UL shall ignore parameter actPmiSinrUlMuMimo and shall compute rank:

- using noise from all DMRS REs,

- using own UE antennas or UE beams.

[End CB012115-IB]

The computation of the UL rank metric is done when PUSCH is received with spatial multiplexing rank 2 (numOfLayers==2). The computed UL rank shall be sent as *ulRank to 5G-L2-PS in the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs(U) message.\*\*

For spatial multiplexing rank 1 or single antenna Tx/singleTx, the UL rank metric is not computed and the parameter *ulRank is forced to INVALID value and sent to 5G-L2-PS in the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs(U) message.\*\*

*ulRank parameter is described in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs (response after PUSCH reception) in [11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).\*\*

New parameter *ulRiSelectionThreshold in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq shall be support for rank metric computation for 2 layer PUSCH reception. L1 shall use this parameter instead of R&D parameter *rdUlRiSelectionThresh under the doncitions described in [11404689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9VZ8vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).*\*\*

Note: where *ulRiSelectionThreshold in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is *an UE specific parameter 'RI selection threshold', and is "alpha" in 5GMax Algorithm Specification. See [11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).*\*\*

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

---

#### 5.4.7.10.2 Rank Indicator (RI) selection threshold

##### 5.4.7.10.2.0-1 Rank Indicator (RI) selection threshold As of CNI-108786, the R&amp;D parameter rdUlRiSelectionThresh has been replaced with IE ulRiSelectionThresh. Refer to 11404652: Rank computation . (ID: 12733155)

##### 5.4.7.10.2.1 Duplicate; RI selection threshold

###### 5.4.7.10.2.1.0-6 Duplicate; RI selection threshold When C8 [CB013442-D] or A8 [End CB013442-D] subcell is configured L1 shall use PuschReceiveReq parameter ulRiSelectionThreshold and pBoost for ulRank (RI) computation in 2 layers PUSCH reception. For the parameter ulRiSelectionThreshold and pBoost detail, refer to 5G_L1_IF_191_replaced( 11513756 ). (ID: 12687087)

### 5.4.7.11 Transmitted Precoding Matrix Indicator (TPMI) selection

#### 5.4.7.11.1 Legacy

**5.4.7.11.1.0-1**  (ID: `11404704`)

Legacy
TPMI selection is described in subclause 2.8 of 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_L1\_2794).

The computation of the TPMI is done when PUSCH is received with spatial multiplexing rank 2 (numOfLayers =2 from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq). The computed TPMIs and corresponding SINR values shall be sent to 5G-L2-PS in the UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message:

- ulRank

- ulPmiRank1 is the result of MECG metric. Supported values are [0..5].

- ulPmiRank1Sinr is the SINR for given ulPmiRank1 expressed in dB.

- ulPmiRank2 is the result of [Before 5GC001981-B] MMP metric [end 5GC001981-B][5GC001981-B] SLP metric [end 5GC001981-B].

Supported values are [0..2]. The simulation report is available at “[NR\_UplinkPMISelection.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/CFAMs/5GC1800-5GC1999/5GC001981%20-%20L1%20algorithm%20changes%20-%205G19B/Simulation%20Reports/NR_UplinkPMISelection.pptx)”.

- ulPmiRank2Sinr  is the SINR for given ulPmiRank2 expressed in dB.

The number of equidistant subcarriers considered for PMI evaluation on PUSCH is 12.

The codebook used for the computation of the metrics depends on parameter value of spatialMode, numOfLayer and codebookIndex as described in the algorithm document. These parameters come within the UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message described in 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[5GC000532-H] Also, the codebook used for the computation of the metrics depend on puschTransCoherence IE that comes within the UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message described in 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). when puschTransCoherence == nonCoherent, the codebooks are given in 5G\_L1\_4935\_replaced([11398799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e84G9_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When puschTransCoherence == fullCoherent, the codebooks are given in 5G\_L1\_4935\_replaced([11398799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e84G9_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For spatial multiplexing rank 1 (numOfLayers =1 from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq), the UL TPMI selection metrics are not computed and the parameters:

- ulRank,

- ulPmiRank1,

- ulPmiRank2

are set to INVALID value (255) and sent to 5G-L2-PS in the UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. (Note: UlData\_SrsReceiveRespPs (5G\_L1\_254\_replaced([11483756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fDtHufvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is used instead.)

In that case, also corresponding SINR values of

- ulPmiRank1Sinr and

- ulPmiRank2Sinr

are not meaningful. [CB007166-C] In such a case, SINR value shall be set to INVALID value as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8414 (-255 dB) [End CB007166-C].

For spatial mode single antenna (singleTx), the UL TPMI selection metrics are not computed and the parameter ulPmiRank1 and ulPmiRank2 are set to INVALID value and sent to 5G-L2-PS in the UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. Values of ulPmiRank1Sinr and ulPmiRank2Sinr are also not meaningful in that case.

[CB007166-C] In such a case, SINR value shall be set to INVALID value as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8414 (-255 dB) [End CB007166-C].

The UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs is described in 5G\_L1\_IF\_200\_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[5GC001868-A] 5G-L1-UL shall not compute UL TPMI selection metrics and set ulPmiRank1 and ulPmiRank2 to INVALID in the response to 5G-L2-PS in Loki L1 HW [End 5GC001868-A]

[5GC001868-C, 5GC001868-D, 5GC001868-E] 5G-L1-UL shall support TPMI selection metrics algorithm described above in Loki L1 HW [End 5GC001868-C, 5GC001868-D, 5GC001868-E]

[CB007814-G] This requirement also applies to ABIO FR1-FDD-eCPRI. [End CB007814-G]

[RP003187-D] L1 Thor shall support all rank metric computation as described in section "2.8 PMI selection" of the 5GMax algorithm specification for FR1-TDD-eCPRI [End RP003187-D]

[RP003187-K] L1 Thor shall support all rank metric computation as described in section "2.8 PMI selection" of the 5GMax algorithm specification for FR1-FDD-CPRI [End RP003187-K]

[CB008205-B, CB008205-C] 5G-L1-UL shall also support requirements above in ABIO for O-RAN compliant FR1 FDD eCPRI. [End CB008205-B, CB008205-C]

[CB009444-B] This requirement is applicable for RINLINE1 for FR1-FDD-eCPRI. [End CB009444-B]

---

#### 5.4.7.11.2 Loner

**5.4.7.11.2.0-1**  (ID: `12771947`)

Loner
TPMI selection is described in subclause 2.8 of 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_L1\_2794).

The computation of the TPMI is done when PUSCH is received with spatial multiplexing rank 2 (*numOfLayers =2 from* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq). The computed TPMIs and corresponding SINR values shall be sent to 5G-L2-PS in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message:*\*\*

- *ulRank*

- *ulPmiRank1 is the result of MECG metric. Supported values are [0..5].*

- *ulPmiRank1Sinr is the SINR for given* ulPmiRank1 expressed in dB.\*\*

- *ulPmiRank2 is the result of SLP metric.*

Supported values are [0..2]. The simulation report is available at “[NR\_UplinkPMISelection.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/CFAMs/5GC1800-5GC1999/5GC001981%20-%20L1%20algorithm%20changes%20-%205G19B/Simulation%20Reports/NR_UplinkPMISelection.pptx)”.

- *ulPmiRank2Sinr*is the SINR for given *ulPmiRank2 expressed in dB.*\*\*

The number of equidistant subcarriers considered for PMI evaluation on PUSCH is 12.

The codebook used for the computation of the metrics depends on parameter value of *spatialMode,* numOfLayer and *codebookIndex as described in the algorithm document. These parameters come within the* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message described in 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

Also, the codebook used for the computation of the metrics depend on *puschTransCoherence IE that comes within the* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message described in 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). when *puschTransCoherence == nonCoherent, the codebooks are given in 5G\_L1\_4935\_replaced([11398799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e84G9_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When* puschTransCoherence == fullCoherent, the codebooks are given in 5G\_L1\_4935\_replaced([11398799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e84G9_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

For spatial multiplexing rank 1 (*numOfLayers =1 from* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq), the UL TPMI selection metrics are not computed and the parameters:\*\*

- *ulRank,*

- *ulPmiRank1,*

*- ulPmiRank2*

are set to INVALID value (255) and sent to 5G-L2-PS in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. (Note: UlData\_SrsReceiveRespPs (5G\_L1\_254\_replaced([11483756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fDtHufvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is used instead.)*

In that case, also corresponding SINR values of

- *ulPmiRank1Sinr and*

- *ulPmiRank2Sinr*

are not meaningful.

For spatial mode single antenna (singleTx), the UL TPMI selection metrics are not computed and the parameter *ulPmiRank1 and ulPmiRank2 are set to INVALID value and sent to 5G-L2-PS in the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. Values of *ulPmiRank1Sinr and* ulPmiRank2Sinr are also not meaningful in that case.\*\*\*\*

The *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs is described in 5G\_L1\_IF\_200\_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

---

#### 5.4.7.11.3 Loki and Thor

**5.4.7.11.3.0-4**  (ID: `12687131`)

Loki and Thor
5G-L1-Ul shall perform TPMI selection according to section [#sec-PmiSelect](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PmiSelect) of 5GMax Algorithm Specification.

The computation of the TPMI is done when PUSCH is received with spatial multiplexing rank 2 (*numOfLayers =2 from* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq). The computed TPMIs and corresponding SINR values shall be sent to 5G-L2-PS in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message:*\*\*

- *ulRank*

- *ulPmiRank1 is the result of MECG metric. Supported values are [0..5].*

- *ulPmiRank1Sinr is the SINR for given* ulPmiRank1 expressed in dB. **If the computed value is larger than 40dB, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall cap it to 40dB.**\*\*

- *ulPmiRank2 is the result of SLP metric.*

Supported values are [0..2]. The simulation report is available at “[NR\_UplinkPMISelection.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/CFAMs/5GC1800-5GC1999/5GC001981%20-%20L1%20algorithm%20changes%20-%205G19B/Simulation%20Reports/NR_UplinkPMISelection.pptx)”.

- *ulPmiRank2Sinr*is the SINR for given *ulPmiRank2 expressed in dB. If the computed value is larger than 40dB, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall cap it to 40dB.*\*\*

The number of equidistant subcarriers considered for PMI evaluation on PUSCH is 12.

The codebook used for the computation of the metrics depends on parameter value of *spatialMode,* numOfLayers*, and in case of more than or equal to 4Rx receiver is used,* pboost, also *codebookIndex as described in the algorithm document. These parameters come within the* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message described in 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**\***\*

Also, the codebook used for the computation of the metrics depend on *puschTransCoherence IE that comes within the* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message described in 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When *puschTransCoherence == nonCoherent, the codebooks are given in 5G\_L1\_4935\_replaced([11398799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e84G9_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When* puschTransCoherence == fullCoherent, the codebooks are given in 5G\_L1\_4935\_replaced([11398799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e84G9_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

For MU MIMO UEs following rules shall be applied:

1. For cell type C8 with **disabled enhancement L1 noise computation algorithm (actPmiSinrUlMuMimo==false in messages UlCell\_SetupReq or UlCell\_ParameterReconfigurationReq) 5G-L1-UL shall compute TPMI:**

- using noise from all DMRS REs

- using own UE antennas or UE beams.

1. For cell type C8 with **enabled enhancement L1 noise computation algorithm (actPmiSinrUlMuMimo==true in messages UlCell\_SetupReq or UlCell\_ParameterReconfigurationReq) 5G-L1-UL shall compute TPMI:**

- using noise from own DMRS REs only;

* using all antennas.
* When subcell types not equal to C8, 5G-L1-UL shall ignore parameter actPmiSinrUlMuMimo and shall compute TPMI:

- using noise from all DMRS REs

- using own UE antennas or UE beams.

For spatial multiplexing rank 1 (*numOfLayers =1 from* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq), the UL TPMI selection metrics are not computed and the parameters:\*\*

- *ulRank,*

- *ulPmiRank1,*

*- ulPmiRank2*

are set to INVALID value (255) and sent to 5G-L2-PS in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. (Note: UlData\_SrsReceiveRespPs (5G\_L1\_254\_replaced([11483756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fDtHufvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is used instead.)*

In that case, also corresponding SINR values of

- *ulPmiRank1Sinr and*

- *ulPmiRank2Sinr*

are not meaningful.

For spatial mode single antenna (singleTx), the UL TPMI selection metrics are not computed and the parameter *ulPmiRank1 and ulPmiRank2 are set to INVALID value and sent to 5G-L2-PS in the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. Values of *ulPmiRank1Sinr and* ulPmiRank2Sinr are also not meaningful in that case.\*\*\*\*

The *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs is described in 5G\_L1\_IF\_200\_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

New parameters *ulRiSelectionThreshold  and* pBoost in *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq  shall be supported for TPMI computation for 2 layer PUSCH reception.*\*\*

Note:

*ulRiSelectionThreshold in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is *a UE specific parameter 'RI selection threshold', and is "alpha" in 5GMax Algorithm Specification. See 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*\*\*

*pBoost corresponds to the P\_Boost value for the SNR and PMI computation. See 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

This requirement also applies to Thor for FR2-TDD-eCPRI.

For power scaling ratio refer to [12733560: Power Scaling for TPMI](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_a4VIsHXbEfCdZt6bzaG3fw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[CB013442-D] 5G-L1-UL for Thor shall perform TPMI selection for SU receiver according to this requirement when A8 subcell is configured. [End CB013442-D]

---

#### 5.4.7.11.4 Odin

**5.4.7.11.4.0-1**  (ID: `12733184`)

Odin
5G-L1-Ul shall perform TPMI selection according to section [#sec-PmiSelect](https://sv-ul-5gmax.dyn.nesc.nokia.net/AlgoSpec/main.pdf#sec-PmiSelect) of 5GMax Algorithm Specification.

Rank 2 UE:

The computation of the TPMI is done when PUSCH is received with spatial multiplexing rank 2 (*numOfLayers =2 from* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq). The computed TPMIs and corresponding SINR values shall be sent to 5G-L2-PS in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message:*\*\*

- *ulRank*

- *ulPmiRank1 is the result of MECG metric. Supported values are [0..5].*

- *ulPmiRank1Sinr is the SINR for given* ulPmiRank1 expressed in dB. **If the computed value is larger than 40dB, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall cap it to 40dB.**\*\*

- *ulPmiRank2 is the result of SLP metric.*

Supported values are [0..2]. The simulation report is available at “[NR\_UplinkPMISelection.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/CFAMs/5GC1800-5GC1999/5GC001981%20-%20L1%20algorithm%20changes%20-%205G19B/Simulation%20Reports/NR_UplinkPMISelection.pptx)”.

- *ulPmiRank2Sinr*is the SINR for given *ulPmiRank2 expressed in dB. If the computed value is larger than 40dB, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall cap it to 40dB.*\*\*

The number of equidistant subcarriers considered for PMI evaluation on PUSCH is 12.

The codebook used for the computation of the metrics depends on parameter value of *spatialMode,* numOfLayers*, and in case of more than or equal to 4Rx receiver is used,* pboost, also *codebookIndex as described in the algorithm document. These parameters come within the* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message described in [11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).**\***\*

Also, the codebook used for the computation of the metrics depend on *puschTransCoherence IE that comes within the* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message described in [11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). When *puschTransCoherence == nonCoherent, the codebooks are given in [11398799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e84G9_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). When* puschTransCoherence == fullCoherent, the codebooks are given in [11398799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e84G9_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).\*\*\*\*

[CB012115-IB]

For MU MIMO UEs following rules shall be applied:

1. For cell type C8 with **disabled enhancement L1 noise computation algorithm (actPmiSinrUlMuMimo==false in messages UlCell\_SetupReq or UlCell\_ParameterReconfigurationReq) 5G-L1-UL shall compute TPMI:**

- using noise from all DMRS REs

- using own UE antennas or UE beams.

1. For cell type C8 with **enabled enhancement L1 noise computation algorithm (actPmiSinrUlMuMimo==true in messages UlCell\_SetupReq or UlCell\_ParameterReconfigurationReq) 5G-L1-UL shall compute TPMI:**

- using noise from own DMRS REs only;

* using all antennas.
* When subcell types not equal to C8, 5G-L1-UL shall ignore parameter actPmiSinrUlMuMimo and shall compute TPMI:

- using noise from all DMRS REs

- using own UE antennas or UE beams.

[End CB012115-IB]

Rank 1 UE:

For spatial multiplexing rank 1 (*numOfLayers =1 from* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq), the UL TPMI selection metrics are not computed and the parameters:\*\*

- *ulRank,*

- *ulPmiRank1,*

*- ulPmiRank2*

are set to INVALID value (255) and sent to 5G-L2-PS in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. (Note: UlData\_SrsReceiveRespPs ([11483756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fDtHufvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is used instead.)*

In that case, also corresponding SINR values of

- *ulPmiRank1Sinr and*

- *ulPmiRank2Sinr*

are not meaningful.

For spatial mode single antenna (singleTx), the UL TPMI selection metrics are not computed and the parameter *ulPmiRank1 and ulPmiRank2 are set to INVALID value and sent to 5G-L2-PS in the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. Values of *ulPmiRank1Sinr and* ulPmiRank2Sinr are also not meaningful in that case.\*\*\*\*

The *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs is described in [11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).*

The parameters *ulRiSelectionThreshold  and* pBoost in *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq  shall be supported for TPMI computation for 2 layer PUSCH reception.*\*\*

*ulRiSelectionThreshold in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is *a UE specific parameter 'RI selection threshold', and is "alpha" in 5GMax Algorithm Specification. See [11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).*\*\*

*pBoost corresponds to the P\_Boost value for the SNR and PMI computation. See [11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).*

The computation of PMI SINR (*ulPmiRank1 and/or* ulPmiRank2) uses all Rx antennas. **For 8-stream subcells and MU-MIMO receiver only, PMI SINR (*ulPmiRank1 and/or \*ulPmiRank2) estimation is performed only with each paired UE's own Rx antennas.***\*\*\*

For power scaling ratio refer to [12733560: Power Scaling for TPMI](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_a4VIsHXbEfCdZt6bzaG3fw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

5G-L1-UL shall follow the following rules for Measurements in 8Rx MU receiver (compared to 8Rx SU receiver):

 Antenna-wise ST CFO and TOE measurements on PUSCH are computed on each UE's own beams, other beams are INVALID values. Refer to [11403852](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9RIjPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11403730](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9QhdvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

 The following other measurements still use 8 streams, the number of VALID measurements is equal to 8. Refer to [11109582](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko570PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

- Antenna-wise UL Received power for PUSCH

- Antenna-wise SINR for PUSCH

- Antenna-wise RSSI on PUSCH

- Antenna-wise noise power on PUSCH

For example, for ST CFO and TOE measurements:

4 rank1 UE  for each UE has 1 layer and uses one best beam, UE1 has best beam on ceAxC(2,3) and valid measurements on ceAxC(2,3), remaining measurements are INVALID.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a communication system. On the left, there are four rectangular boxes, each labeled with "UE" followed by a number (0, 1, 2, or 3) and "best beam". These represent four different User Equipments (UEs) and their selected best beams.
From each of these UE boxes, there are two horizontal blue arrows pointing to the right. These arrows are grouped together by a curly brace symbol. This suggests that each UE is transmitting some form of data or signal, possibly in multiple streams or using multiple antennas, which are then combined or processed.
On the right side of the diagram, there is a large blue rectangular box labeled "8RX IRC MU receiver". This represents the receiver in the system, which has 8 receive antennas (8RX), uses Interference Rejection Combining (IRC), and is designed for Multi-User (MU) reception. The arrows from the UE boxes feed into this receiver, indicating that the receiver is processing the signals from all four UEs.
The diagram visually depicts a scenario where multiple UEs are communicating with a single receiver, and the receiver is equipped to handle signals from multiple users simultaneously, with a significant number of receive antennas and advanced signal processing techniques like IRC.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1352.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

### 5.4.7.13 Measurements from L1(Radio), 7-2e split

#### 5.4.7.13.1 Thor

**5.4.7.13.1.0-2**  (ID: `11404766`)

Thor
L1-BB gets the following measurements from L1(Radio) in messages *UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespCellPs and* UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespUePs, which shall be forwarded to L2-PS without any other processing:\*\*

* *noisePower*
* *shortTermCfoMetric*
* *shortTermTaMetric*
* *shortTermTaPeakAmp*
* *rxPower*
* *rssi*
* *ulRank*
* *ulPmiRank1*
* *ulPmiRank1Sinr*
* *ulPmiRank2*
* *ulPmiRank2Sinr*

**[CB009514-F]**

-NumOfNoisePerPrbElem

-NoisePerPrb

***\*[End CB009514-F]***\*

L1-BB shall get all antenna-wise measurements computed by L1(Radio) from structures *fhPushAntMeas of message* UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespUePs (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_15103\_replaced([11517124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSI8N_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and directly forward them to L2-PS in structures *puschAntMeas of message* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_202\_replaced([11513842](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).\*\*\*\*

---

#### 5.4.7.13.2 Odin

**5.4.7.13.2.0-1**  (ID: `12789615`)

Odin
L1-BB gets the following measurements from L1(Radio) in messages *UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespCellPs and* UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespUePs, which shall be forwarded to L2-PS without any other processing:\*\*

* *noisePower*
* *shortTermCfoMetric*
* *shortTermTaMetric*
* *shortTermTaPeakAmp*
* *rxPower*
* *rssi*
* *ulRank*
* *ulPmiRank1*
* *ulPmiRank1Sinr*
* *ulPmiRank2*
* *ulPmiRank2Sinr*

L1-BB shall get all antenna-wise measurements computed by L1(Radio) from structures *fhPushAntMeas of message* UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespUePs (see [11517124: fhPuschAntMeas structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FSI8N_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and directly forward them to L2-PS in structures *puschAntMeas of message* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs (see [11513842: PuschAntMeas](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).\*\*\*\*

### 5.4.7.14 UL measurements buffering for Loner

#### 5.4.7.14.0-1 UL measurements buffering for Loner As part of the CNI-63826 which improved 5GC001120, PUSCH measurement report includes on_air_sfn, on_air_slot_number, proc_sfn, proc_slot_number ; where former SFN/SlotNumber is replaced by procSFN/procSlotNumber. (ID: 11404777)

## 5.4.8 Uplink Control Information

### 5.4.8.1 Supported UCI cases

#### 5.4.8.1.1 Loner

**5.4.8.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `11405234`)

Loner
Loner: Following cases are supported:

AN only

CSI Part 1

CSI Part 1 + AN

CSI Part 1 + CSI Part 2 (CB007798; FDD feature)

CSI Part 1 + CSI Part 2 + AN (CB007798; FDD feature)

In the future, CSI Part 2 in TDD FR1 will be supported in the future with CB008632

---

#### 5.4.8.1.2 Loki

**5.4.8.1.2.0-1**  (ID: `11405240`)

Loki
Loki:

With UL-SCH, following cases are supported:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| case | FDD | TDD FR1 | TDD FR2 |
| AN only | 5GC001868 | 5GC001868 |  |
| CSI Part 1 | 5GC000885 | CB006064 |  |
| CSI Part 1 + AN | 5GC000885 | CB006064 |  |
| CSI Part 1 + CSI Part 2 | 5GC001817 | CB007220 |  |
| CSI Part 1 + CSI Part 2 + AN | 5GC001817 | CB007220 |  |

Without UL-SCH (uciOnly), see cases in 5G\_L1\_5720\_replaced([11407050](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9gZHvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 5.4.8.1.3 Thor

##### 5.4.8.1.3.0-3 Thor 5G-L1-UL shall support 8Rx IRC receiver for PUSCH with UCI (SR, AN, P-CSI) when C8 [CB013442-D] or A8 [End CB013442-D] subcell is configured. ( 12683264 ) (ID: 12683263)

#### 5.4.8.1.4 Odin

**5.4.8.1.4.0-1**  (ID: `12733455`)

Odin
5G-L1-UL shall support the following cases when UL-SCH is available.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| case | First Feature |
| AN only | 5GC001868 |
| CSI Part 1 | CB006064 |
| CSI Part 1 + AN | CB006064 |
| CSI Part 1 + CSI Part 2 | CB007220 |
| CSI Part 1 + CSI Part 2 + AN | CB007220 |

"AN only" case is not supported without UL-SCH (uciOnly).

[CB012115-IA] AN only, CSI part 1, and CSI Part 1 + AN shall be supported in Odin L1 Call. [End CB012115-IA]

11405314:

11405314:
Legacy: ACK/ NACK feedback management (UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD)
AN reception in uciOnly case.

AN reception cases are similar to case which has UL-SCH, except in the case when PUSCH does not have CSI Part 2.

In that case AN does not puncture CSI part 2, and 1 bit AN transmission is optimised to use all reserved 2 bit AN resouces. See more details in this document (changes compared to with UL-SCH are visible with track changes):

[docx\_desc]AN processing in uciOnly case:
When CSI2 is , AN is processed as with UL-SCH case.
When CSI2 is not, AN processing is following (changes compared to case with UL-SCH visible with track changes):
When numOfUciAckBits == 1, these parameters need to be set:
-numOfUciAckSymbols, which is calculated based on 2 AN bits, and used for AN resource element reservation. This affects CSI Part 1 resource mapping, see example in the below.
-numOfUciAckSymbols1bit
-Bit AN is decoded
When numOfUciAckBits == 2, these parameters need to be set:
-numOfUciAckSymbols, which is calculated based on 2 AN bits, and used for AN resource element reservation. This affects CSI Part 1 resource mapping, see example in the below.
-numOfUciAckSymbols1bit
-L1 2 bit ANthus L1 does double decoding
When numOfUciAckBits >= 3, these parameters need to be set:
-numOfUciAckSymbols, which is calculated based on numOfUciAckBits
-L1 decodes AN which is numOfUciAckBits
Note special case when CSI Part 1 reception is requested and UE does not send AN bits.
Then numOfUciAckSymbols has to contain value which is > 0, and is calculated based on 2 AN bits. Reason for this is that CSI Part 1 mapping is started from resource elements which can depend from numOfUciAckSymbols.
[/docx\_desc][docx\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/attachments/pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file1396\_0.docx[/docx\_path]

12733435:
Ack/Nack (AN) Reception
12733435:
Ack/Nack (AN) Reception
Upon reception of the *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq ([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) message with a non-zero field* numOfUciAckBits, the 5G-L1-UL shall attempt detection of HARQ A/N bits multiplexed on PUSCH in the SFN and NR-slot specified in the *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message.*\*\*

* if *numOfUciAckBits in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is 1 bit, *ackNack1BitUci ([11513924](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR0MEfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) contains valid measurement in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD. However *ackNackUciMoreThan1Bit contain invalid measurements which should be ignored by 5G-L2-PS.*\*\*\*\*
* if *numOfUciAckBits in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is 2 bits, there might be either one or two Ack/Nack bits in the UCI, hence *ackNack1BitUci is used for 1 bit UCI decoding in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD and *ackNackUciMoreThan1Bit is used for 2-bit UCI decoding.* ackNack1BitUci*and* ackNackUciMoreThan1Bit  are sent simultaneously*.****\****\*
* if *numOfUciAckBits in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is  higher than 2 bits, *ackNack1BitUci contains invalid measurement in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD should be ignored by 5G-L2-PS. However *dackNackUciMoreThan1Bit contains valid measurements.*\*\*\*\*

The below IEs are found in *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq:*

*- numOfUciAckBits:* [0..50]\*\*

Note: For E2E perspective only up to 2 bits could be used for *numOfUciAckBits.*

Note: For Loner/Loki L1 HW, E2E perspective 3 bits could be used for *numOfUciAckBits additionally.*

*- uciOnly:* False or*True are set if PUSCH carries UL-SCH or not, respectively.*\*\*

* *ackNack1BitUci and* ackNackUciMoreThan1Bit are sent via *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD (5G\_L1\_IF\_210\_replaced([11513919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR0MEPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to 5G-L2-PS.* numOfUciAckSymbols is used to ensure that there is enough modulation symbol resources available for HARQ A/N transmission ([image-0][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1391.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] in 38.212 6.3.2.4.1.1 ).\*\*\*\*

The requirement is supported in Loner, Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

###### 5.4.8.2.3 Ack/Nack (AN) Logic

**5.4.8.2.3.0-2**  (ID: `12733447`)

Ack/Nack (AN) Logic
AN reception is requested when numOfUciAckBits > 0

When numOfUciAckBits == 1, these parameters need to be set:

-numOfUciAckSymbols, which is calculated based on 2 AN bits, and used for AN resource element reservation. This affects CSI Part 1 resource mapping, see example in the below.

-numOfUciAckSymbols1bit, which tells how many resource elements are used for 1 bit AN

-1Bit AN is decoded

When numOfUciAckBits == 2, these parameters need to be set:

-numOfUciAckSymbols, which is calculated based on 2 AN bits, and used for AN resource element reservation. This affects CSI Part 1 resource mapping, see example in the below.

-numOfUciAckSymbols1bit, which tells how many resource elements are used for 1 bit AN

-L1 does not know if UE has sent 1 bit or 2 bit AN, thus L1 does double decoding

When numOfUciAckBits >= 3, these parameters need to be set:

-numOfUciAckSymbols, which is calculated based on  numOfUciAckBits

-L1 decodes AN which is numOfUciAckBits

Note special case when CSI Part 1 (and CSI Part 2) reception is requested and UE does not send AN bits.

Then numOfUciAckSymbols has to contain value which is > 0, and is calculated based on 2 AN bits. Reason for this is that CSI Part 1 mapping is started from resource elements which can depend from numOfUciAckSymbols.

See AN + CSI Part 1 and 2 resource mapping examples with 0,1,2,3 bit AN in object [11406180](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9cHovvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

See also:

-38.212 chapters 6.2.7 and 6.3.2.4.1.1

-L2 parameter setting in [11204128](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwjb8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

In other words:

numOfUciAckBits1Bit is mandatory for O\_AN==1 and 0\_AN==2

numOfUciAckSymbols1bit is ignored if numOfUciAckBits > 2

numOfUciAckBits > 0 when 0\_AN !=0 or O\_CSI1 != 0

The requirement is valid for **Thor and [CB012115-IA] Odin [End CB012115-IA].**

---

###### 5.4.8.2.4 Maximum Number of Ack/Nack (AN) bits

###### 5.4.8.2.4.1 Frozen Requirements

**5.4.8.2.4.1.0-4**  (ID: `11405349`)

Frozen Requirements
Content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 23R3 release. For later releases please refer to the objects below

The maximum number of A/N bits supported on PUSCH is:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| TDD FR1 | Max A/N bits | Feature |
|  | 14 | CB008532-A |
|  | 16 | CB008053-A |
|  | 17 | CB008053-C |

[CB008827-5G-A] Parity from CB008532:

L1 on Thor shall support 14 A/N bits from the requirement above in FR1 TDD.

[End CB008827-5G-A]

[CB008053-A] New amount of A/N bits: 16 is introduced. [End CB008053-A]

[CB008053-C] New amount of A/N bits: 17 is introduced. [End CB008053-C]

---

###### 5.4.8.2.4.2 Loner

**5.4.8.2.4.2.0-2**  (ID: `11405366`)

Loner
For Loner, the maximum number of A/N bits supported on PUSCH is:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| FDD FR1 | Max A/N bits | Feature |
|  | 9 | CB011081-A |

---

###### 5.4.8.2.4.3 Loki

**5.4.8.2.4.3.0-5**  (ID: `11405400`)

Loki
For Loki, the maximum number of A/N bits supported on PUSCH is:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| TDD FR1 | Max A/N bits | Feature |
|  | 14 | CB008532 |
|  | 16 | CB008053-A |
|  | 17 | CB008053-C |
|  | 20 | CB009855-A&B |
|  | 23 | CB011080-A |

---

###### 5.4.8.2.4.4 Thor FR1-TDD

**5.4.8.2.4.4.0-5**  (ID: `11405431`)

Thor FR1-TDD
For Thor, the maximum number of A/N bits supported on PUSCH is:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| TDD FR1 | Max A/N bits | Feature |
|  | 14 | CB008827-5G-A(Parity from CB008532) |
|  | 16 | CB009029-5G-A(Parity from CB008053-A) |
|  | 17 | CB009029-5G-A(Parity from CB008053-C) |
|  | 20 | CB009855-A&B |
|  | 23 | CB011080-A |

[Before CB009936-H] In eCPRI 7.2e, up to 17 A/N bits are supported.

[CB009936-H] This requirement also applies to FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI [End CB009936-H]

---

###### 5.4.8.2.4.5 Thor FR1-FDD

**5.4.8.2.4.5.0-1**  (ID: `11405443`)

Thor FR1-FDD
For Thor, the maximum number of A/N bits supported on PUSCH is:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| FDD FR1 | Max A/N bits | Feature |
|  | 9 | CB011081-A |

---

###### 5.4.8.2.4.6 Odin FR1-TDD

**5.4.8.2.4.6.0-1**  (ID: `12733417`)

Odin FR1-TDD
For Odin, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support the maximum number of 23 ACK/NACK (AN) bits.

Polar code plus 6 CRC bits are required for more than 11 bits of information.

There is no Schedule Request (SR) bit on PUSCH.

[CB012115-GA] Note: Even though supporting only 17 AN bits for 7-2e split is enough at this stage, 5G-L1-UL(BB) shall not impose any restriction due to FH mode. The requirement is common for TDD. [End CB012115-GA]

---

###### 5.4.8.2.4.7 Odin FR1-FDD

###### 5.4.8.2.4.7.0-1 Odin FR1-FDD For Odin, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support the maximum number of 9 ACK/NACK (AN) bits. [CB012115-JA] for both CPRI or eCPRI [End CB012115-JA] (ID: 12733431)

###### 5.4.8.2.5 ACK/NACK bits for supported Carrier Aggregation cases

###### 5.4.8.2.5.1 Legacy

**5.4.8.2.5.1.0-1**  (ID: `11405525`)

Legacy
Content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 24R2 release. For later releases please refer to 5G\_L1\_14924\_replaced([11405530](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9ZEUvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L1\_14925\_replaced([11405497](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9YdS_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

L1 shall support carrier aggregation up to 5 CCs in DL and up to 2 CCs in UL with TDD cell as PCell for the following cases:

* 3 CCs TDD + 2 CCs FDD
* 2 CCs TDD + 3 CCs FDD

L1 shall support up to 20 bits A/N on PUSCH(20 bits are needed with 3 CCs TDD + 2 CCs FDD and 18 bits for 2 CCs TDD + 3 CCs FDD).

Supported L1 HW and technology is as below:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Loner | Loki | Thor |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Not supported | **[CB009855-A, CB009855-B]** Supported **[End CB009855-A, CB009855-B]** | Not supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Not supported | **[CB009855-A, CB009855-B]** Supported **[End CB009855-A, CB009855-B]** | **[CB009855-A, CB009855-B]** Supported **[End CB009855-A, CB009855-B]** |

**Note:**

There is no [SR](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_JBezUHRkEfCRE7eh4Ndkrw) bit on PUSCH.

Polar code plus 6 CRC bits are required for more than 11 bits information.

---

###### 5.4.8.2.5.2 Loner

**5.4.8.2.5.2.0-2**  (ID: `11405465`)

Loner
L1 shall support carrier aggregation up to 6 CCs in DL and up to 2 CCs in UL with FDD cell as PCell for the following cases:

* 3 CCs FDD + 3 CCs TDD
* 4 CCs FDD + 2 CCs TDD

L1 shall support up to 9 bits A/N on PUSCH(9 bits are needed with 3 CCs FDD + 3 CCs TDD and 8 bits for 4 CCs FDD + 2 CCs TDD).

Note: There is no SR bit on PUSCH.

Note: This requirement is generic, and not dependent upon Technology.

---

###### 5.4.8.2.5.3 Loki

**5.4.8.2.5.3.0-4**  (ID: `11405497`)

Loki
L1 shall support carrier aggregation up to 6 CCs in DL and up to 2 CCs in UL with TDD cell as PCell for the following cases:

* 3 CCs TDD + 3 CCs FDD
* 2 CCs TDD + 4 CCs FDD

L1 shall support up to 23 bits A/N on PUSCH(23 bits are needed with 3 CCs TDD + 3 CCs FDD and 21 bits for 2 CCs TDD + 4 CCs FDD).

Note:

There is no SR bit on PUSCH.

Polar code plus 6 CRC bits are required for more than 11 bits information.

---

###### 5.4.8.2.5.4 Thor

**5.4.8.2.5.4.0-4**  (ID: `11405530`)

Thor
L1 shall support carrier aggregation up to 6 CCs in DL and up to 2 CCs in UL with TDD cell as PCell for the following cases:

* 3 CCs TDD + 3 CCs FDD
* 2 CCs TDD + 4 CCs FDD

L1 shall support up to 23 bits A/N on PUSCH(23 bits are needed with 3 CCs TDD + 3 CCs FDD and 21 bits for 2 CCs TDD + 4 CCs FDD).

HW Technology map:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | Thor-BB |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Not supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI | **[CB009936-H] Supported** [End CB009936-H]\*\*\*\* |

**Note:**

There is no [SR](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_JBezUHRkEfCRE7eh4Ndkrw) bit on PUSCH.

Polar code plus 6 CRC bits are required for more than 11 bits information.

---

###### 5.4.8.2.5.5 Odin

**5.4.8.2.5.5.0-1**  (ID: `12733409`)

Odin
L1 shall support carrier aggregation up to 6 CCs in DL and up to 2 CCs in UL with TDD cell as PCell for the following cases:

* 3 CCs TDD + 3 CCs FDD
* 2 CCs TDD + 4 CCs FDD

L1 shall support up to 23 bits A/N on PUSCH (23 bits are needed with 3 CCs TDD + 3 CCs FDD and 21 bits for 2 CCs TDD + 4 CCs FDD).

**Note:**

There is no [SR](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_JBezUHRkEfCRE7eh4Ndkrw) bit on PUSCH.

Polar code plus 6 CRC bits are required for more than 11 bits information.

CA TDD shall be supported in CB012115-IA. [CB012115-JA][CB012115-LA] CA FDD shall also be supported. [End CB012115-LA] for both CPRI and eCPRI. [End CB012115-JA]

---

### 5.4.8.3 UCI on PUSCH with Carrier Aggregation

**5.4.8.3.0-2**  (ID: `12733638`)

UCI on PUSCH with Carrier Aggregation
5G-L1-UL shall support carrier aggregation up to 2 CCs contiguous or non-contiguous, also no UL CA. Both CCs need to be served by the same DU. L1 shall support the same frame structure and same numerology between CCs, with or without BF.

Note that the maximum number of bits used for the UCI by L2 is of 40 bits: CSIPart1 by L2 is of 40bits and for CSIPart2 by L2 is of 11bits. For FR1 TDD CA, the number of HARQ A/N is up to 8 bits. See [11406332: Min & Max number CSI Part 1, CSI Part 2 (and RI) bits](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9cutfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

The requirement is supported in Loner, Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IC] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IC]

---

(Periodic CSI)" process. It depicts interactions between two entities: L1 and L2PS. The diagram shows the following sequence of events: 1. L1 has a condition represented by a yellow sticky note: * numOfUciCsiPart1Bits &gt; 0 * numOfUciCsiPart1Symbols &gt; 0 2. L2PS sends a request to L1 : UIData_PuschReceiveReq () . 3. L1 indicates that "UL reception proceeds". 4. L1 sends a response to L2PS : UIData_PuschReceiveRespPs . 5. L2PS receives CSI1 parameters, which are listed in a yellow sticky note: * -uciCsiPart1 Bits * -dtxCsiPart1 * -dtxMetricCsiPart1 * -dtxThresholdCsiPart1 * -csiPart1 CrcCheck The diagram uses dashed lines to represent the lifelines of L1 and L2PS, and solid arrows to indicate the messages exchanged between them. The sticky notes represent conditions or data payloads.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_1443.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] (ID: 11405602)

#### 5.4.8.4.2 Supported Configurations for CSI Part 1 only (periodic CSI)

##### 5.4.8.4.2.1 Loner

**5.4.8.4.2.1.0-2**  (ID: `11405612`)

Loner
L1 shall support,

* PUSCH with periodic CSI and DL Harq ACK/NACK indications multiplexed in UCI. Up to 11 bits only in 20B.
* slot type 63 (replacing, e.g., C5 in FDD), moving SRS symbol to last symbol and extending PUSCH to 13 symbols.

New parameters are introduced,

* The parameters numOfUciCsiPart1Bits, numOfUciCsiPart1Symbols, uciCsiPart1Bits, csiPart1Only to the message UlData\_PuschReceiveReq.
* The parameters numOfUciCsiPart1Bits, numOfUciCsiPart1Symbols, numOfCsiReports, csiPart1Only, dtxCsiPart1, dtxMetricCsiPart1, dtxThresholdCsiPart1 to the message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs.

---

##### 5.4.8.4.2.2 Loner, Loki

**5.4.8.4.2.2.0-2**  (ID: `11405663`)

Loner, Loki
CB006064-E is a spillover from 5GC000885-A and 5GC000885-E subfeatures for TDD FR1 Long PUCCH.

PUSCH shall support UCI on PUSCH with polar coding in TDD FR1 CPRI and eCPRI.

L1 shall support,

* PUSCH with only periodic CSI and DL Harq ACK/NACK indications.
* slot type 31 (E2E) with 14 symbols for PUSCH (already supported for FDD in 5G20B).
* restriction on startPRB and numOfPRB when other channels are multiplexed with PUSCH (already supported for FDD in 5G20B).
* uciOnly set to "False" since PUSCH always carries UL-SCH (already the case for FDD in 5G20B, meaning 3GPP PUSCH without UL-SCH mode is not supported).
* numOfUciCsiPart1Bits range extension from [0..11] to [0..50].
* the DTX UCI tables and write them in the respective registers at decoder setup. (already supported for FDD in 5G20B). L1 shall support new DTX tables for Polar codes.
* UCI on PUSCH polar coding introduction for TDD, see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_204.

-- numOfUciAckBits u8[0..11], see 5G\_RAN\_UP\_2205.

-- numOfUciCsiPart1Bits u8[0..50].

DTX range is updated, according to 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_202 and 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_210, for dtxAck1Bit, dtxAckMoreThan1Bit, dtxCsiPart1, dtxCsiPart2

* 0: nonDTX
* Any value > 0 and in the right range shall be considered as DTX. This new DTX codes shall allow to insert additional information regarding the type of the observed DTX.

[CB007271-C] This requirement applies to FDD CPRI cells on Loki-based HW. [End CB007271].

[CB010575-D] This requirement shall be supported by Thor for FR1-TDD-CPRI. [End CB010575-D]

[CB011178-A] 5G-L1-UL shall also support requirements above for FR1 FDD 7-2a eCPRI for Thor. [End CB011178-A]

Notice: The following table contains the history of implementation. (table to be completed during the Spec refactoring in compliance with Release Based Versioning)

HW-Technology Map:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Loner | Loki | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI |  |  | Supported |
| FR1 FDD OBSAI |  |  | [CB010969-SR-CB] Supported [End CB010969-SR-CB] |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI |  |  | [CB011178-A] Supported [End CB011178-A] |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI |  |  | [CB010575-D] Supported [End CB010575-D] |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI |  |  |  |
| FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI |  |  |  |
| FR2-TDD-CPRI |  |  |  |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI |  |  |  |
