##### 5.4.8.4.2.3 Thor

**5.4.8.4.2.3.0-1**  (ID: `11405671`)

Thor
PUSCH shall support UCI on PUSCH with polar coding in TDD FR1 CPRI and eCPRI.

L1 shall support,

* PUSCH with only periodic CSI and DL Harq ACK/NACK indications.
* slot type 31 (E2E) with 14 symbols for PUSCH (already supported for FDD in 5G20B).
* restriction on startPRB and numOfPRB when other channels are multiplexed with PUSCH (already supported for FDD in 5G20B).
* uciOnly set to "False" since PUSCH always carries UL-SCH (already the case for FDD in 5G20B, meaning 3GPP PUSCH without UL-SCH mode is not supported).
* numOfUciCsiPart1Bits range extension from [0..11] to [0..50].
* the DTX UCI tables and write them in the respective registers at decoder setup. (already supported for FDD in 5G20B). L1 shall support new DTX tables for Polar codes.
* UCI on PUSCH polar coding introduction for TDD, see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_204.

-- numOfUciAckBits u8[0..11], see 5G\_RAN\_UP\_2205.

-- numOfUciCsiPart1Bits u8[0..50].

DTX range is updated, according to 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_202 and 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_210, for dtxAck1Bit, dtxAckMoreThan1Bit, dtxCsiPart1, ~~dtxCsiPart2~~

* 0: nonDTX
* Any value > 0 and in the right range shall be considered as DTX. This new DTX codes shall allow to insert additional information regarding the type of the observed DTX.

This requirement applies to FDD CPRI cells on Loki-based HW.

This requirement shall be supported by Thor for FR1-TDD-CPRI.

**[CB007595-B] This requirement appliies to Thor HW in FR1-TDD-eCPRI-7-2e.** [End CB007595-B]\*\*\*\*

HW-Technology Map:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Loner | Loki | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | ? | ? | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Not Supported | ? | ? |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | ? | ? | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | ? | ? | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI | Not Supported | Not Supported | **[CB007595-B] Supported** [End CB007595-B]\*\*\*\* |
| FR2-TDD-CPRI |  |  |  |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI |  |  |  |

##### 5.4.8.4.2.4 Odin

**5.4.8.4.2.4.0-1**  (ID: `12733495`)

Odin
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support UCI on PUSCH with polar coding in TDD FR1 CPRI and eCPRI.

5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support,

* PUSCH with only periodic CSI and DL Harq ACK/NACK indications.
* slot type 31 (E2E) with 14 symbols for PUSCH (already supported for FDD in 5G20B).
* restriction on startPRB and numOfPRB when other channels are multiplexed with PUSCH (already supported for FDD in 5G20B).
* numOfUciCsiPart1Bits range [0..50].
* UCI on PUSCH polar coding introduction for TDD, see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_204.

-- numOfUciAckBits u8[0..11], see 5G\_RAN\_UP\_2205.

-- numOfUciCsiPart1Bits u8[0..50].

* uciOnly for both True and False are supported.
* the DTX UCI tables and write them in the respective registers at decoder setup. (already supported for FDD in 5G20B). L1 shall support new DTX tables for Polar codes.

-- 0: nonDTX

-- Any value > 0 and in the right range shall be considered as DTX. This new DTX codes shall allow to insert additional information regarding the type of the observed DTX.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported in Odin L1 Call. DTX is not required. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.8.4.3 pCSI Bundling

##### 5.4.8.4.3.1 Loner

**5.4.8.4.3.1.0-1**  (ID: `11405684`)

Loner
[CB009854-B]

L1 shall support polar coded CSI part 1 report in PUSCH for FR1 FDD CPRI when number of bits in CSI part 1 is bigger than 11.

No pCSI bundling, L1 shall support up to 21 bits for CSI part1 in PUSCH:  
- Up to 19 bits of CSI report for channel acquisition  
- Up to 21 bits of CSI report for beam management  
[End CB009854-B]

[CB009854-D]  
With pCSI bundling, L1 shall support up to 40 bits for CSI part1 in PUSCH including:  
- Up to 19 bits of CSI report for channel acquisition  
- Up to 21 bits of CSI report for beam management  
[End CB009854-D]

Note:

1) pCSI bundling means that CSI report for channel acquisition and beam management can be carried with same resource. So it is needed to sum maximum bit of CSI report for channel acquisition and maximum bit of CSI report for beam management when calculating maximum bit number of CSI part1 in PUSCH.

2) No pCSI bundling means that CSI report for channel acquisition and beam management should be carried with separate resource.So it is needed to maximize(maximum bit number of CSI report for channel acquisition, maximum bit number of CSI report for beam management) when calculating maximum bit number CSI part1 in PUSCH.

---

##### 5.4.8.4.3.2 Loki

**5.4.8.4.3.2.0-2**  (ID: `11405697`)

Loki
L1 shall support polar coded CSI part 1 report in PUSCH for FR1 FDD CPRI when number of bits in CSI part 1 is bigger than 11.

No pCSI bundling, L1 shall support up to 21 bits for CSI part1 in PUSCH:  
- Up to 19 bits of CSI report for channel acquisition  
- Up to 21 bits of CSI report for beam management

With pCSI bundling, L1 shall support up to 40 bits for CSI part1 in PUSCH including:  
- Up to 19 bits of CSI report for channel acquisition  
- Up to 21 bits of CSI report for beam management

Note:

1) pCSI bundling means that CSI report for channel acquisition and beam management can be carried with same resource. So it is needed to sum maximum bit of CSI report for channel acquisition and maximum bit of CSI report for beam management when calculating maximum bit number of CSI part1 in PUSCH.

2) No pCSI bundling means that CSI report for channel acquisition and beam management should be carried with separate resource.So it is needed to maximize(maximum bit number of CSI report for channel acquisition, maximum bit number of CSI report for beam management) when calculating maximum bit number CSI part1 in PUSCH.

[CB011066-A]This requirement is also valid for FR1-FDD-eCPRI. [End CB011066-A]

Thor
L1 shall support polar coded CSI part 1 report in PUSCH for FR1 FDD CPRI when number of bits in CSI part 1 is bigger than 11.

No pCSI bundling, L1 shall support up to 21 bits for CSI part1 in PUSCH:  
- Up to 19 bits of CSI report for channel acquisition  
- Up to 21 bits of CSI report for beam management

With pCSI bundling, L1 shall support up to 40 bits for CSI part1 in PUSCH including:  
- Up to 19 bits of CSI report for channel acquisition  
- Up to 21 bits of CSI report for beam management

Note:

1) pCSI bundling means that CSI report for channel acquisition and beam management can be carried with same resource. So it is needed to sum maximum bit of CSI report for channel acquisition and maximum bit of CSI report for beam management when calculating maximum bit number of CSI part1 in PUSCH.

2) No pCSI bundling means that CSI report for channel acquisition and beam management should be carried with separate resource.So it is needed to maximize(maximum bit number of CSI report for channel acquisition, maximum bit number of CSI report for beam management) when calculating maximum bit number CSI part1 in PUSCH.

[CB010413-F] 5G-L1-UL shall also support requirements above for FDD FR1 eCPRI-7-2a for Thor. [End CB010413-F]

##### 5.4.8.4.3.4 Odin

**5.4.8.4.3.4.0-1**  (ID: `12733500`)

Odin
L1 shall support polar coded CSI part 1 report in PUSCH for FR1 FDD [CB012115-JA] for both CPRI and eCPRI [End CB012115-JA] when number of bits in CSI part 1 is bigger than 11.

No pCSI bundling, L1 shall support up to 21 bits for CSI part1 in PUSCH:  
- Up to 19 bits of CSI report for channel acquisition  
- Up to 21 bits of CSI report for beam management

With pCSI bundling, L1 shall support up to 40 bits for CSI part1 in PUSCH including:  
- Up to 19 bits of CSI report for channel acquisition  
- Up to 21 bits of CSI report for beam management

**Note:**

1) pCSI bundling means that CSI report for channel acquisition and beam management can be carried with same resource. So it is needed to sum maximum bit of CSI report for channel acquisition and maximum bit of CSI report for beam management when calculating maximum bit number of CSI part1 in PUSCH.

2) No pCSI bundling means that CSI report for channel acquisition and beam management should be carried with separate resource.So it is needed to maximize(maximum bit number of CSI report for channel acquisition, maximum bit number of CSI report for beam management) when calculating maximum bit number CSI part1 in PUSCH.

#### 5.4.8.4.4 Rank Indicator Extraction - CSI Part 1

**5.4.8.4.4.0-1**  (ID: `11406143`)

Rank Indicator Extraction - CSI Part 1
RI value extraction from CSI Part 1, example:

CSI1 bits are: c0,c1,c2,c3,c4

numOfRiBits=2

riStartPos=2

->RI bits are (c2,c3)

If c2=1, c3=0

->RI value is 2

Thus, RI bits are read from left to right

See also TS38.212 6.3.2.1.2-3, and PUCCH CSI chapter where it refers

---

#### 5.4.8.4.5 Maximum Number of CSI 1 Bits

##### 5.4.8.4.5.1 For Loner:

###### 5.4.8.4.5.1.0-2 For Loner: Maximum number of CSI Part 1 bits for FR1 FDD CPRI is increased to [CB009854-B] 21 [End CB009854-B] then to  [CB009854-D] 40 [End CB009854-D]. (ID: 11405729)

##### 5.4.8.4.5.2 For Loki:

###### 5.4.8.4.5.2.0-3 For Loki: Maximum number of CSI Part 1 bits for FR1 FDD CPRI is increased to 21 then to 40. [CB011066-A]This requirement is also valid for FR1-FDD-eCPRI. [End CB011066-A]**** (ID: 11405757)

##### 5.4.8.4.5.3 For Thor:

###### 5.4.8.4.5.3.0-3 For Thor: Maximum number of CSI Part 1 bits for FR1 FDD CPRI is 40. [CB010413-F] 5G-L1-UL shall also support requirements above for FDD FR1 eCPRI-7-2a for Thor. [End CB010413-F] (ID: 11405777)

##### 5.4.8.4.5.4 Odin

###### 5.4.8.4.5.4.0-1 Odin 5G-L1-UL shall support the maximum of 50 bits of CSI Part 1. (ID: 12733502)

### 5.4.8.5 CSI Part 1 and CSI Part 2 (Aperiodic CSI)

#### 5.4.8.5.1 Legacy

##### 5.4.8.5.1.0-8 Legacy L1 shall support for 3 FDD CA and FDD + TDD CA. Edit 25.8.2022: to be deleted?? (ID: 11406070)

#### 5.4.8.5.2 CSI Reception

**5.4.8.5.2.0-4**  (ID: `12733503`)

CSI Reception
Upon reception of the message *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq, indicating* numOfUciCsiPart1Bits > 0, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall decode the CSI part 1 bits in the indicated NR-slot and sent to 5G-L2-PS in the *uciCsiPart1Bits field of the* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message.\*\*\*\*

* When *numOfUciCsiPart1Bits*≥ 12, Polar Code decoder shall be used for the corresponding UCI bits\*\*

To decode the CSI part2, 5G-L1-UL shall use *riStartPos and* riUsageMap to obtain the rank that was used for CSI Part 1. When the rank is obtained, via the *numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRi1 or* numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRi2 or *numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRi3 or* numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRi4 the number of bits in CSI part 2 become available to L1-UL. After decoding the CSI Part 2, the number of bits and the contents are send to 5G-L2-PS via *numOfUciCsiPart2Bits and uciCsiPart2Bits, respectively, in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs. CSI report bundling (for instance in case of CSI reporting of multiple CCs) is possible and the number of the CSI reports are given in *numOfPart2Reports.****\****\*

*numOfUciCsiPart1Bits, numOfUciCsiPart1Symbols, numOfPart2Reports are available in the [11513804: PuschUciConfig:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9__vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) structure of* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq.\*\*

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

The requirement is supported in Loner, Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.8.5.3 Supported Cases

**5.4.8.5.3.0-6**  (ID: `12733505`)

Supported Cases
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support CSI part 1 with CSI part 2 transmission on PUSCH with UL-SCH.

5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support numOfCsiPart2Reports = 8 CSI Part 2 reports.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

The requirement is supported in Loner, Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.8.5.4 CSI Part 2 Operations

**5.4.8.5.4.0-3**  (ID: `12733504`)

CSI Part 2 Operations
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support CSI Part 2 omission check, DTX detection, DTX metric and DTX threshold calculation.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

The requirement is supported in Loner, Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

L2 PS request CSI Part 2 reception is requested with UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message and response message is UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs. Following chart presents CSI Part 2 parameters:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating a communication flow related to "Basic CSI Part 2 reception" in a 5G network. The diagram depicts interactions between three entities: UE (User Equipment), L1 (Layer 1), and L2PS (Layer 2 Protocol Stack).
The diagram shows the following sequence of events:
1. **UE to L1:** A yellow box originating from UE contains information about when CSI Part 2 is requested. This occurs when CSI Part 1 reception is requested AND the number of CSI Part 2 reports is greater than 0. It also lists CSI2 related parameters such as `maxNumOfUciCsiPart2Symbols`, `maxNumOfUciCsiPart2BitsPlusCrcUpTo11Bits`, `maxNumOfUciCsiPart2BitsPlusCrcMoreThan11Bits`, `csiPart2CodeRateUpTo11Bits`, `csiPart2CodeRateMoreThan11Bits`, and per CSI Part 2 report parameters: `-riStartPos`, `-numOfRiBits`, `-numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRio`, `-numOfWidebandCsiPart2Bits Ri1`, `-numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRi2`, and `-numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRi3`.
2. **L1 to L2PS:** An arrow labeled `UIData_PuschReceiveReq()` goes from L1 to L2PS.
3. **UE to L1:** An arrow labeled `PUSCH (CSI1 with/wo RI bit(s) + CSI2 + ULSCH)` goes from UE to L1.
4. **L1 to L2PS:** A yellow box originating from L1 states "CSI Part 1 & 2 reception".
5. **L1 to L2PS:** An arrow labeled `UIData_PuschReceiveRespPs()` goes from L1 to L2PS.
6. **L2PS:** A yellow box originating from L2PS lists "CSI2 parameters": `-numOfUciCsiPart2Bits`, `-uciCsiPart2Bits`, `-dtxCsiPart2`, `-dtxMetricCsiPart2`, `-dtxThresholdCsiPart2`, and `-csiPart2CrcCheck`.
The title of the diagram is "5GC001817-A/CB007220-A: Basic CSI Part 2 reception".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1495.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

(review comment: numOfUciCsiPart2Bits added into resp message)

---

#### 5.4.8.5.6 CSI Part 2 reception cases

**5.4.8.5.6.0-2**  (ID: `12733508`)

CSI Part 2 reception cases
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support the following CSI report cases:

* Part 1 + Part 2 report requiring Ri
* Part 1 + Part 2 report not requiring Ri

AN may be present in PUSCH. There is no restriction on the relative number of AN bits beyond the maximum number of AN bits.

There is also no restriction in PUSCH parameter setting, and especially, PUSCH may contain as many layers as it may contain without Part 1 and Part 2.

CSI Part 2 reception is requested only for the new transmissions, and not for the re-transmissions.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

The requirement is supported in Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.8.5.7 CSI Part 2 Omission Check

**5.4.8.5.7.0-2**  (ID: `12733509`)

CSI Part 2 Omission Check
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall check CSI Part 2 Omission.

Omission means that UE may omit CSI Part 2 transmission, and L1 needs to check this. Omission happens when UE does not have enough resources for CSI Part 2 transmission.

When subband reports are supported, it is possible that omission is partial.

For this check, L1 uses parameters:

maxNumOfUciCsiPart2BitsPlusCrcUpTo11Bits,

maxNumOfUciCsiPart2BitsPlusCrcMoreThan11Bits

from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq and identified number of CSI Part 2 bits (OCSI-2) and number of CRC bits (LCSI-2), see [11406238](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9cHqPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Rule is following:

If (OCSI-2) <=11 and

If (OCSI-2+LCSI-2) > maxNumOfUciCsiPart2BitsPlusCrcUpTo11Bits then CSI Part 2 is omitted

If (OCSI-2) >11 and

If (OCSI-2+LCSI-2) > maxNumOfUciCsiPart2BitsPlusCrcMoreThan11Bits then CSI Part 2 is omitted

When CSI Part 2 is omitted, UE sends Pusch as it is transmitted without CSI Part 2 at all, thus UE uses all possible Q'CSI-2 resources for Pusch data.

When CSI Part 2 is omitted, UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message contains numOfuciCsiPart2Bits parameter set to 0.

These parameters can be set to any value:

-dtxCsiPart2

-dtxMetricCsiPart2

-dtxThresholdCsiPart2

-csiPart2CrcCheck

Note that above check is always done in L1, even in the case there is no RI reported in CSI Part1.

For further info, see

-TS 38.214 chapter 5.2.3,

-maxNumOfUciCsiPart2BitsPlusCrcUpTo11Bits calculation: 5G\_UP\_IF\_2125 and 5G UP ALG [11160619](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktPCPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

The requirement is supported in Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.8.5.8 Number of bits Determination - CSI Part 2

**5.4.8.5.8.0-1**  (ID: `11406135`)

Number of bits Determination - CSI Part 2
When L2 requests CSI Part 2 reception, L1 needs to check how many bits are in CSI Part 2.

There is two different cases how this is done, and it depends whether UE sends rank bits in CSI Part 1 or not

Below calculation is done for each CSI Part 2 report separately:

(But note that only one report is supported in 21B, and multiple reports are supported in 22R1 feature CB008131). Strikethrough done 25.8.2022 with CNI-86611

CSI Part 2 does not require rank bits

In this case UE is configured to use only one rank and L2 sets parameter numOfRiBits to 0 and numOfWidebandUciCsiPart2BitsRi0 contains number of CSI Part 2 bits, thus:

* OCSI-2 += numOfWidebandUciCsiPart2BitsRi0

CSI Part 2 requires ranks bits

In this case UE is allowed to use multiple ranks, and selected rank is transmitted in CSI Part 1. Logic to determine number of CSI Part 2 bits is following:

* L1 receives first CSI Part 1, then
* extracts RI bits from decoded CSI Part 1 for this CSI Part 2 report\* by using UlData\_PuschReceiveReq.puschUciConfig parameters riStartPos and numOfRiBits.
* Note, if CSI Part 1 was detected as DTX, RI is still checked from decoded CSI Part 1.
* checks the RI bit value.
* determines number of CSI Part 2 bits (OCSI-2) with this kind of mapping:
* If RI bit value=0, OCSI-2 += numOfWidebandUciCsiPart2BitsRi0
* If RI bit value=1, OCSI-2 += numOfWidebandUciCsiPart2BitsRi1
* If RI bit value=2, OCSI-2 += numOfWidebandUciCsiPart2BitsRi2
* If RI bit value=3, OCS-I2 += numOfWidebandUciCsiPart2BitsRi3

After calculating all CSI Part 2 reports, OCSI-2 is sum of all CSI part2 report sizes.

See 5G\_L1\_5471\_replaced([11406350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9cut_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), which contains info how many reports are supported.

3GPP definitions for these values are these:

* OCSI-2=Number of CSI Part 2 bits
* Q'CSI-2=Number of Resource elements CSI Part 2 takes

See TS38.212 chapter 6.3.2.4.1.3.

---

#### 5.4.8.5.9 Number of Resource Elements Determination - CSI Part 2

**5.4.8.5.9.0-1**  (ID: `11406175`)

Number of Resource Elements Determination - CSI Part 2
The number of CSI part 2 resource elements (Q'CSI-2) is calculated with following formula:

Q’CSI-2 = roundup(csiPart2CoderateUpTo11Bits\*OCSI-2)

Note that parameter csiPart2CoderateUpTo11Bits is float type, and it is not 100% precise. So, in some cases it is possible that Q'CSI-2 is calculated incorrectly, and in that case CSI Part 2 and UL-SCH TB decoding fails.

Interface is changed and csiPart2CoderateUpTo11Bits parameter is not used any more.

Necessary parameters are transmitted by L2, and L1 calculates code rates itself. Necessary parameters are these:

-csiPart2BetaOffsetUpto11Bits

-csiPart2BetaOffsetMoreThan11Bits (This parameter is supported in ABIO in 22R1, CB008131 feature)

-numOfUciRes

Number of CSI Part 2 resource elements (Q'CSI-2) are calculated with following formulas:

First, determine used beta parameter like this:

If ((OCSI-2) <=11) then beta=csiPart2BetaOffsetUpto11Bits

else beta=csiPart2BetaOffsetMoreThan11Bits

Then calculate resource elements:

Q'CSI-2= roundup(OCSI-2*beta*numOfUciRes/(numOfCodeBlocks\*codeBlockSize))

Note, and to be checked?:

csiPart2BetaOffsetUpto11Bits

csiPart2BetaOffsetMoreThan11Bits

parameters need to be divided by 8, before calculating Q'CSI-2

This is not needed because Omission check covers also this part:

(remove in 21C timeframe)

If Q'CSI-2 > maxNumOfUciCsiPart2Symbols then

Q'CSI-2 = maxNumOfUciCsiPart2Symbols

[RP003187-M] The requirement is also applicable for L1 Thor for FR1-FDD-CPRI as parity (CB008131). [End RP003187-M]

---

After decoding CSI Part1 by 5G-L1-UL, RI is obtained via *riStartPos,* riUsageMap (see [11160689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktPpSfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Number of CSI Part 2 bits ([image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of the letters "A" and "(a)". The letters are in a blue and orange gradient, with a slight blur effect. The background is black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]) is provided by (Formula-1) for the ranks from x=1 to x=4. The csi reports are indexed by j where [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "j ∈ [1..numOfCsiReports]". The text is blurry and appears to be in a mathematical or programming context.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]. The Jth CSI report part 2 is constructed from wideband ([image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Ocsi-2 wideband j". The text is in a dark color against a light background. The font is a sans-serif font. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]) parts.\*\*

The CSI report part 2 is constructed of multiple csi reports as shown below:

**Table 1**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **UCI bit sequence** | **CSI report number** |
| [image-3][image\_desc]The image is blurry and pixelated, making it difficult to discern specific details. The text is slightly blurry, but still legible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.013.png[/image\_path][/image-12] |  |

Where

*OCSI-2 wideband 1 corresponds to the wideband component of the of the CSI report number 1 which contains numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRix bits.*

*OCSI-2 wideband numOfCsiReports corresponds to the wideband component of the of the CSI report number numOfCsiReports, which contains numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRix bits.*

RI is not aperiory known from *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq, hence the bit-lengths of wideband components for all the possible RIs are given via numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRix , where [image-13][image\_desc]x ∈ [1 ... maximum available RI][/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.014.png[/image\_path][/image-13].*

[image-14][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$A^{(2)} = \sum\_{j=1}^{numOfCsiReports} numOfWidebandCsiPart2BitsRx\_{csiReport(j)}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.015.png[/image\_path][/image-14] (Formula-1)

Number of CRC bits ([image-15][image\_desc]The image contains the text "LCSL-2" in a stylized font. The text is colored with a gradient that transitions from a light brown to a darker brown, with hints of orange and yellow. The letters are slightly blurred, giving them a soft appearance. The background is a solid black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.016.png[/image\_path][/image-15]) is set as follows:

-  [image-16][image\_desc]The image contains the text "L CSI-2 = 0". The text is in a sans-serif font and is slightly blurred. The letters "L CSI" are in black, the hyphen and the number "2" are in a dark gray, and the equals sign and the number "0" are in a lighter gray. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.017.png[/image\_path][/image-16] bit when [image-17][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "A(3) ≤ 11". The expression is written in a light blue font against a white background. The letter "A" is capitalized, and the number "3" is a superscript. The symbol "≤" represents "less than or equal to". The number "11" is written in standard font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.018.png[/image\_path][/image-17]

-  [image-18][image\_desc]The image contains the text "L CSI-2 = 6". The text is in a dark color against a light background. The letters and numbers are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.019.png[/image\_path][/image-18] bits when [image-19][image\_desc]12 ≤ A⁽²⁾ ≤ 19[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.020.png[/image\_path][/image-19]

-  [image-20][image\_desc]The image contains the text "L CSI-2 = 11". The text is in a dark color against a white background. The letters and numbers are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.021.png[/image\_path][/image-20] bits when [image-21][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "A(3) >= 20". The text is in a light blue color against a white background. The characters are clear and legible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.022.png[/image\_path][/image-21]

5G-L1-UL requires the following information (provided in *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq) to understand whether a portion or all the CSI part 2 is omitted or not.*

* csiPart2CodeRate ([image-22][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is a fraction. The numerator is $\beta\_{\text{offset}}^{\text{PUSCH}} \cdot \sum\_{l=0}^{N\_{\text{symb.all}}^{\text{NPUSCH}}} M\_{\text{sc}}^{\text{UCI}}(l)^{-1}$. The denominator is $\sum\_{r=0}^{C\_{\text{UL-SCH}}} K\_r^{-1}$.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.023.png[/image\_path][/image-22])
* maxNumOfUciCsiPart2Symbols ([image-23][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible objects, text, or features.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.024.png[/image\_path][/image-23])

[image-24][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a blurry font. The formula is:
`[(A(2) + Lcsi-2) * csiPart2CodeRate] ≤ maxNumOfUciCsiPart2Symbols`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1474.025.png[/image\_path][/image-24] (Formula-2)

If Formula-2 is not held, all the wideband CSI reports are omitted, and L1-UL understands that CSI report does not contain any CSI Part 2.

Remark1: maxNumOfUciCsiPart2BitsPlusCrc contains the maximum number of CSI Part 2 bits with their corresponding CRC bits (*O*CSI-2*+L* CSI-2 , 38.212 6.3.2.4.1.3)  that are needed for 5G-L1-Ul internal procedures.\*\*\*\*

Remark2: the above IEs are used by 5G-L1-UL as explained in (~~5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1026~~ 5G\_L1\_2924, 5G\_RAN\_UP\_26789)

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

The requirement is supported in Loner, Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

[][/xlsm_desc][xlsm_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/attachments/pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file1508_0.xlsm[/xlsm_path] It contains vba macro named "mapUci()", which works like this: Private Sub mapUci() clearResourceGrid symbol_ctr = 0 readInputParameters reserve_AN2 printUci (8) map_CSI1 printUci (9) map_CSI2 printUci (10) 'map_data map_AN1 printUci (11) End Sub It does resource mapping with these restrictions: -DMRS Mapping Type B, in which case UCI is mapped from 2nd symbol onwards, -mapping is shown for 1st UCI OFDM symbol -AN 1 bit -UL-SCH mapping is not shown Here is presented principle how resource mapping is done with 0,1,2,3 bit AN: [xlsx_desc]--- AN_CSI Part1&amp;2_B --- NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN ##### 5.4.8.5.11.1 Supported UCI code rate

###### 5.4.8.5.11.1.0-1 Supported UCI code rate L1 shall support any UCI (AN, CSI Part 1 and Part 2) code rates, taking into account what is maximum amount of rate matched bits, see 5G_L1_6288_replaced( 11406979 ). If UCI have zero Resource Element (RE) available, then "decoding" is done, and all response messages are sent to L2. (ID: 11406218)

##### 5.4.8.5.11.2 Remaining TB code rate after UCI mapping

**5.4.8.5.11.2.0-1**  (ID: `11406231`)

Remaining TB code rate after UCI mapping
After (AN), CSI Part 1 and CSI Part 2 are mapped, UL allocation should still have suffient amount of REs available to Transport Block (TB).

TB coding rate is controlled by L2, and is described in 5G\_UP\_2641.

Regardless of the L2 code rate control, L1 shall be able to handle any remaining TB code rate.

When there is zero RE remaining, then:

On [PR649774 22R2 only, PR642696 22R3 included onward] Loner, [End PR649774 22R2 only, PR642696 22R3 included onward] Loki and Thor HW:

TB decoding is not done, and L1 does not send these messages for the grant:

UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLo

UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU

In ABIL:

TB decoding is started, and L1 sends this message:

UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU

Mostly like this is not sent:

UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLo

Difference between ABIN/O and ABIL is because LDEC in loki crashed if there was zero REs, therefore it was agreed that LDEC decoding is not done/started. This also means that response messages are not sent via normal processing flow, because LDEC completion triggers them.

This functionality is preferred to be changed in future, so that both products works in same manner.

---

#### 5.4.8.5.12 Rank Indicator

**5.4.8.5.12.0-3**  (ID: `12733511`)

Rank Indicator
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support 0-2 Rank bits in CSI Part 1 per CSI Part 2 report.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

The requirement is supported in Loner, Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.8.5.13 Beta Offset

##### 5.4.8.5.13.0-1 Beta Offset L1 shall support dynamic beta offset configuration from L2-RT/CP. (ID: 11405971)

#### 5.4.8.5.14 CSI Part 2 decoding, CRC and DTX

**5.4.8.5.14.0-3**  (ID: `12733513`)

CSI Part 2 decoding, CRC and DTX
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall decode CSI according according to the followings.

The bumber of CSI Part 2 payload bits determines

* used decoding algorithm,
* if CRC is added,
* whether CSI2 DTX, CSI2 CRC, PUSCH DTX are considered when setting for final DTX for CSI Part 2.

First two bullets are defined in 3GPP standard, and last one is defined 5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification, in chapter 2.3.10.

See summary from below table:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CSI2, #ofbits | decoding | CRC | considered for final CSI2 DTX | Feature |
| <3 | simplex | na | CSI2 DTX and PUSCH DTX | 5GC001817-A/CB007220-A |
| 3-11 | rm | na | CSI2 DTX and PUSCH DTX | 5GC001817-A/CB007220-A |
| 12-19 | polar | 6 bits | CSI2 DTX and PUSCH DTX with  special offset | CB008131 |
| >19 | polar | 11 bits | CSI2 CRC | na |

For more details on decoding algorithms, see 3GPP TS 38.212 chapters:

-simplex: chapters 5.3.3.1 and 5.3.3.2 for 1 bit and 2 bits, respectively

-reed muller (rm): chapter 5.3.3.3; Encoding of other small block lenghts

-polar: chapter 5.3.1

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.8.5.15 CSI Part 2 DTX detection algorithm

**5.4.8.5.15.0-1**  (ID: `11406258`)

CSI Part 2 DTX detection algorithm
L1 shall detect DTX from CSI Part 2, as is described in 5GMax algorithm specification chapter:

2.3.10 UCI on PUSCH decoding and detection

Selected algorithms are the following:

Simplex code (1-2 bits):

-LLR based method

Reed muller code:

-Method ‘Rel0d5SumAbsSoftBit’

Polar code:

-if CSI2 bits <= 20, Path metric based DTX detection

-if CSI2 bits > 20 CRC check only is used (CRC is used directly as DTX info, thus it may contain values > 0).

---

#### 5.4.8.5.16 Adjusted CSI2 DTX thresholds

**5.4.8.5.16.0-3**  (ID: `12733514`)

Adjusted CSI2 DTX thresholds
To improve missed detection rate of A-CSI CSI Part2, 5G-L1-UL (BB) shall apply a specific DTX Detection threshold for A-CSI CSI Part2 on PUSCH when all following conditions are met:

 parameter actPuschACSIPart2ThrAdj in UlCell\_SetupReq message (defined in [11513449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRwhwfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is set to true

 PUSCH MCS is in modulation order Qm <=2.

 CSI part2 is transmitted with over 1 or 2 bits, or [3..11] bits using Reed-Muller code

Detailed information is described in 5GMax algorithm specification chapters:

* 2.3.10.1.2.2 Adjusted Threshold Set for Csi2

###### - 2.3.10.2.2.2 Adjusted Threshold Set for Csi2

The requirement above applies to PUSCH with 2RX and 4RX on Loki, **PUSCH with 2RX, 4RX, 8Rx, and 16Rx on Thor and** [CB012115-MH] Odin. [End CB012115-MH]\*\*\*\*

Note:

* PUSCH with 8RX on Thor is only supported with FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI and FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI.
* PUSCH with 16RX on Thor is only supported with FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI

[CB012115-MH]

- PUSCH with 8RX on Odin is supported with FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI.

* PUSCH with 16Rx and 8Rx is supported in FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI.

[End CB012115-MH]

---

#### 5.4.8.5.17 CSI Part 2 final DTX setting

**5.4.8.5.17.0-1**  (ID: `11406303`)

CSI Part 2 final DTX setting
When number of CSI Part 2 payload bits is < 20 bits, CSI2 DTX and Pusch DTX has to be checked together when setting final CSI2 DTX, which is reported to L2.

See logic from this table:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CSI2 < 20 bits, PUSCH DTX and CSI2 DTX cases |  |  |
| Pusch DTX | CSI2 DTX | final CSI2 DTX |
| DTX | DTX | DTX |
| TX | TX | TX |
| DTX | TX | DTX |
| TX | DTX | DTX |
|  |  |  |

This case is not supported yet:

If number of CSI Part 2 payload bits is > 19, only CSI2 CRC is considered

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| CSI2 | final CSI2 DTX |
| CRC NOK | DTX |
| CRC OK | TX |

---

#### 5.4.8.5.18 Sending CSI Part 2 bits to L2

**5.4.8.5.18.0-2**  (ID: `12733515`)

Sending CSI Part 2 bits to L2
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall send the CSI Part 2 bits to L2 in message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs with the following parameters:

-numOfUciCsiPart2Bits, this parameter contains value what was determined above as parameter OCSI-2, see [11406135](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9bgnPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

-uciCsiPart2Bits, this parameter contains bits which were decoded.

In case DTX was detected, decoded uciCsiPart1Bits and uciCsiPart2Bits shall be sent to L2. They can possibly be used for debugging, or conformance test counters (see 5G\_L2\_1772)

-dtxCsiPart2, see [11406252](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9cHqvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

-dtxMetricCsiPart2, see [11406252](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9cHqvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

-dtxThresholdCsiPart2, see [11406252](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9cHqvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

-csiPart2CrcCheck, see [11406160](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9bgn_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

The requirement is supported in Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.8.5.19 Min &amp; Max number CSI Part 1, CSI Part 2 (and RI) bits

**5.4.8.5.19.0-2**  (ID: `12733521`)

Min & Max number CSI Part 1, CSI Part 2 (and RI) bits
5G-L1-UL shall support the following limits for the number bit of the CSI.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 5GC001817-A | NrOfBits min, max |  |
| FDD case | P1 (min, max) | P2 (min, max) |
| P1with Ri+P2 | 5,6 | 1,5 (1x5) |
| P1witout Ri+P2 | 4,6 | 1,5 |
|  |  |  |
| Note, values are without 5GC002200 feature (8 port CSI-RS) |  |  |
| CB007220-A | NrOfBits min, max |  |
| TDD FR1 case | P1 (min, max) | P2 (min, max) |
| P1with Ri+P2 | 5,8 | 1,11 |
| P1without Ri+P2 | 4,8 | 1,11 |

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Note, values are w/ 5GC002200 feature (8 port CSI-RS) |  |  |
| CB007922 | NrOfBits min, max with 2 CC |  |
| TDD+TDD CA FR1 case | P1 (min, max) | P2 (min, max) |
| P1+P2 | 4,40 (up to 19 bits for channel acquisition and up to 21 for Beam Management) | 1,11 |

Maximum Number of RI bits in CSI Part 1 is two, meaning that CSI Part 2 may have 4 different lengths.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

The requirement is supported in Loner, Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.8.5.20 Number of CSI Part 2 reports

**5.4.8.5.20.0-2**  (ID: `12733522`)

Number of CSI Part 2 reports
5G-L1-UL (BB) shall support a maximum of 8 CSI Part 2 reports. The parameter numOfCsiPart2Reports in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq indicates how many CSI Part 2 reports are multiplexed on PUSCH.

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

The requirement is supported in Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.8.5.21 Number of UEs receiving A-CSI per slot

**5.4.8.5.21.0-1**  (ID: `11406371`)

Number of UEs receiving A-CSI per slot
Number of UEs receiving A-CSI in loki is defined in :

TDD FR1: 5G\_UP\_3216

FDD: 5G\_UP\_3226

---

Following picture shows principle how CSI Part 1 + CSI Part 2 processing goes in Loki when at least one CSI Part 2 requires RI information from CSI Part 1.

Orange color shows additions for the 5GC001817/CB007220-C and CB008131 features.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process for receiving UCI (Uplink Control Information) in a telecommunications system, specifically focusing on a scenario where CSI Part 1 and at least one CSI Part 2 report require RI (Rank Indicator).
The flowchart begins with a box labeled "CSI omission check Final CSI2 DTX setting" which receives input from "Pusch DTX". This box then leads to a process that "creates job to process CSI1". This process receives a "Req" signal and outputs a "resp" signal.
The "creates job to process CSI1" box is connected to a "DMAP" box, which is described as "Separates CSI1 soft symbols (and CSI2 and data)". The DMAP box then feeds into an "RDEC/PDEC" box, which outputs "Csi1 bits". These "Csi1 bits" are then processed by a "CSI1 decoder" that "Separates RI bits".
There's a parallel path for CSI Part 2. The "creates job to process CSI1" box also has an output that leads to a decision point: "If Multiple CSI Part 2s, calculates sum of CSI2 bits". This decision point has two branches. One branch leads to a box labeled "Not done if no data". The other branch is not explicitly defined but implies a continuation if data is present.
The "After receiving RI Creates job to decode CS12 and Pusch data" box is a central component. It receives input from the "CSI1 decoder" (specifically, the RI bits) and also from the "If Multiple CSI Part 2s, calculates sum of CSI2 bits" path. This box then splits into multiple outputs.
One output from "After receiving RI Creates job to decode CS12 and Pusch data" goes to an "RDEC/PDEC" box, which takes input labeled ">2 bits". This RDEC/PDEC box outputs "Csi2 bits". These "Csi2 bits" are then fed into a "Simplex decoder CS12 decoder/".
Another output from "After receiving RI Creates job to decode CS12 and Pusch data" goes to an "LDEC" box, which outputs "Pusch data".
The "Simplex decoder CS12 decoder/" box receives "Csi2 bits" and also takes input from the "RDEC/PDEC" box that processes the ">2 bits". The Simplex decoder outputs "uciCsiPart2bits".
The "Simplex decoder CS12 decoder/" box also feeds into a "CRC check DTX detection DTX Threshold and DTX metric calculation" box.
Finally, there are three colored boxes at the bottom right: "MHAB" (blue), "MDAB" (green), and "ARM(rt)" (black with green text). The connections from these boxes to the rest of the flowchart are not explicitly shown, but they likely represent different processing units or modes.
The overall flow suggests a process where CSI Part 1 is decoded to obtain RI, and then based on the presence of CSI Part 2 reports, further decoding of CSI Part 2 and Pusch data occurs, with checks for DTX and CRC.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1532.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Pictures can be found from this powerpoint slideset:

Following picture shows principle how CSI Part 1 + CSI Part 2 processing goes when no RI is needed for any CSI Part 2 report.

Difference to above case is that number of CSI Part 2 bits are known before CSI Part 1 processing, and thus CSI Part 2, and Pusch data processing can be started immediately, parallel after soft symbols from DMAP are available.

Orange color shows additions for the 5GC001817/CB007220-C and CB008131.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a "UCI reception case: CSI Part 1 + at least one CSI Part 2 report requires RI". The flowchart uses rectangular boxes for processes and diamond shapes for decisions or conditional inputs. Arrows indicate the flow of data and control between these elements.
Here's a breakdown of the elements and their connections:
**Top Section:**
\* **"CSI omission. check Final CSI2 DTX setting"**: A blue rectangular box with text. An arrow points from this box to the "creates job to process CSI1" box.
\* **"Pusch DTX"**: A red wavy text label. An arrow points from this label to the "Measurement" box.
\* **"Measurement"**: A green rectangular box. An arrow points from this box to the "DMAP" box.
**Main Processing Flow:**
\* **"Req"**: A label indicating a request input. An arrow points from "Req" to the "creates job to process CSI1" box.
\* **"creates job to process CSI1"**: A black rectangular box. Arrows point from this box to "DMAP" and "RDEC/PDEC".
\* **"DMAP Separates CSI1 soft symbols (and CSI2 and data)"**: A blue rectangular box. An arrow points from "DMAP" to "RDEC/PDEC".
\* **"RDEC/PDEC"**: A blue rectangular box. An arrow points from this box to "Csi1 bits".
\* **"Csi1 bits"**: A blue rectangular box. An arrow points from this box to "CSI1 decoder".
\* **"CSI1 decoder Separates RI bits."**: A green rectangular box. An arrow points from this box to "RI+uciCsiPart1bits".
\* **"RI+uciCsiPart1bits"**: A blue rectangular box. An arrow points from this box to "After receiving RI Creates job to decode CSI2 and Pusch data".
**Conditional/Parallel Processing:**
\* **"resp"**: A label indicating a response output. An arrow points from "After receiving RI Creates job to decode CSI2 and Pusch data" to "resp".
\* **"If Multiple CSI Part 2s, calculates sum of CSI2 bits"**: An orange speech bubble shape. An arrow points from this shape to the "After receiving RI Creates job to decode CSI2 and Pusch data" box.
\* **"Not done if no data"**: An orange rectangular box. An arrow points from this box to the "After receiving RI Creates job to decode CSI2 and Pusch data" box.
\* **">2 bits"**: A label. An arrow points from "DMAP" to this label, and another arrow points from this label to "RDEC/PDEC" (the one below the first RDEC/PDEC).
\* **"RDEC/PDEC"**: A blue rectangular box. An arrow points from this box to "Csi2 bits".
\* **"Csi2 bits"**: A blue rectangular box. An arrow points from this box to "Simplex decoder CS12 decoder/".
\* **"Simplex decoder CS12 decoder/"**: A green rectangular box. An arrow points from this box to "CRC check DTX detection DTX Threashold and DTX metric. calculation".
\* **"1-2 bits"**: A label. An arrow points from "DMAP" to this label, and another arrow points from this label to "Simplex decoder CS12 decoder/".
\* **"LDEC"**: A blue rectangular box. An arrow points from "DMAP" to "LDEC". An arrow also points from "LDEC" to "Pusch data".
\* **"Pusch data"**: A red wavy text label. An arrow points from "LDEC" to this label. An arrow points from "Pusch data" to "uciCsiPart2bits".
\* **"uciCsiPart2bits"**: A blue rectangular box. An arrow points from "Pusch data" to this box. An arrow also points from "Simplex decoder CS12 decoder/" to "uciCsiPart2bits".
**Final Output/Check:**
\* **"CRC check DTX detection DTX Threashold and DTX metric. calculation"**: A blue rectangular box. An arrow points from "Simplex decoder CS12 decoder/" to this box.
**Legend:**
\* **"MHAB"**: A blue rectangular box.
\* **"MDAB"**: A green rectangular box.
\* **"ARM(rt)"**: A black rectangular box.
The overall diagram depicts a complex reception process involving CSI (Channel State Information) data, with different parts (Part 1, Part 2) and associated metrics like RI (Rank Indicator), DTX (Discontinuous Transmission), and Pusch (Physical Uplink Shared Channel). The process involves decoding, checking for omissions, and handling different bit lengths and data types.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1534.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[CNI-122313-A]

RINLINE1 HW is supported until 24R1 inclusive. RINLINE1 HW is not supported since 24R2.

[End CNI-122313-A]

---

### 5.4.8.6 Discontinued Transmission (DTX) - Final Detection

#### 5.4.8.6.1 DTX using Uplink Channel Information - FDD

**5.4.8.6.1.0-1**  (ID: `11406432`)

DTX using Uplink Channel Information - FDD
FDD FR1 L1 Loner: 5G-L1-UL shall compute the PUSCH DTX metric for each component of the UCI (ACK, CSI Part1) separately, if they are transmitted, according to the algorithm described in section 2.3.10 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

* If the UCI component (ACK bits or CSI Part1 bits) is transmitted with over 1 or 2 bits, LLR based Dtx detection method , as well as the corresponding threshold and metric values, are described in in section 2.3.10.1.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications.
* If the UCI component (ACK bits or CSI Part1 bits) is transmitted with over [3..11] bits (Reed-Muller coding), Rel0d5SumAbsSoftBit Dtx detection method, as well as the corresponding threshold and metric values, are described in in section 2.3.10.2.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications
* if numOfUciAckBits in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is 1 bit,  dtxAck1Bit, dtxMetricAck1Bit and dtxThresholdAck1Bit (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_210 5G\_L1\_IF\_211\_replaced([11513924](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR0MEfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) contain valid measurements in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD. However dtxAckMoreThan1Bit, dtxMetricAckMoreThan1Bit and dtxThresholdAckMoreThan1Bit contain invalid measurements which  should be ignored by L2-Ps.
* if numOfUciAckBits in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is set to 2 bits, there might be either one or two bits in the UCI, hence dtxAck1Bit, dtxMetricAck1Bit and dtxThresholdAck1Bit are used for 1 bit UCI decoding in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD and dtxAckMoreThan1Bit, dtxMetricAckMoreThan1Bit and dtxThresholdAckMoreThan1Bit are used for 2-bit UCI decoding.
* if numOfUciAckBits in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is higher than 2 bits,  dtxAck1Bit, dtxMetricAck1Bit and dtxThresholdAck1Bit contain invalid measurements in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD should be ignored by L2-Ps. However dtxAckMoreThan1Bit, dtxMetricAckMoreThan1Bit and dtxThresholdAckMoreThan1Bit contain valid measurements .

Then, 5G-L1-UL shall report its measurements to 5G-L2-PS in the two messages UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs and UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU.

Note: The false alarm target for this metric is set to 0.5% with false alarm is defined as the probability to detect UCI bits whereas nothing was sent on PUSCH.

[5GC002481-AA] For Loki L1 HW, 5G-L1-UL shall report this metric to 5G-L2-PS as described above.[End 5GC002481-AA]

[RP003187-M] The requirement is also applicable for L1 Thor for FR1-FDD-CPRI. [End RP003187-M]

---

#### 5.4.8.6.2 DTX using Uplink Channel Information - TDD, 7-2a split

**5.4.8.6.2.0-3**  (ID: `13254098`)

DTX using Uplink Channel Information - TDD, 7-2a split
TDD FR1 L1 Loner/~~Loki~~, Thor: 5G-L1-UL shall compute the PUSCH DTX metric for each component of the UCI (ACK, CSI Part1) separately, if they are transmitted, according to the algorithm described in section 2.3.10 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications (see ~~5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916~~ 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

* If the UCI component (ACK bits or CSI Part1 bits) is transmitted with over 1 or 2 bits, LLR based Dtx detection method , as well as the corresponding threshold and metric values, are described in in section 2.3.10.1.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications.
* If the UCI component (ACK bits or CSI Part1 bits) is transmitted with over [3..11] bits (Reed-Muller coding), Rel0d5SumAbsSoftBit Dtx detection method, as well as the corresponding threshold and metric values, are described in in section 2.3.10.2.2 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications
* If the UCI component (ACK bits or CSI Part1 bits) is transmitted with equal or more than 12 bits (Polar coding), Path metric Dtx detection method, as well as the corresponding threshold and metric values, are described in in section 2.3.10.3 of the 5Gmax algorithm specifications
* if *numOfUciAckBits in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is 1 bit,  *dtxAck1Bit,* dtxMetricAck1Bit and *dtxThresholdAck1Bit (~~5G\_L1\_5G21A\_210~~ 5G\_L1\_IF\_211\_replaced([11513924](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR0MEfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) contain valid measurements in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD. However *dtxAckMoreThan1Bit,* dtxMetricAckMoreThan1Bit and *dtxThresholdAckMoreThan1Bit contain invalid measurements which* should be ignored by L2-Ps.***\****\*\*\*
* if *numOfUciAckBits in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is set to 2 bits, there might be either one or two bits in the UCI, hence *dtxAck1Bit,* dtxMetricAck1Bit and *dtxThresholdAck1Bit are used for 1 bit UCI decoding in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD and *dtxAckMoreThan1Bit,* dtxMetricAckMoreThan1Bit and *dtxThresholdAckMoreThan1Bit are used for 2-bit UCI decoding.****\****\*
* if *numOfUciAckBits in* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is higher than 2 bits,  *dtxAck1Bit,* dtxMetricAck1Bit and *dtxThresholdAck1Bit contain invalid measurements in* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD should be ignored by L2-Ps. However *dtxAckMoreThan1Bit,* dtxMetricAckMoreThan1Bit and *dtxThresholdAckMoreThan1Bit contain valid measurements .****\****\*

Then, 5G-L1-UL shall report its measurements to 5G-L2-PS in the two messages *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs and* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD.\*\*

Note: The false alarm target for this metric is set to 0.5% with false alarm is defined as the probability to detect UCI bits whereas nothing was sent on PUSCH.

The calculated values for dtxThresholdAck1Bit, dtxThresholdAckMoreThan1Bit, and dtxThresholdCsiPart1 are subject to overflow protection. If a calculated value exceeds the maximum limit of the u16 data type (65,535), the value shall be saturated at 65,535 to prevent integer overflow and ensure the stability of the DTX detection algorithm.

The requirement is also applicable for **L1 Thor for FR1-TDD-eCPRI.**

[CB013932-A] The requirement is applied for L1 THOR for FR1-TDD-CPRI [End CB013932-A]

---

#### 5.4.8.6.3 DTX using Uplink Channel Information - TDD, 7-2e split

**5.4.8.6.3.0-3**  (ID: `13257371`)

DTX using Uplink Channel Information - TDD, 7-2e split
L1-BB shall get partial DTX detection result from message *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespUePs and determine final DTX detection status from CRC as follows:*

if (crcResult == TRUE) *dtx=FALSE*

else *dtx is unchanged.*

Final DTX detection status is reported to L2-PS in IE *dtx of message* UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_201\_replaced([11513837](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and message *UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_209\_replaced([11513897](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlD_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).*\*\*

L1-BB shall use DTX metric and threshold of PUSCH data (IE *dtxMetric and* dtxThreshold of message *UlDataFH\_PuschReceiveRespUePs) for determining DTX status of UCI over PUSCH (see 5G\_L1\_14241\_replaced([11406892](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9fyB_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). DTX metric and threshold are not sent to L2-PS.*\*\*

[CB013932-A] The calculated values for dtxThresholdAck1Bit, dtxThresholdAckMoreThan1Bit, and dtxThresholdCsiPart1 are subject to overflow protection. If a calculated value exceeds the maximum limit of the u16 data type (65,535), the value shall be saturated at 65,535 to prevent integer overflow and ensure the stability of the DTX detection algorithm. [End CB013932-A]

---

#### 5.4.8.6.4 DTX Threshold Adjustment for Configured Grant

##### 5.4.8.6.4.1 Loner

**5.4.8.6.4.1.0-1**  (ID: `11404632`)

Loner
For Loner.

For Configured Grant (CG) transmission on PUSCH selected DTX threshold shall be multiplied by a specific factor depending on number of antennas, rank and number of PRBs to obtain a target False Alarm Rate.

DtxThreshold = ThresholdTable(NumAnt, NumPrb) \* factor

[Before PR723504]

The value of the factor is 1.

[PR723504]

The value of the factor is:

For 2RX and 4RX, rank1 (0.1% FA):

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| numOfPrbs | factor |
| <= 4 | 1.28 |
| 5…8 | 1.18 |
| 9…12 | 1.2 |
| 13…16 | 1.14 |
| 17…20 | 1.18 |
| 21…24 | 1.16 |
| 25…32 | 1.2 |
| 33…36 | 1.18 |
| >= 37 | 1.16 |

For 2RX and 4RX, rank2 (0.1% FA):

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| numOfPrbs | factor |
| <= 4 | 1.28 |
| 5…12 | 1.2 |
| 13…16 | 1.16 |
| 17…20 | 1.18 |
| 21…24 | 1.14 |
| 25…28 | 1.18 |
| 29…32 | 1.14 |
| >= 33 | 1.12 |

[End PR723504]

Note: CG transmission can be detected if for a given grant rnti != cRnti in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq.

---

##### 5.4.8.6.4.2 SoC (Loki, Thor, Odin)

**5.4.8.6.4.2.0-2**  (ID: `12733047`)

SoC (Loki, Thor, Odin)
In Loki, Thor, and Odin, for Configured Grant (CG) transmission on PUSCH (if for a given grant, *rnti is not equal to* cRnti in *UlData\_PuschReceiveReq) the selected DTX threshold shall be multiplied by a specific factor depending on number of antennas, rank and number of PRBs to obtain a target False Alarm Rate.*\*\*

*DtxThreshold = ThresholdTable(NumAnt, NumPrb) \* factor*

where the value of the *factor is:*

For 2RX, rank1 (0.2% FA):

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| *numOfPrbs* | *factor* |
| <= 4 | 1.2 |
| 5…12 | 1.12 |
| 13…16 | 1.1 |
| 17…20 | 1.12 |
| 21…32 | 1.14 |
| >= 33 | 1.12 |

For 2RX, rank2 (0.2% FA):

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| *numOfPrbs* | *factor* |
| <= 4 | 1.18 |
| 5…12 | 1.12 |
| 13…16 | 1.14 |
| 17…20 | 1.12 |
| 21…24 | 1.06 |
| 25…28 | 1.12 |
| 29…32 | 1.1 |
| >= 33 | 1.08 |

For 4RX, rank1 (0.1% FA):

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| *numOfPrbs* | *factor* |
| <= 4 | 1.28 |
| 5…8 | 1.18 |
| 9…12 | 1.2 |
| 13…16 | 1.14 |
| 17…20 | 1.18 |
| 21…24 | 1.16 |
| 25…32 | 1.2 |
| 33…36 | 1.18 |
| >= 37 | 1.16 |

For 4RX, rank2 (0.1% FA):

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| *numOfPrbs* | *factor* |
| <= 4 | 1.28 |
| 5…12 | 1.2 |
| 13…16 | 1.16 |
| 17…20 | 1.18 |
| 21…24 | 1.14 |
| 25…28 | 1.18 |
| 29…32 | 1.14 |
| >= 33 | 1.12 |

The requirement is common for all the Technologies. (Including 7-2e: [11406459: DTX using Uplink Channel Information - TDD, 7-2e split](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9dVyvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

[CB012115-IA] The requirement shall be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---

#### 5.4.8.6.5 DTX with SAg (Slot Aggregation)

**5.4.8.6.5.0-2**  (ID: `11403627`)

DTX with SAg (Slot Aggregation)
5G-L1-UL shall support the measurement of DTX for PUSCH with SAg as below :

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | DTX for data on PUSCH (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7185\_replaced([11109150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Algorithm based on: | 5GMAX algo. Spec. section\* | First Feature |
| Loner | supported | "antenna-wise" with NO averaging\*\* | Section 2.3.8 | CB008236-A |
| Loki | supported | "antenna-wise" with Method3\*\*\* | Section 2.3.8 | CB008236-A |
| vDU | - | - | - | - |
| Thor | - | - | - | - |

\*Detail of the DTX computation algorithm  can be found in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for 5G21A version.

\*\* No averaging: only the SINR of the last repetition will be used, DTX thresholds same as SAgOFF

\***Method3: Mean\_slots[10*log10(Mean\_rx(Power\_rx\_i)) – 10*log10(Mean\_rx(Noise\_rx\_i))], half DTX thredholds compared to SAgOFF

[CB011066-A] This requirement is valid for FR1-FDD-eCPRI. [End CB011066-A]

---

**5.4.8.6.5.1.0-1**  (ID: `11403645`)

Loner
5G-L1-UL shall support the measurement of DTX for PUSCH with SAg as below :

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | DTX for data on PUSCH (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7185\_replaced([11109150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Algorithm based on: | 5GMAX algo. Spec. section\* | First Feature |
| Loner | supported | "antenna-wise" with NO averaging\*\* | Section 2.3.8 | CB008236-A |

\*Detail of the DTX computation algorithm  can be finded in 5GMAX algorithm specification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for 5G21A version.

\*\* No averaging: only the SINR of the last repetition will be used, DTX thresholds same as SAgOFF

Loki
5G-L1-UL shall support the measurement of DTX for PUSCH with SAg as below :

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | DTX for data on PUSCH (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7185\_replaced([11109150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Algorithm based on: | 5GMAX algo. Spec. section\* | First Feature |

**5.4.8.6.5.3.0-1**  (ID: `11403667`)

Thor
5G-L1-UL shall support the measurement of DTX for PUSCH with SAg as below :

|  |  |  |  |  |

| L1 HW | DTX for data on PUSCH (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7185\_replaced([11109150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ko0cQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Algorithm based on: | 5GMAX algo. Spec. section\* | First Feature |

Odin
5G-L1-UL shall support the measurement of DTX for PUSCH with SAg as below :

#### 5.4.8.6.6 IfftThrFactor selection for Final DTX Detection

##### 5.4.8.6.6.1 Legacy 'adap' method

###### 5.4.8.6.6.1.1 For Loner

**5.4.8.6.6.1.1.0-5**  (ID: `11406534`)

For Loner
Content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 23R2 release. For later releases please refer to 5G\_L1\_11718\_replaced([11406669](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9ej6PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[PR606240]

If {Polar code case} the below adjustment shall be done

IfftThrFactor=10^(dmrsDtxIfftOffset1Db/10)

endif

where,

dmrsDtxIfftOffset1Db: 5G\_L1\_7735\_replaced([11408095](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9mfs_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

See section 2.3.10.1.3 of 5GMAX document for the usage of IfftThrFactor:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/5gmax/Shared%20Documents/GeneralDocuments/5G%20Algorithm%20Specifications/5GMax-5G%20New%20Radio%20Algorithm%20Specification.docx?d=w50d50ac3a43e4a00808f8d6f256d3a5d&csf=1&web=1&e=XHbngC>

[End PR606240]

---

###### 5.4.8.6.6.1.2 For Loki

**5.4.8.6.6.1.2.0-4**  (ID: `11406573`)

For Loki
Content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 22R4 release. For later releases please refer to 5G\_L1\_12690\_replaced([11406698](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9ej7PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[PR606240]

If {Reed-Muller CSI case} AND {MCS <= nMcs\_Csi\_RM AND numOfPrb <= nPrb\_Csi\_RM}:

IfftThrFactor = 10^(adjustFactorCsiDb\_RM/10)

else:

IfftThrFactor = 1

where,

adjustFactorCsiDb\_RM: 5G\_L1\_8384\_replaced([11408580](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9o7-_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64 QAM), 5G\_L1\_8390\_replaced([11408722](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9qKEfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (256 QAM)

nMcs\_Csi\_RM: 5G\_L1\_8385\_replaced([11408585](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9o7_PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64QAM), 5G\_L1\_8391\_replaced([11408729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9qKEvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (256QAM)

nPrb\_Csi\_RM: 5G\_L1\_8386\_replaced([11408592](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9o7_fvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64QAM), 5G\_L1\_8392\_replaced([11408736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9qKE_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (256QAM)

See section 2.3.10.1.3 of 5GMAX document for the usage of IfftThrFactor:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/5gmax/Shared%20Documents/GeneralDocuments/5G%20Algorithm%20Specifications/5GMax-5G%20New%20Radio%20Algorithm%20Specification.docx?d=w50d50ac3a43e4a00808f8d6f256d3a5d&csf=1&web=1&e=XHbngC>

Informative text: For Polar case with 12..19 bits: IfftThrFactor = 0.5

[End PR606240]

---

###### 5.4.8.6.6.1.3 For Thor

**5.4.8.6.6.1.3.0-4**  (ID: `11406606`)

For Thor
Content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 23R2 release. For later releases please refer to 5G\_L1\_12725\_replaced([11406720](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9ej7_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

for Thor:

If {Reed-Muller CSI case} AND {MCS <= nMcs\_Csi\_RM AND numOfPrb <= nPrb\_Csi\_RM}:

IfftThrFactor = 10^(adjustFactorCsiDb\_RM/10)

else:

IfftThrFactor = 1

where,

adjustFactorCsiDb\_RM: 5G\_L1\_8384\_replaced([11408580](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9o7-_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64 QAM), 5G\_L1\_8390\_replaced([11408722](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9qKEfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (256 QAM)

nMcs\_Csi\_RM: 5G\_L1\_8385\_replaced([11408585](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9o7_PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64QAM), 5G\_L1\_8391\_replaced([11408729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9qKEvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (256QAM)

nPrb\_Csi\_RM: 5G\_L1\_8386\_replaced([11408592](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9o7_fvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64QAM), 5G\_L1\_8392\_replaced([11408736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9qKE_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (256QAM)

See section 2.3.10.1.3 of 5GMAX document for the usage of IfftThrFactor:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/5gmax/Shared%20Documents/GeneralDocuments/5G%20Algorithm%20Specifications/5GMax-5G%20New%20Radio%20Algorithm%20Specification.docx?d=w50d50ac3a43e4a00808f8d6f256d3a5d&csf=1&web=1&e=XHbngC>

When the PUSCH transmission is scheduled with reserved MCS, the MCS value in the condition above shall be replaced with corresponding MCS according to 5G\_L1\_10275\_replaced([11406937](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9fyDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note that,For Polar case with 12..19 bits: IfftThrFactor = 0.5

---

##### 5.4.8.6.6.2 'adapV2.x' methods overview

###### 5.4.8.6.6.2.1 Loner, Loki

**5.4.8.6.6.2.1.0-3**  (ID: `11406633`)

Loner, Loki
5G-L1-UL shall use the AdapV2.1 only in case of SAg mode (PUSCH Slot Aggregation) or 64QAM LowSE, otherwise the AdapV2.0 shall be used.

For more detail :

AdapV2.0 :

Loner : 5G\_L1\_12686\_replaced([11406684](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9ej6vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Loki : 5G\_L1\_12702\_replaced([11406706](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9ej7fvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

AdapV2.1 :

Loner : 5G\_L1\_12661\_replaced([11406756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9fK8_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (SAg mode) and 5G\_L1\_13046\_replaced([11406818](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9fK_vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64QAM LowSE)

Loki : 5G\_L1\_12664\_replaced([11406769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9fK9fvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (SAg mode) and 5G\_L1\_11680\_replaced([11406832](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9fLAPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64QAM LowSE)

###### 5.4.8.6.6.2.2 Thor

**5.4.8.6.6.2.2.0-2**  (ID: `11406647`)

Thor
5G-L1-UL shall use the AdapV2.1 in case of 64QAM LowSE, otherwise the AdapV2.0 shall be used.

For more detail :

AdapV2.0 : 5G\_L1\_13433\_replaced([11406727](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9ej8PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

AdapV2.1 :  [CB008246-N] 5G\_L1\_13322\_replaced([11406847](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9fyAfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64QAM LowSE) [End CB008246-N]

###### 5.4.8.6.6.2.3 Odin

**5.4.8.6.6.2.3.0-1**  (ID: `12733643`)

Odin
When PUSCH with UCI transmission is configured with Low SE MCS Table 5G-L1-UL(BB) shall use method '**AdapV2.1' to determine final DTX detection threshold, as described in 5GMax 5G New Radio Algorithm Specification v.11.155, chapter 2.3.10.**

The parameters related to UCI DTX detection are implemented as R&D parameters defined in chapter [11408082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9mfsfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

---

##### 5.4.8.6.6.3 'adapV2.0' method

###### 5.4.8.6.6.3.0-1 'adapV2.0' method When PUSCH with UCI transmission is configured with Low SE MCS Table and the number of encoded UCI bits is ≤ 11, 5G-L1-UL shall use method 'AdapV2' to determine final DTX detection threshold, as described in 5GMax 5G New Radio Algorithm Specification v.11.057, chapter 2.3.10. The parameters related to UCI DTX detection are implemented as R&amp;D parameters defined in chapter 5G_L1_7635_replaced( 11408082 ). (ID: 11405572)

###### 5.4.8.6.6.3.1 For Loner

**5.4.8.6.6.3.1.0-3**  (ID: `11406684`)

For Loner
When (mcsTable == 64QAM && non-SAg mode) or (mcsTable == 256QAM) the IfftThrFactor selection for UCI DTX detection (Ack/Nack or CSI) shall be done according to following pseudocode (AdapV2.0):

if (number of UCI bits\* <= 11) // simplex or Reed-Muller coding

if (MCS <= nMcs) AND (numOfPrb <= nPrb)

IfftThrFactor=10^(dmrsDtxIfftOffset1Db/10)

else

IfftThrFactor=1

endif

else  // polar coding

IfftThrFactor=10^(dmrsDtxIfftOffset1Db/10)

endif

where:

nPrb: 5G\_L1\_11649\_replaced([11408274](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9nGz_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64QAM table), 5G\_L1\_11653\_replaced([11408390](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9nt4PvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (256QAM table)

nMcs: 5G\_L1\_11648\_replaced([11408265](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9nGzvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64QAM table), 5G\_L1\_11652\_replaced([11408383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9nt3_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (256QAM table)

dmrsDtxIfftOffset1Db: 5G\_L1\_7735\_replaced([11408095](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9mfs_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Notes\* :

UCI bits = 0 No UCI detection

UCI bits >19 The algorithm Adap is not used

Only the values of UCI bits from 1 to 19 are impacted by the AdapV2.0

When the PUSCH transmission is scheduled with reserved MCS, the MCS value in the condition above shall be replaced with corresponding MCS according to  [CB008246-K] 5G\_L1\_11820\_replaced([11406921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9fyC_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008246-K]

---

###### 5.4.8.6.6.3.2 For Loki

**5.4.8.6.6.3.2.0-2**  (ID: `11406706`)

For Loki
When (*mcsTable == 64QAM* *[CB008236-A] && non-SAg mode* *[End CB008236-A]) or (*mcsTable == 256QAM) the IfftThrFactor selection for UCI DTX detection (Ack/Nack or CSI) shall be done according to following pseudocode (AdapV2.0):**\***\*

**if (number of UCI bits\* <= 11) // simplex or Reed-Muller coding**

**if (MCS <= nMcs) AND (numOfPrb <= nPrb)**

IfftThrFactor = 10^(adjustFactorDb/10)

**else**

IfftThrFactor = 1

**endif**

**else**// polar coding\*\*\*\*

IfftThrFactor = 10^(adjustFactorDb/10)

**endif**

where:

nPrb: 5G\_L1\_11667\_replaced([11408644](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9pjB_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64QAM table), 5G\_L1\_11670\_replaced([11408760](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9qKF_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (256QAM table)

nMcs: 5G\_L1\_11666\_replaced([11408636](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9pjBvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64QAM table), 5G\_L1\_11669\_replaced([11408753](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9qKFfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (256QAM table)

adjustFactorDb: 5G\_L1\_11665\_replaced([11408629](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9pjBfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64QAM table), 5G\_L1\_11668\_replaced([11408745](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9qKFPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (256QAM table)

*Notes* :\*

*UCI bits = 0 No UCI detection*

*UCI bits >19 The algorithm Adap is not used*

*Only the values of UCI bits from 1 to 19 are impacted by the AdapV2.0*

When the PUSCH transmission is scheduled with reserved MCS, the MCS value in the condition above shall be replaced with corresponding MCS according to 5G\_L1\_10275\_replaced([11406937](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9fyDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**[CB009322-A] This requirement is valid for FR1-FDD-eCPRI.** [End CB009322-A]\*\*\*\*

###### 5.4.8.6.6.3.3 For Thor

**5.4.8.6.6.3.3.0-3**  (ID: `11406735`)

For Thor
When (mcsTable == 64QAM) or (mcsTable == 256QAM), the IfftThrFactor selection for UCI DTX detection (Ack/Nack or CSI) shall be done according to following pseudocode (AdapV2.0):

if (number of UCI bits\* <= 11) // simplex or Reed-Muller coding

if (MCS <= nMcs) AND (numOfPrb <= nPrb)

IfftThrFactor = 10^(adjustFactorDb/10)

else

IfftThrFactor = 1

endif

else  // polar coding

IfftThrFactor = 10^(adjustFactorDb/10)

endif

where:

nPrb: 5G\_L1\_12728\_replaced([11408896](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9qxKvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (64QAM table), 5G\_L1\_12729\_replaced([11408947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9rYM_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (256QAM table)

Notes\* :

###### 5.4.8.6.6.3.4 Odin

**5.4.8.6.6.3.4.0-1**  (ID: `12733645`)

Odin
When (*mcsTable == 64QAM) or (*mcsTable == 256QAM), the IfftThrFactor selection for UCI DTX detection (Ack/Nack or CSI) shall be done by 5G-L1-UL(BB) according to following pseudocode (AdapV2.0):\*\*

**if (number of UCI bits\* <= 11) // simplex or Reed-Muller coding**

**if (MCS <= nMcs) AND (numOfPrb <= nPrb)**

IfftThrFactor = 10^(adjustFactorDb/10)

**else**

IfftThrFactor = 1

**endif**

**else**// polar coding\*\*\*\*

IfftThrFactor = 10^(adjustFactorDb/10)

**endif**

where nPrb, nMcs, and adjustFactorDb shall be selected according to [12733676: DTX Detection Thresholds (Odin)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_6Kl-AHYYEfCdZt6bzaG3fw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

*Notes* :\*

*UCI bits = 0 No UCI detection*

*UCI bits >19 The algorithm Adap is not used*

*Only the values of UCI bits from 1 to 19 are impacted by the AdapV2.0*

When the PUSCH transmission is scheduled with reserved MCS, the MCS value in the condition above shall be replaced with corresponding MCS according to 5G\_L1\_12754\_replaced([11406955](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9gZEPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

##### 5.4.8.6.6.5 'adapV2.1' method for high-reliability DRB

**5.4.8.6.6.5.0-1**  (ID: `11405578`)

'adapV2.1' method for high-reliability DRB
When PUSCH with UCI transmission is configured with Low SE MCS Table 5G-L1-UL shall use method 'AdapV2.1' to determine final DTX detection threshold, as described in 5GMax 5G New Radio Algorithm Specification v.11.125, chapter 2.3.10.

The parameters related to UCI DTX detection are implemented as R&D parameters defined in chapter 5G\_L1\_7635\_replaced([11408082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9mfsfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB012255-A] This requiremnt is also applicable for Loki FR1-FDD-eCPRI 7.2a [End CB012255-A]

[CB009936-F] This requirement is also applicable to FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI on Thor-BB. [End CB009936-F]

if (MCS <= nMcs) AND (numOfPrb <= nPrb)

*UCI bits >19 The algorithm Adap is not used*

*Only the values of UCI bits from 1 to 19 are impacted by the AdapV2.1*

When the PUSCH transmission is scheduled with reserved MCS, the MCS value in the condition above shall be replaced with corresponding MCS according to 5G\_L1\_12754\_replaced([11406955](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9gZEPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Thor BB** |  |
| **FR1-FDD-OBSAI** | **[CB010970-B] Supported** [End CB010970-B]\*\*\*\* |
| **FR1-FDD-CPRI** | **[CB008246-N] Supported** [End CB008246-N]\*\*\*\* |
| **FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | **[CB010414-AB] Supported** [End CB010414-AB]\*\*\*\* |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI** | **[CB008246-N] Supported** [End CB008246-N]\*\*\*\* |
| **FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI** | **[CB009936-F] Supported** [End CB009936-F]\*\*\*\* |

---

##### 5.4.8.6.6.6 One and Two PRB case

**5.4.8.6.6.6.0-1**  (ID: `11406861`)

One and Two PRB case
In Loki and Thor Hw, *ifftThrFactor selection for UCI DTX detection shall be done according to the following pseudocode:*

In Uldata\_PuschReceiveReq/UlFhData\_PuschReceiveReq:

~~IF (numOfPrb is <=2 and modulationOrder <= 4)~~

IF (uciOnly=0) //PUSCH contains data and UCI

{

IF ((numOfPrb is <=2) and (modulationOrder <= 2))

Then ifftThrFactor = 0.1

Else ifftThrFactor = 1

}

Edit 25.4.2025, modulationOrder changed from 4 to 2

Edit 28.4.2025, ifftThr is used only when Pusch contain data and UCI. If UciOnly case, there is no PUSCH Data IFFT DTX detection result

---

#### 5.4.8.6.7 DTX and CRC Feedback Management (UlData_PuschReceiveRespHarqU)

**5.4.8.6.7.0-4**  (ID: `12733650`)

DTX and CRC Feedback Management (UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU)
5G-L1-UL(BB) shall support the DTX methods for UCI and UL-SCH as summarized in the following table.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **UCI or UL-SCH** | **Number of UCI info bits** | **Decoder** | **Reliability based detect** | **CRC based detect** | **Final DTX detection result** |
| UCI | A=1 | 'SoftBitMeth' | 'SoftBitMeth' | Not available |  |
| UCI | A<=2 (no CRC) | 'SoftBitMeth' | 'SoftBitMeth' | Not available | ReliabilityBasedResult AND IFFTBasedResult |
| UCI | 3<=A<=11 (no CRC) | RM decoder | ‘Rel0d5SumAbsSoftBit’ | Not available |  |
| UCI | 12<=A<=19 (6-bit CRC) | Polar decoder | PM (path metric) based DTX detection | 6 bit CRC | CRCResult AND IFFTBasedResult   (After PM based method is available, it will be changed to CRCResult AND PMBasedResult AND IFFTBasedResult) |
| UCI | 20<=A (11-bit CRC for each code block) | CRC based detection only | 11 bit CRC | CRCResult only (as 11-bit CRC is sufficient.) |  |
| UL-SCH | N/A | LDPC | Shorter IFFT method (partial DTX) | 24 bit CRC | if(crc==TRUE) dtx=FALSE else partial DTX |

When 20 or more bits of HARQ A/N is needed, only CRC based DTX detection is applied. For detail please refer to  5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification, section 2.3.10.3.2 (For number of information bits equal or more than 20).

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

The requirement is supported in Loki, and Thor. [CB012115-IA] The requirement shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

---
