CSI2 DTX detection enhancement by using modified CSI1 DTX detection result
12590520:
CSI2 DTX detection enhancement by using modified CSI1 DTX detection result
12590520:
CSI2 DTX detection enhancement by using modified CSI1 DTX detection result
To improve the miss detection performance of PUSCH aperiodic CSI Part2, 5G-L1-UL(BB) shall support the new method of CSI part2 DTX detection by using modified CSI Part1 DTX detection result.

From NR 5GMAX L1 Algorithm Specifications there are different ways to determine Csi2 DTX detection result:

* ‘OnlyCsi2’: Csi2 DTX detection is based on only Csi2 result. Missed detection and false alarm are evaluated based on only Csi2 L1 results.
* ‘AndCsi1’: Csi2 DTX detection is based on result of ‘AND’ operation between CSI1 and CSI2 DTX.
* ‘AndCsi1p85’ - new method proposed in this feature: CSI2 detection is based on the result of ‘AND’ operation between CSI2 DTX and biased CSI1 DTX result in order to achieve target false alarm below 0.005. Biased CSI1 DTX detection consists in applying a 15% lowered CSI1 DTX threshold compared to than regular one.

Related information about above methods is described in 5GMax algorithm specification chapters:

* 2.3.13.1.3.1 Csi2 Final DTX Detection
* 2.3.13.2.3.1 Csi2 Final DTX Detection

Biased CSI1 DTX detection consists in applying 15% lowered CSI1 DTX threshold compared to regular one. 5G-L1-UL(BB) shall provide an additional output from CSI1 DTX detection module which shall be used only for CSI2 DTX detection purpose.

Though CB010981 does not depend on CB010764, the new CSI2 DTX detection improvement is defined on top of CSI2 DTX threshold adjustment proposed in CB010764. If this new algorithm is activated, CSI2 DTX threshold adjustment based on modulation order is applied whatever the status of NIDD parameter NRCELL.actPuschACsiPart2ThrAdj.

Different methods for CSI2 DTX detection are used as follows :

* IF the new method is enabled by parameter 'csi2EnhancedDetection' in [UlData\_PuschReceiveReq(11513756)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) message
* 5G-L1-UL(BB) applies adjusted CSI2 DTX threshold for BPSK and QPSK modulations(refer to [11406289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9cHrvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) without checking parameter actPuschACSIPart2ThrAdj in [UlCell\_SetupReq(11513449)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRwhwfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) message
* IF CSI Part1 payload size is less than 12 bits
  + 5G-L1-UL(BB) applies 'AndCsi1p85' and reports CSI2 DTX status after 'AND' operation with biased CSI1 DTX result.
* ELSE
  + 5G-L1-UL(BB) applies 'AndCsi1' and reports CSI2 DTX status after 'AND' operation with regular CSI1 DTX result
* END IF
* ELSE
* IF parameter 'actPuschACSIPart2ThrAdj' in [UlCell\_SetupReq(11513449)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRwhwfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) message is set to true
  + 5G-L1-UL(BB) applies adjusted CSI2 DTX threshold for BPSK and QPSK modulations(refer to [11406289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9cHrvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))
* ELSE
  + 5G-L1-UL(BB) applies the regular CSI2 DTX threshold, regardless of the modulation
* END IF
* 5G-L1-UL(BB) applies 'OnlyCsi2' method. CSI2 DTX is reported to L2 as determined at previous step.
* ENDIF

The figure below illustrates the impact of introducing 'AndCSI1p85' method into L1 processing chain.

* If CSI1 payload size is less than 12 bits

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating the common PUSCH receiver steps with or without UCI on PUSCH. The flowchart is divided into several stages, starting with the Equalizer and Layer Demapping, followed by LLR Computation and Soft bit Sequence Descrambling.
From the Soft bit Sequence Descrambling, the process splits into two main paths: ACK and CSI1.
The ACK path involves ACK Decoding/Coding Based DTX detection, leading to Final (*) ACK DTX Detection, and finally ACK DTX Status to L2.
The CSI1 path involves CSI1 Decoding/Coding Based DTX detection, which then leads to Final (*) CSI1 DTX Detection, outputting CSI1 DTX status to L2 (dtxCsiPart1).
There is also a CSI2 size (**) output from the CSI1 Decoding/Coding Based DTX detection block. This CSI2 size, along with the Descrambled bit sequence, feeds into a DATA & CSI2 DE-Multiplexing block.
The flowchart also shows a parallel processing path for CSI1 detection. The CSI1 Decoding/Coding Based DTX detection block also outputs to a "Csi1 detection @85% Threshold" block, which is highlighted in red. This block receives input labeled "CB010981". The output of the threshold block goes to a "CSI1 p85 DTX" block.
The "CSI1 p85 DTX" block and the "Final (*) CSI1 DTX Detection" block both feed into an "L1SW 'AND' operation" block, also highlighted in red. The output of the "L1SW 'AND' operation" block goes to a "Final CSI2 DTX Detection" block.
The "DATA & CSI2 DE-Multiplexing" block feeds into a "CSI2 Decoding / Coding Based DTX detection" block. This block, along with the output from the "CSI1 p85 DTX" block, feeds into a block labeled "'AndCsi1p85' method Csi2 detection / DTX (*)", also highlighted in red. The output of this block also feeds into the "Final CSI2 DTX Detection" block.
Finally, the "Final CSI2 DTX Detection" block outputs CSI2 DTX Status to L2 (dtxCsiPart2).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/UCIonPUSCH\_ProcessingChain.jpg[/image\_path][/image-0]

Figure 1: UCI decoding chain - CSI Part1 payload size less than 12 bits

* If CSI Par1 payload size is 12 bits or more

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a digital signal processing system, likely for wireless communication. The flowchart depicts a series of processing blocks connected by arrows, indicating the flow of data.
Here's a breakdown of the components and their connections:
**Left Side (Input Processing):**
\* **Equalizer:** The process begins with an "Equalizer" block, suggesting the initial stage of signal conditioning.
\* **Layer Demapping:** The output of the equalizer feeds into a "Layer Demapping" block.
\* **LLR Computation:** The output of the layer demapping is processed by an "LLR Computation" block, likely calculating Log-Likelihood Ratios.
\* **Soft bit Sequence descrambling:** The LLRs are then fed into a "Soft bit Sequence descrambling" block. This entire section is enclosed in a dashed box, possibly indicating a common processing unit.
**Middle Section (Demultiplexing and Decoding):**
\* **Demultiplexing:** The output of the "Soft bit Sequence descrambling" is split into three streams, indicated by labels "ACK", "CSI1", and "CSI2". There's also a "DE-Multiplexing" block that receives the "Descrambled bit sequence" and outputs CSI2.
\* **ACK Path:**
\* "ACK soft bits" are fed into an "ACK Decoding / Coding Based DTX detection" block.
\* The output of this block goes to a "Final (*) ACK DTX Detection" block.
\* The output of the "Final (*) ACK DTX Detection" block is labeled "ACK DTX Status to L2".
\* **CSI1 Path:**
\* "CSI1 soft bits" are fed into a "CSI1 Decoding / Coding Based DTX detection" block.
\* The output of this block goes to a "Final (*) CSI1 DTX Detection" block.
\* The output of the "Final (*) CSI1 DTX Detection" block is labeled "CSI1 DTX status to L2 (dtxCsiPart1)".
\* **CSI2 Path:**
\* The "Descrambled bit sequence" is fed into a "DATA & CSI2 DE-Multiplexing" block.
\* The output of this block, labeled "CSI2 size (**)", is fed into a "CSI2 Decoding / Coding Based DTX detection" block.
\* The output of the "CSI2 Decoding / Coding Based DTX detection" block is fed into a block labeled "'Csi2Only' detection / DTX (\*)".**Right Side (Final Detection and Output):**\*** L1SW 'AND' operation: **A red arrow from the "Final (*) CSI1 DTX Detection" block and another red arrow from the "'Csi2Only' detection / DTX (*)" block converge into a red box labeled "L1SW 'AND' operation".
\*** Final CSI2 DTX Detection: **The output of the "L1SW 'AND' operation" block is fed into a "Final CSI2 DTX Detection" block.
\*** CSI2 DTX Status to L2: **The output of the "Final CSI2 DTX Detection" block is labeled "CSI2 DTX Status to L2 (dtxCsiPart2)".**Annotations:**\* "(\*)": Indicates a note or special condition.
\* "(**)": Indicates a note or special condition.
\* "CB010981": A reference number or identifier.
The flowchart visually represents a system that processes incoming data, performs decoding and detection for different channels (ACK, CSI1, CSI2), and incorporates a mechanism (L1SW 'AND' operation) that likely combines information from CSI1 and CSI2 for a final CSI2 detection. The "DTX detection" suggests the system is designed to handle Discontinuous Transmission.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/UCIonPUSCH\_ProcessingChain\_AndCsi1.jpg[/image\_path][/image-1]

Figure 2: UCI decoding chain - CSI Part1 payload size 12 bits or more

* \*: Final CSI2 DTX detection thresholds are adjusted based on 3GPP 38.214 MCS table, if threshold adjustment is enabled.
* \*\*: RI bits extracted from decoded CSI1 information are used to obtain CSI2 size.

Note:

As modified CSI1 DTX threshold is lower than CSI1 regular threshold, the following conditions can be met:

* CSI1 DTX metric is lower than regular CSI1 DTX threshold
* CSI1 DTX metric is higher than lowered CSI1 DTX threshold
* CSI2 DTX metric is higher than CSI2 DTX threshold.

In this situation, L1 reports CSI1 detection as DTX and CSI2 as non-DTX. L2PS shall be able to decode CSI2 information in this situation. To enable this:

* L1 shall report decoded CSI1 to L2PS, whatever is the CSI1 DTX status
* L2 shall read RI bits from CSI1 information bits whatever CSI1 DTX status and shall use them for parsing CSI2 information.
* If CSI1 detection is reported as DTX, L2-PS shall not use RI bits for feeding rank IIR filter.

Notice!

The csi2EnhancedDetection IE is added to tag L1-L2 IF tag 700 (26R1), however, the feature is postponed to 26R2.

In 26R1, 5G-L1-UL shall ignore csi2EnhancedDetection IE and hardcode it to 0 (deactivated).

The requirement above is supported by Loki with 2RX and 4RX,**and by Thor with 2RX, 4Rx, 8Rx and 16RX.**

HW-Technology Map:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Loner | Loki | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Not Supported | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Not Supported | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Not Supported | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Not Supported | Supported | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI | - | - | Supported |
| FR2-TDD-CPRI | Not Supported | Not Supported | **-** |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Not Supported | Not Supported | **-** |

### 5.4.8.7 UCI Transmission with Reserved MCS

#### 5.4.8.7.1 For Loner

**5.4.8.7.1.0-2**  (ID: `11406921`)

For Loner
For Loner:

When the received PUSCH with UCI transmission is configured with reserved MCS, then MCS value used in UCI DTX algorithm is replaced with corresponding non-reserved MCS according to tables below. The corresponding MCS values are selected by taking the highest MCS value available for modulation scheme that is mapped to given reserved MCS.

Correspondence of reserved MCS for Transform Precoding disabled (CP-OFDM)

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| MCS Table | MCS correspondence | First Feature |
| 64QAM | MCS 29 corresponds to 9 |  |
| MCS 30 corresponds to 16 |  |  |
| MCS 31 corresponds to 28 |  |  |
| 256QAM | MCS 28 corresponds to 4 |  |
| MCS 29 corresponds to 10 |  |  |
| MCS 30 corresponds to 19 |  |  |
| MCS 31 corresponds to 27 |  |  |
| 64QAM Low SE | MCS 29 corresponds to 14 | CB008246-K |
| MCS 30 corresponds to 20 |  |  |
| MCS 31 corresponds to 28 |  |  |

Correspondence of reserved MCS for Transform Precoding enabled (DFT-s-OFDM)

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| MCS Table | MCS correspondence | First Feature |
| 64QAM (DFT-s-OFDM) | MCS 28 corresponds to 1 |  |
| MCS 29 corresponds to 9 |  |  |
| MCS 30 corresponds to 16 |  |  |
| MCS 31 corresponds to 27 |  |  |

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **MCS Table** | **MCS correspondence** | **First Feature** |
| **64QAM (DFT-s-OFDM)** | MCS **28 corresponds to** 1\*\*\*\* | **CB009028-5G-A** |
| MCS **29 corresponds to** 9\*\*\*\* |  |  |
| MCS **30 corresponds to** 16\*\*\*\* |  |  |
| MCS **31 corresponds to** 27\*\*\*\* |  |  |

The requirement is common for all the Technologies.

---

### 5.4.8.8 Maximum Number of Rate Matched bits (E max) for UCI

**5.4.8.8.0-2**  (ID: `11406985`)

Maximum Number of Rate Matched bits (E max) for UCI
Content of this requirement is frozen and valid until 23R3 release. For later releases please refer to the objects below.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| NrOfUciBits | Max E |
| <=2 | see below\* |
| >2 | 4096 |

*in 1 or 2 UCI bit case, Max E is not defined by bits, but by modulation symbols which is 1024. Number of bits is Qm*1024 or a maximum of 1024 ratematched modulation symbols or maximum Q'=1024/nu ,where nu is the number of layers.

~~\*except with DFT-s-OFDM if PI/2 BPSK is enabled where it is limited to 1024 bits or 1024 modulation symbols~~

Above maximum values apply with and without UL-SCH cases

Thor

#### 5.4.8.8.4 Odin

**5.4.8.8.4.0-1**  (ID: `12733654`)

Odin
For **Odin, 5G-L1-UL(BB) shall support the total length of rate matching output of Emax as:**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| NrOfUciBits | Max E |
| <=2 | see below\* |
| >2 | 4096 |

*in 1 or 2 UCI bit case, Max E is not defined by bits, but by modulation symbols which is 1024. Number of bits is Qm*1024 or a maximum of 1024 ratematched modulation symbols or maximum Q'=1024/nu ,where nu is the number of layers.

Above maximum values apply with and without UL-SCH cases.

The requirement is common for all Technologies.

Note: Emax refers to the total length of the rate matching output. This shouldn't be confused with Maximum Number of CSI bits. Refer to 38.212, ch. 6.3.1.3 "Channel coding of UCI" and rate-matching.

When UCI without UL-SCH is requested, UlData\_PuschReceiveReq parameter uciOnly is set to True. (See [11513804: PuschUciConfig](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy9__vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).)

Note also that in 5GC001817-C feature, also periodic CSI case (CSI Part 1 only) is supported in uciOnly case.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating a communication flow related to "Uci only" in a 5G network context, identified by the title "5GC001817-C/CB007220-C: Uci only".
The diagram depicts interactions between three entities: L1, L2PS, and L2LO.
Here's a breakdown of the sequence:
1. **Initial State:** A yellow note labeled "uciOnly=True" is present, indicating a specific configuration or condition.
2. **Message 1:** L1 sends a message "UIData\_PuschReceiveReq ()" to L2PS. This message is depicted as an arrow originating from L1 and pointing towards L2PS.
3. **Action:** Following the message from L1, a yellow box appears with the text "UL reception proceeds". This suggests that the reception of uplink data is initiated or continues.
4. **Message 2:** L1 sends a message "PuschReceiveRespPs" to L2PS. This is shown as an arrow from L1 to L2PS.
5. **Message 3:** L1 sends a message "If AN: PuschReceiveRespHarqD" to L2PS. This is also an arrow from L1 to L2PS. The "If AN" suggests a conditional aspect to this message.
6. **Message 4:** L1 sends a message "NOT sent: PuschReceiveRespHarqU" to L2PS. This is an arrow from L1 to L2PS, with "NOT sent" indicating that this specific response is not transmitted under certain conditions.
7. **Message 5:** L1 sends a message "NOT sent: PuschReceiveRespLo" to L2LO. This is an arrow from L1 to L2LO, also preceded by "NOT sent".
8. **Final State:** The diagram concludes with the entities L1, L2PS, and L2LO, implying the end of this particular interaction sequence.
The dashed lines represent the lifelines of each entity, indicating their presence and availability throughout the depicted sequence. The arrows represent messages being sent between these entities.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1623.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**[RP003187-L] The requirement is also applicable for** Thor for FR1-FDD-CPRI. **[End RP003187-L]**\*\*\*\*

#### 5.4.8.9.3 Odin

**5.4.8.9.3.0-1**  (ID: `12733641`)

Odin
5G-L1-UL(BB) shall support UCI without UL-SCH, that is, when uciOnly = True.

When PUSCH does not contain UL-SCH, UL reception is similar to case with UL-SCH with these differencies:

-UL-SCH related parameters can be ignored, see object 5G\_L1\_5721\_replaced([11407081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9hAIfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

-in UCI bit sequence generation, there is difference for AN bit setting. See 38212 chapter 6.3.2.1.1.

-resource calculation is different. See 38.212 chapter 6.3.2.4 Rate matching, where logic is following:

--first, if AN is present, resources are calculated for AN

--then resources are assigned to CSI Part 1 which is always present. CSI Part 1 gets all the remaining resources if Part 2 is not present, and if Part 2 is present, Part 1 resources needs to be calculated.

--then, if CSI Part 2 is present, remaining resouces are given to CSI Part 2. Note that these parameters are not used from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq in uciOnly case:

--maxNumOfUciCsiPart2Symbols

(--csiPart2Coderate)

--csiPart2BetaOffsetUpto11Bits

--csiPart2BetaOffsetMoreThan11Bits

--numOfUciRes

-CSI Part 2 Omission case is a bit different, as if CSI Part 2 is omitted, its resources are left unused, not given to CSI Part 1 (and not to UL-SCH of course). see 38.214 chapter 5.2.3. It is also not fully clear if UE would still send CSI Part 2 with higher code rate, because it could make sense in this uciOnly context, however because L1 expects L2 to avoid this corner case L1 does not decode CSI Part 2, and sets numOfUciCsiPart2Bits to 0 in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs.

note, In case of uciOnly, FDM between UL-SCH and DMRS REs (numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData=1) does not make sense, and therefore L2 sets numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData=2, therefore L1 does not need to support numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData=1 with uciOnly.

CSI Part 1 and CSI Part 2 resource demapping and processing is similar to case with UL-SCH.

AN resource demapping is generally same as with UL-SCH, but there is difference when CSI Part 2 is not present, see 5G\_L1\_6221\_replaced([11405314](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9XPK_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Same number of UCI payload bits are supported as with UL-SCH.

Note that as PUSCH does not have UL-SCH/TB, some parameters in PuschReceiveReq are not used, and these messages are not sent to L2:

-PuschReceiveRespHarqU

-PuschReceiveRespLo

---

### 5.4.8.10 One and Two PRB support

**5.4.8.10.0-2**  (ID: `11407115`)

One and Two PRB support
5G-L1-UL(RU)  with 72e FH shall support 1 and 2 PRB reception.

When 1 or 2 PRBs is allocated for the UE, ~~and UE has at least 2 DMRS symbols,~~ PUSCH DTX detection will change ~~so that 2 first DMRS are taken into use~~.

Algorithm to calculate PUSCH DTX detection is described in 5GMax algorithm document; see [PDF tag eq-PuschIfftDtxDetMetricPrb12]

General view of DTX, see 5G\_L1\_14241\_replaced([11406892](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9fyB_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note that 2 Prbs is already supported~~, but its PUSCH DTX detection will change if UE has at least 2 DMRS symbols,~~see following objects:

5G\_L1\_13526\_replaced([11411586](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e92-Z_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)): Number of PRB for PUSCH on Thor RU

Note!

CB013204-A contained originally DTX detection algorithm which used 2 DMRS but that was changed with CB013204-A-UPD001 so that only 1 DMRS is used.

CB013204-B contains 1 DMRS case, but as CB013204-A-UPD001 is already using 1 DMRS for DTX detection, this sub feature does not contain change.

---

### 5.4.9.5 DTX detection threshold

**5.4.9.5.0-1**  (ID: `12733094`)

DTX detection threshold
5G-UL-L1 implements the DTX Detection Thresholds as look-up tables in:  
/[l1sw](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/l1sw/)/[app](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/l1sw/app/)/[common](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/l1sw/app/common/)/[nr](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/l1sw/app/common/nr/)/[ulTables](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/l1sw/app/common/nr/ulTables/)/[PuschDtxThresholds.cpp](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/l1sw/app/common/nr/ulTables/PuschDtxThresholds.cpp)

in accordance with 5GMax code, i.e., the latest version of:

/[5gmax](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/5gmax/)/[source](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/5gmax/source/)/[SymbolProcessing](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/5gmax/source/SymbolProcessing/)/[NR5G](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/5gmax/source/SymbolProcessing/NR5G/)/[ParamEst](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/5gmax/source/SymbolProcessing/NR5G/ParamEst/)/[matlab](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/5gmax/source/SymbolProcessing/NR5G/ParamEst/matlab/)/[NrDtxDetSnrBasedThrTablesPUSCH\_f.m](https://opengrok.loki.devops-fwk.dynamic.nsn-net.net/xref/5gmax/source/SymbolProcessing/NR5G/ParamEst/matlab/NrDtxDetSnrBasedThrTablesPUSCH_f.m)

**5.4.9.6.1.0-2**  (ID: `11409028`)

Loner
rdCrcMaxIterThreshold

Maximum number of LDPC decoder iterations from which LDPC syndrome checking is applied.

Default value: 10

### 5.4.9.7 Code-block-based decoding

#### 5.4.9.7.1 Thor BB, Odin BB

**5.4.9.7.1.0-2**  (ID: `12792213`)

Thor BB, Odin BB
*rdSymbolsStartingLdec*

Bit-mask indicating symbols on which CB-based decoder shall be triggered.

Value range: [0x0000...0x3FFF]

Default value: 0

Specified in:  5G\_L1\_15858\_replaced([11403237](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9M3H_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The RD parameter is supported in Thor. [CB012115-GA] It shall also be supported in Odin. [End CB012115-GA]

### 5.4.9.8 RDD Averaging

#### 5.4.9.8.1 rdIsRddAvg8RxEnabled

**5.4.9.8.1.0-2**  (ID: `12733627`)

rdIsRddAvg8RxEnabled
rdIsRddAvg8RxEnabled

Type: boolean

Values:

* true: Rdd averaging is enabled
* false: Rdd averaging is disabled

Online modifiable: yes

Default value: false

Specified in: [11403091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9MQBfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

Defined for Thor, and [CB012115-IA] Odin. [End CB012115-IA]

#### 5.4.9.10.2 Number of PRBs for SINR in SU

## 5.4.10 Capacity

### 5.4.10.1 Supported Number of Scheduled UEs

#### 5.4.10.1.1 Loki

**5.4.10.1.1.0-7**  (ID: `12553186`)

Loki
For Loki, the maximum number of UEs per slot is:

FR1 FDD (eCPRI & CPRI):

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 Pool size [CS]** | **Max UL grants (UEs) per slot per subcell** | **Max UL grants (UEs) per slot per L1 subpool** | **Max UL grants (UEs) per slot per Loki** | **Release** |
| 96 | 10 | **[Before CB014208-SR-A, CB014208-SR-B]** 32  **[CB014208-SR-A,**CB014208-SR-B]***\** \*38** **[End CB014208-SR-A, CB014208-SR-B]** | **[Before CB014208-SR-A, CB014208-SR-B]** **32** **[CB014208-SR-A, CB014208-SR-B]** **38** **[End CB014208-SR-A, CB014208-SR-B]** | 24R1-SR |

TDD FR1 (eCPRI & CPRI):

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 Pool size [CS]** | **Max UL grants (UEs) per slot per subcell** | **Max UL grants (UEs) per slot per L1 subpool** | **Max UL grants (UEs) per slot per Loki** | **Release** |
| 96 | **[Before CB013895-A] 16 (with pooling)[CB013895-A] 24 (with pooling), see Note 2 [End CB013895-A] 16 (without pooling, see note 2** | 30 40 (In case of tdLte or 1/4 frame configuration) In case of 6 consecutive UL slots, 24, see Note 1 | 30 40 (In case of tdLte or 1/4 frame configuration) In case of 6 consecutive UL slots, 24, see Note 1 | 25R3-SR |
| 48 | 16 | 15 | 15 | 25R1-SR |

TDD FR2 eCPRI:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 Pool size [CS]** | **Max UL grants (UEs) per slot per subcell** | **Max UL grants (UEs) per slot per L1 subpool** | **Max UL grants (UEs) per slot per Loki** | **Release** |
|  | 1 | **-** | 4 | legacy |

**Note 1: 6 consecutive UL slots are configured when MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.frameStructureType = tdLte and MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.tdLteUlDlConfig = tdLteConfig0. In this configuration number of scheduled UEs are limited due DU(L1) processing capability limitation.**

[CB013895-A]

Note 2: The scheduling of 24 UL UEs is achieved through the allocation of UL DCIs resources over 2 slots (with 16 UL DCIs per slot [11442257](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_hyWPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) using different K2 values enabled by CB013305 ([11199196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwLojPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).  
[End CB013895-A]

[CB013932-SR-A] Note 1: When 24 UL UEs require scheduling, the scheduler shall allocate UL DCI resources over 2 slots (with 16 UL DCIs per slot [12692412](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_P7I8QlfoEfCMqIX_zEVxAQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) using different K2 values enabled by CB013305 ([11199196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwLojPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note 2: The number of scheduled UEs per subcell per slot shall be 24 If NRBTS/NRCELL.actIncreaseMaxUlScheduleUE is True, otherwise the number of scheduled UEs per subcell per slot shall be 16 [End CB013932-SR-A]

| Cell Set Type | Bw | Max PRB | Max Layer | Layer\*PRB limit/Instance | Layer\*PRB limit/Pool | UE Max PDU | Subcell | Subpool | Pool | Instance(bits) |
| **TDD FR1** **SU/MU MIMO** | 100MHz | 273 | 2 | 3276 | 1638 | 647337,6 | 1362816 |  | 8176896 | 16353792 |

**Parameters used for Dimensioning:**

* Coding rate of 1st transmission = 0,95
* rdMaxCrRetransmissionUl = 2 (Maximum Allowed Code Rate in Retransmission, [11209005: rdMaxCrReTransmissionUl](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kww3XvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))
* PUSCH Data Symbol = 13
* RE per RB = 12
* Modulation order = 8 (256QAM)

### 5.4.10.3 UCI Capacity

#### 5.4.10.3.1 Number of PUSCH UCI per slot

##### 5.4.10.3.1.1 Loki

###### 5.4.10.3.1.1.0-4 Loki LOKI L1 HW: For TDD FR1: maximum number of UCI resources on PUSCH has been defined in 5G_UP_11577, 5G_UP_13673, [Before CB013895-A] 5G_UP_12818, [CB013895-A] 12560463 [End CB013895-A] [CB014208-SR-A] LOKI L1 HW: For FDD FR1: maximum number of UCI resources on PUSCH has been defined in 12649588 [End CB014208-SR-A] (ID: 12575964)

##### 5.4.10.3.1.2 Thor

##### 5.4.10.3.1.2-1 Thor

##### 5.4.10.3.1.2-2 Thor

###### 5.4.10.3.1.2.0-3 Thor THOR L1 HW: For TDD FR1: maximum number of UCI resources on PUSCH has been defined in [Before CB013932-SR-A]5G_UP_11577[CB013932-SR-A] 12659619 [End CB013932-SR-A]. THOR L1 HW: For FDD FR1: maximum number of UCI resources on PUSCH has been defined in 12649588 . (ID: 12721558)

#### 5.4.10.3.2 Number of scheduled UEs with UCI per slot

**5.4.10.3.2.0-1**  (ID: `11405557`)

Number of scheduled UEs with UCI per slot
On Loki-based HW, the maximum number of UE reporting UCI on PUSCH that can be multiplexed per subcell and per slot is the same as for PUSCH with data:

* 10 UE per subcell in FDD if 3 A2/A4 subcells per Loki are supported,
* 5 UE per subcell in FDD if 6 A2/A4 subcells per Loki are supported,
* 8 UE per C2/C4 subcell in FR1-TDD with CPRI or eCPRI 7.2 frounthaul. [CB007326-B] This line is applicable for ASOE [End CB007326-B]

The required resources for decoding UCI on PUSCH (RDEC, PDEC) are not shared with PUCCH F3 resources.

[CB007814-D] 5G-L1-UL shall also support requirements above in ABIO for FR1 FDD eCPRI. [End CB007814-D]

---

#### 5.4.10.3.3 Number of Schedules UEs with UCI per slot per pool for Odin

**5.4.10.3.3.0-1**  (ID: `12733634`)

Number of Schedules UEs with UCI per slot per pool for Odin
5G-L1-UL(BB) shall support the scheduling of multiple UEs with HARQ-A/N resources on PUSCH in the single slot within the same L1 pool.

The maximum number of scheduled UEs is 26.

The maximum number of CSI 1 and 2 is 26, that is, CSI part 1 + 26 CSI part 2.

The requirement is on par with Thor for CB012445. Refer to [11397338: Capacity for UCI (Maximum Number of UCI resources on PUSCH per L1 instance in RT)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf2Gk_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw) in 5G UP System.

verified) 11409801: Selection of the 5G-L1-UL modes (Loner, to be verified) Figure representing conformance test mode. [image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a data flow from a gNB (gNodeB) to a Test Equipment (TE). The flowchart consists of three main components: L1-SW, L1-FPGA, and Test Equipment (TE). The flow starts with a green arrow pointing from "UIData_PuschReceiveReq (gNB)" to "L1-SW". This indicates a request or data being sent to the L1-SW (Layer 1 Software). From L1-SW, a dotted red arrow labeled "Payload for TE" points downwards, indicating data being passed to the L1-FPGA (Layer 1 FPGA). Within the L1-FPGA block, there is a highlighted red dashed oval containing the text "Additional logic (PIO) needed in the FPGA to send ACK/NACK for conformance testing". This suggests that specific logic is implemented in the FPGA for handling acknowledgments and negative acknowledgments, likely for testing purposes. Below this highlighted section, another dotted red arrow originates and is labeled "Serial Transmission on (HDMI) PIN AM28 to send serialAgilent signals". This indicates that the data is being serially transmitted via a specific pin (AM28) on an HDMI interface, likely to communicate with test equipment. Finally, a red arrow points from L1-FPGA to the "Test Equipment (TE)" block, signifying the transmission of data to the test equipment. The overall diagram depicts a process where data received by the L1-SW is processed and then transmitted through the L1-FPGA to a Test Equipment for conformance testing, with specific logic and transmission methods outlined.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_1991.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] (ID: 11409801)

### 5.4.11.2 Normal mode (Loner and Loki, to be verified)

#### 5.4.11.2.1 Loner

The flowchart begins with a box labeled "Normal Mode". From this box, a line connects to another box labeled "UICell_SetupReq". Below this, a box reads "Cell setup procedure". This is followed by a box labeled "UIData_AddressReq". The next box in the sequence is "Uplink Addressing Procedure". From the "Uplink Addressing Procedure" box, two branches emerge. The left branch leads to a box labeled "UIData_PuschReceiveReq", which then connects to a box labeled "PUSCH reception procedure". The right branch leads to a box labeled "Measurement reports request", which then connects to a box labeled "Measurement report procedure". The flowchart uses blue lines to indicate the flow of the process. The boxes have a distinctive shape with a notched right edge, except for the "Normal Mode" box which has a rounded right edge and a notched left edge, and the "Cell setup procedure" and "PUSCH reception procedure" and "Measurement report procedure" boxes which have notched left and right edges.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_1994.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] (ID: 11409828)

This requirement shows PUSCH normal operation mode for a given sub-cell.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating communication between different layers in a system, likely a cellular network. The layers involved are C-plane, L2-PS, L2-Lo, and L1.
The diagram depicts the following sequence of events:
1. **Initialization:** L1 initiates a process.
2. **UlCell\_SetupReq:** C-plane sends a setup request to L2-PS with a subcell ID of 'x'.
3. **UlCell\_SetupResp:** L2-PS responds to C-plane with a setup response, also with a subcell ID of 'x'.
4. **UlData\_AddressReq:** C-plane sends an address request to L2-PS with a subcell ID of 'x'.
5. **Define puschReceiveReqAddress:** L2-PS defines the address for a PUSCH receive request.
6. **UIData\_AddressResp:** L2-PS responds to C-plane with an address response, with a subcell ID of 'x'.
7. **UIData\_PuschReceiveReq:** C-plane sends a PUSCH receive request to L2-PS with a subcell ID of 'x'.
8. **PUSCH reception for slot #j:** L2-Lo indicates PUSCH reception for slot #j.
9. **UIData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqu:** L2-Lo sends a PUSCH receive response (HARQ-U) to L2-PS with a subcell ID of 'x'.
10. **UIData\_PuschReceiveRespLo:** L2-Lo sends a PUSCH receive response (Lo) to L2-PS with a subcell ID of 'x'.
11. **UIData\_PuschReceiveRespPs:** L2-Lo sends a PUSCH receive response (Ps) to L2-PS with a subcell ID of 'x'.
The diagram uses arrows to indicate the direction of communication between the layers. The subcell ID is consistently 'x' for most of the messages.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1998.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

### 5.4.11.3 Standalone test mode (Loner)

#### 5.4.11.3.1 Overview

**5.4.11.3.1.0-3**  (ID: `11409904`)

Overview
It shall be possible to test the PUSCH receiver of 5G-L1-UL in the absence of the higher layers. In this document, the corresponding modes of operation are therefore referred to as “L1 standalone test modes”.

The following L1 standalone test mode options shall be available:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| L1 release | gNB 5G-L1-UL |
| 5G21B | Options A, B, C |

Where options A, B and C are summarized and described in requirement 5G\_L1\_3803\_replaced([11409874](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9wQs_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), standalone test mode.

---

#### 5.4.11.3.2 Option A

**5.4.11.3.2.0-1**  (ID: `11409917`)

Option A
L1 is tested with no higher layer and no CPRI or eCPRI interface.

At gNB L1: the uplink IQ samples (in time domain for CPRI and frequency domain for eCPRI) are read from the L1-FPGA Rx snapshot buffer, previously initialized with pre determined samples.

IQ samples are read from the Rx antenna snapshot buffer of the L1-FPGA. The data are first generated with MATLAB, then converted to binary format by Python scripts from I&V team.

A high-level description of the test mode implemented in L1-SW is provided below.

There should be a snapshot buffer in the L1-FPGA to store received IQ symbols received over CPRI or preconfigured by L1-SW.

The L1-SW writes IQ data to the L1-FPGA snapshot reader buffer located in the DDR5 associated with the L1-FPGA and starts the insert mode in L1-FPGA.

A continuous stream of IQ samples must be loaded into the snapshot buffer, for 20 (FR1) or 80 (FR2) or 10 (below 3GHz) consecutive NR-slots to be played in a continuous loop mode. The first IQ sample of this stream must correspond to the first IQ sample of a 10ms radio frame.

One sub-cell can be tested at a time.

When the test mode is enabled for a sub-cell, the L1-FPGA takes the IQ samples from the snapshot buffer, instead of the CPRI Rx port. Except this, the L1-FPGA works like in the normal mode.

All needed L2-L1 messages (UlData\_PuschReceiveReq at gNB) are generated in real time by L1-SW.

The PUSCH processing parameters such as the MCS index, number of PRB, slot type, etc. can be configured.

The response messages from L1 to L2 are not (necessarily) sent out to the 10GE interface, but instead are routed internally for L1-SW to print an entry to syslog on reception of those messages.

L1-FPGA TB CRC error counter can be checked to detect CRC errors.

---

#### 5.4.11.3.3 Option B

**5.4.11.3.3.0-1**  (ID: `11409931`)

Option B
L1 is tested with no higher layer where gNB and AirPhone are connected through CPRI or CPRI/eCPRI in conducted mode at gNB and CPRI at AirPhone as shown in the requirement 5G\_L1\_4617\_replaced([11409898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9wQtfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

At gNB L1: the uplink IQ samples (in time domain for CPRI and frequency domain for eCPRI) are received from the CPRI or eCPRI interface

In uplink, AirPhone generates uplink IQ sample (in time domain for CPRI) towards the gNB and performs Noise injection and delay adjustment (see the requirement 5G\_L1\_3678\_replaced([11396931](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8vkEvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for further details). Note: Noise injection is only supported only when gNB and AirPhone are connected directly through CPRI. The noise injection in gNB and Airphone shall be disabled in the case of RF conducted mode.

From PUSCH (gNB) reception perspective, this is like option A, except that the IQ samples are read from the CPRI interface (as in the normal mode) and therefore do not need to be initialized in the snapshot buffer.

The gNB and AirPhone sides must be configured in a consistent way. Note that for this to work, the transmitter (outside the scope of this document) must perform noise injection

---

#### 5.4.11.3.4 Option C

**5.4.11.3.4.0-1**  (ID: `11409946`)

Option C
L1 is tested with no higher layer where gNB L1 is connected via CPRI or eCPRI to the RF to a third-party test UE.

At gNB L1: same functionalities as in option B (except noise injection which is not needed when the air interface is used).

At gNB: from PUSCH reception perspective (gNB) it is the same as option B.

---

The conformance test, from 22R2-5G onward will be done according to the following figure:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a test setup for a gNB (gNodeB, a base station in 5G networks). The diagram shows the interaction between different components.
At the top left, a green rounded rectangle contains the text "WebUi/ WebEM/ Test Tool". This likely represents the user interface or software used to control and monitor the test.
At the top right, another green rounded rectangle contains the text "Controller". This component is responsible for managing the overall test process.
In the center, a white rectangle with rounded corners contains the text "Frame Pulse". This indicates a timing signal used to synchronize operations.
Below the "Frame Pulse", there are two light blue rounded rectangles. The left one is labeled "gNB in test Dedicated mode", representing the device under test. The right one is labeled "Test equipment", which is used to perform measurements and generate test signals.
An oval shape connects the "gNB in test Dedicated mode" and "Test equipment" with the text "Conducted or radiated mode" inside. This signifies that the connection between the gNB and the test equipment can be either through a direct cable (conducted) or over the air (radiated).
Below the "gNB in test Dedicated mode" and "Test equipment", there are two white rectangles with text. The upper one reads "Reference Clock 10 MHz", indicating a synchronized clock signal provided to the setup. The lower one reads "HARQ Info/TA Info", suggesting that information related to Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request (HARQ) and Timing Advance (TA) is exchanged or monitored.
The overall diagram depicts a typical setup for testing the functionality and performance of a gNB in a controlled environment, with a controller, test tools, and specialized test equipment.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2014.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

* In PUSCH performance testing, HARQ info and TA info ( Timing  Advance) are sent to test equipment in a serial interface, supported serial symbol rates are 115.2kbps, 460.8kbps, 1.92Mbps.
* Possible used frame pulse: 800Hz, 200Hz, 100Hz, 50Hz, 25Hz, 12.5Hz, 4.167Hz.

[PR623424]

In case of stationary and moving UE scenario, only HARQ+TA feedback for moving UE is needed. HARQ feedback for stationary UE shall not be provided.

[End PR623424]

Notice: This requirement is not HW, FH mode, nor Duplex Mode dependent. The following table does not impose any technical restriction, nor it provides any information in that regard. It only contains the history. (table to be completed during the Spec refactoring in compliance with Release Based Versioning)

HW-Technology Map:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI |  |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI |  |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | [CB010575-D] Supported [End CB010575-D] |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI |  |
| FR2-TDD-CPRI | Not supported |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | [CB010576-D] Supported [End CB010576-D] |

---

#### 5.4.11.4.2 taMacCeValueTM: Timing Advance Value in Conformance Test

**5.4.11.4.2.0-2**  (ID: `11400798`)

taMacCeValueTM: Timing Advance Value in Conformance Test
L1L2 interface shall support a new IE in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq called taMacCeValueTM.

This IE shall contain the timing advanced command value for conformance test. In conformance test mode, the following value ranges are used:

When only Harq is reported in conformance test, the value is fixed to 255.

When Harq + TA (Time advance) is used the value range is assigned to : [0...63].

[CB010576-D] This requirement also applies to Thor FR2-TDD-eCPRI. [End CB010576-D]

HW-Technology Map:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | Thor |
| FR1-FDD-CPRI | Supported |
| FR1 FDD OBSAI | [CB010969-SR-CB] Supported [End CB010969-SR-CB] |
| FR1-FDD-7-2a-eCPRI |  |
| FR1-TDD-CPRI | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | Supported |
| FR1-TDD-7-2e-eCPRI |  |
| FR2-TDD-CPRI | Not supported |
| FR2-TDD-7-2a-eCPRI | [CB010576-D] Supported [End CB010576-D] |

---

HARQ reporting in UL conformance tests
If digitalOutputEnabled from UlCell\_SetupReq is set to True, then following timings apply to sending HARQ feedback to Test Equipment:

For FR1 FDD:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating timing windows and latencies related to HARQ feedback in a communication system. The diagram uses vertical dashed red lines to denote time progression.
Key elements and text extracted:
\* **R&S HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2 H {option 1}**: This indicates a timing window for R&S HARQ feedback, associated with slot n+k2, with a parameter H and specifically for option 1.
\* **KS HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2 H KS k2 = k2 + 1**: This describes a timing window for KS HARQ feedback, also for slot n+k2, with parameter H, and a relationship defined as KS k2 = k2 + 1.
\* **R&S HARQ feedback transmission latency L**: This denotes the latency for R&S HARQ feedback transmission, labeled as L.
\* **KS HARQ feedback transmission latency L**: This denotes the latency for KS HARQ feedback transmission, also labeled as L.
\* **AUD option 1 (slots)**: This text indicates "AUD option 1" and specifies it is measured in "slots".
The diagram visually represents these timing windows and latencies, showing how they relate to each other over time. There are also shaded rectangular areas at the right end of the diagram, likely representing the actual transmission slots or periods. One shaded area is a series of small, closely spaced vertical bars, and below it is a single, thinner vertical bar.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2021.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

For FR1 TDD:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different options for AUD (Automatic Detection) and R&S HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) feedback timing and transmission latency. The diagram is divided by vertical dashed red lines, representing time slots.
On the left side, under "AUD option 2 (slots)", there is a label "0.1 slot". Below this, in green text, are two labels: "R&S HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2 H {option 2}" and "R&S HARQ feedback transmission latency L". To the right of these, there is another "0.1 slot".
Below the green text, in blue text, are two labels: "KS HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2 H {option 2}" and "KS k2 = k2 + 2". To the right of these, in blue text, are "KS HARQ feedback transmission latency L".
In the middle section, under "AUD option 1 (slots)", there are similar labels in green and blue text, but with variations in the options. The green text reads: "R&S HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2 H {option 1}" and "R&S HARQ feedback transmission latency L". There are "0.1 slot" labels associated with these. The blue text reads: "KS HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2 H {option 1}" and "KS k2 = k2 + 1". There is also "KS HARQ feedback transmission latency L".
The rightmost section of the diagram shows a visual representation of data transmission. There is a block of horizontal purple lines, suggesting a data burst, followed by a single vertical purple bar. This section is also bounded by dashed red lines.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2021.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

For FR2:

[image-2][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating different feedback timing windows and latencies in a communication system, likely related to HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat reQuest). The diagram uses vertical dashed red lines to denote time slots or points in time. Different colored text (green, blue, and black) indicates different types of feedback mechanisms or options.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Top Section (Green Text):**
\* **R&S HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2:**
\* **H** (likely representing a header or a specific parameter)
\* **(option 2, AUD = -2)**
\* **R&S HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2:**
\* **H**
\* **(option 1, AUD=0)**
\* **R&S HARQ feedback transmission latency:**
\* **L**
\* **0.1 slot**
\* **AUD option 2 (slots)**
**Middle Section (Blue Text):**
\* **KS HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2:**
\* **H**
\* **(option 1)**
\* **KS k2 = k2 + 2**
\* **KS HARQ feedback transmission latency:**
\* **L**
\* **Multiple KS HARQ feedback timing windows for the slot n+k2:**
\* **H**
\* **(option 1)**
\* **KS k2 = k2 + x**
\* **KS HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2:**
\* **H**
\* **(option 1)**
\* **KS k2 = 1**
\* **KS HARQ feedback transmission latency:**
\* **L**
**Bottom Section (Blue Text):**
\* **KS HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2:**
\* **H**
\* **(option 2)**
\* **KS k2 = k2 + 3**
\* **KS HARQ feedback transmission latency:**
\* **L**
\* **KS HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2:**
\* **H**
\* **(option 2)**
\* **KS k2 = k2 + 2**
\* **KS HARQ feedback transmission latency:**
\* **L**
\* **KS HARQ feedback timing window for the slot n+k2:**
\* **H**
\* **(option 2)**
\* **KS k2 = k2 + 1**
\* **KS HARQ feedback transmission latency:**
\* **L**
**Visual Elements:**
\* **Vertical dashed red lines:** These divide the diagram into distinct time segments.
\* **Rectangular blocks with vertical lines:** In the upper right section, there are two such blocks. The top one is filled with horizontal white and grey stripes, suggesting a duration or a window. The bottom one is a solid white rectangle, also likely representing a time duration or event.
The diagram appears to be comparing different configurations (options) and their associated timing parameters (windows and latencies) for HARQ feedback, possibly in different scenarios or for different types of HARQ (R&S vs. KS). The equations like "KS k2 = k2 + 2" suggest calculations or offsets related to time slots.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2021.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

Where:

"0.1 slot" is the time guard so that HARQ feedback can be properly assigned to a given slot.

T\_BB1ToAnt is assumed to be negligibly small (cable connection between gNB and RF of a few meters).

L (HARQ feedback transmission latency) for 16bits data and 14 bits overhead (assuming Idle period equal to 0);

H (HARQ feedback timing window length);

AUD is the Additional User Delay which is the configurable parameter and applicable only in Rohde&Schwarz Test Equipment.

KS k2 is the k2 parameter configurable at Keysight Test Equipment.

Values of L, H and AUD are specified in the table below.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | FDD |  | TDD FR1 |  | TDD FR2 |  |
| L | 260.4 us |  |  |  | 15.6 us |  |
| [(16 + 14) bits / 115.2 kbps] |  |  |  | [(16 + 14) bits / 1.92 Mbps] |  |  |
| H | 539.6 us |  | 139.6 us |  | Option 1 | Option 2 |
| 471.9 us | 333.4 us |  |  |  |  |  |
| [1000 - L - 2*0.1*slot] |  | [500 - L - 2*0.1*slot] |  | [500 - L - 0.1\*slot] | [374 - L - 2*0.1*slot] |  |
| AUD | Option 1 | Option 2 | Option 1 | Option 2 | Option 1 | Option 2 |
| -2 slots | N/A | -2 slots | -(2+1) = -3 slots | 0 | -2 slots |  |

[5GC001716-A] If digitalOutputType from UlCell\_SetupReq is set to serialKeysight, then usage of Keysight TE is assumed.

Feedback to the Keysight's TE can be done in within any time in the slot and the PUSCH transmission will occur after "KS k2" slots, counting from the beginning of the slot then HARQ feedback has been sent. This means that HARQ feedback acts as DCI for Keysight TE, see diagram below.

[image-3][image\_desc]The image displays a timeline or sequence of events, indicated by vertical dashed red lines. The timeline progresses from left to right.
At the beginning of the timeline, there are three labels associated with specific points in time:
- "KS k2 = k2 + 3"
- "KS k2 = k2 + 2"
- "KS k2 = k2 + 1"
Between the second and third labels, there is a purple box labeled "HARQ feedback".
Further to the right on the timeline, there are two distinct visual elements:
- A thin, light gray vertical bar.
- A wider, light purple rectangular block composed of multiple thin vertical segments.
The overall image appears to be a diagram illustrating a process or communication protocol, likely related to wireless communication systems given the "HARQ feedback" label, which stands for Hybrid Automatic Repeat reQuest. The labels "KS k2 = k2 + n" suggest some form of indexing or counter incrementing over time.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2021.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

Table below specifies the L, H and KS k2 values in case of Keysight TE usage.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | FDD |  | TDD FR1 |  | TDD FR2 |  |
| L | 86.8 us |  |  |  | 21.7 us |  |
| [(8 + 2) bits / 115.2 kbps] |  |  |  | [(8 + 2) bits / 460.8 kbps] |  |  |
| H | 713.2 us |  | 313.2 us |  | Option 1 | Option 2 |
| 465.8 us | 327.3 us |  |  |  |  |  |
| [1000 - L - 2*0.1*slot] |  | [500 - L - 2*0.1*slot] |  | [500 - L - 0.1\*slot] | [374 - L - 2*0.1*slot] |  |
| KS k2 | Option 1 | Option 2 | Option 1 | Option 2 | See note below. |  |
| 3 | N/A | 3 | 4 |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

Note: For FR2 TDD, k2 value set at the Keysight Test Equipment (KS k2) depends on the L1 implementation as HARQ feedback in FR2 tests can be sent during multiple slots (because of the DL/UL ratio = 4:1), and this affects used "KS k2" value.

[5GC001868-F] 5G-L1-UL shall also support the above timings for PUSCH HARQ feedback in UL performance test for FR1 TDD in Loki L1 HW [End 5GC001868-F]

[5GC001402-N] 5G-L1-UL shall also support the above timings for PUSCH HARQ feedback in UL performance test for FR2 TDD in Loki L1 HW [End 5GC001402-N]

[CB007814-F] This requirement also applies to ABIO FR1-FDD-eCPRI. [End CB007814-F]

[CB007487-F] 5G-L1-UL shall also support the above timings for PUSCH HARQ feedback in UL performance test for L1 Thor TDD-FR1-eCPRI and FDD-FR1-CPRI [End CB007487-F]

Notice: This requirement is not dependent on SoC nor FH mode. The following table does not impose any further technical restrictions, nor it provides any information in that regard. It only contains the history. (table to be completed during the Spec refactoring in compliance with Release Based Versioning)

#### 5.4.11.4.4 Fixed Reference Channel (FRC) to be tested

**5.4.11.4.4.0-6**  (ID: `11410166`)

Fixed Reference Channel (FRC) to be tested
L1 shall support new FRCs, namely, G-FR2-A4-3 and G-FR2-A4-13 for the test scenarios designed by 3GPP for PUSCH with 30% of maximum throughput in TDD-FR2. The figure below shows the details of these new FRCs.  Refer to 3GPP standard 38.141-2 table 8.2.1.5.2-7 and tables A.4-5 and A.4-7 for more details.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Type | Technology | FRC | SCS | PRB | Modulation | Code Rate | Number of code blocks- C | Data symbols | UL-DMRS- add-pos | DMRS-symbol | BW | PTRS | layer | Transform precoding |
| Performance | TDD FR2 | G-FR2-A4-3 | 120 | 32 | 16QAM | 658/1024 | 2 | 9 | 0 | 1 | 50 | Y/N | 1 | disable |
| Performance | TDD FR2 | G-FR2-A4-13 | 120 | 32 | 16QAM | 658/1024 | 1 | 8 | 1 | 2 | 50 | Y/N | 1 | disable |

---

#### 5.4.11.4.5 Frozen Requirements: Conformance Testing(Complete refactoring of the content is required.)

**5.4.11.4.5.0-29**  (ID: `11410385`)

Frozen Requirements: Conformance Testing(Complete refactoring of the content is required.)
L1 shall support test cases described as uplink Fixed Reference Channels (FRCs) for PUSCH performances with 8RX TDD cell configuration in compliance with the 3GPP specification 38.141-1:

1. G-FR1-A3-12, G-FR1-A3-26, G-FR1-A4-12, G-FR1-A4-26, G-FR1-A5-12 with supported cell bandwidth equal to 20MHz for TDD-eCPRI in Thor for ABIP and ABIQ HW.

2. G-FR1-A3-13, G-FR1-A3-27, G-FR1-A3-34, G-FR1-A4-13, G-FR1-A4-30, G-FR1-A4-32, G-FR1-A4-27, G-FR1-A5-13, G-FR1-A9-4 with supported cell bandwidth equal to 40MHz for TDD-eCPRI in Thor for ABIP and ABIQ HW.

3. G-FR1-A3-14, G-FR1-A3-28, G-FR1-A4-14, G-FR1-A4-28, G-FR1-A5-14, G-FR1-A9-5 with supported cell bandwidth equal to 100MHz for TDD-eCPRI in Thor for ABIP and ABIQ HW.

---

## 5.4.12 Detailed design of Loner (Frozen Requirements)

### 5.4.12.0-1 Detailed design of Loner (Frozen Requirements) Loner will not be further developed. This legacy chapter is kept, as is, for future references. (ID: 11410530)

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating communication between different components: 3GCF RT, 3G-L2-FS, 3G-L2-UD, L1-SW, and L1-FPGA. The diagram depicts a series of steps, numbered from 1 to 12, showing messages exchanged between these components. Here's a breakdown of the text elements and their likely meaning in the context of a sequence diagram: Component Labels: * 3GCF RT * 3G-L2-FS * 3G-L2-UD * L1-SW * L1-FPGA Message Labels (Steps): * [step 1] UICol SetupRec * [step 2] Initialization writing of static parameters * [step 3] UICell SetupResp * [step 4] UIData Address Rec * [step 5] UIData AddressResp * [step 6] UIData PuschReceiveRec * [step 7] NR-slot parameters * [step 8] UIData_PuschReceiveRespHarqU UIData PuschReceiveRespHardD * [step 9] UIData PuschReceiveRespLC * [step 10] The measurements results and DTX decision are transferred from L1-FPGA to HIPS memory accessible by L1-SW * [step 11] Trigger * [step 12] UIData PuschReceiveRespP Other Text: * IQ samples (indicated by an arrow pointing towards L1-FPGA) The diagram shows a flow of control and data. For example, step 1 shows a "SetupRec" message from 3GCF RT to 3G-L2-FS. Step 2 shows an "Initialization" action by L1-SW. Step 6 shows "UIData PuschReceiveRec" from 3G-L2-UD to L1-SW. Step 10 describes a transfer of measurement results and DTX decisions from L1-FPGA to L1-SW. The arrow labeled "IQ samples" suggests that IQ samples are being processed or sent to L1-FPGA.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_2086.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] (ID: 11410600)

### 5.4.12.2 L1-related Interfaces

#### 5.4.12.2.0-1 L1-related Interfaces For the PUSCH reception, several interfaces are available according to the type of information and the functionality handled by the interface. They are used for configuration, for data exchange and control. (ID: 11410613)

#### 5.4.12.2.1 Configuration files

**5.4.12.2.1.0-1**  (ID: `11410628`)

Configuration files
The configuration files are read at the start-up phase of the L1-SW. They allow to configure the L1-FPGA with parameters not obtained from the higher layers and/or to activate a dedicated mode.

The configuration files are managed during the L1-SW start-up according to the operation mode.

The start-up configuration files are used to:

• Configure the overall PUSCH (e.g. output buffer allocation).

• Initialize L1-FPGA registers for the hardware configuration, the Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) processing per sub-cell and for dynamic configuration per channel and sub-cell.

---

#### 5.4.12.2.2 L2-L1 Ethernet interface

**5.4.12.2.2.0-2**  (ID: `11410650`)

L2-L1 Ethernet interface
This Figure represents Messages for the PUSCH (gNB) receive procedure.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Message | Source | Destination | Per subcell or per UE |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveReq | 5G-L2-PS | L1-SW | Subcell |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLo (PUSCH transmission is properly decoded) | L1-FPGA | 5G-L2-LO | UE |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU | L1-FPGA | 5G-L2-PS | UE |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD | L1-FPGA | 5G-L2-PS | UE |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs | L1-SW | 5G-L2-PS | Subcell |

---

#### 5.4.12.2.3 CPRI and eCPRI

##### 5.4.12.2.3.0-1 CPRI and eCPRI Please refer to the requirement 5G_L1_3676_replaced( 11396917 ) for CPRI and the requirement 5G_L1_3706_replaced( 11396924 ) for eCPRI. (ID: 11410664)

### 5.4.12.3 PUSCH context creation in L1-SW

**5.4.12.3.0-1**  (ID: `11410677`)

PUSCH context creation in L1-SW
The UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message can support only one sub-cell per message.

If timing validation (see the requirement 5G\_L1\_4082\_replaced([11397713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8ynavvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and parameter validation (see the requirement 5G\_L1\_4081\_replaced([11397699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8ynaPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of the “UlData\_PuschReceiveReq” message are successful, the L1-SW creates one set of parameters called “context” hereafter, for this NR-slot and for each sub-cell present in this message. One context is created per sub-cell.

It is assumed here that the contexts may be stored in a ring buffer (for each sub-cell), indexed by the NR-slot number, but the actual implementation is left up to L1-SW design decision. At least some of the context parameters shall be stored for the duration determined by the maximum delay between the receive request reception and the PUSCH reception because they are needed for the response message creation. The data structure of each index including the debug information and data for the PUSCH configuration is defined below.

It must be possible to dump the whole ring buffer through the debug interface (see [the requirement 5G\_L1\_3675\_replaced([11396910](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8u9DvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))] for more details).

The structure of data per NR-slot context contains the parameters received in the “UlData\_PuschReceiveReq message and other parameters created by the L1-SW. Some of these parameters are configured in the L1-FPGA later. The format of parameters in the L1-SW PUSCH context is not defined in this document, but the following principles are applicable:

• The formats of parameters in the messages received from, or sent to higher layers are defined in the UlData message specification.

• The formats of parameters to be sent to L1-FPGA are defined in the corresponding L1-FPGA programming model.

The slot context fields are the followings:

* L1-FPGA Configuration flag ({0,1}), (notConfigured, configured).

For debugging purpose, this flag, added by the L1-SW indicates whether the PUSCH resource is configured or not in the L1-FPGA. The L1-SW sets it to 0 at the context creation phase.

---

11410735:
Parameters from UlData\_PuschReceiveReq
Number of PRB allocated to UE.

Please note that not all MCS/PRB allocations are supported. For the list of possible combinations of MCS and PRB allocations, please refer to 5G\_RAN\_UP\_12403 and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20401\_replaced([11124480](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqq2bPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 mcs, [0..28].

This is the Modulation and Coding Scheme (MCS) index. In L1 release 5G21B: (as per 3GPP specification) it is not used for decoding anymore. Used only for determining PNE threshold.

 mcsTable, enum {max64QAM, max256QAM}

MCS table depends on used QAM. Used only for debugging purposes as new concept of providing coding parameters is introduced. max64QAM: up to 64QAM, max256QAM: up to 256 QAM. In 5G21B, max64QAM is supported.

 antPort.

Antenna ports bitmap used to indicate the PUSCH DMRS antenna port indexes before the precoding block. Most significant bit (MSB) represents antenna port 0, MSB-1 represents antenna port 1 etc. The PUSCH DMRS antenna port indexes can be derived based on the antPort bitmap and spatialMode parameter. The antenna ports indexes are used in the RE indexes mapping (see section 6.4.1.1.3 in [1])

Note that the PUSCH antenna ports (after precoding block) use the same SRS antenna ports (see 5G\_UP\_IF\_219). The PUSCH antenna ports indexes are needed for determining whether Orthogonal Covering Code (OCC) is applied or not. In 5G21B, there are two PUSCH antenna ports indexes

Examples to show how to read the binary expension:

0xC000: Antenna port 0 and 1.

0xF000: Antenna ports 0 to 3.

0xCC00: Antenna ports 0, 1, 4, 5.

Parameter not used when spatialMode = "single antenna". In that case antPort is hardcoded in 5G-L1-UL to 0.

For TDD and FDD: Supported antenna port configurations are

0x8000 = rank1 antenna port 0

0xC000 = rank2 antenna port (0,1)

0xA000 = rank2 antenna port (0,2) (not supported when numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData=1)

 spatialMode, [0..2].

This parameter defines the precoding mode of the PUSCH. In L1 release 5G21B

For TDD: values 0 (i.e. single antenna) and 2 (i.e. CLSM) are supported.

For FDD: values 0 (i.e. single antenna) and 2 (i.e. CLSM) are supported.

 codebookIndex, [0..5].

The supported parameter ranges for L1 release 5G21B are as follows:

For TDD and FDD: [0..5] for Rank 1 reception and [0..2] for Rank 2 reception.

 nscId, {0,1}.

This parameter is part of the scrambling sequence initialization equation, and of the DMRS and PTRS init value from 3GPP, it is implemented in L1 release 5G21B.

 puschTransformPrecoderFlag U8 {0, 1} PUSCH Tranform precoder Flag: 0: disabled 1: enabled (For more details, see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1004)

 dmrsScramblingSequenceInt, [0.. 65535].

DMRS Scrambling sequence initialization, which is systematically used for descrambling (Note that descrambling is a decoder feature, whereas DMRS is a reference sequence for receiver, not per say related to scrambling, even though the name would imply it. It is a suitably changing and has relatively large value). Can be set to Physical Cell Id in case RRC did not configure it at UE side. Field used; [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N nscid ID". The text is blurry and appears to be in a light blue color.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2101.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] from 38.211, section 6.4.1.1.1.

When Transform Precoding (DFT-s-OFDM) is enabled (i.e. puschTransformPrecoderFlag=1)  this parameter  stands for the 3GPP parameter  [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a close-up of text with a blurred background. The visible text appears to be "n RS ID". The letters are in a sans-serif font and have a gradient effect, with colors ranging from blue to yellow and pink. The text is positioned on the left side of the image, with the "n" being the leftmost character. The "RS" is positioned above and to the right of "ID". The overall impression is that of a logo or a snippet of a larger text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2101.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] from 38.211, section 6.4.1.1.1.2

When transform precoding is disabled, puschTransformPrecoderFlag= 0.

 dmrsScramblingSequenceInt is sent to L1-FPGA. L1-FPGA should compute the initialization value (dmrsCInit [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains text that is blurry and difficult to read. The visible text appears to be "DMRS" and "cimir".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2101.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] hereunder, as well as PtrsCInit [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "c PTRS init". The text is blurry and appears to be in a light blue color with a slight gradient. The letters are capitalized, except for "init" which is in lowercase.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2101.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]) of the second m-sequence ([image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "x₂(t)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2101.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]), used for the DMRS pseudo-random sequence generation.

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$C\_{init}^{DMRS} = (2^{17} \cdot (14 \cdot n\_s + 1 + 1) \times (2 \cdot N\_{ID}^{nscid} + 1) + 2 \cdot N\_{ID}^{nscid} + n\_{SCID}) \pmod{2^{31}}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2101.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

Parameters Figure representing the role of PuschCFgldx: [image-0][image_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating a configuration buffer for a given processing slot. On the left, a vertical label reads "PUSCH_NUM-1=n". A curly brace connects this label to a stack of rectangular boxes representing User Equipment (UE) configurations. The boxes are labeled sequentially from "UE @0" at the top, followed by "UE @1", and then a series of dots indicating intermediate entries, culminating in "UE @n". Below "UE @n", there's a black box labeled "UE", followed by more dots, and finally "UE @39" at the bottom. To the right of the UE configuration boxes, there are corresponding labels indicating a "Pusch Ofgldx" value for each UE. "UE @0" is associated with "Pusch Ofgldx = 0", "UE @1" with "Pusch Ofgldx = 1", and "UE @n" with "Pusch Ofgldx = n". The diagram suggests a mapping between the UE index in the configuration buffer and a specific "Pusch Ofgldx" value. The title above the UE configuration boxes reads "Configuration buffer In a given processing slot".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_2106.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] (ID: 11411143)

In order to post process the measurements at L1-SW, the scaling factors described above are combined into a single scaling factor [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "powerUnitPuschdBm" in a stylized, italicized font. The text is light gray and appears to be on a white background. The letters are slightly blurred, giving the impression of motion or a soft focus effect. The word "powerUnitPusch" is written in a larger, more prominent font, while "dBm" is smaller and appears to be a subscript or a unit of measurement.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1363.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] which is described in [21]. WoP!

This parameter shall be used in the next subsection by L1-SW. For CPRI (5G21B\_CPRI and 5G21B\_FDD):

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a stylized font. The formula is:
`scalerPusch = 2^(-(Q1Q-in)) * 2^(3-agcShiftL1-FPGA) * xScale * alphaAgcPusch`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1363.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

 For eCPRI (5G21B\_eCPRI):

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a blurry, light-colored font on a white background. The formula appears to be:
`scalerPusch = 2`
`2(-(Q1Q-in+shift)) 2(3-ageShiftLi-FPGA)`
The text is difficult to read due to the blurriness and low resolution of the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1363.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "\* xScale \* alphaAgcPusch". The text is in a light gray color and is slightly blurred. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1363.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

Where

The font is sans-serif and appears to be slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1363.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4].

 shift = 0 for CPRI and eCPRI.

 [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "xScale = 0.". The text is light gray and appears to be slightly blurred. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1363.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]5 for 5G21B.      pCorChannelType

For CPRI:  PUSCH Scaler parameter

[image-14][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in blue text on a white background. The formula is split into two lines.
The first line reads:
`powerUnitPuschdBm = 0.1 * rxScalingFactor - 20 * log10(NFFT) - 20 *`
The second line reads:
`log10(scalerPusch) + pCorChannelType`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1363.014.png[/image\_path][/image-14].

For eCPRI: PUSCH Sclaer Parameter

[image-15][image\_desc]powerUnitPusch$*{Dm}$ = 0.1 \* rxScalingFactor - 20 \* log$*{10}$(scalerPusch)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1363.015.png[/image\_path][/image-15].

If ( cpriDialectIndication==CPRI-A)

[image-16][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression defining a variable `pCorChannelType`. The expression is a piecewise function.
The variable `pCorChannelType` is equal to:
- `-5dB` in case of FDD 5MHz
- `-2dB` in case of FDD 10MHz
- `0` otherwise[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1363.016.png[/image\_path][/image-16]

Else if(cpriDialectIndication==CPRI-N)

[image-17][image\_desc]The image contains the text "pCorChannelType = 0". The text is slightly blurred and appears to be in a light gray color against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1363.017.png[/image\_path][/image-17]0.1

### 5.4.12.5 L1-FPGA processing

#### 5.4.12.5.0-1 L1-FPGA processing The role of the L1-FPGA is summarized in requirements 5G_L1_4005_replaced( 11410543 )  and 5G_L1_3787_replaced( 11400559 ). It supports the PUSCH channel as specified in: the 3GPP Specification (applicable versions are indicated in section 1.1), DOORS requirements and 5GMax see requirement 5G_L1_3665_replaced( 11396859 ), for the L1 releases addressed in this specification. (ID: 11411155)

#### 5.4.12.5.1 Overview

**5.4.12.5.1.0-1**  (ID: `11411170`)

Overview
The L1-FPGA performs the following processing steps, per NR-slot and per sub-cell:

Step 1: L1-FPGA checks if all required receiver configuration parameters for the NR-slot are available.

Step 2: If the parameters are available, the L1-FPGA processes the received IQ samples per OFDM symbol for the considered NR-slot.

---

| --- | --- | --- |
| L1-FPGA output parameter | No. of samples per NR-slot per subcell per grant | Comments |
| shortTermTaMetric | numRx | 1 value per antenna. At L1-FPGA, the number of samples for FR1 and FR2 and FDD are for compressed and uncompressed CPRI scenarios for different bandwidth configurations. |
| shortTermTaPeakAmp | numRx | 1 value per antenna |
| agcShift | 1 | See requirement 5G\_L1\_4652\_replaced([11404788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WBCPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| noisePower | numRx | value in linear scale, see requirement 5G\_L1\_4742\_replaced([11404902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WBDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| Rssi | numRx | value in linear scale, see requirement 5G\_L1\_4742\_replaced([11404902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WBDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| Sinr | ( [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains text that appears to be a mathematical expression or code snippet. The text is blurry and difficult to read clearly. It reads: "ulDmr sLen + ulDmr sAddPos ) numOf Layers".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2113.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] | DMRS-pilot-wise and layer-wise value in linear scale: o Maximum of 3 DMRS pilot are supported in 5G21B. o Maximum of 2 layers are supported in 5G21B. See requirement 5G\_L1\_4742\_replaced([11404902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WBDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| shortTermCfoMetric | 1 | 1 complex value . See requirement 5G\_L1\_4742\_replaced([11404902](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e9WBDfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| shiftShortTerm | 1 | Magnitude amplification for both Re and Im CFO outputs. |
| ulPmiRank1 | 1 | Please refer to section 5.2.4.4.14 for details on this parameter. This parameter (8-bit) is only computed when the numOfLayers value is equal to 2 otherwise L1-FPGA reports -1 to indicate invalid value. |
| ulPmiRank1Sinr | 1 | SINR for given ulPmiRank1. Computation of ulPmiRank1Sinr is done only when the number of layers in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is 2. |
| ulPmiRank2 | 1 | Please refer to requirement 5G\_L1\_4878\_replaced([11411332](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e91wQPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details on this parameter. This parameter (8-bit) is only computed when the numOfLayers value is equal to 2 otherwise L1-FPGA reports -1 to indicate invalid value. |
| ulPmiRank2Sinr | 2 | SINR for given ulPmiRank2, calculated per layer. Computation of ulPmiRank2Sinr is done only when the number of layers in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is 2. |
| ulRank | 1 | Please refer to requirement 5G\_L1\_4948\_replaced([11497549](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fEzT6_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details on this parameter. UL rank as defined in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1015. Computation of ulRank is done only when the number of layers in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq is 2. Otherwise ulRank is set to INVALID i.e. -1. |
| selfCorr | 1*numRx (Rank 1) 2*numRx (Rank 2) | autocorrelation calculated per RX antenna and per layer. |
| shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt | numRx | numRx complex values. |
| shiftShortTermOfAnt | numRx | Magnitude amplification for both Re and Im CFO of antenna outputs. |
