11411248:
TOE
The time offset estimation (TOE) module takes as input the raw channel of the first DMRS symbol. Where FFT\_offset=  (32\*N\_FFT)/2048
In 5G21B The time offset compensation (TOC) is moved to front end, it is performed before frequency offset compensation for PUSCH DMRS and data symbols. The time offset compensation algorithm on PUSCH is described in section "2.3.2 Time Offset Compensation" of the 5GMax algorithm specification (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_916 5G\_L1\_7703\_replaced([11396538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8tH1vvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). In 5G21B, the time offset is estimated antenna wise using the shorter IFFT method as decribed in 5GMax [the requirement 5G\_L1\_3640\_replaced([11396785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV-_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))]. The output of the TOE module are: the TO values (shortTermTaMetric) and their peak amplitudes (shortTermTaPeakAmp) which shall be provided to L1-SW to build the response messages.
The following constraints are considered:
 HW complexity reduction: the maximum IFFT size is constrained to 256.
 TO precision: the minimum IFFT size is constrained to 64.
 When DMRS RE number exceeds 256, only the first 256 raw channel estimates are selected to perform TO estimation.
Thus the following IFFT size is applied based on the number of DMRS. Note that in 5G21B, the DMRS spacing is equal to 2. Thus the DMRS RE number is equal to [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression written in a fraction format. The numerator is "N\_SC^RB \* numOfPrb" and the denominator is "NpilotSpacing". The text is in a light gray color against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2117.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0], where [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written in a light blue color on a white background. The equation reads:
$N\_{sc}^{RB} = 12$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2117.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1], is the number of subcarriers per PRB.[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "NpilotSpacing" in a light gray color against a white background. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2117.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] is the DMRS REs spacing for one port. In 5G21B, [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "NpilotSpacing = 2.". The text is written in a light gray color against a white background. The font is italicized.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_pukkola\_5G\_L1\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2117.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] Then the IFFT size is selected as following:
If DMRS RE number <= 64
  IFFT size = 64
Elseif DMRS RE number <= 128
  IFFT size = 128
Else
  IFFT size = 256. (Only the the first 256 raw channel estimates are selected to perform TO estimation.
End

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter estimations | Udm OFDM symbols and signals used for Msg3 Rx processing (Msg3 slot) | Udm OFDM symbols and signals used for data Rx processing (data slot) |
| TOE | TOE based on 1st DMRS symbol. |  |
| The output of TOE component is not used for TOC, it is only reported to L2-PS in the response message.The output of TOE component is not used for TOC, it is only reported to L2-PS in the response message. |  |  |

---

#### 5.4.12.5.5 FOC

**5.4.12.5.5.0-1**  (ID: `11411264`)

FOC
From release 5G21B, FOC inside of Demapper is performed as follows:

 inter-symbol and intra-symbol long-term FO are the inputs of FOC for PUSCH data. For 1st RACH MSG3 transmission, long-term FO is not available hence FOC PNC is done with short term FOE value.

 upconversion (OFDM phase compensation ):

o CPRI: it is always performed in FOC block.

o eCPRI: it is performed in RU.

 For beamforming case: the FOC should be done for each beam (a pair of antennas). L2 will send to L1-SW two LT FO for each beam.

For more details see 5GMax [the requirement 5G\_L1\_3640\_replaced([11396785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8uV-_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))].

---

##### 5.4.12.5.6 FOE / corrShort measurement

**5.4.12.5.6.0-1**  (ID: `11411278`)

FOE / corrShort measurement
The FOE performs,

if (numDmrsSymbols > 1)

corrShort = corrShortDmrs from foeDmrs, meas output pre-IDFT foePtrs

else if (PTRS on)

if (transformPrecoding off)

corrShort = corrShortPtrs, meas output from pre-IDFT foePtrs

else

corrShort = corrShortPtrs, meas output from post-IDFT foePtrs

end

else

no FOE (corrShort == 0, meas output from pre-IDFT foePtrs as a HW design decision)

end

---

#### 5.4.12.5.7 PNE-PNC

**5.4.12.5.7.0-1**  (ID: `11411291`)

PNE-PNC
Following the rename of register to “pncEnable / PNC\_ENABLE” (formerly “USE\_FOE\_TO\_PNC”), the PNE-PNC performs,

if (pncEnable == 1)

if (ptrsEnable == 1)

if (transformPrecoding off)

PNE based PNC @ pre-IDFT pnePnc

else

PNE based PNC @ post-IDFT pnePnc

end

else if (msg3\_enable == 1) // [PR586917] refer to Msg3 flag definition in 5G\_L1\_4821\_replaced([11410880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e90iJPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End PR586917]

PNC based on ST FOE @ pre-IDFT pnePnc // active for transformPrecoding ON and OFF

[PR586917]

else if (foeValid == 1) // Long-term frequency offset is available //thakuli: in this case PNC is not needed because FOC is done based on LT FO

PNC based on LT FOE @ pre-IDFT pnePnc // active for transformPrecoding ON and OFF

else if (numDmrs == 1) //thakuli: in this branch, there is no PTRS and only 1 DMRS, thus it is not possible to calculate ST FOE

PNC based on ST FOE @ pre-IDFT pnePnc // active for transformPrecoding ON and OFF

[End PR586917]

else

no PNC // is this a valid branch, should L1-SW guarantee that this is not hit

end

else

no PNC

end

---

#### 5.4.12.5.8 DTX detection

**5.4.12.5.8.0-2**  (ID: `11411313`)

DTX detection
Table summarising DTX method for UCI and UL-SCH over PUSCH:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| UCI or UL-SCH | Number of UCI info bits | Decoder | Reliability based detect | CRC based detect | Final TX detection result |
| UCI | A=1 | 'SoftBitMeth' | 'SoftBitMeth' | Not available |  |
| UCI | A<=2 (no CRC) | 'SoftBitMeth' | 'SoftBitMeth' | Not available | ReliabilityBasedResult AND IFFTBasedResult |
| UCI | 3<=A<=11 (no CRC) | RM decoder | ‘Rel0d5SumAbsSoftBit’ | Not available |  |
| UCI | 12<=A<=19 (6-bit CRC) | Polar decoder | PM (path metric) based DTX detection | 6 bit CRC | CRCResult AND IFFTBasedResult   (After PM based method is available, it will be changed to CRCResult AND PMBasedResult AND IFFTBasedResult) |
| UCI | 20<=A (11-bit CRC for each code block) | CRC based detection only | 11 bit CRC | CRCResult only (as 11-bit CRC is sufficient.) |  |
| UL-SCH | N/A | LDPC | Shorter IFFT method (partial DTX) | 24 bit CRC | if(crc==TRUE) dtx=FALSE else partial DTX |

---

#### 5.4.12.5.9 TPMI, SINR and RI

**5.4.12.5.9.0-4**  (ID: `11411345`)

TPMI, SINR and RI
Requirement for Rank Indication (RI) calculation:

The computation of the uplink rank is done when PUSCH is received with spatial multiplexing rank 2. The computed RI shall be sent as ulRank {1,2} to 5G-L2-PS in the UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message. PUSCH receiver must calculate preferred rank indicator (RI) on its own. Calculation of RI is based on the channel estimates (obtained from DMRS) and is possible only in case of 2-layer transmission (CLSM2) thus L2-PS must then schedule regularly this type of transmission.

The detailed calculations and RI selection methods in 5G21B are described in the 5GMax specification  (see requirement 5G\_L1\_3665\_replaced([11396859](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8u9BvvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

### 5.4.12.6 Latency

#### 5.4.12.6.1 Related Messages and Corresponding Timings

**5.4.12.6.1.0-2**  (ID: `11409619`)

Related Messages and Corresponding Timings
The table lists L1-L2 meesages and related timings.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Message name | To / from | Timings (the naming used below is internal to this document) |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveReq | To L1-SW | T1: time from reception of this message from L2-PS, until the start of the DL NR-slot at the antenna, in which PUSCH is received (boundary of the DL slot). |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs | From L1-SW | T3: time from the start of the DL NR-slot at the antenna until L1 sends this message to L2-PS. |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU | From L1-FPGA | T4: time from the start of the DL NR-slot at the antenna until L1 sends this message to L2-PS. |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD | From L1-FPGA | T6: time from the start of the DL NR-slot at the antenna until L1 sends this message to L2-PS. |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLo | From L1-FPGA | T5: time from the start of the DL NR-slot at the antenna until L1 sends this message to L2-LO. |

Note: in the table, the names “T1, T3, T4, T5, T6” are not defined in DOORS and used in this requirement only.

---

#### 5.4.12.6.2 CPRI timing

**5.4.12.6.2.0-8**  (ID: `11409685`)

CPRI timing

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | M |
| FR1 | If(sfnForProcessing== sfn)   M=500us (slotforProcessing-slot) Else   M=500us (20+slotforProcessing-slot) |
| FR2 | If(sfnForProcessing>= sfn)   M=125us (slotforProcessing-slot) Else   M=125us (80+slotforProcessing-slot) |

#### 5.4.12.6.3 eCPRI 7.2a timing

**5.4.12.6.3.0-3**  (ID: `11409714`)

eCPRI 7.2a timing
Timing requirements for L1-L2 interface (TDD FR2) for eCPRI

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Message name | Time | Min (µs) | Max (µs) |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveReq | T1 | Please refer to [13](b) | Please refer to [13](b) |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs | T3 | 125-T\_TAoffset+ receiveWindowClose(a) | 550-T\_TAoffset+ receiveWindowClose(a) |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU | T4 | 125-T\_TAoffset+ receiveWindowClose(a) | 250-T\_TAoffset+ receiveWindowClose(a) |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqD | T6 | 125-T\_TAoffset+ receiveWindowClose(a) | 250-T\_TAoffset+ receiveWindowClose(a) |
| UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLo | T5 | 125-T\_TAoffset+ receiveWindowClose(a) | 550-T\_TAoffset+ receiveWindowClose(a) |
| Notes: (a) 5G\_UP\_3423: Where receiveWindowClose=Ta3\_max+T34max (Ta3\_max and T34max are provided in requirment 5G\_L1\_3694\_replaced([11396993](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e8vkHfvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  (b) The reference for "UlData\_PuschReceiveReq" for "time T1" can be found from 5G\_L1\_IF\_283\_replaced([11514791](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR5rpPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L1\_IF\_285\_replaced([11514834](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR5rqvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). |  |  |  |

diagram (gNB, FR2) for CPRI (TDD): [image-0][image_desc]The image is a timing diagram illustrating the data flow and processing within a system, likely related to wireless communication (NR - New Radio). The diagram depicts events and messages exchanged between different components over time. Here's a breakdown of the elements: Components/Entities: * L1 ingress point: Represents the entry point for data into the Layer 1 (L1) processing. * Antennas: Likely refers to the radio frequency antennas. * L1-SW: Software component of Layer 1. * Loner CPRI Rx port: A specific port for receiving data via CPRI (Common Public Radio Interface). * L1-FPGA: Field-Programmable Gate Array component of Layer 1. Timing References and Events: * Timing reference at gNB: Start of DL data NR-slot at the order: A primary timing reference point. * T_BB1ToAnt &gt; 0: Start of DL data NR-slot at the CPRI Tx port: Indicates the start of downlink (DL) data for a NR slot at the CPRI transmit port, with a condition related to T_BB1ToAnt . * T_AntToBB1 - NTA_Offset &gt; 0: Start of UL data NR-slot at the CPRI Rx port: Indicates the start of uplink (UL) data for a NR slot at the CPRI receive port, with a condition related to T_AntToBB1 and NTA_Offset . * T_BB1ToAnt: A time marker. * T_AntToBB1 - T_TAoffset: A time marker. * Slot #n: Represents a specific time slot, likely in a frame structure. * T1max, T1min, T3max, T3min, T4min, T5min, T6min: Various time constraints or durations. * This time &gt;= T1min - 100: A time condition. * time: The horizontal axis represents time, flowing from left to right. Messages/Data Flows: * UIData_PuschReceiveReq receive window: A request for receiving data, with a specified window. * L1 SW processing latency &lt; 100µs (assumption): An assumed processing latency for L1 software. * L1 SW processes the request up to L1 FPGA configuration (ind.) for the UL NR-slot #n: Describes the action of L1 SW processing a request for UL NR-slot configuration. * (processing): Indicates a processing phase. * (L2 messages): Suggests the involvement of Layer 2 (L2) messages. * UIData_PuschReceiveRespPs: A response message related to PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) reception. * UIData_Pusch_ReceiveRespHarqu: A response message related to PUSCH reception with HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) - likely for the "upper" or "uplink" part. * UIData_PuschReceiveResplo: A response message related to PUSCH reception - likely for the "lower" part. * UIData_Pusch Receive RespHarqD: A response message related to PUSCH reception with HARQ - likely for the "downlink" part. The diagram visually represents the sequence of operations, including data reception, processing by L1 SW and L1 FPGA, and the generation of various response messages, all synchronized with specific timing references and slot boundaries.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_pukkola_5G_L1_Entity_Level_file_1987.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] (ID: 11409767)
